### Penny — 2017-08-12T21:35:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-nasal-congestion.19459/post-221548>

Mary now for the complex: Tyrosine is the base amino acid for all pigmented proteins like dopamine, melatonin, serotonin and melanin. All aromatic amino acids absorb UV and are excited by it and they activate or deactivate S-S bonds in cysteine or cystine residues. These AA are usually found in surface membrane receptors. Photo-excited tyrosine can fluoresce, decay non-radiatively, or undergo intersystem crossing to the triplet state, from which most of the photochemistry proceeds in the skin. The triplet state tyrosine is rapidly quenched by molecular oxygen (from blood plasma) or nearby residues like tryptophan or disulfide bridges in sulfated proteins (Bent & Hayon, 1975b). Here you begin to see where the Tensegrity 7 blog and sulfated lipids and proteins work with sunlight. CBS defects are present because your lineage was generally from places with very high UV exposure naturally. That defect acts to allow you too cool down easier in equatorial regions.

An important photochemical mechanism in proteins involves reduction of disulfide bridges (S-S) upon UV excitation of Tryptophan and Tyrosine side chains (Kerwin & Rammele, 2007, Neves-Petersen et al., 2002 & 2009a). UV-excitation of tryptophan or tyrosine can result in their photoionization and to the generation of solvated electrons. The generated solvated electrons can subsequently undergo fast geminate recombination with their parent molecule, or they can be captured by electrophillic species like molecular oxygen. Molecular oxygen decreases the chance of pseudohypoxia and this make formation of an exclusion zone (EZ) in water in skin cells. This leads to coherent domains of H3O+, (at a lower pH) that allows photo molecular interactions to occur between cysteine and cystines (EE series of blogs on cysteine/cystine). In the case where the electron is captured by the cysteine, the result can also be the breakage of the disulfide bridge in proteins like glutathione. (Hoffman & Hayon, 1972). Here you begin to see how UV light and the aromatic AA begin to work to create energies that can modify sulfated proteins in the skin to control optical signal of sunlight. Activated electrons from the incident UV light is where the process begins. The UV light creates thiol free radicals.
The resultant free thiol radicals/groups can then subsequently react with other free thiol groups in lipids and proteins in skin to create a new disulfide bridge. Reduction of disulfide bridge with UV excitation of aromatic residues has been shown for proteins such as cutinase and lysozyme (Neves-Petersen et al., 2009a, 2006 & 2002), bovine serum albumin (Skovsen et al., 2009a; Parracino et al., 2011) prostate specific antigen (Parracino et al., 2010), and antibody Fab fragments (Duroux et al., 2007). These phenomenon have actually led to a new technology for protein immobilization called light assisted molecular immobilization (LAMI) LAMI is being used now to design drugs using UV light to PREVENT cancer. This stands in counter distinction of the modern view point that UV is the major cause of cancer. The UV light creates thiol groups that can bind thiol reactive chemicals on surfaces leading to oriented covalent protein immobilization. This is precisely what sunlight does naturally in our skin as I mentioned in Tensegrity 7.
In LAMI, pulsed UV illumination and the interaction of aromatic amino acids can halt activation of cancer cell membrane receptors. HERCEPTIN is a membrane receptor in the breast and epidermal derived growth factor (EDGF) is the main receptor in the skin associated with cancers. The UV illumination affects all downstream reactions that would lead to cancer, shutting down the cells’ biological functions stopping oncogenesis. LAMI uses the same process to halt cancer that is present in normal cells. This new treatment is based upon the fact that all stressed cells release ELF-UV light that cells use to activate the cell’s own cell death program called apoptosis. This has already been documented on two human epidermal cancer cell lines (Olsen B.B. et al., 2007). The photonic dosage necessary for therapeutical results has additionally been determined. It turns out it is pretty low frequency, matching the experimental data we see published in Russian experiments and in Roeland van Wijk’s book, Light Sculpting Light.
This pathway is how cells use UV light from the sun to activate apoptosis pathways and prevents all cancers by deactivating disulfide bridges in cell membrane proteins. HOW?

### Penny — 2017-08-12T21:36:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-nasal-congestion.19459/post-221549>

Throughout 4.5 billion year of molecular evolution, proteins have evolved in order to maintain the spatial proximity between aromatic residues (Trp, Tyr and Phe) and disulfide bridges (S-S) (Petersen et al, 1999). There is a very special spatial geometry relationship that exists because of the interaction with the most powerful part of the solar spectrum of light (UV) measures the collisions. The aromatic amino acids become the first step in determining where the position and geometry of residues to act as nanosized antennas in the protein world that can capture UV light (from ~250-298nm). Once excited by UV light they can enter photochemical pathways likely to have harmful or beneficial effects on protein structures by affecting specific bonds like disulfide bonds in cysteine/cystine which are the most rare amino acids in our proteins. It turns out cysteine/cystine disulfide bridges in proteins are excellent quenchers of the excited state of aromatic residues by UV light. This means they decrease the power present in UV light in excited aromatic amino acids. In this way, they contribute to protein stability and activity, thereby stabilizing ubiquitin rates. UV light excitation of the aromatic residues is known to trigger electron ejection from their side chains (Bent & Hayon, 1975a; Bent & Hayon, 1975b; Bent & Hayon, 1975c; Creed, 1984a; Creed, 1984b; Kerwin & Rammele, 2007, Neves-Petersen et al., 2009a). These electrons can be captured by disulfide bridges in things like glutathione, leading to the formation of a transient disulfide electron adduct radical, which will dissociate leading to the formation of free thiol groups in the protein. This photochemical change leads to non optical signaling. Once disulfide bonds are broken we can inactive detrimental cell membrane receptors that cause epidermal cancers like EDGF. The irony in all this is that UV prevents and does not cause cancer by these mechanisms. More irony for the skin and eye docs: This mechanism is now being used to develop drugs using nanotechnology and the ability of cells to make ELF-UV light. There is more…………

Light can change the properties of biomolecules and the number of drugs found to be photochemically unstable is steadily increasing daily in medicine. The effects of light on drugs include not only degradation reactions but also other processes, such as the formation of radicals, energy transfer, and luminescence. Adequate protection for most drug products during storage and distribution is needed. Indeed, proper storage conditions that secure protection from UV and visible radiation are essential for the efficacy of many common dermatologic drugs. If a drug is exposed to fluorescent tubes and/or filtered daylight for several weeks or months before it is finally administered to the patient, the drug may be altered. # Thread: Going on holiday to Turkey, but I do NOT sweat, how to cope?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-on-holiday-to-turkey-but-i-do-not-sweat-how-to-cope.19477/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Paul Keenan — 2017-05-14T16:54:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-on-holiday-to-turkey-but-i-do-not-sweat-how-to-cope.19477/post-214031>

I can not tolerate the heat. My heart races, my adrenaline is up and I have breathing problems.

I am in cloudy Manchester, UK now, I am goinf to Dalaman Turkey for a family wedding in 12 days.

Any tips? cheers

### seanb4 — 2017-05-14T17:49:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-on-holiday-to-turkey-but-i-do-not-sweat-how-to-cope.19477/post-214043>

I have the same problem as you. You may want to look at the symptoms for POTS. Many things can cause it. I have been able to cause some sweating through constant water and some salt intake. If your aldosterone/vasopressin is low then you are constantly dehydrated even when you are drinking water and peeing it all out 30mins later.

You might want to read through @satorihearts journal also. He has some interesting ideas on similar subject.

### Paul Keenan — 2017-05-14T20:37:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-on-holiday-to-turkey-but-i-do-not-sweat-how-to-cope.19477/post-214053>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=214043)
>
> I have the same problem as you. You may want to look at the symptoms for POTS. Many things can cause it. I have been able to cause some sweating through constant water and some salt intake. If your aldosterone/vasopressin is low then you are constantly dehydrated even when you are drinking water and peeing it all out 30mins later.
>
> You might want to read through @satorihearts journal also. He has some interesting ideas on similar subject.

Hi, I have POTS.

The more I drink, the more I am dehydrated.

### JanSz — 2017-05-14T23:57:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-on-holiday-to-turkey-but-i-do-not-sweat-how-to-cope.19477/post-214059>

> [Paul Keenan said:](/goto/post?id=214053)
>
> Hi, I have POTS.
>
> The more I drink, the more I am dehydrated.

I use this to dilute desmopressin that comes in the bottle, so I can use it for prevention of many nightly bathroom trips.
If you are low on vassopressin, may need it at full strength.

..

### FNJORD — 2017-05-15T04:33:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-on-holiday-to-turkey-but-i-do-not-sweat-how-to-cope.19477/post-214067>

I used to abhor the heat. I had very low tolerance and it made me feel ill. Hot yoga changed that completely... I handle heat beautifully now and even enjoy it. Maybe buy some classes and check into a GENTLE hot yoga class every day until then. It will change your perspective and possibly your body's ability to tolerate heat. Incidentally, it did not make my tolerance to cold worse.. I am better with both though if I skip cold for a while I start to feel very wimpy.

Hey everybody,

I'm staying in Indonesia at the moment (living in germany usually). I get a lot of full spectrum light most of the day. I get a nice tanning even though my skin can be very red in the evening. Since 1 or 2 days some areas stay white, building disseminated spots, others beeing connected. I knew about on area since my 18/19th birthday being like that at my chest but it seems there are a lot more by now (31years old). Any ideas what to do? I drink about 4liters to stay hydrated, try to eat seefood though most food ist vegetarian because of the area where I'm staying at. Compared to my days in Germany i feel pretty good, sleep well, don't get hungry a lot. So it seems light and darkness is doing its job.
The white spots look weird though.

# Thread: Involuntary Tummo: My Spine Is Turning Cold Water Warm
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-tummo-my-spine-is-turning-cold-water-warm.19490/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2017-05-16T20:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-tummo-my-spine-is-turning-cold-water-warm.19490/post-214151>

Got in shower at 56 degrees today after doing 53 deg. the last two, and the following happened:

Around five minutes in, I put the shower head up and down my spine and areas with brown fat (upper shoulders, etc...), and the water actually started getting warm. Was able to repeat this several times and then I was able to warm the water up almost everywhere except top of my head. This was not like the water just getting less cold, this water was _warming_ me up.

I thought the water temperature must have gotten warmer since I turned the shower on, but the thermometer showed _the water temperature had actually dropped while this was happening.
_
It was most pronounced in areas of brown fat concentration, so I wonder if I get more brown fat activation by targeting areas other than the BF, itself.

So since I've been getting way more light (not been drinking any more water than usual) I have been peeing a lot more. Like, A LOT more. Wondered if there was any science behind this or if it's just a coincidence...

### drezy — 2017-05-17T19:47:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-ass-question-about-peeing.19493/post-214204>

How would you self evaluate your recent light hygiene and sleep quality lately?

[Tensegrity 5](https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-5-magnetic-sense/) might have a puzzle piece for you. During my weightloss/trimming phase, due mostly to the sun and improved sleep, my son said "Dad why do you need to pee so much?" We read this excerpt:

> Melatonin at night is designed to really clamp down our protein polymers even tighter, by increasing our magnetic sense. It does this by increasing oxygen tensions in our tissues by **increasing electron flow to mitochondria by breaking down our fat at night**. Excess light makes us fat. Certain foods only grown in excessive light periods.
>
> When fat is broken down during sleep in your body, **it turns into water and carbon dioxide. The water is in the form of sweat and urine**. The sweat cools you down to increase your mitochondria’s magnetic sense. Urine is old recycled water that has poor electron carrying capacity. It is less dense than your blood plasma and your CSF. Less dense water is expelled electrically and magnetically by your kidneys. The more dense water is the more electrons it can carry. CSF is more dense by design. This happens by the action of your choroid plexus in your brain. This is why in cold temperatures make you urinate quickly. You are ridding your body of less dense water and structuring more dense water in your CSF naturally. This cycle occurs via water carried in your blood plasma. This is how you structure your own water naturally.

It does this by increasing oxygen tensions in our tissues by **increasing electron flow to mitochondria by breaking down our fat at night**. Excess light makes us fat. Certain foods only grown in excessive light periods.

The sweat cools you down to increase your mitochondria’s magnetic sense. Urine is old recycled water that has poor electron carrying capacity. It is less dense than your blood plasma and your CSF. Less dense water is expelled electrically and magnetically by your kidneys. The more dense water is the more electrons it can carry. CSF is more dense by design. This happens by the action of your choroid plexus in your brain. This is why in cold temperatures make you urinate quickly. You are ridding your body of less dense water and **structuring more dense water in your CSF** naturally. This cycle occurs via water carried in your blood plasma. This is how you structure your own water naturally.

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)
When I see description "structured water"
I think of EZ water (H3O2).
So, structuring is a process of converting bulk water into EZ.
EZ water is able to exist only in small confined places or as very thin thickness next to any surface.
So, it is easy for me to see discussions about structured water in mitochondria or inside most cells. (They are small enough to hold EZ in their whole volume)
But not so when discussions are about extra cellular spaces.
CSF exist mostly in large extra cellular spaces, so it should be mostly H2O, bulk water, (not H3O2).

Mitochondria makes water.
Most (**or all** ??) intra-cellular water is originated in mitochodria.
So, there is a (uni-directional) flow from (mitochondria) to (inside of cell) to (outside of cell)

So I am confused when Jack said:
**structuring more dense water in your CSF** naturally
it seems that he talks about different structuring than converting bulk water into EZ.
or may be he is saying that thickness of EZ over say surface of brain ventricles is increasing because temperature there have fallen?

Do you have something to add?

......

# Thread: De-Fluoridating Bath Water For CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/de-fluoridating-bath-water-for-ct.19494/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2017-05-17T22:20:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/de-fluoridating-bath-water-for-ct.19494/post-214218>

Dr. K,

What's the best way to get all of the toxic bullshit out of the tap water I'm using for CT until I move and get a better water source?

I've read about everything from Vitamin C to Benotinite Clay for removing fluoride/chloride, etc...

What's the protocol or tools that you would recommend?

Thank you,

Adam

### WildForWesties — 2017-08-13T00:21:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/de-fluoridating-bath-water-for-ct.19494/post-221563>

I have the same question. There are chlorine filters you hang on the faucet of the tub - lots of models on Amazon. This has been asked several times and I haven't seen an answer yet for fluoride. Maybe there isn't one.

### Just Breathe — 2017-09-05T23:35:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/de-fluoridating-bath-water-for-ct.19494/post-222939>

I use an epsom salt product that has vitamin C in it. A tablespoon seems to do the job. It's my understanding Vitamin C neutralizes the chlorine and fluoride.
For me, it's a noticeable effect for bathwater over my non-vitamin C showers.

there are vitamin C shower filters, berkey shower filters, etc. I've not seen anyone recommend those bathballs that filter tub water.

### Greenie — 2018-01-24T01:17:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/de-fluoridating-bath-water-for-ct.19494/post-233289>

Cool, didn't know Berkey makes a fluoride removal filter for the shower/tub. I have wondered how to modify one to fit onto a faucet.

### Sandra — 2018-01-24T04:54:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/de-fluoridating-bath-water-for-ct.19494/post-233315>

I seem to remember reading a few years ago that Jack said the benefits to CTing far outweighs the presence of chlorine in the water (though I totally agree it would be great to have it out of the water).

I am fairly certain the only way to remove fluoride from water is by reverse osmosis.

### Alison N — 2018-07-13T18:51:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/de-fluoridating-bath-water-for-ct.19494/post-245824>

> For me, it's a noticeable effect for bathwater over my non-vitamin C showers.

Are you still using this for your water? Are you mixing the Epsom/Vitamin C together then using 1 tbs per an entire tub? I've been looking for a filter, both for my shower/tub. Even though Portland doesn't actively add Flouride to the water, the zip code that I live in has vetoed the states decision and they DO add flouride. Yay for me.. I think I'll try the Vitamin C.

# Thread: Clearlight Saunas and EMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearlight-saunas-and-emf.19515/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2017-05-21T06:38:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearlight-saunas-and-emf.19515/post-214365>

**Summary**
The Clearlight Sauna I purchased new in December 2016 had a high powered WiFi radio and gave off very high levels of RF EMF. The following is a description of how I discovered this, and what I had to do to eliminate it.

**My Sauna Experience**
Last December I purchased a Clearlight Full Spectrum Sanctuary Sauna. This line of saunas is marketed as having low EMF while providing full spectrum IR.

When I first setup the sauna I measured the sauna panels for electric/magnetic fields and everything checked out as very low. This compared to high EMF measurements in my old sauna with ceramic far IR emitters. I was very happy.

A few weeks later I was measuring the smart meter RF in my basement near my sauna. When I walked past my sauna (which was turned off) I detected a spike in RF. I was surprised, but followed the signal and found it was indeed coming from my sauna. The signal was stronger than a cordless phone. I was blown away.

Initially, I thought it was some kind of high-powered bluetooth radio I was detecting. The audio system has bluetooth capability to let you stream audio to the stereo.
It turns out that Clearlight also installs a high power wifi radio in their saunas so that the sauna can be controlled from a wireless device throughout a persons home. I was not told my sauna had this feature, and was not aware of it

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

From that point, it took a few months and about 20 emails to finally get directions from Clearlight on how to disable the radio.

**Image Album**
Here's an album of 22 images showing what I did to detect & disable the radio. In the description for each image is more information & directions.

[http://imgur.com/a/C3PZJ](http://imgur.com/a/C3PZJ)

The process also required completely disabling the audio system to turn off the bluetooth. If you have a Clearlight Sauna and want to disable your radio, the procedure may be similar, but Clearlight tells me that there are several different designs out there that may vary slightly.

**Conclusion**
Throughout this process, I found Clearlight was very pleasant to deal with and they seem to have very good intentions to build the healthiest sauna they can with the features that people want. However, even with that they unknowingly shipped me a super high EMF sauna marketed as low EMF. The problem apparently is that people want too many wireless features and they're added without even considering/appreciating the implications.

Having a meter and testing everything has become a necessity of life.

**Sauna Biohacks**
With the RF EMF eliminated, I tweaked the sauna by lining the walls with aluminum foil to more evenly reflect the IR. I notice the difference and it's great.

You can see the external speakers I now use due to the sauna stereo being completely disabled to turn off the bluetooth. The wires for my external speakers slip under the door. No big deal, but definitely not what I was expecting.

### thrive1992 — 2018-10-05T01:58:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearlight-saunas-and-emf.19515/post-252378>

How are you going with your FIR sauna protocol?

I am thinking of getting the Sun Stream Evolve Mini as clearlight is out of my budget.

Jack,

I live at 42 deg Lat./87 deg. Long. (right outside Chicago), and I'm confused on summer eating.

I've seen you recommend the following in various places:

1) Higher carbs in summer and ketosis being a dumb thing to do out of season

2) Ketogenic epi-paleo diet when cold adapting

How do I reconcile these two when cold adapting in the summer through CT?

Is it a matter of prioritizing which is more important based on my labs, or would you just follow summer eating recommendations while proceeding with the CT protocol as normal?

32 yr. old Male, no acute health conditions to speak of/have never been diagnosed with any serious illness or condition.

Thank you,

Adam

# Thread: collection of scientific papers finding adverse biological effects or damage to health from wifi,etc
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collection-of-scientific-papers-finding-adverse-biological-effects-or-damage-to-health-from-wifi-etc.19532/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-05-24T14:21:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collection-of-scientific-papers-finding-adverse-biological-effects-or-damage-to-health-from-wifi-etc.19532/post-214566>

Immunohistopathologic demonstration of deleterious effects on growing rat testes of radiofrequency waves emitted from conventional Wi-Fi devices. Journal of Pediatric Urology
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22465825](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22465825)

Use of laptop computers connected to internet through Wi-Fi **decreases human sperm motility and increases sperm DNA fragmentation**. Fertility and Sterility
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22112647](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22112647)

Laptop expositions affect motility and induce **DNA fragmentation in human spermatozoa** in vitro by a non-thermal effect: a preliminary report
[http://wifiinschools.org.uk/resources/laptops+and+sperm.pdf](http://wifiinschools.org.uk/resources/laptops+and+sperm.pdf)

**Modulation of wireless (2.45 GHz)-induced oxidative toxicity in laryngotracheal mucosa of rat by melatonin.**
**[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23479077](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23479077)**

****Effects of selenium and L-carnitine on oxidative stress in blood of rat induced by 2.45-GHz radiation from wireless devices.**
**[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19396408](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19396408)**

**Non-Thermal Effects and Mechanisms of Interaction Between Electromagnetic Fields and Living Matter", edited by Livio Giuliani and Morando Soffritti for the "European Journal of Oncology" - Library Vol. 5 of the National Institute for the Study and Control of Cancer and Environmental Diseases "Bernardo Ramazzini", Bologna, Italy, 2010, Part I and Part II.**
**[http://www.icems.eu/papers.htm?f=/c/a/2009/12/15/MNHJ1B49KH.DTL](http://www.icems.eu/papers.htm?f=/c/a/2009/12/15/MNHJ1B49KH.DTL)**

****Drosophila oogenesis as a bio-marker responding to EMF sources**
**[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23915130](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23915130)**

****2.45-Gz wireless devices induce oxidative stress and proliferation through cytosolic Ca²⁺ influx in human leukemia cancer cells.**
**[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22489926](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22489926)**

****Melatonin modulates wireless (2.45 GHz)-induced oxidative injury through TRPM2 and voltage gated Ca(2+) channels in brain and dorsal root ganglion in rat.**
**[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22019785](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22019785)**

****Protective effects of melatonin against oxidative injury in rat testis induced by wireless (2.45 GHz) devices.**
**[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23145464](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23145464)**

****Effects of wi-fi signals on the p300 component of event-related potentials during an auditory hayling task.**
**[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21714138](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21714138)**

**2.45 GHz microwave irradiation-induced oxidative stress affects implantation or pregnancy in mice, Mus musculus.**
**[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23334843](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23334843)**

****Selenium and L-carnitine reduce oxidative stress in the heart of rat induced by 2.45-GHz radiation from wireless devices.**
**[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21360060](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21360060)**

# Thread: Why Did Spaceflight Lengthen Telomeres?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-spaceflight-lengthen-telomeres.19551/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2017-05-27T06:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-spaceflight-lengthen-telomeres.19551/post-214826>

Jack,

Reading your Ubiquination 1 blog post; why did this astronaut's telomeres get longer in space? I guess you could argue it was some kind of pre-cancerous state? Even the article title has nothing positive to say about space travel.

[http://www.nature.com/news/astronaut-twin-study-hints-at-stress-of-space-travel-1.21380](http://www.nature.com/news/astronaut-twin-study-hints-at-stress-of-space-travel-1.21380)

Relevant part is right after the heading "Space Case":

"Studies of the twins' telomeres, the caps on the ends of their chromosomes, showed that during spaceflight Scott's telomeres grew to be longer than his brother's. “That is exactly the opposite of what we thought,” says Susan Bailey, a radiation biologist at Colorado State University in Fort Collins. A second lab has studied the same samples and confirmed this puzzling increase in telomere length, she says.

Once Scott returned to the ground, the length of his telomeres returned to his pre-flight levels relatively quickly. The scientists are working to figure out what this means, and are running a separate study of telomere length in ten unrelated astronauts that, when completed in 2018, may shed more light on how spaceflight affects telomeres.
"

# Thread: Taoist BIGu State - Ketosis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taoist-bigu-state-ketosis.19568/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mobeus — 2017-05-30T23:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taoist-bigu-state-ketosis.19568/post-215142>

BIGu is an ancient Taoist practice that literally translates to "grain avoidance". The kings started feeding grain to the peasants, but the Qigong practitioners knew better, based upon several thousand years of written history.

In 2003 at Penn State there was a conference which included over 150 professionals (many with PhD's) who have not eaten any solid foods for many years. Even whole families with children who almost never eat. They might have an egg or some nuts or a fruit maybe once a month. Their bodies are in the BIGu state, they are living on light, and most practice Qigong.

[https://www.scribd.com/document/40294944/Bigu-Conference-Report-PennState-June-2000](https://www.scribd.com/document/40294944/Bigu-Conference-Report-PennState-June-2000)

I did not see anything in these forums about BIGu, so figured best to post this here for discussion. Thanks to the late/great Professor Rustum Roy for organizing this conference.

### tola — 2017-06-19T20:42:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taoist-bigu-state-ketosis.19568/post-216559>

I'd bet most on this forum realize why you would retire to the mountains, live in a cave, drink spring water, get lots of sun, build an energetic relationship with your organs and glands, and reap the benefits of the stars at night. it's kinda cool to see what's going on in this forum as it paralells with taoist alchemy in so many ways.

# Thread: CT madness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-madness.19577/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Brett Jacques ND — 2017-05-31T22:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-madness.19577/post-215191>

Here in Huntington Beach I do my CT in the Pacific. Yes we have a lot of Great Whites right now but I continue. Yes I've been stung once or ten times by stingrays still I persist. I've harassed by young sea lions, entertained by dolphins, scared by water birds popping up right next to me but today took the prize for weirdness. I was approaching the shoreline when I noticed lots of birds diving in and out of the water. So maybe some fish were running, I don't know. I enter the water with my usual stingray shuffle get to about waist deep and bam! I'm set upon by a cast of crabs, I kid you not. Nipping and pinching my toes, not giving ground as I dance away. Then they coalesce and scurry away. I started laughing out loud for which I'm certain that if anyone saw me would have questioned by sanity. No blood was drawn and no human was harmed in the telling of this story so therefore I had no additional worry with any juvenile great whites in the area looking for trouble.

### drezy — 2017-05-31T23:08:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-madness.19577/post-215193>

Mr. Jacques

If you were not injured and you live a more neolithic life than the crabs do, then who gets the last laugh when it comes to electron transfer? You do! You totally stole a whole bunch of electrons from crabs right there. Just try not to start walking sideways.

### Brett Jacques ND — 2017-06-01T03:18:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-madness.19577/post-215214>

My sideways walking has more to do my skewed view of life at large in the OC particularly HB.

# Thread: Weaning
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2017-06-01T10:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215237>

What are the best foods for weaning from breastfeeding?

### JanSz — 2017-06-01T14:52:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215246>

The longer child is breast fed the better for the child.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-06-01T16:04:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215257>

I'm trying to remember what I weaned my kids with. How old are we talking? I started mushed up foods at six months. I breastfed my boy til he was 3 and my girl til she was 2.

My boy was picky. My girl ate everything and anything! I think we did bits of apple, berries went down well. Some mashed potatoe with stew or mince. Tiny bits of meat.

That's all I can remember

You can still be breastfeeding whilst offering your child food @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)

### healthy2015 — 2017-06-02T17:35:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215310>

He is only 5 months at the moment, I am in no rush to stop breastfeeding just wondered what food others recommended to start the weaning process
Thanks

### ScottishEmma — 2017-06-02T17:39:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215311>

Yoghurt and kefir went down a storm too. And tomatoes...

### Danco3636 — 2017-06-02T17:56:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215315>

My son now turning 6 months on the 10th.
Since month 5 been giving him some real food along with Breast milk.
He likes avocado and egg yolks with Cream from GF cows mashed together.
He has tried real food.
Banana's and cream
Salmon and cream
Sweet potato and cream
Mushed avocados.
Has even tasted a sardine and oyster.

We have just been getting him use to flavors of whole good foods.

But so far his go to food is the egg yolk, avocado and cream mix.

He's also had fermented cod liver oil with his milk at times.

He was born 1 month early but now is strong and viberent.
No sign of being premature.

He gets daily morning and evening sun. In the water daily. Eithier in the bathtub with dad or pool. Has been under water and gets outdoors for long walks with dad.
We also block most if not all blue light at night.
No cell phones around him.
Sleeps on a magnetico pad.
Wifi and electronics off at night.

He now sleeps through the night and often doesn't eat until a big breakfast after about 11-12 hours.

### Valsun12 — 2017-06-12T16:37:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215937>

My recent daughter now 7 months gets eggs(loves them), squash and other veggies mashed (I alternate, I noticed she seems to be spitting up when I mix more than two foods together , cant give her anything from jars so far because I tried 2x and she threw up both times. ...but breastfeeding still--> and I notice she eats lots more breast milk during the night, than when she is has pumped bottled milk during the day.(when I am work)

### JuicerMoz — 2017-07-31T12:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-220499>

Weaning is the process of moving your little one on from a diet of just milk to solid food too. The first stage of weaning helps lay the foundations for healthy eating habits, and teaches your baby how to eat from a spoon, swallow and eventually chew.

### shiran — 2017-07-31T13:42:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-220502>

I gave my kids food only when the teeth came out...
The first foods were:
Bone broth
Avocado
Sweet potato
Thini

Does anyone know much about Cell Salts? My son's teeth have no cavities but they look dingy colored. I asked my pediatrician about REDOX potential and he recommended cell salts and Dr. Schuessler. I need to read up on REDOX. Is there a way to measure it?

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-03T09:07:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-salts.19589/post-215338>

Hi Brenda
If you go to the home page and put redox RX in the search you can find some relevant blogs. DS2 was massively affected by fluoride from a children's toothpaste when he was a toddler (our water isn't fluoridated), and I used complex homeopathic remedies as part of his recovery after finding and removing the cause, and went on to train in it. DH and his father used to swear by cell salts for migraine, (I was always asking _why_ have you got migraine, its symptom treating without looking for causes).... But that was 20 years ago. What worked then doesn't have the same effect now, I think its because the changes in the environment the cells face that cause mitochondrial dysfunction and cellular dehydration affects how the remedies work . Had I used the same homeopathic remedies with DS2 without removing the cause, I don't think they would have been as successful. I think dingy teeth themselves are a sign of a poor redox, and I've noticed that since I've hacked my environment, follow the circadian rhythm and gone full on with the light water and magnetism, (and without cell salts or any other homeopathic treatment or supplements) my teeth are getting whiter, some more than others, but its a process not an event. I'm 60 this year, so its never too late!

Since beginning Jack's protocols obviously I've seen (literally) an improvement in my myopia.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-10-12T18:24:28+0300

>
> I think for 5G shielding isn't going to be enough and is really a shortsighted strategy.

Dr Karim's **Hemburg**, Switzerland project was a fascinating case study in RF/MW radiation mitigation. It certainly seemed to help symptoms of people exposed to radiation in this little town.

However, this project concluded by the mid-2000s and I have not yet seen a follow up study to examine long-term results. Did people remain symptomatically unaffected up to present day? That would be a revealing insight.

Someone mentioned using **Biokinesiology** methods to measure RF/MW mitigation effectiveness using subtle energy devices and that sounds like a good start in trying to obtain reliable metrics. However, I don't suppose there are any published studies on this. We are still in the wild, wild west period of electrosmog protection.

Bear in mind that mainstream consensus is still that 'subtle energy healing' is at best unproven, at worst pure hokum.

Physical RF shielding materials are indeed imperfect at this juncture, and may even present some dangers. So everyone is basically on their own until we get some hard, evidence-based info are what works safely and effectively and what doesn't.

_Caveat emptor._

# Thread: Oh my, getting a little dark over here...

### Maria Bacarella Mole' — 2017-06-06T15:51:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oh-my-getting-a-little-dark-over-here.19600/post-215486>

I've been using the Dminder app and I'm getting ridiculously tan and I was hoping for help.

• Walking around in shorts and tee shirt without sunscreen (which is an incredible departure from being covered up anytime I've been outside in the past)
• We've only had "sun" a handful of days in the last three weeks (it's pretty gloomy here)
• I've been out in sun typically mid morning, way before Solar Noon
• I am Sicilian -- the Scandinavian type with blue eyes, light hair and light skin -- and no facial hair )
• According to Dminder, I'm only at 28% for the month and I'm in it for three weeks with lots of rainy days and clouds.

I really want to up my Vit D naturally but am finding this "Something About Mary, Magda tan" a deterrent.

Any insight or advice would be really appreciated.

### nonchalant — 2019-06-03T22:28:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-bath-ct-and-dry-skin.19605/post-271929>

Eating lots of seafood changes the skin for the better. Stronger, denser, smoother, and moister. I haven't had dry elbows/knees in a couple years. Nice change for me. I used to use butter for an emergency fix. Quickly available when traveling, etc.

I have for a while, however, had something that looks like dry skin... a hazy covering on the visible parts of my arms and legs. I'd wipe them with a damp washcloth to remove it and it would smell a bit sulfurous, like fingernail filings. A little disconcerting. It was more difficult to find the root cause. It was nnEMF, of course, but where? Changing my bed quickly eliminated the problem.

edit: No, now that I think about it some more, the hazy skin started to go away when I stopped wearing exposed elastic waistbands. The new bed just finished the problem.

### Christine_L — 2019-06-03T23:27:22+0300

### Martha Ray — 2019-06-04T01:57:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-bath-ct-and-dry-skin.19605/post-271958>

[**Enjoying posts here on the Jack Kruse Optimal Health Forum!**](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php)
I was fond of lots of skin products in the past but do like the thinking of _not putting on you skin anything you would not eat._ Tallow, Olive Oil and Ghee all sound good. I have those and one retail Tallow cream. I've also been trying Puree of Aloe Vera Lotion with Glycerin by _Forces in Nature (_a _local_ Crozet, VA _product_ sold at Whole Foods) for my forearm radiation damage... my somewhat functional doctor recommended I try to find a body lotion with Neem and Karanja Oils she did not have a source. I did Find a Canadian product from Honey Pot Herbals. Also used _Alaffia_ Unscented Shea Butter for rough hands I get in the winters here in 'Dr. Zach Bush' country where I live... formerly from Houston, TX was a patient of 'preventative doctor' Owen Robbins MD there. I Houston I had lots of humidity and moisture very dry here plus some health crisis and poor lifestyle choices. Been 10+ years now improving Health but have not drilled down on a lot of things. Have to look up every single ingredient in everything. Sensitive to chemicals as well as gluten, nightshades, conventional citric acid....etc.
I was just outside and came and I can confirm that the Tallow does appeal the Great Dane next door was paying attention to my hand.. the one I used as soon as I read this post is _Mother Makings_ _Tallowed Touch_ Sweet Earth Grassfed Beef Tallow Handcrafted in Asheville, NC where our daughters live.
I have more than just dry skin- seriously damaged forearms looking for help with my arms. Paper thing easily cuts and tiny bumps causes large red blotches. I normally wear long sleeves out or hemmed socks when I sun.. not sure if sun helps or hurts but I avoid during the day not on AM sun rise viewing or walks to view sunsets. Oh those Honey Pot Herbals and Mother Makings Tallowed Touch are sold on Etsy if anyone wants to check them out. Hope that's ok to share... I've not posted here 2 times. I am retired and study different 'healths' most of moments-- seriously seeking. Crossed Jack Kruse path but latched on a bit with the passing of time. So glad. I'm a newbie and will be saying that for about 5+ years I suspect.

### Kathy1967 — 2019-08-18T02:07:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-bath-ct-and-dry-skin.19605/post-276184>

Hi Martha, I hope you enjoy it here! My name is Kathy and I’m not a new member… I don’t post often because I am unfortunately inept at anything social media… I always seem to ask questions in the wrong place and I can’t figure out how to get to the right place! I wish I could give you some advice about your skin but what I have been using lately is Emu oil from Walkabout Health Products and it feels really really good! I’m curious if we put on our skin if it will prevent the solar callus that Jack talks about from building up. I’m a redhead and I always get Burns because I hate to wear sunscreen but now that I’m going out in the UV light every morning at sunrise I’m hoping to change all that… I’m just afraid the Emu oil might hinder the benefits of the UV. If anybody knows the answer to that I think myself and Martha would be appreciative!

Thank you for your kindness Kathy ... I will look into Emu Oil. One practionier years ago suggested grapeseed oil... I used that and olive oil for a while but did not see much improvement. Whipped Shea butter feels good on my skin but my skin is so very thin on my forarms that I'd rather use food so will try tallow and Ghee next. My forarm skin thinness surfaced after 2.5 months of AZ CTCA cancer treatment. I was atrophied and in a wheel chair when I came home and after the long flight home I developed a blood clot in my left left that did not respond to using **Coumadin/** (**warfarin**) had to have vien surgery and was then gave myself the blood thinner Arixtra shots daily in my stomach for 3 months.... all the drugs they provided me that year had nasty side effects.-- If my genetics were checked before cancer treatment they might have noticed to provide more protective care against blood clots as I am genetically at more risk for them. Genetics would have also helped them know typical doseing of **Coumadin/** (**warfarin**) would not work on me...I did not respond to it and had to have the vien surgery. Hindsight of things I learned years later.

Hoping someone can answer you about the Emu Oil hindering or not with your sun intake...

Here’s hoping that we all learn more ourselves so we can provide our own protection.

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-13T01:53:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-and-emf.19612/post-218627>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=218561)
>
> I'd love to hear Jack talk about the connection between the moon and menstruation. I'm not sure this has been spoken about before? @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/)

He did just post on the FB about the moon cycles affecting marine life and a specific protein that has been discovered that can help animals sense moon phase wrt mating.

If I knew how to link to a FB post I would, but here is the article he linked: [http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2007/10/071019-coral-spawning.html](http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2007/10/071019-coral-spawning.html). and the text he wrote below. You can scroll his FB page for a moment, and look for a moon. He posts a lot so it's not immediate, but it was only a few days ago

"Tonight is the key night for living things. July 9 is also auspicious for celestial reasons: It’s the longest day of the year with a full moon and this is critical for many animals to reproduce. On Mexican and Caribbean beaches tonight turtles will be laying eggs. Corals in the sea will be cementing their futures with lunar spawning. The full moom will create interesting changes in tides. The periodic rise and fall of water every 12.4 hours plays a vital role in marine environments, giving rise to a vibrant, uniquely adapted array of intertidal organisms. But beyond the tides, many species in water and on land also make use of the moon’s evocative glow tonight.

Most of all, evolution seems fond of using moonlight as mood lighting for sex: Many species of all shapes and sizes use the moon’s ghostly irradiance, and its 29.5-day waxing and waning, to cue various phases of reproduction. A 2007 study of the coral species Acropora millepora shed light on how the corals do it: The animals possess a light-detecting protein called CRY2 that’s exceptionally sensitive to moonlight’s characteristic blue wavelengths. CRY2, along with the protein CRY1, are widely used among animals to help calibrate their internal biochemical clocks. While other cues such as day length and ocean temperature appear to signal the corals to prepare, moonlight acts as the crucial trigger—telling the corals to let loose clouds of millions of eggs and sperm. That cue used to be important for human females before modern changes.[http://news.nationalgeographic.com/…/071019-coral-spawning.…](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fnews.nationalgeographic.com%2Fnews%2F2007%2F10%2F071019-coral-spawning.html&h=ATMMli_ObXILZPrkBCwAswmxaADh3uvKCarXhzU9CV332WInz0SzGWn65amO4mpuTjLoICzZk4N0nT_TMgrJ9nKDHOq4XMkoGssVs_8KOxJx6cHLEVZvEmAHjoUIdS5HvBHew0kflIORXfGgCJdHGftSkDd0YoTEXcLY&enc=AZO1qeuX_WVUKrFM50AaeBRn2xES-0PvVIVy-2QVMSG1xSy_II9AvUKhqZvIST1D7Mk_IW5b7sfjQDCv3bajnI4qI0AsbGw9_qqgiWTGubgqUf6meF9Z6kurfZhstOafb9E4CAKCdLrjH-DetbAe4nmYxGt-m8QxiCH4uMveH1348vUjF_2O73iOz2M3KKxnAR7Q9l2oFH0gN3xna2K8ROew&s=1)"

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-13T09:06:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-and-emf.19612/post-218657>

> [ValerieBee said:](/goto/post?id=218627)

CRY2, along with the protein CRY1, are widely used among animals to help calibrate their internal biochemical clocks. While other cues such as day length and ocean temperature appear to signal the corals to prepare, moonlight acts as the crucial trigger—telling the corals to let loose clouds of millions of eggs and sperm. That cue used to be important for human females before modern changes.[http://news.nationalgeographic.com/…/071019-coral-spawning.…](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fnews.nationalgeographic.com%2Fnews%2F2007%2F10%2F071019-coral-spawning.html&h=ATMMli_ObXILZPrkBCwAswmxaADh3uvKCarXhzU9CV332WInz0SzGWn65amO4mpuTjLoICzZk4N0nT_TMgrJ9nKDHOq4XMkoGssVs_8KOxJx6cHLEVZvEmAHjoUIdS5HvBHew0kflIORXfGgCJdHGftSkDd0YoTEXcLY&enc=AZO1qeuX_WVUKrFM50AaeBRn2xES-0PvVIVy-2QVMSG1xSy_II9AvUKhqZvIST1D7Mk_IW5b7sfjQDCv3bajnI4qI0AsbGw9_qqgiWTGubgqUf6meF9Z6kurfZhstOafb9E4CAKCdLrjH-DetbAe4nmYxGt-m8QxiCH4uMveH1348vUjF_2O73iOz2M3KKxnAR7Q9l2oFH0gN3xna2K8ROew&s=1)"

### drezy — 2017-06-23T16:01:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-intermittent-fasting-best-practices.19619/post-216887>

If you have no debilitating issues then go try out a few combinations.

> I'm finding it challenging to get 5-7 hours of general sunlight exposure though.

I understand that. I try to at least get the morning IR and some solar noon light. I used to use dminder to remind me, but I think it's built into my habits now.

I just tell people I talked to a neurosurgeon and he told me I've got a condition where I need morning and solar noon light otherwise I deteriorate. Give it a try while everyone else self identifies with their disease as their badge you'll fit right in except your condition is the human condition. Maybe you'll even lure a few people outside with you.

EDIT: There are also tan through t-shirts. If you can't increase time you could use these to increase surface area exposed during lunch break walks.

Yea man technically I was a former minor diety lol, but I had the advantage of a undergrad in math/physics and part of a masters in biophysics before I went to med school. I was already intuiting that the interaction of the electromagnetic force is something biology is seriously overlooking! I'm just starting to feel chi in my body , I didn't have enough energy to feel it before. One of the oddest experiences I had since practicing sun gazing and the other changes in Dr. K's protocols is that I can feel the earth for the first time in my life, I feel the plants like I can feel whatever field they project, it feels like smooth static electricity, its not jolty like static electricity but has a similiar subjective sensory feeling to that. There's so freakin much to learn man!

Antenna's up! haha
Asad

# Thread: Trashed Hormones!

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs

### freeheartbk — 2017-06-13T22:34:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trashed-hormones.19638/post-216099>

Hi all! I have chronic lyme and chronic active EBV, my hormones are totally trashed. Saliva test about a year ago showed very low cortisol throughout the day (as well as low estrogen and even lower progesterone) so I started taking hydrocortisone, which has helped a bit with energy.

I recently had other hormones checked as well and the results were terrible: DHEA-s at 16.5, Pregnenolone is 19 and Testosterone is less than 3. I just ordered Pure Encapsulations DHEA and Pregnenolone and am very excited to start taking them, but am a bit concerned about combining with the hydrocortisone. How would you suggested I titrate the dosages and possibly wean off the HC while going up on the DHEA and Pregnenolone?

# Thread: lemon/dill/cauliflower sauce
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lemon-dill-cauliflower-sauce.19640/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Coldsnow — 2017-06-14T05:52:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lemon-dill-cauliflower-sauce.19640/post-216126>

this one borders on a soup but can easily go into the sauce world. Its my girlfriends
invention; Marisol, so she gets the credit - the sauce muse is speaking

[http://www.marisolkim.com/](http://www.marisolkim.com/)

\-----

a cup of steamed cauliflower
lots of lemon juice, fresh with some zest peal - like a lot of lemon
sea salt
lots of dill
cilantro
diced ginger simmered in a pan of butter with tumeric and black pepper
till things are soft; really soft and you can smell.

When the flavors come together in this one it's like a music; like a stringed section on the Clifford
Brown L.P. - i really love to go overboard with the herbs though

\*i like the dance with the dill on the fish but you can sub for rosemary or really any herb
as the cauliflower is a great vehicle for various flavors

\* you can easily take out the dill and add curry paste and serve on chicken; jus sayin

### caroline — 2017-06-14T09:51:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lemon-dill-cauliflower-sauce.19640/post-216140>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lemon-dill-cauliflower-sauce.19640/post-216232>

We really need some simpler ways of doing stuff on the road. Last night we tried something we had heard on the radio.

Place salmon fillets in a bowl and fill with boiling water, cover with saran wrap and leave for ten minutes. It was beautiful. next time I will put some aromatics in the bowl with the salmon. This time I put some coriander stocks in with the salmon.

Then I made a slurry of coriander and basil and coconut aminos and MCT oil and also a tiny bit of honey.

Somehow I have to learn how to live without my magic bullet on the road!

# Thread: Are you addicted to the internet?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-addicted-to-the-internet.19648/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2017-06-15T09:19:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-addicted-to-the-internet.19648/post-216233>

[What Happens to Your Body When You Use the Internet](http://everlast.mercola.com/r/?id=h79c33496,12c5aae9,131cddff&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118).

This is a great blog by Dr. Mercola. For sure it is all stuff that Dr. K. has been telling us for years now. I was thinking that this format would be great for teenagers to "get it" but it sure struck home with me. I have been trying to spend way less time on my laptop - but I am afraid - my name is Caroline ....and I am an internet junky! It is really hard to limit my time - I always feel like I am missing something! I have been programmed it seems and I hate it!

see what you think......

**By Dr. Mercola**

The internet has made connecting with new business partners, discovering health information and finding long lost friends, easier and cheaper. However, while digital connections have distinct advantages, digital dependence does not. You’ve likely seen — or have been part of — a family outing where one by one, everyone pulls out their phone to check notifications, text messages or email.

It happens in restaurants, on busy streets and commuter trains. The desire to be rewarded by your phone may have even been so great that you endangered your life by attempting to read a text or send one while driving. Toddlers get their own devices to keep them busy and 10-year-old children are carrying their own phones.[1](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn1) Where once children talked on the phone, set up face-to-face time and engaged with real people in real situations, they now spend hours keeping a “streak” alive.

Dependence on digital communication presents several physical and emotional health challenges. Mitochondrial damage, exposure to electromagnetic radiation and failing social skills are just the tip of an iceberg that may have deeper roots than anticipated. Recent research has now identified immediate physical symptoms that occur when your digital devices are just out of reach.

**Digital Dependence Affecting More Than Teens**
Cellphone ownership has reached 95 percent in America, up from 68 percent measured in 2015. Of those owners, 77 percent use a smartphone.[2](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn2) The people who own the smartphones are distributed equally across gender, age and ethnicity, with the lowest number of people owning smartphones being over the age of 65.

Dependence or [addiction to a digital device](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/02/02/are-you-addicted-to-smartphone.aspx) hooked to the internet affected 6 percent of the world population in 2014.[3](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn3) This number may not appear to be significant on the surface, but consider that 6 percent of the world population was over 420 million people.[4](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn4)Comparatively speaking, according to the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime, 3.5 to 7 percent of the world population between 15 and 64 years had used an illicit drug in the past year.[5](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn5)

The percentage of those addicted to the internet may actually be higher as only 39 percent of the world in 2014 had access to the internet,[6](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn6) driving the real percentage of those addicted to 15 percent. Symptoms of addiction are similar to other types of addiction, but are more socially acceptable. The authors of the study found an internet addiction (IA) is:[7](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn7)

_“… \[G\]enerally regarded as a disorder of concern because the neural abnormalities (e.g., atrophies in dorsolateral prefrontal cortex) and cognitive dysfunctions (e.g., impaired working memory) associated with IA mimic those related to substance and behavioral addiction. Moreover, IA is often comorbid with mental disorders, such as attention deficit hyperactivity disorder and depression.”_

Reach Out Recovery identifies conditions that may trigger internet addiction or compulsions, including anxiety, [depression](http://articles.mercola.com/depression/what-is-depression.aspx), other addictions, social isolation and stress.[8](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn8) Internet activity may stimulate your brain’s reward system, much like [drugs and alcohol](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2016/07/28/opioid-painkiller-addiction-conspiracy.aspx), providing a constant source of information and entertainment. While each person’s internet use is different, the results may be the same. Long-term effects may include:

Irritation when someone interrupts your interaction online

Difficulty completing tasks

Increasing isolation

Experiencing euphoria while online

Inability to stop despite the consequences

Increasing stress

**Physical Effects of Internet Withdrawal**
The physical and mental effects of addiction, coupled with the physical effects of withdrawal, may increase your risks for long-term health conditions. In a recent study involving 144 people between the ages of 18 and 33, researchers discovered both heart rate and blood pressure are affected in those who report spending extended periods of time online.[9](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn9)

Past research has associated cold turkey withdrawal of the internet from heavy users will produce anxiety type symptoms, similar to those experienced by people addicted to drugs or alcohol.[10](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn10) The current study also linked physiological changes, including an average of a 3 to 4 percent [increase in blood pressure](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2016/11/30/how-to-lower-blood-pressure.aspx) and heart rate of the participants.[11](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn11) Some participants experienced up to an 8 percent increase.

This was the first controlled demonstration of physiological changes triggered by internet use.[12](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn12) The increases noted during the study were not enough to be immediately life-threatening; however, these types of changes are associated with anxiety and a reduction in the function of the immune system.

The changes in anxiety levels may also be a physiological trigger for users to re-engage with their digital devices in order to reduce the physical response and anxiety level. Dr. Lisa Osborne, co-author of the study from Swansea University, commented:[13](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn13)

_“A problem with experiencing physiological changes like increased heart rate is that they can be misinterpreted as something more physically threatening, especially by those with high levels of anxiety, which can lead to more anxiety, and more need to reduce it.”_

In other words, especially in people who may experience anxiety more frequently, the physical symptoms of internet withdrawal may increase their anxiety and lead to behaviors to reduce it — namely, going back to using the internet.

**Putting Down Your Phone May Raise Your Anxiety Level**
Forty percent of the participants in this study admitted they had some level of an internet-related problem and acknowledged they spent too much time online. Participants reportedly spent an average of five hours each day on the internet and 20 percent spent over six hours a day. By far the most common reasons for engaging online were social media and shopping.

Previous studies from this same group of researchers have demonstrated study participants would experience short-term increases in anxiety levels when their digital devices were removed.[14](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn14) When those devices were removed for longer periods of time, they reported increases in loneliness and depression, with some researchers finding changes to the actual structures in the brain.

Research psychologist Larry Rosen, Ph.D., and his colleagues at California State University looked at the effect technology has had on our anxiety levels. They have found the typical person checks their phone every 15 minutes, whether or not they heard a notification from the device. In his words you may be thinking:[15](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn15)

_“Gee, I haven’t \[checked\] in \[on\] Facebook in a while. I haven’t checked on this Twitter feed for a while. I wonder if somebody commented on my Instagram post.”_

These thoughts generate increased secretion of cortisol, which begins to increase your anxiety levels. Eventually, you notice the rising anxiety and seek a way to reduce the experience. Checking in to your social network on your phone may be one of the ways you’ve found to reduce your anxiety.

The authors of the study from Swansea University speculate that internet use is driven by more than short-term excitement or the joy of using [technology](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2015/04/01/wearable-technology-devices.aspx). Instead, it may produce negative physiological and psychological changes, such as anxiety that may drive you back to the device that is causing the problem in the first place.[16](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn16)

Multiple studies from around the world have demonstrated overuse of the internet and digital devices leads to physical and psychological symptoms of addiction[17](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn17) and family dysfunction.[18](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn18) Poor health, unhappiness and depression were found in men and women who report overuse of the internet, but depressed girls demonstrate a higher rate of internet addiction than boys.[19](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn19) Overall, those with an addiction to the internet have lower impulsivity control.[20](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn20)

**Google Would Like You to Keep On Using**
It should come as no surprise that companies that make money when more people spend more time and money on the internet are consciously trying to manipulate your behavior. Former Google product manager Tristan Harris revealed how digital giants are engineering smartphone apps and social media feedback to get you checking and double-checking online.[21](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn21)

However, while internet use is more socially acceptable, digital companies aren’t the only businesses using neurological and psychological strategies to increase their profit margins.[22](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn22) Behavior patterns are often etched into neural pathways,[23](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn23) and when those behaviors are also linked to hormone secretion and physiological responses, they become even more powerful.

In fact, Harris describes the reward process of using a smartphone as “playing the slot machine.”[24](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn24) And, Google has discovered a way to embed that [reward system](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2014/02/06/hugging.aspx) as you use the apps on your phone. This process is so important to digital corporations that Apple turned down a new smartphone app for their store that would help people to reduce their use of the internet and their smartphones.

The goal of any corporation is to increase your use of their product and the potential you will spend more money with them. In the case of smartphone devices, these companies are contributing to programing your actions, and how you think and feel. This is how companies satisfy their advertisers, who are paying for the privilege of your eyes on their ads.

**Have You Been ‘Brain Hacked?’**
Some programmers call this process “brain hacking,” as they incorporate more information from neuropsychology into the development of digital interfaces that increase your interaction with the program. For instance, getting likes on Facebook and Instagram, the “streaks” on Snapchat or cute emojis on text messaging, are all designed to increase your engagement and desire to return.

The continual scroll on [Facebook](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/01/05/facebook-negative-health-effects.aspx) keeps you engaged on the page longer, with a greater chance you’ll click an advertisement on the page. Keeping a “streak” alive on Snapchat keeps you coming back to the app, especially when you have multiple streaks going with multiple people.

Harris describes it as a race to the bottom of the brainstem where fear and anxiety live, two of the most powerful motivators known to advertisers. Both advertisers and computer software developers are using these techniques to write code that will engage your attention.[25](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn25)

**More Physical Effects From Your Digital** ​

# Thread: Epiphany about hormones
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epiphany-about-hormones.19649/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2017-06-15T16:42:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epiphany-about-hormones.19649/post-216267>

My most recent area of interest is hormone levels; that is because I myself have had low hormone levels, which some (including myself) may have considered suboptimal, however I did not have symptoms, and have functioned well despite the levels.

I think I have just come to an epiphany, so I just wrote this thought down:

"Food controls hormone levels. Light controls hormone function".

Am I correct? And if so, what is more important, your hormone levels, or your hormone function? That proves light trumps food. Think about leptin: it increases when we eat food. That is because food provides electrons (and photons from the sun that created it via photosynthesis). So technically, one would think we want to increase our leptin levels, because leptin shuts off hunger in the brain, and increases the metabolic rate via signalling the thyroid. But we all know the key is to be leptin sensitive. It is about being able to activate the leptin receptor in the brain, and that is why light is the key (the leptin molecule is full of photon traps).

I absolutely know I am very leptin sensitive based on how well I thrive and function and how intact my circadian rhythm is, and that food doesn't matter for me: i.e. I can eat 1,500 calories and feel satisfied, or I can stuff my face at a family gathering and eat 4,000 calories, and activate uncoupling so I release the excess as heat, and do not store fat. I attribute this metabolic flexibility under the control of an optimally functioning leptin receptor to my cold adaptation, fasting and abiding by the natural light cycle.

Now the hormone in question it testosterone. Again, it also has photon traps, and so if I have a suprasensitive leptin receptor because I yield a lot of energy from my environment, and less from food (thus keeping leptin levels low assuming food controls hormone levels), then having low testosterone levels, or any low hormone levels should not matter. When you maximize function of something, less is more.

Perhaps E=mc2 can fit into this, where E = energy/function, M = hormone, and c2 = light.

If we can increase c2 (light), then we can increase energy (function) without increasing mass (hormone). But on the contrary, when decrease c2 (light), then we must increase mass (hormone) in order to increase energy (function). This is likely why the food gurus have it all wrong.

Thoughts?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Dysautonomia- i messed something up
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dysautonomia-i-messed-something-up.19657/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aerose91 — 2017-06-17T08:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dysautonomia-i-messed-something-up.19657/post-216411>

I'm not even certain if this is because of dysautonomia but i think it is.
I do everything pretty much right- sun early morning and evening, no emf, if i need to use my phone at night i have a red filter on it, grounding several hours/day and only drink spring water from a spring that i fill from. The other day, after a coffee enema, i was craving salt so i added a little to my water.
Within minutes, my dissociation and light sensitivity thinned out a little for the first time in 4 years, which was amazing! But a host of crappy effects came as well; i got immediatly fluish, headache, diarrhea, kidney pain, peeing non stop and i became emotionally volitale. I'm talking, blubbering like a teenage girl at a Hanson concert.
My guess was the coffee enema dehydrated me past a certain point and now my dysautanomia is all out of whack? I've been messing with balancing salt and potassium the past couple days but it's really tough to get the ratio right. Too much salt, or even too much pure water and im back to emotional basket case, peeing and fluishness within seconds. Also, i get incredibly hungry only _after_ eating now.
Im going to start taking electrolytes again that my doctor used to give me but i don't think that's any panacea. What did i do? And is there any way i can get back to the thinner dissociation without all the side effects? That would be a blessing, at this point.

### JanSz — 2017-06-17T14:20:27+0300

The other day, after a coffee enema, i was craving salt so i added a little to my water.
> Within minutes, my dissociation and light sensitivity thinned out a little for the first time in 4 years, which was amazing! But a host of crappy effects came as well; i got immediatly fluish, headache, diarrhea, kidney pain, peeing non stop and i became emotionally volitale. I'm talking, blubbering like a teenage girl at a Hanson concert.
> My guess was the coffee enema dehydrated me past a certain point and now my dysautanomia is all out of whack? I've been messing with balancing salt and potassium the past couple days but it's really tough to get the ratio right. Too much salt, or even too much pure water and im back to emotional basket case, peeing and fluishness within seconds. Also, i get incredibly hungry only _after_ eating now.
> Im going to start taking electrolytes again that my doctor used to give me but i don't think that's any panacea. What did i do? And is there any way i can get back to the thinner dissociation without all the side effects? That would be a blessing, at this point.
