# Thread: Mick's Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/micks-labs.19661/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Micka — 2017-06-18T08:13:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/micks-labs.19661/post-216470>

Hi there! Longtime kruse listener, first time poster.

I was wanting to get some insight on some recent labs that I have run recently. Did a basic blood panel and DUTCH test, which i’ll attach for reference but below are the main points. If anyone have any recommendations or suggestions further to the advice provided by the supporting report, i'd be very grateful.

27 year old Male
Ocean Grove, VIC, Australia (38.25\*S, 144.51\*E)
187cm, ~95kg, 22% BF (Dexa)

Blood Panel

HBA1c - 5.1% - 32 mmol/mol (Ref <7.1%, <54mmol/mol)

Reverse T3 - 202 pmol/L (Ref range 170-450)

OH-Vit D - 102 nmol/L (Ref >50)

HSCRP - <0.10 mg/L (Ref 0-10.00)

FreeT4 - 15.8 pmol/L (Ref 10.0-19.0)

TSH 1.71 mIU/L (Ref 0.50-4.00)

Free T3 - 5.6 pmol/L (Ref 3.5-6.5)

Fasting Blood Glucose 5.2 mmol/L (Ref 4.0-6.0)

Total Cholesterol- **6.5 mmol/L** (Ref <4.0)

Triglyceride- 0.9 mol/L (Ref <2.0)

HDL-C 1.5 mmol/L (Ref > 1.0)

LDL-C **4.6 mmol/L** (Ref <2.0)

Non HDL-C **5.0 mmol/L** (<2.5)

Chol/HDL- 4.3

DUTCH test (main points - see attached report)

Progesterone markers are high

DHEAs and metabolites are high

Higher preference for 5a reductase

**E1, E2 and E3 are all high**

Methylation activity is low

Preference for cortisone metabolites

My main concerns are the estrogen pathways.
Second concern is the LDL/nonHDL panels. Should I be concerned about these considering there’s no correlating increase in total TG’s and HSCRP?

For some background, my home and work environments are reasonable. I’m eating seasonally, within a time restricted feeding window of 6-8 hours, during daylight hours. No wifi at home, blue blocking, good sleep, plenty of spring/filtered water, barefoot beach walks and cold-adapting swim sessions.

Any insights would be greatly appreciated. Thanks!

# Thread: Drinking Juice instead of water in summer- effect of constant insulin spikes?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drinking-juice-instead-of-water-in-summer-effect-of-constant-insulin-spikes.19664/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2017-06-18T20:45:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drinking-juice-instead-of-water-in-summer-effect-of-constant-insulin-spikes.19664/post-216506>

I tried drinking a gallon of orange juice yesterday instead of water for the more structured water of the fruit.

I am on vacation in Monterey California and in very strong sunlight and 55° water.

**If any, what would be the negative effect of constant insulin spikes?** _Note that I am in strong UV sunlight most of the day.
_
Does anyone else drink juice instead of water in summer? Anyone aware of native people doing this?

### Sean Waters — 2017-06-28T15:51:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-this-and-a-dumb-question.19686/post-217337>

Are you on your iPhone a lot? How many hours a day do you use it/ is it turned on?

I had to turn mine off and replace it with a 2G phone. Even then, it's on Airplane mode from after work @ 5pm until when I get to work @ 8am. On weekends I turn it off Airplane mode to contact friends but then off again as soon as I'm with them, or leave it on in a different room.

I notice so many symptoms if its near me when it's on... Initially its:
\- Headaches
\- Sneezing
\- Blocked nose
\- Eczema on my hands flares up and itches me badly.
\- Pains in the hands
\- Irritation/ aggravation (often ends up being in an arsey and snappy mood with my other half which is shit.)

After a while I get more of the above... and some different ones:
\- heart palpitations
\- a need to run the toilet
\- bubbling/ gas from my gut
\- sharp stomach pains
\- depression/ anxiety/ paranoia
\- serious fatigue and aching, similar to fibromyalgia

That's just my 2G phone. I also get the above ^ after using iPads, Laptops etc. (albeit a lot quicker/ more intensely). Sometimes it happens just walking past the EMF hell of McDonald's on the way to work. Thing is, I have an autoimmune disease called Crohn's disease, or I like to say **HAD,** as I have it controlled (on no medication - 18 months and counting). Jack believes autoimmune diseases are effectively an "_**ALLERGY TO EMF**_". So, you may not have the same severity of symptoms as me. But, I believe, honestly, that all mitochondrial diseases are due to non-native EMF of some sort. Whether it's Artificial Light (nnEMF) or higher frequency of EMF such as WiFi or iPhones.

In the wild, where there is none of this nnEMF, they don't see the same mitochondrial diseases as we do...

Look at this website on Grizzly Bear diseases, for example:
[http://animals.mom.me/diseases-grizzly-bears-1944.html](http://animals.mom.me/diseases-grizzly-bears-1944.html)

"Some bears suffer from Arthritis during old age"... Hmm, really? Or is that just in your Blue Lit Captivity Cells?

Otherwise - it's largely just infections, but it notes at the top... that these are RARE.

That means, on the whole Grizzly Bears are 850805860589653495 times more healthy than us. I don't think I need to say WHY to anyone reading this.

### Sean Waters — 2017-07-13T11:02:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-this-and-a-dumb-question.19686/post-218662>

> [TANN said:](/goto/post?id=218612)
>
> I know I'm asking a question that I should find out on here for myself but how did you hack your environment?

My environment is probably the worst in the UK... It's London.

I hack it right now by doing the following, in order of importance:
1\. Avoid EMF as much as humanly possible:

-   Sit far away from laptop, use keyboard and mouse.
-   Got rid of iphone, got a 2G phone and keep on airplane mode before and after work
-   Keep your room tech free, better if your house is but if you share a house its difficult
-   Cover your cookers and washing machines etc. panels and LCD displays with Tin Foil (thanks Emma)
-   Generally stand far away from electrics and tech. Use a breaker at night where possible

2\. bathing myself in 10kg of ice and cold water in a bath every night (creates a faraday cage, protective of EMF)
3\. Sun + Grounding - in my garden with the morning sunlight for at least 15 minutes. Generally get as much sun as possible.
4\. Sleep - strictly no food or light in my eyes (unless it is infrared) after 8pm.
5\. Eyes - blocking out blue light with Cyxus blue blockers at work, and UVEX safety blue blockers at night.
6\. Skin - cover up indoors under artificial lighting
7\. Food - eating a Seafood only, ketogenic diet. Always eating BIG in the morning with sunrise. Skip lunch if you can and eat at 4-7pm for dinner. Seafoods Expensive, but so worth it. It increases your bodys electric current. Mussels, Shrimp and Clams cooked in tomato spicy broths for the win.

Wow, I didn't realise. Fuck living in London @[Sean Waters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19464/)

Like Sean, I do a lot of those things minus CT, I'm still being a dick about the cold baths. I mean, working my way up to them.

Here are some things I do: -

1.No WIFI in house. Now actively avoiding going to places with lots of WIFI (like city centre or venues with loads of people).
2.Smart phone on airplane mode during the day.
3.CT face dunks every day.
4.Morning AM light + grounding - as soon as I'm awake I sit outside for 20 mins to get the light. It ain't sun #Scotland
5.As much natural daylight as possible
6.As much grounding as possible - I have leather soled shoes
7.Eating as much fish as humanely possible
8.All electric trip switches get put off at night. With the exception of the kitchen
9.We don't use any artificial light in the house - it's Summer, this is going to be fun in Winter. Think we'll get candles
10.Blue blockers for all screen use no matter what time of day
11.Blue blockers for any shop or supermarket that has LED or fluorescent lights (that's fucking all of them btw) with a baseball cap and fully clothed <-----literally care factor: zero with what people think when I'm fully in my gear

### drezy — 2017-07-14T17:02:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-this-and-a-dumb-question.19686/post-218819>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=218789)
>
> I don't think there's an easy fix like that but clearly I don't know anything about the Wave rider. Just based on my instinct.

Stick with that instinct Emma. Any product that tells you it's "cancelling" the emf is not believable to me. I layed it out in a overly-geeky way here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/hello-from-the-florida-space-coast.19474/#post-214080](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/hello-from-the-florida-space-coast.19474/#post-214080)

I think I can make a better analogy though. Say you challenge Hussein Bolt to a race where all the spectators are your mitochondria and they are right at the finish line.

-   Mr Bolt is very fast (aka the speed of any rf/emf through air).
-   You're allowed to start the race partway into the track instead having to start at the official start line, but you can't start running until the second Mr. Bolt is shoulder to shoulder with you(detection of a rf signal coming your way).
-   You will have to be carrying one of your babies though (aka the speed of signals in any consumer/prosumer circuitry)
-   Lastly to qualify as finished at the end you'll have to write Mr. Bolts name backward on a piece of paper somewhere along the way (like a processor would have to do to invert a signal after detection)

The above product doesn't make that particular claim.

In that scenario it would be more like you came up with the idea of shining bright lights of varying colors and intensities and maybe a confetti cannon or two into the eyes of your spectators for the whole race.

Could it help? I'm not behind it for a similar reason that I wouldn't buy a PEMF device, way more study needed and enough people willing to experiment on themselves.

### Sean Waters — 2017-07-21T10:50:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-this-and-a-dumb-question.19686/post-219584>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=219581)
>

>
> Is there no way you can leave London and relocate whilst saving? I imagine it's pretty hard saving in London due to cost of living? Hey, move to Scotland

I owe my company £5000 for paying for my tuition, I have to stay for another 2 years by law for it to be wiped to zero. I don't want to break that, as I did it last year and had to pay £11,500 back to my previous company in one hit. Won't be doing that again... My only option would be to say to them "I'm not moving to another company, and I'm quitting due to ill health"... and ask them to wipe it off early as a gesture of good will. Otherwise, I will be leaving the country with Debts... may get a CCJ.. and may not be allowed into another country due to fraud. If I leave now, they will likely want the money back, but give it a year or so and things might be a little easier to swing.

The other option is to relocate back to my Mom's in Birmingham. It's in the countryside, much better environment. However, I'd still need to work in an office 5 days a week and drive an EMF hell hole vehicle everyday. I may also have to take a pay cut, due to getting extra £ in London. Although, it's definitely an option, as I'd be rent free so it would probably work out the same. The other issue is that my missus would find it difficult to get work down here, and there is no space at her mom's for her to move back to, so she would have to live at my mom's which is not a problem but not ideal either.

I'm considering buying some EMF shielding for my bedroom to improve my sleep, but I'm not sure what's the best way to do it. I also have had success with some Lodestone and Hematite iron stones around my bed. Anyone know if these really work?

If I can make it until next year, then I can have enough money to get out for good and forever.

You'd need to lay it on thick during the ill health process that the stress of the money you owe the company is exacerbating your condition. Most companies want shot of someone on long term ill health (I used to work in HR) so you might find them more willing to compensate you...

Certainly looks like there are some options even if it's not all that straight forward. To me. the fact that you have this long-term vision in mind about a non EMF place in Americas speaks volumes.

Like Jack wrote on the YouTube comments on the Vermont vid - have you read them? - having that goal and keeping it front and centre creates opportunities!

# Thread: Daytime indoor glasses that do not affect alertness yet support circadian rhythm?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daytime-indoor-glasses-that-do-not-affect-alertness-yet-support-circadian-rhythm.19693/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2017-06-26T19:09:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daytime-indoor-glasses-that-do-not-affect-alertness-yet-support-circadian-rhythm.19693/post-217124>

I have the attention of an optic lab tech and I would like him to design glasses that can be worn to reduce blue exposure in an environment akin to an OR, yet not affect alertness. Would I be correct in saying that you would need two sets of glasses one to reduce the "midday"blue glare to the early ramp up morning light spectrum after 10 am, wear these until solar noon and at X hour change to a second pair that reduces the artificial light spectrum to a "ramp down" spectrum and after hours Blueblockers as normal. A key consideration would probably be to support and respect heart circadian rhythm as much as possible. What frequencies would be the desired ones ? Would a set up of this kind be better tan nothing at all or are there pitfalls? Is this the basic idea behind your glasses Dr K or am I way off base? @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) Would you mind explaining your glasses set up a bit more in detail ? Thank you.

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-27T10:47:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daytime-indoor-glasses-that-do-not-affect-alertness-yet-support-circadian-rhythm.19693/post-217168>

Lah, A potential pitfall could depend on what your n=1 "blueblockers as normal" after hours means in terms of additive or subtractive frequencies. I'm considering my opsins.

# Thread: Green Lights for Healing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-lights-for-healing.19701/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dean6789 — 2017-06-27T21:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-lights-for-healing.19701/post-217287>

I talked to a woman who had a Healing Retreat for 40 years. She said that sometimes people would be carried into the retreat, and she would use green lights on them (a 250w "indoor/outdoor" light, as she called it) for 15 to 20 minutes at a time, four times a day, about 10 to 15 feet away from them -- and four days later they would walk out. She said that she had lots of "miraculous healings" over the decades using those lights.

She knows nothing about wavelength, just has certain ideas about certain colors. Says green is so healing because it's "like the color of grass and trees". So I found a manufacturer with some green incandescent 150w bulbs (haven't found 250w bulbs out there, but was told by this woman that 2 - 150w bulbs would work just as well) and they told me that they were "approximately 526 nm". I'm just learning about this stuff so I don't know much, but it seems to me that more like 380nm or 830nm - 850nm would be more ideal.

What do you all think about these green lights for healing?

I don't think its just about the colour we "see" with camera vision, the mitochondria and cells detect frequencies. I've got stage lighting filter gels, the Dinshah filters, and a Bioptron with standard and colour therapy filters. The important thing seems to be seeing what frequencies they subtract, add, dilute or intensify, not relying on the colour I see from camera vision cone stimulation. When I can't get natural sunlight, I use them with natural light through the window, incandescent, or halogen lights, which includes the IR frequencies. I'd be wary of using a restrictive range of one colour, particularly in an LED. Alright in an emergency, but used chronically or as a lifestyle choice .... ? That could become a real problem.

# Thread: Science fat question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Billybats — 2017-06-27T22:11:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217291>

Hello to the geeks,

First I had no idea were to post this question. Sorry if it is weird or stupid one.

When one loses/ burns fat how does it leave the body. Is energy the only way you loss the fat. Does it become a liquid to convert to energy. Also does sweating mean anything with this process.

Thank you.

### JCDC — 2017-06-27T23:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217295>

Primarily CO2 gas. You literally breathe it out. About 80% CO2 and 20% water I believe

### Billybats — 2017-06-27T23:26:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217296>

> [JCDC said:](/goto/post?id=217295)
>
> Primarily CO2 gas. You literally breathe it out. About 80% CO2 and 20% water I believe

Wow. That I didn't expect. .really interesting. What do you mean water, that the fat becomes water, then comes out in the form of pee and sweat.

### kris90 — 2017-06-27T23:39:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217298>

Yes exactly. Humans reverse the photosynthesis process (that is using light to make CHO and O2 out of CO2 and H2O) via cellular respiration (taking the CHO and O2 and converting it back to CO2 and H2O).

Fat is essentially CHO, so when it's burned via cellular respiration, we are releasing CO2, H2O and heat. So we breathe it out, pee it out and sweat it out.

### Billybats — 2017-06-27T23:49:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217299>

Thank you guys. Awesome to know tje mechanisms. You would think then I would be thinner. .lol since I wake up though the night to pee and during the day. Also wake up thirsty and sweat alot. I know since getting more sun and eating at the correct time (daylight) I have experienced some change, and like you Kris and others have said it isn't all about food. I guess I am a mini geek deep down. I was the one asking why is the sky blue.lol no need to answer that one..Lolhahaaaaa

Thank you again.

### Optimalbound — 2017-06-27T23:55:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217301>

This Is Where Body Fat Ends Up When You Lose Weight

[http://www.sciencealert.com/this-is-where-body-fat-ends-up-when-you-lose-weight](http://www.sciencealert.com/this-is-where-body-fat-ends-up-when-you-lose-weight)

### Billybats — 2017-06-28T00:10:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217302>

So say sprinting as a pose to slow walking would be more effective, I wonder. Just thinking out loud. I find this very interesting and very cool.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-products-do-you-use-for-emf-protection.19708/post-217829>

Image nnEMF as the noise interferring with a signal, e.g. FM radio signal. In electrical engineering, we take noise for granted, as a factor that is there and affects the quality of the signal we want to transfer.
So what we do, is to find a way to amplify the signal and ground the circuit properly so as the noise's effect to be insignificant. That's what you should do as well.

Leaving in the city is very hard to avoid nnEMF, but instead of going through expensive solutions, you can simply make sure you are properly hydrated and get adequate salt (or simply drink - high quality sea - salt water) to increase body's conductivity - i.e. allow current flow more easily - and do not forget to be grounded as much time as possible during the day.
I know this is not a protection from nnEMF reaching your body, but this is definitely a way to "flush" nnEMF-produced current in your body, to the ground easily.
... and last - but not least - don't forget to get you daily dose of sun, as this is you amplifier.
**
The stronger the signal from the preferable source (sun and earth), the less the effect of the noise (nnEMF) in the system (your body).**

# Thread: 6 day water fast with loads of sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/6-day-water-fast-with-loads-of-sun.19709/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2017-06-29T03:57:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/6-day-water-fast-with-loads-of-sun.19709/post-217398>

Hi There,

Today is the last day of my 6 day water fast. Over this time i have only drunk water, got loads of sun, taken psyllium and activated charcoal 3 x a day. My routine has been normal. Wake before sunrise, go to beach with dogs....walk for an hour n a half, get ready and off to work at 9, work til lunch, come home and nude 15min front and back in midday sun (UV 9-12), go back to work finish at 5.30 , go to beach for sunset, then bed by 9.30pm. In addition to this I have had daily coloncs , i know Jack is not a fan of these but due to my regular travels i find its good to clean out every once in a while, especially with GMOs and alike lurkng in our food chain.

The point i have found very interesting is that with all the sun i have been allowing myself i really have had hardly any blood sugar ie insulin response. I would usually crash pretty hard about the end of day 2 into day 3 but this time it has been smooth sailing all the way through.

I have not been physically exerting myself but have been working and socialising as per normal (sans food).

The last 3.3.5 days i have been very keto and yday and today i have began to feel this in my muscles.
Today i will break my fast and get back on the food but the reason for my writing this is with all of the good water i was drinking and the sporadic periods of sunshine I really have had not blood sugar issues at all and , comparitivey to past experiences i was really amazed. This time it was comparatively very easy.

Just thought to add my brief experience

Cheers

Simon

Opened it up last night and walked round the house. RF in the living room was off the charts even though we'd switched off wifi.

Discovered the 'alarm' system in the corner of the room was the perpetrator - we live in a rented home. I'd seen it flashing away in the corner when I moved but never really thought about it!! I guess that is Jack's comment about humans being shit observers

So my husband disconnected the system by pulling out the wire and wham, back to next to nothing.

The house seems good but the upstairs bedroom has a higher reading especially when the window is open - there is a cell phone mast in that direction about quarter mile - would that be it?

FREAKED THE FUCK OUT when I put the meter against my iphone. Seriously. Switched that bastard off and it hasn't been touched since. Now figuring out how to model my life around no phone or buy a model that accepts only 2G.

The induction hob as I feared was off the chart, even before I switched it on just holding it at the LED clock which we stand in front of ALLL the time.

Depressing.

The wifi was quite scary and now I'm working out how to extract TV from my kids as our TV runs on the internet - if this is well known forgive me, I stopped watching TV back in the terrestrial channel days.

So here's my question - I'm not sure what the 'normal' readings should be for using this meter. I still haven't figured out what the third setting is for? EFIELD?

Does the EMF Webinar cover all this @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

Or is there somewhere I can get decent information about what readings mean what using the Cornet? @[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

### Scompy — 2017-06-30T18:41:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-my-cornet-meter-this-happened.19713/post-217621>

Heya Emma,
The quarter mile away phone tower could bring in higher readings depending on convergences and geography. But knowing the direction of the tower (if this is the only one at the moment), it can be easier to mitigate. If you are lower than the masts, like more in a valley, you may be getting more geographical mitigation also. I literally had convergence of a phone tower when I was working in Seattle where they had a small-cell on a rooftop and the building I was in was only about 300 feet away--looking out the other side of the building realizing I was parallel to the full blast of about 5 mW/m2 on average. I had to do radical things to minimize exposures... ultimately it meant moving. I know x2 more people that have come down with cancer since I've left the building. RF from a phone is about x10 this amount averaging around 50 mW/m2 within an inch from the body, but varies widely based on conditions like proximity to the tower (farther away, higher the number you'll see). In coffee shops, I see numbers like 5 mW/m2 if I'm in line and 2 feet away from the person in front of me entranced by the blue-light smurf.

Memory is max 65 text messages. Sometimes calls just don't come through, and then 3 days later you will receive a missed call notification.

Overall it's not bad but there's probably better ones, NOKIA 3310 etc. Nokias are about £30-40 so bit more expensive. That generation of phone is what you are after (2000-2003).

### Sean Waters — 2017-07-04T13:10:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-my-cornet-meter-this-happened.19713/post-217924>

I sent them all my new number on WhatsApp, and then turned it off and waited.

All my close family text me the same day. Most of my best friends got in touch within a day or 2. A few others after a month or so. Some haven't bothered, but the ones that matter have come through.

It's strange, the ones I considered closest and most loyal came through the quickest... with an almost pro-rata of time/care for the others... more time = less care.

It's a good way to suss out who really wants to be in your life and who doesn't. Those that matter will send you letters on an Owl if they love you enough,

Waiting to confirm with saliva cortisol test, but I'd bet on it. I'm waking up at 2:30-3:00 am and CANT get back to sleep.

I'm doing CT every night, eating epi paleo, wear blu blockers in front of any screen, I get morning sun for my circadian setting as well. I walk barefoot when I can but daily at least in my yard.

I read the dhea blog by jack and he wrote that anyone over 40 should me taking dhea. I have been taking it for at least 3 months & upped mine from 10mg to 25 as I tested at the low end of normal.

Recent pregnenalone was at 22 ( range 22-237) So a case of pregnenalone steal going on.

I have hashimotos, with only sliver of thyroid and my tsh was very low and causing palpitations and amxierty and even worse sleep. Since lowering Tirosint dose palps have gone away, still some anxiety.

The lack of sleep is causing me to lose hope.

Please share some bread crumb or hack to help me get more than a few hours of sleep I can't go on like this....

### Ski coach — 2017-07-01T17:30:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-down.19722/post-217717>

A few more crumbs to chew on:

Have you checked for nnEMF? Have you removed all electronics from sleep area? Do you turn off the power breaker and cell phone, wifi etc. at night?
Do you have any fake lights outside your window? If so, do you use black out curtains/shades and or sleep mask? Are you wearing your blue blockers when you look at some sort of time piece at 2:30 in the morning? Hope you are not looking at your phone! (If I want to check the time I keep eyes closed while moving sleep mask out of way and putting blueblockers on to check my watch.)
I find evening CT helpful, but not if the CT is too intense.
Do you get up at actual sunrise and go outside? Morning light is critical, but sunrise is really special.
Is your bedroom cool?
Do you get plenty of good water in the evening?
Have you considered going camping for a week (without technology) to jumps start the circadian reset?
Good luck!

# Thread: I am going to say it: Lucas was a Bio-Thief

### Scompy — 2017-07-01T21:29:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-going-to-say-it-lucas-was-a-bio-thief.19723/post-217732>

Seriously, take the word Mitochondria, alter it up a bit, and you get Midichlorian.

"_Without the midi-chlorians, life could not exist, and we would have no knowledge of the Force. They continually speak to us, telling us the will of the Force. When you learn to quiet your mind, you'll hear them speaking to you._"
―[Qui-Gon Jinn](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Qui-Gon_Jinn), to Anakin Skywalker[\[src\]](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Star_Wars:_Episode_I_The_Phantom_Menace)

**Midi-chlorians** were microscopic, [intelligent](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Sentience) [life forms](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Species) that originated from the[foundation of life](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Wellspring_of_Life) in the [center](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Deep_Core) of [the galaxy](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/The_galaxy), and ultimately resided within the cells of all[living organisms](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Species), thereby forming a symbiotic relationship with their hosts. [The Force](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/The_Force)spoke through the midi-chlorians, allowing certain beings to use the Force if they were[sensitive](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Force-sensitive) enough to its [powers](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Force_power). In order to gauge an individual's potential in the Force,[blood](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/DNA) tests were used to estimate the number of midi-chlorians within the subject's cells. [Anakin Skywalker](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Anakin_Skywalker), the [Chosen One](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Chosen_One) [born](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Reproduction) of a [mother](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Shmi_Skywalker_Lars) but without a [father](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Parent), possessed over 20,000 midi-chlorians—the highest known count in [galactic history](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Timeline_of_galactic_history).

[http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Midi-chlorian](http://starwars.wikia.com/wiki/Midi-chlorian)

The real message is to look closer at everything.

You've been on this path longer than I have. Have you noticed that even past interactions have new meaning now?

For example the paleo GP that I sometimes go to said the following 3-5 years ago:

"You know the old 60 and 70 yo ranchers who work out in the sun all day have the highest testosterone out of all men I test."

Spectracell will let you bye with homocysteine=11
Please double-check, it should be 5-7
High can mess you up royally, possibly including SHBG
High SHBG is a sign of a mess somewhere; yours is very high.
Higher TotalTestosterone will definitely help (while messing up something else).
Check your growth hormone, but compare to what 20yo would want
IGF-1=(300-350)
IGF-BP-3=3000
imperatively, measure IGF-1 using radioimmunoassay

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) wrote about SHBG.
Please read it, and after you understand it, help me figure it up, but using second-grade level of explanations.
For now, look for (too high) estrogens in DUTCHtest results,
16-OH-E1
4-OH-E1---->Methylation detox
QUINONE----->Glutathione detox
\--------------
low progesterone, something is stealing pregnenolone, usually to make cortisol (that your life depends on)
you may want to up on DHEAs

you would get a better picture from DUTCHtest
Other than overall picture, you would get info on two progesterone metabolites
a-Pregnanediol
b-Pregnanediol
\--------------

Cholesterol 248 is ok. But look at the chart where large LDL meets small LDL.
Mine is too high there, but according to [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) as long as it is large LDL it should be ok.
Large balls are bouncing gracefully, little balls are bullets that may destroy what they bounce off.

### John Schumacher — 2019-10-24T18:48:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measing-of-heteroplasmy.19726/post-281173>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=281117)
>
> The "Mr" bit is way too much. My name is Dan. It's good to correspond with you.
>
> For insulin/diabetes issues I can't recommend the use of CT enough.

Thank you - Dan

For me Cold Therapy is vital. It was Tim Ferriss popularized cold therapy for fat loss in 2010. Thus began my journey towards the cold. Whim Hof took it to next level for me. Though I do not and will not do superhuman feats, I do believe the positive effects of cold are significant. What I enjoy most is to immersion in moving water, outside in nature, grounded feet to the earth, standing in 50-degree water, with the water up to my chin so that I back of my neck is submerged. My minimum goal is 20 minutes daily. Winter time is best for cold therapy which includes the cold air against my bare torso, walking out to the American river, then dripping wet in the cold air walking back from the river. The hormonal effect accompanying the shiver over the next couple of hours is wonderful.

Whim Hof’s story includes depression over the loss of his beloved wife. Depression can feel numb; the nervous system is overwhelmed, shutting down peripheral sensation. The cold awakened his CNS. As Whim states, “Cold is my teacher.” The chill up his spine, into his head, the tingling of his fingers, all provided feedback that he was alive. “Feeling is reality,” he says. Note: I’ve never been depressed. It is a powerful force towards self-pity. I love life too much to entertain self-defeating practices.

For me cold therapy has taught me – surrender. As the water moves over my body, the peripheral sensations are heightened. I immerse my conscience feeling into the moving cold, allowing its' influence to take my emotional response. I watch and feel the water swirl towards me, around me and pass by me. Embodying the many feels cold engenders are rich. The goal is to not hold onto any one feeling, but to observe it, feel the continually changing intensity of it, “enjoy” it, appreciate it, let it go and watch it leave. It’s almost “relaxing”, persistent awareness, relief. I’ve used this opportunity to practice - surrender.

There are many ways to eat pork, this is one simple way to introduce you to this type of food.
Go to Costco or Mexican food store, buy pork belly or bacon.
Fry it.
Use grease only.
Add some oregano or other spice to make it easier to eat.
\----
Do not buy ready-made lard. Usually, it is hydrogenated.

....
Olive oil, buy Californian virgin oil, cold pressed.
California supposedly is the only place that make sure that what is written on the label is actually in the bottle.
Other places just sell labels. That is Greek or Italian or other bottle with wonderful writing on their labels may contain axle grease in the bottle.

.....

# Thread: Neuropathy Symptoms down the legs and hormone problems?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neuropathy-symptoms-down-the-legs-and-hormone-problems.19739/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joyzoggia — 2017-07-06T17:08:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neuropathy-symptoms-down-the-legs-and-hormone-problems.19739/post-218088>

Hello Everyone,

I'm new to the forum, I have ended up here multiple times during my long journey trying to figure out what is wrong with me so I figure I should maybe post in here and see if I get a good opinion...

It has been over a year that I have been dealing with strange sensations down my legs ( mainly at night at the beginning). Felt a lot like Peripheral Neuropathy at the beginning. At the time I went ahead and got an MRI to find out I didn't really have anything in my spine, got checked for diabetes also nothing (did a 2hr glucose test). Most of the time these sensations down my legs would get worse when I would eat dairy, gluten, sugar in general, certain nuts and sometimes even chicken. It was almost impossible to predict what would give me these things. I also always felt very angry, a lot of mood swings, depression, anxiety, the list keeps going...

I went to an Endocrinologist and got my thyroid and other hormones checked:

**LH** 15.4 mIU/mL / **FSH** 6.4 mIU/mL

**Calcium** 5.6 mg/dL / **DHEA-Sulfate** 206.2 ug/dL

**TSH** 2.080 uIU/ml / **Prolactin** 24.8 ng/ml (came out high)

**Estradiol** 34 pg/ml / **17-OH-Progesterone LCMS** 91 ng/dL

**Vitamin D 25-Hydroxy** 37 ng/mL / **Total T4** 7.2 ug/dL

**Free T3** 2.2 pg/mL / **PTH** 28 pg/mL / **Cortisol-AM** 28.8 ug/dL (high)

**Thyroid Peroxidase (TPO)Ab** 23 IU/mL

( I have many more labs that I have done but thought these would be the most important)

While my Endocrinologist said "Everything looks fine" my primary care physician really didn't think so... she put me on a low dose Nature-Throid because she thinks my t3 was too low. At the beginning I got some relief ( but it literally lasted only 3 days..) She also told me to go back to my vitamin d3, something my endo had told me to stop because my d3 was "fine". I always feel a little bit of relief when I take it but doesn't last very long during the day.

I also haven't had my period in over 3 years naturally... since I was a kid I was always given birth control pills but no one really told me why, I'm from brazil and there I was given one called " Ciclo Elani" which is drospirenona 3mg and etinilestradiol 0.03mg. 4 years ago I decided to stop because I was tired of the bad rep birth control pills had and it also always messed up my stomach.

My primary care has different opinion as far as what is going on with me, she is positive I have leaky gut, which with the fact that I respond terribly to multiple foods makes me agree with it. I suspect candida but the symptoms of it match a lot estrogen dominance as well, which I'm not 100% sure if I do have, I read online about the progesterone/Estradiol ratio and when I calculated it, it seemed like I was really estrogen dominant.

I know Dr Jack talks about neuropathy and the effect estrogen, and multiple other factors that a lot of neurologist wouldn't think about, have on the nerves. He also talks about how any cause of leaky gut can also be an etiology for neuropathy even in a non diabetics.

I'm so lost at this point, I went to a gastro and he didn't want to test me, he put me right away on Xifaxan (antibiotics) and gave me VSL#3 (powerful probiotic) to do right after the treatment. I haven't been feeling really good since then... Not sure if it would be die off symptoms at this point or if it is just all of the other problems that could be going on with me. I know the antibiotics wouldn't take care of candida if I did have it so I'm on a very low car, low sugar diet during the treatment, and yet gaining weight. I constipate a lot, and it's always a surprise how my stool is gonna look like when I do get to use the bathroom.

I'm only 24, and all of this came to me as a surprise, it's been over a year that I have been dealing with this, and yet I was the healthiest person you could think of, I used to compete as a bodybuilder so to me diet and health was top priority. Not being able to barely walk my dogs now has been extremely depressing... exercising has been the biggest pain of my day too, sometimes I can barely get out of bed. I have a feeling of inflammation all over my body constantly during the day at this point, my legs almost feel like they have extreme water retention and causes to give me these symptoms. and I'm getting pretty frustrated with doctors, jumping from one place to another, a many trying to push me to go to a reumatologist so they can tell me is all in my head and I have fibromyalgia... which I do not believe on, is a symptoms, not a disease.

Any help or thoughts would be extremely appreciated, thank you so much for your time.

Joy


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### JediDavidHacker — 2017-07-07T08:09:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reversing-gastro-paresis.19740/post-218144>

So, I could use some puzzle piecing and dot connecting on this issueive been dealing with for a while..like over a year. I'm in the process of hacking my environment right now. Got my grounding sheet. West my blue blockers...got earthing sandals coming, and bout to make a bunch of emf protective under garments made out of argen mesh...of course I go out first thing and ground blah blsh blsh...now I have structural issues...pinched nerves and all that, that I'm self treating with yoga self massage..stretching...etc etc...if anyone can accelerate my process I would be eternally grateful. Thanks mil?ion

### seanb4 — 2017-07-10T11:31:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reversing-gastro-paresis.19740/post-218345>

I have gastroparesis. It would be helpful to know what you think caused it. If it's diabetes, I think ketosis would help, but I am sure you are already doing that. If it's pinched nerve, I am not sure. For me, it is problems with the autonomic system. I have noticed an increase in transit time from Helminthic therapy. There is the usual advice of not drinking water around meals, but I find it helpful to not do anything that requires high blood flow after eating, or vasodialation. I now avoid heat and make sure I am cold, I think this helps blood flow as heart doesn't have to pump extra to the gut and skin at the same time.

# Thread: Cold Adaptation Effects
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2017-07-07T22:22:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/post-218192>

I wanted to start a thread for those who consider themselves cold adapted, and living in the ancient pathway. What sort of effects do you notice? I've been observing closely and its funny how different I am/feel than the average warm-adapted human.

Here is what I've noticed:
\-Extremely warm upon waking/radiating heat/dilated blood vessels
\-Feeling cold at night right before bed (especially if I cold shower right before bed)
\-During the day if stuck in the office, drinking cold water all day, always have goosebumps (until I go outside)
\-Radiating heat in the sun, but feeling comfortable/very high heat tolerance
\-Radiating heat after eating (especially high fat meals)
\-Never shivering, but goosebumps instantly as soon as there is any perception of cold on skin
\-Craving the cold (especially after eating when I'm radiating heat)
\-Flush/reddening of the face after exercising or eating
\-Enjoying freezing my ass off (sense of wellbeing), always finding ways to cool: cold pool, ice packs, cold showers, AC cranked in the car, drinking lots of ice water, etc.
\-Crave high fat foods, with no desire for sugar.

Anyone else?

### Gary — 2017-07-11T18:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/post-218503>

Hello,

I'm in the process of cold adaptation with my daughter...can I ask you how long it took you to achieve your current effects and what type of CT you do?

Thanks

Gary

### kris90 — 2017-07-11T20:03:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/post-218507>

I think I became cold adapted before I even did any CT, just by fasting for years and drinking a lot of cold water during the fast. So even when I added CT last year, I believe I was already cold adapted, so it was quite easy for me to get into it. But, I did actually go back to warm adaptation during a biohack I did this spring. And to be honest, I re-cold adapted within 1 weekend (Vermont) just by being outside in minimal clothing on a cold weekend, and taking a dip in the cold lake, and sleeping in a tent with temperatures as low as 45 F.

I normally do a cold shower upon waking (50 F water temp) and before bed. During the day, I'm always finding ways to keep cool (i.e. fasting, lots of ice water, AC cranked, cold dips in pool, etc). I'll sometimes do a cold bath with ice, but not often. Actually, that's something I want to start adding a few times a week since I'm so addicted to the cold now.

I think if you follow Jack's CT protocol, it shouldn't take more than a couple weeks if consistent. Face dunks is probably the best way to start if you know about the mammalian dive reflex.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-11T20:30:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/post-218512>

I've been face dunking about four weeks now. I'm only recently ending my shower on cold (I shower every three days) and finding it not entirely unpleasant but I'm not all YAY! about it.

I'm taking it super slow. Because I've got so many protocols on the go right now I can't say for sure what the CT is doing specifically but I have noticed weirdly that I'm enjoying the rain more!!

### kris90 — 2017-07-11T21:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/post-218515>

I find adaptation is the hardest part of CT, because for instance, a 70 F pool to me sucks lol... It's cold that it's not an enjoyable swim, but not cold enough to induce the numbness that makes CT easy.

It's similar to how I feel about the fall or early winter here where I am. When it hits just below freezing it's cold and uncomfortable. But when it goes quite south of freezing, to me, it doesn't make a difference, if anything, it's better. It's that in-between phase that is the toughest I find. This probably helps mammals fatten in fall: they start getting uncomfortable with the cold, and carbs are still available, and make them feel comfortable and cozy. So for me now, the colder the water, the better, and I just jump right in now, and it feels amazing. The B-endorphin is through the roof and my skin is just numb, and there's no pain perception.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-12T08:40:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/post-218564>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=218515)
>

>
> It's similar to how I feel about the fall or early winter here where I am. When it hits just below freezing it's cold and uncomfortable. But when it goes quite south of freezing, to me, it doesn't make a difference, if anything, it's better. It's that in-between phase that is the toughest I find. So for me now, the colder the water, the better, and I just jump right in now, and it feels amazing. The B-endorphin is through the roof and my skin is just numb, and there's no pain perception.

Yeah, I resonate with that. Sometimes the cold is just not cold enough to be more comfortably cold. <------ Reading that back you'd think I was stoned

@[Sean Waters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19464/) was talking about this on another thread. Are you wanting model suggestions or practical suggestions?

I'm so ready to push the button on ditching my smart phone with just one stumbling block. When we move to Spain I'm leaving my friends behind and in all honesty I prefer leaving voice memos over WhatsApp with my friends than typing - it feels more real. My hack for keeping this aspect of my "social life" is to switch on my phone in the morning, wait for messages to download then switch to airplane mode, record my replies, switch off airplane mode to allow it to send then the phone goes back off. My goal is to do this once in the morning and once at night.

Other than that, I'd happily ditch the phone. Although the D Minder app is also pretty useful!

Damn these bloody smart phones!!

My wife keeps her phone on "low power" mode all the time that seems to broadcast less.

# Thread: Antifungal photodynamic therapy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antifungal-photodynamic-therapy.19753/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-07-09T22:51:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antifungal-photodynamic-therapy.19753/post-218310>

**Summary**
In photodynamic antimicrobial [chemotherapy](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0944501307000912#) (PACT), a combination of a sensitising drug and visible light causes selective destruction of microbial cells. The ability of light–drug combinations to kill microorganisms has been known for over 100 years. However, it is only recently with the beginning of the search for alternative treatments for antibiotic-resistant pathogens that the phenomenon has been investigated in detail. Numerous studies have shown PACT to be highly effective in the _in vitro_ destruction of viruses and protozoa, as well as Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria and fungi. Results of experimental investigations have demonstrated conclusively that both dermatomycetes and yeasts can be effectively killed by photodynamic action employing phenothiazinium, porphyrin and phthalocyanine photosensitisers. Importantly, considerable selectivity for fungi over human cells has been demonstrated, no reports of fungal resistance exist and the treatment is not associated with genotoxic or mutagenic effects to fungi or human cells. In spite of the success of cell culture investigations, only a very small number of _in vivo_ animal and human trials have been published. The present paper reviews the studies published to date on antifungal applications of PACT and aims to raise awareness of this area of research, which has the potential to make a significant impact in future treatment of fungal [infections](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0944501307000912#).

[http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0944501307000912](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0944501307000912)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Kiva Lights?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kiva-lights.19754/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gary — 2017-07-10T02:34:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kiva-lights.19754/post-218319>

My first time posting, I'm excited to be a Gold member and hope I can eventually digest most of the information on this site, it's incredible!

I followed a link from another member here, Paul, which spoke about Kiva lights and their benefits. The only issue is, they are fluorescent lights...I was hoping to get Jack's opinion on them.

(obviously, sunlight is best!)

Info from this site:

[http://www.anti-aging-today.org/water/clustered/structured-water1.htm](http://www.anti-aging-today.org/water/clustered/structured-water1.htm)

**KIVA LIGHTS**

Chemical mechanisms on One Bachechi's eight year study of the effects of full spectrum Kiva lights submitted to One for his use in grant proposals and the designing of future experiments.

Kiva light removes dust and static electricity from the air while holding the humidity between 45 and 55%. This balances the positive and negative ion contents in a room. Rooms, which are dry and dusty, are depleted in negative ions while rooms, which are very humid, have few positive or negative ions. The Kiva light produces highly active water molecules, which precipitate the dust (positive ions), and excess humidity (negative ions) to produce a room, which has an equal balance of, charged positive and negative ions. Negative ion studies show a balance of positive and negative ions increases oxygen absorption in man.

The Kiva light changes the pH of water. This change comes from the light structuring or activating the water to form free radicals or highly reactive positive and negative ions within the water. These charged ions will react with other water molecules to form ionized chains of water molecules and will ionize dissolved gases and minerals. Dissolved minerals become more highly soluble because there are more water hydration shells around each mineral. This increase in the charged molecule changes the chemical strength of the dissolved minerals and changes the pH of the water. Dissolved gases become ionized in structured water. Kiva lights change dissolved chlorine gas into chloride ions by the structured water ionizing the Cl2 molecules into two Cl free radicals which then react with two H free radicals to form two HCl molecules. This reaction will change the water to a more acid pH.

Dissolved CO2 gas will become HCOO-1 (carboxylic acid) while dissolved 02 will react with H free radicals to become two basic hydroxyl (OH-1 ions).

Kiva lights seem to inhibit several well-known free radical organic and biochemical reactions. Epoxy glue will not bond under Kiva light but will stick when placed under incandescent lights.

These reactions probably need an acid environment to go from the free radical condition to the ionic or covalently bonded state. Fats and oils do not become rancid under Kiva lights so meat and vegetables do not spoil as readily with Kiva light as they do under acid causing yellow or pink lights.

Kiva lights are modified 4 foot fluorescent Vita-Lites which when placed over the kitchen sink structure the tap water and the food prepared in the kitchen. People using these lights in this manner experience periodic detoxification reactions, which include a slight fever and diarrhea. Many diseased conditions are improved by this use of structured water including poor circulation, high blood pressure, cellulite loss, dry skin, kidney and gall bladder stones, toxicity, anemia, ulcers, diabetes, arthritis, fibroid tumors and paralysis (Bachechi, 1982).

In the body, poor circulation, high blood pressure, arterial sclerosis and cellulite from cholesterol deposits are dissolved after KIVA activated water has been used for cooking. Also skin becomes softer and less wrinkled. Cholesterol deposits are due to free radical oxidation while skin wrinkles are caused by free radical cross linkage of collagen molecules.

Arthritis, kidney stones and gall bladder stones are alleviated by a rebalancing of the calcium metabolism.

Thanks

Gary

### caroline — 2017-07-14T05:30:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-dr-k.19786/post-218770>

We read your long FB post this morning over breakfast \[sitting on the grass btw\]. All of a sudden everything fell into focus.

We are making some big changes in our life - and we are very okay with that ....but we are all over the freaking map! We are trying to think of everything and all possibilities and so we are very fractured.

One step at a time is now our mantra - It is amazing what we have accomplished the last couple of hours! Things are falling into place ...and with that comes confidence that we can accomplish, at least in some variation, what is most important to both of us.

As always - thanks Dr. Kruse.

Much appreciation for sharing your thoughts and your journey with all of us.

### HopingMom — 2017-07-16T04:21:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-noon-sleep-none.19803/post-219019>

Hi!
I recently added in scheduled solar noonish time direct sun daily...I get a lot of outside time and always am sun but this I did more calculated. Something is wrenched in my system because I've found when I do this my sleep is really poor.
No scheduled vit D sun=Rockstar sleep,
Add in the scheduled sun and I can't fall asleep and sleep is restless and horrible.
I don't want to know what to take, just wondering what could be happening? I tan nicely, don't shower very much ( I work from home so I don't mind) and again without this D sun I'm sleeping amazingly.
I would say I am absorbing it because it's getting into my system obviously, but what steps after that are faulty? Any suggestions how I can figure it out? Is it a sulfur thing? I don't seem to properly assimilate/utilize sulfur foods. Any thoughts would be appreciated!

### caroline — 2017-07-17T08:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-noon-sleep-none.19803/post-219134>

really interesting question ........Lately I am having trouble with sulphur foods too.

What time is your last meal? and what is it?

Anything else changed? nnEMF?

### HopingMom — 2017-07-18T15:17:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-noon-sleep-none.19803/post-219251>

Interestingly...seems my cortisol conversion is wonky (credit to the gal here with low cortisol and hair loss). Found this [https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/11β-hydroxysteroid\_dehydrogenase\_type\_1](https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/11β-hydroxysteroid_dehydrogenase_type_1)

The only reason I can confirm this is going on is because I don't eat very much/ feel hunger but you better believe if there is licorice around I will feast!
We had a few rain days and like someone flipped a switch, back to Rockstar sleep.
I'm certain the sulfur is connected but not clear yet how. Caroline do you know why you are experiencing sulfur problems?

### HopingMom — 2017-07-18T15:19:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-noon-sleep-none.19803/post-219252>

I will add that I am also probably anemic or similar...that thing with spooning fingernails really happens!!

no - I have no idea why sulphur foods are bothering me. I never used to have a problem with any foods except for wheat/grains.

### caroline — 2017-07-19T02:53:52+0300

### Robert Marking — 2017-08-02T07:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-noon-sleep-none.19803/post-220687>

Hi Caroline and HopingMom, I've been reading your posts and I may have an answer as to why you are having a problem with metabolizing sulfur properly.

I just had a patient today with the very same problem that also had lyme and biotoxin mold illness, and this has made a huge difference in his recovery. It's also something that has been over looked in years and years of my patients lab work through other doctors. His recent 23andme and geneticgenie reports revealed this issue, but is was not taken serious by his last functional medicine doc.

I have had this exact same issue for years, and my condition is quite severe.

Also, I have been finding it quite common to some degree in chronically ill patients as well, and I feel that it is one of the reasons why they are not getting better.

There are 4 known gene mutations that may affect sulfur metabolism ,... CBS 699, BHMT 01, BHMT 02, BHMT 04.
More info on these mutations
[http://geneticgenie.org/all-mutations/](http://geneticgenie.org/all-mutations/)
[http://resqua.com/702188759/what-does-a-cbs-gene-mutation-mean](http://resqua.com/702188759/what-does-a-cbs-gene-mutation-mean)

Here are a couple of things that can happen as a result of these mutations.......
1 - poor sulfur metabolism/sulfur sensitivity will negatively affect the sulfur dependant detox pathways = poor detox
2 - sulfur is converted into ammonia which is toxic and inflamatory

Because I have battled Lyme and Mold Illness myself, I have had functional medicine docs recommend for me many sulfur containing supplements that only made me feel much worse these include,... NAC, ALA, Glutathione, MSM, Garlic, HCL, Milk thistle, Curcumin, Garlic, Fish Oil, etc, etc,

I have been educating people for over 17 years on the importance of knowing your own personal hidden food sensitivities and intollerances and even though sulfur containing foods rarely showed in my blood work as a sensitity, through Autonomic Response Testing ART and muscle testing,.... sulfur containing foods always showed to be a problem. broccoli, cauliflower, brussel sprouts, asparagus, eggs, were always a problem.

How I am combating this issue now with success is by taking 1000 - 1500 mcg of Molybdenum and 20 - 30 mg of Magnanese with each meal containing sulfur foods and or supplements. I have a severe issue so I take more than most of my patients. My patients with less of severe issue may take 250 to 500 mcg of Molybdenum and 10 mg of magnanese before each meal containing sulfur. If you have a sulfur issue, it is best not to over eat sulfur rich foods and limit your sulfur rich supplements to the ones that you need the most.

As Dr. Kruse and Dr Doug Wallace have both mentioned, your mitochondrial DNA affect the expression of your nuclear DNA. So if you have healthy Mitochondria, then in theory you may be able to turn off the expression of genetic mutations in your nuclear DNA.

Dr Kruse mentioned in his Vermont 2016 video (17:20 minutes into the video) that in your eye you have a clock and that clock runs the super cosmatic nucleus which controlls every metabolic and growth program in the body, and if your clock doesn't tick right in your eye,.... in front of every gene (25,000 of them) is a peripheral circadium clock that is regulated by the clock in the eye. I would have to believe that this may also affect gene expression.

Hope this helps!!!

# Thread: Dreaming with in a dream

### Billybats — 2017-07-16T16:55:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dreaming-with-in-a-dream.19806/post-219049>

Hello all,

First I want to say I always dream even when I felt I was near death. It is better now meaning for me they are more pleasant, they use to be violent or evil like. Most are vivid. I think I am more active in my dreams then awake. .lol. now as for the dream within a dream. You know when you have been reading to much Dr. Kruse info ( I can't get enough) when the other night I had a dream I was dreaming in that dream I was dreaming in that dream as well. . In the third dream I was dreaming I missed my morning sun, got pissed off and aggravated. Then woke up from that dream and in the second dream I was trying to figure out if it was a dream, which I did figure out it was just a dream but still ify about then forced myself to wske some how. When I actually woke up I still had figure out what time of day it was.. Well it was noonish because I took a nap and it was cloudy out. I did miss morning sun not because I over slept but just because it was cloudy out. I hss been cloudy every morning fir a week. I just read I could still get something on cloudy days, so now I will do morning sun cloudy days too.

I get up to pee at least 3 times a night and wish I could sleep at keast straight through., but every time go back to sleep I dream.

So Dr.K your wirds are with me all the time.

Thank you All.

# Thread: Light RX for Chron's
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-rx-for-chrons.19813/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2017-07-16T21:34:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-rx-for-chrons.19813/post-219078>

A friend of mine has had Chron's for over 20 years and many surgeries to address the disease. I was telling her about light/water/magnetism and she's very receptive. I wrote up a point form "Light RX" to address the issue. Anything I should add or change?

Chron's is a light disease. Fix the light issue, your body will have what it needs to heal & maintain itself, and you won't express the disease any longer. Expose your body's surface to a high dose of strong natural sunlight for 12-18 months. If you live outside the tropics, vacation or ideally stay there during winter months

Daily RX:

-   **Observe first 30 min of sunrise every morning**. Sungaze, or just be in it. The spectrum of the sun in the morning in your eye will signal your pituitary to turn off night time hormones, turn on daytime hormones, and set you up for optimal melatonin production in the evening
-   **Be in the sun 2-3 hours after sunrise**, ideally with 100% body exposure. The IR light will build EZ water in your cells and prepare your body for when higher power UVB will reach the surface
-   **Get 15-60 min of mid-day sun** depending on how you feel. Move into the shade when you feel you've had enough
-   **Observe the sun 30 min before it sets**. The red-shift in the spectrum will boost your nighttime hormone production
-   **After sunset:** Minimize all artificial light sources and cover you skin if they are present. Block 100% of blue light from eyes. Turn off the electricity to the room you sleep in. Eliminate wireless radio transmitters and all forms of nnEMF

Some related Chron's links:
\- Sean's successful reduction in Chron's symptoms [https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1660409414023406:0](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1660409414023406:0)
\- Forum thread [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/long-term-sulfasalazine-effects.13155/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/long-term-sulfasalazine-effects.13155/)
\- Thread on Quantum Health FB Group [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/886910107988551/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/886910107988551/)

# Thread: Swimmers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swimmers.19830/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### John G — 2017-07-18T22:21:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swimmers.19830/post-219304>

[https://psychcentral.com/blog/archives/2010/08/04/how-swimming-reduces-depression/](https://psychcentral.com/blog/archives/2010/08/04/how-swimming-reduces-depression/)

Any other swimmers here who agree? I agree with this article; compared to running, the end of a swim always leaves me feeling euphoric and free. I think it's a combination of low-impact exercise and breathing patterns. The most common breathing pattern in swimming is to breathe every 3rd stroke. I mix it up with 3,5,7,9. When you push of the wall and do underwater dolphin kicks you hold your breath for 5-15 seconds. You can even simply do underwater 25-50 yard streamline dolphin kicks. What's the science here?

I went on vacation recently and was able to do some ocean swimming in cold water. Very exhilarating as a life-long lap swimmer (and slightly terrifying due to murky waters ). My skin always feels fresh compared to the harshness of a chlorinated pool.

### Penny — 2017-07-18T22:58:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swimmers.19830/post-219310>

> [John G said:](/goto/post?id=219304)
>
> [https://psychcentral.com/blog/archives/2010/08/04/how-swimming-reduces-depression/](https://psychcentral.com/blog/archives/2010/08/04/how-swimming-reduces-depression/)
>
> Any other swimmers here who agree? I agree with this article; compared to running, the end of a swim always leaves me feeling euphoric and free. I think it's a combination of low-impact exercise and breathing patterns. The most common breathing pattern in swimming is to breathe every 3rd stroke. I mix it up with 3,5,7,9. When you push of the wall and do underwater dolphin kicks you hold your breath for 5-15 seconds. You can even simply do underwater 25-50 yard streamline dolphin kicks. What's the science here?
>
> I went on vacation recently and was able to do some ocean swimming in cold water. Very exhilarating as a life-long lap swimmer (and slightly terrifying due to murky waters ). My skin always feels fresh compared to the harshness of a chlorinated pool.

I've been swimming in the ocean quite regularly - it's a total high Way better than the pool - in fact, I always had trouble when they wouldn't let you breathe every stroke in a pool but I can go 15 or more strokes in the ocean and not care In fact, I'm going again in another hour

### John G — 2017-07-18T23:16:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swimmers.19830/post-219315>

I'm Jelly.  Beach is 1.5 hours away. I plan to move to a coastal town one day; somewhere the water is cold and the sun is bright.

### harmony — 2017-07-19T00:45:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swimmers.19830/post-219321>

Swimming is always beneficial for me and mostly I can get to the ocean to do so. A little concerned about the next few weeks as we are going to Bali for a holiday and there'll be a beautiful but chlorinated swimming pool right by the door and the people I'm going with will all be happily splashing around in it, while I'm worrying that the chlorine isn't doing me any favours - or the temperature. Hopefully I can convince some people to join me on the beach for swims to lessen exposure! Excited to have a holiday though - maybe enjoyment endorphins will balance out the environment negatives.

### JuicerMoz — 2017-08-01T12:13:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swimmers.19830/post-220604>

Swimming is always good for health. I have been swimming regularly from my childhood.

### BigSkinny — 2017-08-03T18:14:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swimmers.19830/post-220841>

Can ct be done in the ocean or a pool? Does moving in cold water defeat the purpose due to muscle heat?

### WalterNL — 2017-08-04T15:46:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swimmers.19830/post-220904>

> [BigSkinny said:](/goto/post?id=220841)
>
> Can ct be done in the ocean or a pool? Does moving in cold water defeat the purpose due to muscle heat?

That's probably the best way to do CT; cold river or sea.

Moving around does not defeat the purpose no, it might even yield faster results.

### BigSkinny — 2017-08-05T18:03:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swimmers.19830/post-220977>

I am in San Diego, will 60-70 degree water be sufficient?

### WalterNL — 2017-08-05T20:26:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swimmers.19830/post-220990>

> [BigSkinny said:](/goto/post?id=220977)
>
> I am in San Diego, will 60-70 degree water be sufficient?

That's fine, especially if you move around in the water since that increases heat loss.

I believe the CT guide says 50 to 55 F is optimal, but all cooling is good to begin with.

### Benny Hammond — 2017-07-19T22:40:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-music-listening.19843/post-219424>

Hey gang,

I'm looking at optimal options and best practices for listening to music and other audio such as podcasts and videos from YouTube. Currently, I load up podcasts and music to an old iPhone (which I only use for audio media) and take that with me on the road. I was thinking that going back to CDs might be a better option in terms of nnEMF. Do CDs fire off less nnEMF than iPods? Perhaps a USB drive plugged into the car stereo would be an optimal option. What do some of you use?

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-20T08:38:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-music-listening.19843/post-219474>

I love to listen to podcasts on a drive but normally it's through my iphone. I listened to Vermont via my phone plugged into the car stereo with the phone on the other side of the car but I still felt icky.

I can listen to podcasts via iTunes whilst on airplane mode.

Great question!

### Benny Hammond — 2017-07-20T17:56:56+0300

# Thread: Blue Bocker Glasses and Headache +
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-bocker-glasses-and-headache.19853/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2017-07-20T23:39:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-bocker-glasses-and-headache.19853/post-219534>

Yesterday I wore blue blocker glasses I just bought. I wore them while on the computer for about 30 minutes and then while reading (incandescent light) for about an hour. I bought 1.5 strength which was probably a mistake, but I thought for reading they would be good.

About 5 days ago, I began going out to my back lot early in morning where I have a good view of sunrise. I spend anywhere from 1/2 hour (barefoot) to a few hours in am sunlight. My sleep has been much better and nightly bathroom trips dropped to 1 a night and then 0 for 1 night.

Last night, after using the new glasses, I developed a slight headache, got an upset stomach, slept poorly, and when I did fall asleep, had very vivid, strange dreams.

Here's the most important thing. My BP was doing good and after starting am sun, my BP upon waking had dropped to normal. With 1 med, my BP stays under 120/80 all day.

This morning, after using the glasses last evening, BP was very high (155/90) when I got up and then kept going up for the next few hours. After several hours, it did come down but is still running a little higher than normal.

Did I buy the wrong glasses? Could it be that magnified lenses caused the problem? Or should I be looking somewhere else?

[GAMMA RAY 801 Classic Computer Gaming Reading Amber Tinted Glasses](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00O9A69F2/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s00?ie=UTF8&th=1)

### Billybats — 2017-07-21T00:01:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-bocker-glasses-and-headache.19853/post-219537>

Here is a link that has a chart that you can use to see if thay are working.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/)

### Huck — 2017-07-21T15:49:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-bocker-glasses-and-headache.19853/post-219607>

Thanks. With my old pair of glasses that I use for TV, the 2 lines are the same. With the new pair, I see a little bit of blue on the top line. I guess that is what some are calling the good blue light.

Last night I picked up the glasses and had a gut reaction. Same thing this morning. Just looking at the glasses (without putting them on) seems to give me a slight headache.

BP returned to normal late yesterday evening and is OK this morning.

### Billybats — 2017-07-21T15:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-bocker-glasses-and-headache.19853/post-219612>

It says if the bars are the same colors with the blue blockers on they should be okay to use.

### Huck — 2017-07-21T16:14:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-bocker-glasses-and-headache.19853/post-219621>

> [Billybats said:](/goto/post?id=219612)
>
> It says if the bars are the same colors with the blue blockers on they should be okay to use.

I guess I wasn't clear. With the new glasses, I see a very small amount of blue on the top line and none on the bottom line. Some eye glass makers say that there are certain frequencies of blue light that are good and shouldn't be blocked.

I might give the glasses another try in a week and see if I get the same result. if so, they go in the trash.
