# Thread: Question for the UK community
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T18:30:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/post-219643>

Hey,

I'm new, and just about 80% of the way through Epi-Paleo RX, while attempting to digest as much info on the forum and on Jack's posts on the main site as I can in my spare time.

Im thinking, without running labs and tests on yourself, then it would be almost pointless (not saying completely pointless) but too chaotic to make too many changes, without knowing your baseline levels.

I have no idea what RX protocol/s to follow based on my symptoms, so am just muddling along for now. I did note a bunch of links and recommendations in Jack's book, which point to a lot of USA based resources.

Does anyone have a UK specific list of resources for labs and testing to make a start? That would be superb if anyone can help me out in that direction initially.

Thank you

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-21T19:18:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/post-219651>

I know that Genova Diagnostics have a billion tests you can do through the post. Only thing is I'm not sure how you find a practitioner - they ask for that. If you search Genova in the forum you might get some idea there.

The other thing I'd say is that I've used plain old NHS for some tests in the past. As long as you go along with some approximate symptoms and ask specifically for hormone test (you could say you were having issues down below) or thyroid test (you could say you're feeling tired and cold or something) then usually your doc will come up trumps and run some blood work. Once the results are in you can ask for a print out and start there by posting on this forum.

Just an idea,,,

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T19:28:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/post-219654>

Heh great tips. I'll check out the Genova lead now.... I have a phobia of going to the doctor though, haven't been in years and years, so I'd have to try to get over that fear to make an appointment and goto the GP. However, if this is true and you're able to blag your way into free testing for some panels thanks to the NHS, I think I probably would go for it. Especially thyroid ad maybe testosterone testing. Thanks again Emma.

### drezy — 2017-07-22T01:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/post-219692>

Sorry, us Americans have to crash every party... Also I follow Emma. I'd no sooner get in the way of Emma and her children's optimal as I'd run in between a momma polar bear (since Emma is dipping into the cold now) and her cubs. It's a fool's errand.

\*Warning slight political content ahead\* Your NHS and even my "private" insurance seem to do a great job at disease management. No mention of actual health there. It's similar to what I explained to my mom. In her childhood, when you got ice cream it was something like 4 ingredients. Now go get ice cream in a store and you'll fall asleep before you finish reading all the ingredients. Feels like we've overcomplicated things a bit in all domains.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-22T08:49:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/post-219719>

Haha, thanks @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

I definitely think your doc is worth pursuing if you can push through the awkwardness. My husband just did it for full hormone and thyroid panel. You'll literally be in there five mins then back again for the nurse to bleed you dry

I've done it three times now through the doc. First time my iron came back super low and I got a prescription - which I ditched and went for flurodix (this was before I knew much but I still knew I didn't want pills). Second and third time I wasn't given any feedback other than I was 'within range' but I still asked for the print out anyway then went home and googled the shit out of every single result!!

It'll save you a boat tonne of cash - but, that said, genova would probably go deeper than standard NHS test. It's a good starting point though...

# Thread: Patreon.....??

**Posts:** 8

### caroline — 2017-07-22T07:18:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219714>

I am having a little trouble understanding this ...... anyone else?

I understand what it is but I am wondering .... do we pay for every blog that JK writes? do we have access again and again to the same blog we paid for?

Dr. K. mentioned that it is the price of a cup of coffee a month - but there seems to be a charge per blog and we are automatically charged when Jack puts up a new blog?

Given how prolific Jack is ......

and - how do I fit in more reading??????

All this is vitally important to me .......but I need to spend less time on my computer - not more, and I have a life to live with a guy I adore.

Unless I can speed read like Jack does - I think I am sunk.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-22T08:01:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219716>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=219714)
>
> I am having a little trouble understanding this ...... anyone else?
>
> I understand what it is but I am wondering .... do we pay for every blog that JK writes? do we have access again and again to the same blog we paid for?
>
> Dr. K. mentioned that it is the price of a cup of coffee a month - but there seems to be a charge per blog and we are automatically charged when Jack puts up a new blog?
>
> Given how prolific Jack is ......
>
> and - how do I fit in more reading??????
>
> All this is vitally important to me .......but I need to spend less time on my computer - not more, and I have a life to live with a guy I adore.
>
> Unless I can speed read like Jack does - I think I am sunk.

I've joined Caroline. I just copy and paste the article into a word document and print it out to read. You continue to have access, and there's a comments section, a bit like the blogs comments section. You pledge an amount for each article Jack does in the month, (min $1) but you can set a limit on how many articles per month you will pay for. So say you pledge $1 for each article, but set a limit of 4 articles, your max bill should be $4 in a month. (Plus VAT if you are UK). Because you can choose what you pledge, and can change it if you want, I think its great for people who want to support Jack for the blogs, or acknowledge how much help he gives on the forum, but can't swing a silver or gold membership.

### caroline — 2017-07-22T09:50:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219725>

thanks Sue - I will have to go back and check what I actually signed up for.....

I agree - it is great to acknowledge how much help Jack gives us.

### WalterNL — 2017-07-22T20:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219759>

### drezy — 2017-07-22T21:36:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219764>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=219753)
>
> So we have to pay now to read Jacks blog?

Honestly, too much is for free to begin with. Expecting more freebies really isn't productive.

If you can follow the subtle hints, read the comments in the Vermont 2017 youtube. One person was particularly feisty/douchebaggy. In the end I think that person had a valid question. Jack replied very directly and honestly. He's an honest guy and that's why so many end up running away from him or even try burning him. Jack has mentioned that his wife sees degradation and urges him to pull back from much of the effort of helping others.

**I am 1000% on team Sandra Kruse on this one.**

If I had to bet who will outlive the other between @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) and @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) currently.... I have to put my money on JanSz simply because I don't like losing money. Doc is burning the candle at both ends regardless of his knowledge and hacks.

Expecting Doc to crank out this type of cross-disciplinary analysis and connections endlessly for free is untenable.

I need Jack alive for the majority of my son's life and my grandchildren's early life. The man already turned back my own clock 10-20 years. Now I need him in the game longer for my lineage.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-07-22T22:09:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219768>

Well said Drezy! And for all that Jack does for others, I feel it's very important that we carry the torch too and share the message of LWM with anyone who is willing to listen.

I've always supported Doc and have never asked for anything free. I know I don't ask him questions because if I have one and I can find the answer for myself, I'm going to challenge myself and do so.

I love Jack like a little brother (Yes he is younger than me , lol) and have so much gratitude and appreciation for all that he does. I don't want to leave Sandra out of this either because not enough credit goes to her for what she does. And behind every good man stands a great lady who loves and support him and Jack needs to listen to his wife.

Thanks for sharing your thoughts here too Drezy!

Rock on all of you beautiful Starfish!

Personally, my health would be in the toilet hadn't I discovered Jack!

### caroline — 2017-07-23T04:57:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219794>

beautifully said Rose and Dan......

Jack has cared for and about all of us for a long time now - at his own expense in every way ....health wise and monetary.

I know JK hates all this gratitude ...but too darn bad. Suck it up Dr. K. ..... a lot of us owe you so much.

and also ....Thanks to Jack's rock - Sandy for underpinning all of this - more than we will ever know I am sure.

### halvibe — 2019-09-12T19:16:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-277724>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=219764)
>
> Honestly, too much is for free to begin with. Expecting more freebies really isn't productive.
>
> I need Jack alive for the majority of my son's life and my grandchildren's early life. The man already turned back my own clock 10-20 years. Now I need him in the game longer for my lineage.

I agree with you, drezy. I'd like to see Jack around (and healthy) for a long time also. To repeat a quote by a guy named Werner,
"You can care about people, or you can care about what people think about you. And, you can't have both!"
I think you all know which side Jack is on. That's also why he gets all the flak. I hope he takes the support from us and damn the pea-shooter brains out there!

# Thread: Why is my Raynauds appearing?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-my-raynauds-appearing.19864/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-22T09:04:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-my-raynauds-appearing.19864/post-219720>

I've had Raynauds for quite a number of years but normally it only really appears in Winter when I'm obviously cold or touching cold things.

I've had four flare ups in the last week and I'm wondering if Iodine or salt could somehow be causing it?

It obviously isn't the sun and yeah, I've had a handful (pun!) of cold baths but yeah, it's a bit weird, I never usually get it in Summer and I literally can't remember the last time I had it - not even last Winter...

Might the Lugols I've been applying topically be a culprit?

Any thoughts welcome!

### Foxglove — 2017-07-24T03:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-my-raynauds-appearing.19864/post-219883>

I don't know if you are taking anything, but I used to get Raynaud's from certain drugs, even when I wasn't cold. The vasoconstrictors like phenylephrine and, for some reason, a couple hours after using my steroid inhaler (although my doctor couldn't figure that one out). Have you written down every time it happens and try to find a pattern?

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-24T08:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-my-raynauds-appearing.19864/post-219896>

> [Foxglove said:](/goto/post?id=219883)
>
> I don't know if you are taking anything, but I used to get Raynaud's from certain drugs, even when I wasn't cold. The vasoconstrictors like phenylephrine and, for some reason, a couple hours after using my steroid inhaler (although my doctor couldn't figure that one out). Have you written down every time it happens and try to find a pattern?

I've been high dosing salt straight under my tongue twice a day. I believe this may be the cause!

Thanks for your input

# Thread: Sleeping position
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Foxglove — 2017-07-24T04:02:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-219885>

Is there anything I should know about sleeping positions, such as on your back or your side? And I've heard that a firm mattress is better for your joints. Is that true?

I would ideally like to sleep in my back but I don't know if that's bad for you. I stopped sleeping on my back as a young adult, because I had sleep paralysis every time I slept that way. So I developed a bad habit of sleeping on my stomach. I think it's hurting my neck. But if I'm on my side I usually need a pillow under my leg, and not under my head, because it throws off the alignment of my back.

Just wondering if anyone has thoughts on this? I want my sleep to be more restful.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-24T14:52:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-219915>

I'm a very particular sleeper. I need a bed to myself, at least king size. A pillow either side (side-sleeper). Absolute silence - I wear ear plugs and an eye mask. Everything has to be just so for me to get good sleep.

For me, I think if you're getting good sleep then it doesn't matter what position you're in.

I tried back sleeping for a while in an effort to reduce nasolabial lines (vanity). Just couldn't do it. I needed to sleep on my side!

Also tried without a pillow for a while but woke up puffy faced.

Interested to hear other ideas...

### Foxglove — 2017-07-24T17:55:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-219943>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=219915)
>
> I'm a very particular sleeper. I need a bed to myself, at least king size. A pillow either side (side-sleeper). Absolute silence - I wear ear plugs and an eye mask. Everything has to be just so for me to get good sleep.
>
> For me, I think if you're getting good sleep then it doesn't matter what position you're in.
>
> I tried back sleeping for a while in an effort to reduce nasolabial lines (vanity). Just couldn't do it. I needed to sleep on my side!
>
> Also tried without a pillow for a while but woke up puffy faced.
>
> Interested to hear other ideas...

Yeah I'm sort of picky too. I don't like any light and I keep a fan on. But I'm not getting very good sleep overall. That's why I'm asking.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-24T18:56:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-219953>

I think its how you define "good sleep." I was an insomniac who blamed DH's snoring .... I reckoned he could sleep on a clothes line, whereas I needed things to be perfect - and they rarely were. The only decent nights sleep I got was when the sleep pressure of extreme exhaustion took over, but that's not the same as good sleep, to me that's survival mode sleep. The reason DH isn't nearly so bad is that he agreed not to eat after sunset. (Snoring improved considerably and he lost weight in weeks that took me MONTHS to achieve. ) I was moving to another room if he at least didn't try it. Thought my sleep was better until I've kept hacking it. Circadian rhythm, sunlight, grounding, CT, hacking different evening blue blocking options, different black out curtains and bedding. Its an ongoing hack process. Wake feeling refreshed, don't need ear plugs or a mask, asleep within minutes. No idea if he is snoring or not, (but he still doesn't snack in the evening) no full moon differences, I sleep through a storm ... I start on my side facing west, recovery position, 2 pillows, but no idea after that. He's happy because I am, we can sleep together, and I'm not blaming him any more.

### Penny — 2017-07-24T20:05:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-219957>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=219953)
>
> I think its how you define "good sleep." I was an insomniac who blamed DH's snoring .... I reckoned he could sleep on a clothes line, whereas I needed things to be perfect - and they rarely were. The only decent nights sleep I got was when the sleep pressure of extreme exhaustion took over, but that's not the same as good sleep, to me that's survival mode sleep. The reason DH isn't nearly so bad is that he agreed not to eat after sunset. (Snoring improved considerably and he lost weight in weeks that took me MONTHS to achieve. ) I was moving to another room if he at least didn't try it. Thought my sleep was better until I've kept hacking it. Circadian rhythm, sunlight, grounding, CT, hacking different evening blue blocking options, different black out curtains and bedding. Its an ongoing hack process. Wake feeling refreshed, don't need ear plugs or a mask, asleep within minutes. No idea if he is snoring or not, (but he still doesn't snack in the evening) no full moon differences, I sleep through a storm ... I start on my side facing west, recovery position, 2 pillows, but no idea after that. He's happy because I am, we can sleep together, and I'm not blaming him any more.

I've had raging sleep success by putting a lamp with a red incandescent light on the bed and wearing a black out mask - I also can fall asleep quite fast if I shine an IR light on the back of my neck/lower head - be careful - you can burn with them if you fall asleep without turning it off... and being cold of course is everything...

### Penny — 2017-07-24T20:06:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-219958>

I sleep on my side with a tube pillow under my arm/leg - if keeps my shoulders from sagging -

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-25T11:41:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220012>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=219957)
>
> I've had raging sleep success by putting a lamp with a red incandescent light on the bed and wearing a black out mask - I also can fall asleep quite fast if I shine an IR light on the back of my neck/lower head - be careful - you can burn with them if you fall asleep without turning it off... and being cold of course is everything...

Masking my eyes makes them uncomfortable and hot, I've tried several, but maybe my response is because I have a touch of anxiety with them, possibly because with my eyes shut I'm intuitively aware that my eyelids and skin around my eyes can't "breathe" normally. I used to take them on holiday because usually resorts have lights outside to light up paths, and the curtains are never enough. I went from a sleep mask to putting up towels with clothes pegs over the curtains. Even better is thick black wheelie bin liners put on the glass with blue tack. Those are now on the holiday packing essentials list. I'll be ready for night 1.

### caroline — 2017-07-25T12:17:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220018>

Apparently lowering the foot of the bed - so head is higher - helps

I have also read on QFB - about a weighted blanket. It said \[I think\] it really helps autistic kids.

I would give this a go I think. There was a time when my DH was seriously ill and in the hospital. I stayed close by. It was freezing cold in my room and I didn't want to turn the heater on so I just put all the blankets in the room on top of me. I slept like a log and didn't move the whole night. Given the tragic circumstances - I couldn't believe that I even slept at all.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-25T13:30:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220024>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=220018)
>
> I have also read on QFB - about a weighted blanket. It said \[I think\] it really helps autistic kids.

I think that could relate to the senses [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-senses.11361/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-senses.11361/). If an autistic child doesn't sense the right fields, I think they could feel that they aren't anchored to their environment, as if they might float away, so "letting go" to sleep could be extremely stressful, if the environment they sense doesn't make them feel "safe." If its not the sensory issue, I think the weighted blanket may not be as successful. The senses list is one reason why I'm glad I don't need to sleep separately from DH, I could and would if I had to, but being with him I still get the proximal closeness, pheremonal sensing , magnetic field interaction etc...Being able to sleep now anyway, it adds to my sense of well being. Now I am able to sleep because of hacking light water and magnetism etc, his presence makes it a 1+1 = 3.

### Foxglove — 2017-07-25T17:34:27+0300

I just tried this last night and it worked! I had the lamp on for a few minutes and dang, I was so relaxed! I'm going to do this from now on.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-25T19:16:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220051>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=220012)

Does that create a sauna in the room by bin lining the windows??

I said to my two friends I can't sleep with my husband as I'm such a light sleepter. They both balked at the idea of not sleeping with your partner. It's only since having children I'm not able to sleep with someone. I'm now the lightest sleeper ever and it sucks.

I'm up for trying the weighted blanket although wouldn't you then heat up too much during the night?

### Penny — 2017-07-25T21:54:58+0300

Weighted blankets are for sensory issues - there was a blog on this - and I think it would be hotter than hell

It is SO bloody hot right now, I can't use the black out curtains I bought and the sleep mask was too hot - and it's not even August/September yet - our hottest months - I'm going for the ice pack tonight or just sleeping in the motorhome - zippo air flow - if you use the red light, I wouldn't fall asleep with your face pointed to it - every time I do, my eyes are blurry for awhile - this can't be good news for my biology Not sure reading at night with a red light is that great either - I just reread Quantum sleep and it seems to me it really has to be dark right after the sunset transition - or have lots of sex and you will immediately feel sleepy

### caroline — 2017-07-26T04:05:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220090>

I don't think the weighted blankets are actually that hot or heavy. google them - I found them really interesting and was going to get one but I couldn't decide what size ....and then I forgot!

As Sue says - it is about feeling safe.....

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-26T08:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220103>

I might try it on a colder night here and see if I notice a difference.

The other thing I'd add is that my period is due and before then I feel even hotter - as in warm, not sexy - and that makes sleep worse too.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-26T10:35:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220111>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=220051)
>
> Does that create a sauna in the room by bin lining the windows??

I've only done it once in Cornwall in April. The ground floor room was in a converted coach house, couldn't get a phone signal, but they had a floodlight lighting up the whole courtyard outside and we had large windows and a glazed door. Didn't have enough towels to peg up to get it blocked and even if we did there was too much light bleeding through the top bottom and sides of the supposedly blackout curtains.  We went to a supermarket to buy a dark king sized sheet and I suddenly had a picture in my head of bin liners stuck up ... Rigged it so that the windows were covered, but one for ventilation we covered the glass and opened the window, and used a towel over that part of the curtaining. So April in the UK no problem. In Fuerteventura we had an apartment and used towels in the bedroom, which on their own I thought would reduce the circulation of the air coming through the partly opened window, but we left the bedroom door open and opened a window in the bathroom and a sliding door in the lounge so that there was a pull through of air. I'll have to assess the layout in every apartment/hotel room we go to, but I think the liners would still work.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-26T16:55:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220136>

@[ScottishEmma](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20059/) My N=1 with my home made post sunset blue blockers is now an N=2. My friend has hacked them and wearing them for 20 minutes before bed has improved her sleep, and she thinks she is dreaming more. 20-30 minutes is about right for me too, I'm still hacking ones for earlier. If you wanted to try it, PM me, I could post you a piece of the gel, you would need some cheap as chips clear safety glasses, cut a paper template to get an exact fit for the inside of them and then cut the gel to fit.

### Martha Ray — 2019-12-29T02:49:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-284030>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=220090)
>
> I don't think the weighted blankets are actually that hot or heavy. google them - I found them really interesting and was going to get one but I couldn't decide what size ....and then I forgot!

I've also wondeed what size to get if it matters with weighted blankets. A local CT therapy company is selling a crib size weighted blanket for adults and the owner said that size was all that was needed. I will most likely buy one from her/

....I used to sleep with a **_Bed Buddy_** Hot/Cold Pack Thermatherapy from _Bed Bath & Beyond_ on my chest at night in the winter and it was warm and relaxing but used microwave to heat and after I got rid of my microwave I changed to use a **_Fomentek_** _Hot/Cold water bag_ [https://fomentek.com/](https://fomentek.com/) The Fomentek bag are also great for using for castor oil packs.

### caroline — 2019-12-29T03:33:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-284032>

Please keep us posted Martha if you get a weighted blanket.

I have probably posted this before....Once when my DH was in the hospital for an extended stay - I stayed in the Lodge that the hospital provides for family of very ill patients.
It was extremely cold one night and I didn't want to turn the heater on and so I piled all the blankets from the other beds on my single bed. I even had them doubled over. I had the best nights sleep, even tho my DH was gravely ill, and I never moved the entire night.

I think that was my version of a weighted blanket and I have been keen to try one ever since. I think all the weight made me feel safe and comforted.

### Marko Pollo — 2019-12-29T16:31:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-284036>

Have you seen or read about Temple Grandin? She is an autistic university professor that pioneered low stress animal handling. An amazing, inspiring story. I highly recommend her 2010 movie for all , and any of her books for those interested in animals.
Anyway, while in college, struggling mightily with social stress, she built a squeeze chute to calm herself like the ones used in cattle handling. This squeeze effect may be similar to the weighted blanket or that provided by submersion in water. OG biohacker extraordinaire! Changed her environment and the environment of other stakeholders (animals).
I see many parallels for those with developed natural field sensitivities or nnemf . 5GCanary ! We can improve our environment and advocate for other less represented members of our ecosytem.

### Martha Ray — 2020-01-05T04:17:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-284245>

> [Foxglove said:](/goto/post?id=219885)
>
> ...
> Just wondering if anyone has thoughts on this? I want my sleep to be more restful.

**I am very interested in better sleep.**
I deisred for many years (long b4 Jack Kruse).. to go to bed at sunset and wake with sunrise--- always made sense to me. But I never did it until this year and not acomplished at it...yet. Did read a Quirky but purposeful book on sleep titled
[Lights Out: Sleep, Sugar and Survival](http://www.amazon.com/Lights-Out-Sleep-Sugar-Survival/dp/0671038680), T. S. Wiley [https://www.amazon.com/Lights-Out-Sleep-Sugar-Survival/dp/0671038680](https://www.amazon.com/Lights-Out-Sleep-Sugar-Survival/dp/0671038680)
Very ancestral better re-read it now. I also read a great article that I think was by Victoria Boutenko author of _Green for Life_... will go try and find that one ..whoever it was she and her family were sleeping out doors in a 'yurt' ? type round building with high windows that opened .. again I will go try and find that... I hope I kept it--- It amazed me. If I find will post.

I sleep on my back but wish I could more or change positions a little more.... I wonder about sleep wear, beds, bedding etc. I have to have things just so to get to sleep -- find afer that. Have been testing an Avana Comfort Mattress Elevator for _**Incline Bed Therapy**_ and intersted in Weighted Blankets looking at **_Xalm_** [https://xalm.co.uk/products/xalm-weighted-blanket](https://xalm.co.uk/products/xalm-weighted-blanket) also on Amazon... I think this is the brand _Temple Grandin_ recommends but too expenisve for me might try a Yoga Pad like the[RAVI Yoga | For Yoga Practice & Calming](<http://RAVI Yoga | For Yoga Practice & Calming>) [https://myravi.com/shop-ravi-yoga/](https://myravi.com/shop-ravi-yoga/)

I just shared the info below with _Cozy Pure_ and _Flo-Bed_ bedding product companies I've done business with in the past so I cut and pasted it here also mentions _**Mouth Taping**_ that I believe has been a great find for our family. DH uses any wound tape but I started out with blue 3M wound tape for sensitive skin but I have such ultra sensitve skin I use _**Somnifix**_ [https://somnifix.com/products/mouth...MIxtrH4Kzr5gIVOYNaBR02fgfrEAAYASABEgJd1vD\_BwE](https://somnifix.com/products/mouth-strips-snoring-sleep-aid?gclid=EAIaIQobChMIxtrH4Kzr5gIVOYNaBR02fgfrEAAYASABEgJd1vD_BwE)
I have to remove some of the adheisive by stripping it putting in on clean organic fabric and pulling it off numerous times. **Pricey but so critical to our sleep** DH snors and I puff as my dentist explained. The Somnifix are supposed to be single use but I handle with care and get a week to 10 nights.
\-------------------------------
Web articles and videos on **Inclined Bed Therapy
Avana Comfort 7" Elevator Mattress link - I am testing this product out now - so far so good.**
and Tip about Mouth Taping You Tube Video is below.
\------------------------------------

-   Sophia Health Institute's **_INCLINED BED THERAPY_**
    [https://www.sophiahi.com/inclined-bed-therapy/](https://www.sophiahi.com/inclined-bed-therapy/)

-   _**The Surprising Benefits of Inclined Bed Therapy**_
    by Dr Mercola (Article includes Andrew K Fletcher's video....**\--->** **Sweet Fellow**!)
    [https://inclinedbedtherapy.com/mult...enefits-of-inclined-bed-therapy-by-dr-mercola](https://inclinedbedtherapy.com/multimedia/news-articles/185-the-surprising-benefits-of-inclined-bed-therapy-by-dr-mercola)

-   Direct link to the **_You Tube_** Video
    **_Phenomenal Health Benefits Of Inclined Bed Therapy_** Andrew K Fletcher's (UK) hosted by _Patrick Timpone One Radio Network_ show.

-   Dave Asprey's _Bulletproof, **Inclined Bed Therapy: Sleep on an Incline for A Better Night’s Sleep**_
    [https://www.bulletproof.com/sleep/sleep-hacks/inclined-bed-therapy-sleep/](https://www.bulletproof.com/sleep/sleep-hacks/inclined-bed-therapy-sleep/)
-   **_Avana Comfort_ 7" Mattress Elevator Product**
    [https://www.avanacomfort.com/mattress-elevator.html](https://www.avanacomfort.com/mattress-elevator.html)

\---------------------------------
Mouth Taping I know it sounds strange....I put it off for 6 months while DH tried it right away and the very next day he did not have usual afternoon nap. We have now been mouth taping for over 2 years and it has helped with both our sleep and our energy levels.

-   _You Tube_ Video How To Mouth Tape For Better Sleep

    by Mark Burhenne, DDS of _Ask The Dentist dot com_ [https://askthedentist.com/](https://askthedentist.com/) Mark Burhenne DDS is also the author of a great little book called the 8 hours sleep paradox.

    If you check out the articles and the links don't work just google and I'm sure they will come up
    May be too much infomation for some but **I am dead serious about SLEEP I know it is critical.**

# Thread: Can I turn off wifi but use ethernet on a wifi router....and pc ethernet light not on/won't work
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-turn-off-wifi-but-use-ethernet-on-a-wifi-router-and-pc-ethernet-light-not-on-wont-work.19884/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ValerieBee — 2017-07-24T23:56:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-turn-off-wifi-but-use-ethernet-on-a-wifi-router-and-pc-ethernet-light-not-on-wont-work.19884/post-219971>

Embarrassed to ask, but I'd rather be embarrassed than deal with more EMF than my family needs. I want to get rid of WIFI in the house ASAP. However, my partner, the homeowner, does not. I'm SAHM with a 4 and 1 year old. He works about 60 hours per week, so that's all time it could be off, but I still need to connect to the internet sporadically throughout the day. (Bills, emails, help like this on the forum... ) I am trying to configure it so it would only be on during evening hours when he's home and awake and wants to use it. We have a wifi router. Its looking like I cannot disable wifi while still keeping ethernet on? Do I need to buy a different type of router? I'm currently having an issue that Windows 10 has disabled my ethernet. I know it is not a problem with the router or the cord, the green light is not on at the desktop pc. Should I just put the WiFi on a kill switch and only plug it in when it is needed, rather than use the ethernet? Please forgive my ignorance here. I'm learning as much and as fast as I can here and I appreciate any help. TIA.

No need to be embarrassed. That's kind of specific to spread out on a forum maybe. You can PM me and Caroline the site moderator (and or anyone else you are comfortable including in a PM) to keep yourself at ease that I'm not some internet hyper-predator. Hyper-predator even sounds like too much work for me... I'll describe what we did and how we did it.

# Thread: Impending Cataract Surgery?..How about a blu-blocker lens!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impending-cataract-surgery-how-about-a-blu-blocker-lens.19886/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dennis Durost — 2017-07-25T05:11:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impending-cataract-surgery-how-about-a-blu-blocker-lens.19886/post-219997>

I was kind of bummed out when I learned that cataract surgery would block the beneficial morning light from my eyes. I checked with my eye surgeon and he informed me that my lenses were ALCON Acrysof IQ IOL with UA and proprietary blue-blocking technology. They sent me the following information:

**BLU BLOCKER CATARACT REPLACEMENT LENS**
Recently, there have been great advances in artificial lens technology. The Acrysof IQ Intraocular Lens (IOL) is an example. All currently available artificial lenses filter ultraviolet (UV) light, which is harmful to the retina in the eye. The Acrysof IQ IOL filters both UV light and high-energy blue light. Clinical studies have shown that high-energy blue light causes oxidative damage to the retina and may be a risk factor for developing macular degeneration. The Acysof IQ lens incorporates a patented chromophore that blocks harmful blue light and UV light in much the same way as our natural lens does prior to developing cataracts. The FDA clinical studies showed no adverse side effects on color vision or quality of vision. The Acrysof IQ IOL inserts through a micro-incision that requires no stitches, and it is designed to conform to the natural shape of the lens capsule. This helps it stay stable and centered in the eye. But what makes the Acrysof IQ IOL unique is its ability to filter blue light. The Acrysof IQ (Blue-Blocker) IOL enhances the benefits of advanced cataract surgery by filtering out harmful blue light and, thus providing a replacement lens that protects the retina more like your natural lens. Additionally, this lens is designed with wavefront-guided, aspheric optics that help improve contrast sensitivity and overall visual quality.

Downside: $900 an eye (out of pocket)

### evren — 2018-03-08T08:48:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impending-cataract-surgery-how-about-a-blu-blocker-lens.19886/post-236657>

> [Dennis Durost said:](/goto/post?id=219997)
>
> I was kind of bummed out when I learned that cataract surgery would block the beneficial morning light from my eyes. I checked with my eye surgeon and he informed me that my lenses were ALCON Acrysof IQ IOL with UA and proprietary blue-blocking technology. They sent me the following information:
>
> **BLU BLOCKER CATARACT REPLACEMENT LENS**
> Recently, there have been great advances in artificial lens technology. The Acrysof IQ Intraocular Lens (IOL) is an example. All currently available artificial lenses filter ultraviolet (UV) light, which is harmful to the retina in the eye. The Acrysof IQ IOL filters both UV light and high-energy blue light. Clinical studies have shown that high-energy blue light causes oxidative damage to the retina and may be a risk factor for developing macular degeneration. The Acysof IQ lens incorporates a patented chromophore that blocks harmful blue light and UV light in much the same way as our natural lens does prior to developing cataracts. The FDA clinical studies showed no adverse side effects on color vision or quality of vision. The Acrysof IQ IOL inserts through a micro-incision that requires no stitches, and it is designed to conform to the natural shape of the lens capsule. This helps it stay stable and centered in the eye. But what makes the Acrysof IQ IOL unique is its ability to filter blue light. The Acrysof IQ (Blue-Blocker) IOL enhances the benefits of advanced cataract surgery by filtering out harmful blue light and, thus providing a replacement lens that protects the retina more like your natural lens. Additionally, this lens is designed with wavefront-guided, aspheric optics that help improve contrast sensitivity and overall visual quality.
>
> Downside: $900 an eye (out of pocket)

Hi Dennis, did anyone come back to you on this post? My mother is about to undergo cataract surgery and I am also wondering if this is a good option compared to the classic UV blocking lenses. From what I understand, the one you mentioned above blocks both UV (not ideal) and blue light (better than just UV block one would assume?). Many thanks.

### Billybats — 2018-05-03T15:32:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impending-cataract-surgery-how-about-a-blu-blocker-lens.19886/post-240405>

### Billybats — 2018-05-03T21:01:34+0300

# Thread: IR-A Near Infrared Bulb
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ir-a-near-infrared-bulb.19895/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shiran — 2017-07-26T14:12:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ir-a-near-infrared-bulb.19895/post-220116>

I have a regular dry sauna , I want to add IR-A Near Infrared Bulb inside the sauna .
[https://www.rubyluxlights.com/product-page/pre-order-rubylux-nir-a-individual-infrared-bulb](https://www.rubyluxlights.com/product-page/pre-order-rubylux-nir-a-individual-infrared-bulb)
Do you think it is effective? If so
When I'm the day should I use it?
What else do I need to know about a IR-A Near Infrared Bulb and how exactly should I use it in a sauna and inside my house ?
Thanks for the help

### JanSz — 2017-07-26T14:53:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ir-a-near-infrared-bulb.19895/post-220122>

This is really nice question to discuss.

The lamp that you posted, while it is called IR-A it creates lots of heat.
Lots of different frequencies.
So in sauna application it is likely ok.
Saunas work minutes/day.

With discussion on Quantlet and beneficial wavelengths, 850nm stands out.
850nm can also be called IR-A but it is just narrow frequency range (just the one you want).
Its benefit is mostly due to this narrow range.

If lamp creates only 850nm it does not create much heat and uses most of power just for 850nm creation.
It is easier to use that kind of lamp for long hours indoor, it does not raise room temperature, can use DC to power it, which is a plus from pov --->(help pls).

DC power is healthier than AC.

.....

### kris90 — 2017-07-27T19:01:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crushed-by-mother-nature.19901/post-220235>

Is this why GMO foods are everywhere now? Perhaps because photosynthesis DOES NOT occur in cold climates, and we cannot successfully grow crops the way we used to? Another reason that fruits and vegetables are becoming less and less important in our diet, and why seafood should be our primary food source. It is no wonder we need to import most of our food from the equator, and consuming high photonic foods in a low photonic environment is the most efficient way to pack on fat.

Perhaps the obesity epidemic is tied to a premature preparatory program in place for humans to adapt to the massive energy losses on earth to combat the famine and cold we will soon face.

# Thread: Metals and EMFs all up in your Ladyparts-the new frontier of birth control
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metals-and-emfs-all-up-in-your-ladyparts-the-new-frontier-of-birth-control.19927/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ValerieBee — 2017-07-29T23:34:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metals-and-emfs-all-up-in-your-ladyparts-the-new-frontier-of-birth-control.19927/post-220393>

WaPo recently ran a pretty thorough article about Essure, the "permanent nonsurgical birth control". I thought this community would find this very interesting, and may have had some experiences that are similar.

Personally, I had a really bad time with Paraguard, the nonhormonal, copper IUD. I also had crazy, "unexplainable" medical issues pop up. After the birth of my first son I knew I did not want to have hormonal birth control any more. I had experienced serious mood modulation and control issues, extreme breast sensitivity and more. So when my midwife recommended Paraguard I went for it, even though my gut said no. I was just too overwhelmed by new motherhood to make another decision, or research the F\*\*K out of it as I usually would. It took 2 years of misunderstandings, misdiagnosis, or just plain misses, by myself, my gyno, a PT who specializes in women's pelvic health, a therapist, and an eye doc, before I finally just had it removed. My cycle became extremely heavy bleeding for a week, every 3 weeks. I had seriously painful intercourse, chalked up to sensitivity after childbirth. I had RAGE and my depression seriously reached a head, dismissed as PPD. I had dry eye. When I developed a transient cyst by my ovary I finally listened to my gut and had it removed. It is amazing how quickly all of my issues resolved. I remember the first time I started raging after it was removed, it was like I was watching myself angry and screaming... I just kind of stopped and realized, I'm not really that angry, who turned this script on?

I now use Natural Family Planning, and it has changed my life.

Some quotes from the article:

> "The goal wasn’t for the device itself to block the tubes, but rather for it to stimulate what’s called foreign body response: The fibers would irritate or inflame the tissue, which would signal the immune system to surround the foreign body with cells that form scar tissue. This would encase the device and occlude the tubes..."
>
> "For its outer, ribbonlike coil, it chose a relatively new material in the medical device arena called Nitinol, which had been developed at the now-closed Naval Ordnance Laboratory in Silver Spring, Md. (coincidentally, the current location of the FDA). Nitinol, a flexible nickel-titanium “shape memory” alloy, as it is known, can be set to take a shape at a certain temperature, relax at a lower temperature and bounce back once warmed up again. For Essure, shape memory meant that the coils could be packed tightly in a slim delivery catheter. Once they hit body temperature, they would expand, allowing the PET fibers in the device to incite the “acute inflammation followed by chronic inflammation” that would generate the scar tissue and block the tubes, according to the 2004 paper.
>
> While Nitinol had been used in medical devices since the 1980s, there were already published reports of failures with cardiovascular stents made of the material and of patients developing reactions to metal implants in general. According to Peter Schalock, a Boston-area dermatologist who has published extensively on metal hypersensitivity, reactions to implanted devices can include rashes or eczema, chronic inflammation and chronic pain, all of which are among the adverse events reported by Essure women.
>
> Schalock and several experts in immunology or toxicology expressed concern at the 2015 FDA hearing that the nickel in Essure could be triggering allergic or autoimmune reactions, in which the immune system overreacts and attacks healthy cells. In addition to the issue of nickel, he and other physicians have raised concerns about the PET fibers used to incite the inflammatory response: “Maybe the inflammatory state goes a little wild,” Schalock told me. “The question is what’s driving it. Is it the Essure, or is the Essure waking up some sort of predisposition to autoimmune disease?” "

[http://www.washingtonpost.com/sf/style/2017/07/26/essure/?tid=sm\_fb&utm\_term=.550727d6ce14](http://www.washingtonpost.com/sf/style/2017/07/26/essure/?tid=sm_fb&utm_term=.550727d6ce14)

# Thread: My CT trick
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stevie D — 2017-08-04T16:42:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/post-220908>

So I've got a freezer filled with water I use for my CT and during the winter i just use my pool and the worst part when i first started was the "Holy fuck my balls are cold" moment when you first get in. The trick is one I learnt from my mma coach Steve Morris (I'm named after him lol) and it was one he taught me to do when body conditioning i.e when smashing various parts of the body on various wood, metal, and other objects to build toughness into the limb.

You find one of the softest cloths you can and rub it between your fingers constantly as you smash your shin against the target. As long as you rub the cloth it made the impact much easier to deal with. And I discovered it does the exact same thing in CT. I started using a bit of an old soft blanket I cut off and then realized I was an idiot and that I have an entire soft blanket stuck to my head by nature.

Now all I have to do is rub my hair for a few moments as I gently lower into my freezer and bam it totally distracts me from the chill. Hope it helps someone

### Sheddie — 2017-08-04T19:01:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/post-220909>

Very cool trick! I'm only at face-dunks, when I feel brave enough, but maybe it works then, too.

### Stevie D — 2017-08-04T19:26:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/post-220914>

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=220909)
>
> Very cool trick! I'm only at face-dunks, when I feel brave enough, but maybe it works then, too.

yeah it works on face dunking too. I used it when i first started by rubbing my towel between my finger and thumb. Good luck

### ScottishEmma — 2017-08-05T09:21:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/post-220960>

Seriously? Rubbing your hair helps get in the tub? I'll try it!!

### Lahelada — 2017-08-05T16:25:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/post-220971>

Great post . I believe what you are doing is actually creating a paradox in your autonomous nervous system. So you are introducing an action from the feed and breed cycle (caress)to a time when your eye has already signaled "stress " at seeing the cold water in the tub.
We do it unconsciously more often than we think . We rub people who are upset between the shoulder blades, blow on a childs bruised knee, sing a lullaby to a child who cannot sleep , seemingly "stupid" things

# Thread: Ice pack behind the neck
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-pack-behind-the-neck.19962/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Billybats — 2017-08-05T22:32:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-pack-behind-the-neck.19962/post-221004>

Hi all,

This may a realllllly dumb question. Every time I get migraines I put an ice pack behind my head ( neck and shoulder area) which I did today for a slight headache before turning into a migraine. When I do this I fall asleep with it on for over an hour. Questions: is this ok to do (meaning sleeping with the ice and can this be an effective way of CT. If it is should this lead me to tolerate let say bath dunks, etc..

Thank you

### Bonnie — 2017-08-06T01:16:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-pack-behind-the-neck.19962/post-221012>

> [Billybats said:](/goto/post?id=221004)
>
> Hi all,
>
> This may a realllllly dumb question. Every time I get migraines I put an ice pack behind my head ( neck and shoulder area) which I did today for a slight headache before turning into a migraine. When I do this I fall asleep with it on for over an hour. Questions: is this ok to do (meaning sleeping with the ice and can this be an effective way of CT. If it is should this lead me to tolerate let say bath dunks, etc..
>
> Thank you

I fall asleep every night with an ice pack. I have a cooler beside the bed. If I wake up in the night, I have a fresh cold one. It puts me right back to sleep. I've never slept better

### WalterNL — 2017-08-06T10:28:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-pack-behind-the-neck.19962/post-221027>

> [Billybats said:](/goto/post?id=221004)
>
> Hi all,
>
> This may a realllllly dumb question. Every time I get migraines I put an ice pack behind my head ( neck and shoulder area) which I did today for a slight headache before turning into a migraine. When I do this I fall asleep with it on for over an hour. Questions: is this ok to do (meaning sleeping with the ice and can this be an effective way of CT. If it is should this lead me to tolerate let say bath dunks, etc..
>
> Thank you

If you get no adverse effects it's fine. The body is supposed to cool down to sleep. I sleep much better if I do a cold tub near bedtime.

Regarding migraines; I had a few of them each year before implementing the protocols, usually an aura with headache after. The last one I got was more than 1.5 year ago so it's possible to get rid of them. I still live around more nnEMF than I'd like, but I do always follow natural circadian rhythm, block/eliminate artificial blue light and drink spring water. Other hacks that seem to work regardless of what else I do are nicotine, aspirine, MB, kefir and curcumin/ginger. Other foods/supplements have not made much of a difference for me. Migraines usually started while I was doing computer work, and coffee seemed to be a trigger in some cases. So my point is keep at it and hopefully you'll find what works for you.

### Billybats — 2017-08-06T15:31:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-pack-behind-the-neck.19962/post-221035>

Thank guys and gals.

Thank God I didn't have a migraine yet ( they are getting better). So putting ice like I did this would be a CT protocol? This was my main concern. If it is I can do it every night with complete comfort. Also doing it this way would help me adjust to go further to tolerate full body immersion?

# Thread: Ocean swimming
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ocean-swimming.19964/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hello — 2017-08-06T06:52:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ocean-swimming.19964/post-221020>

Hi all,

I wondered whether anyone had thoughts on the 'feel good' effect of cold thermogenesis per se versus cold thermogenesis in the ocean in particular?

I'm doing the protocol in the ocean, in Sydney, where the water is not very cold, even though it is winter: 16 to 20°C (61 to 68°F). Wearing a thin wetsuit, swimming a few kilometres.

When I come out of the water I always feel fantastic: mood amazing, brain awake, very calm (the opposite of my resting state). But I don't know whether to put this down to the cold effect or the magnesium and grounding effect from just being in the ocean itself. Anyone have any thoughts or experiences to share on this?

I'm always extremely cold - even in 20 degree C / 61 degree F I need a jumper and a puffer jacket. So I wonder if it is possible I'm getting a good CT effect simply because my tolerance for cold is so low (often I shiver once out of the water).

Thanks guys.

### caroline — 2017-08-06T07:37:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ocean-swimming.19964/post-221021>

Being in the ocean ...with the sun shining down on us is fantastic! the fournier affect.

enjoy!

# Thread: Egg Tortilla
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-tortilla.19965/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2017-08-06T12:40:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-tortilla.19965/post-221029>

This may be my new favourite thing....

melt 1 tbsp of butter in 10inch frypan - low to medium heat
Beat two eggs with a dash of cream
add to pan and spread out
sprinkle with salt and pepper and what ever spice you like
cover and cook for 4 minutes - Don't touch!

slip onto plate and add your favourite fillings and roll - like a burrito.

I topped it with shrimp and salmon and rocket and avocado and of course, home made herb mayo!

### NDC74 — 2019-03-07T08:21:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-tortilla.19965/post-264672>

> add to pan and spread out

> cover and cook for 4 minutes - Don't touch!
>
> slip onto plate and add your favourite fillings and roll - like a burrito.
>
> I topped it with shrimp and salmon and rocket and avocado and of course, home made herb mayo!

Thanks, Caroline for this recipe. What is the egg specifications in an epi-Paleo diet? Must it be pasture raised, free range or any just any egg?

### caroline — 2019-03-07T20:39:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-tortilla.19965/post-264722>

# Thread: Any success stories educating legislators or educators?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-success-stories-educating-legislators-or-educators.19968/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ValerieBee — 2017-08-06T18:05:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-success-stories-educating-legislators-or-educators.19968/post-221042>

I'm gearing up to change the law in my state. Pennsylvania does not allow citizens to opt out of smart meters. It is in the statute, not in the code (gee thanks corrupt politicians). In my research so far I'm not coming across any PA citizens organized well enough to fight this, so it looks like I may be starting from the ground up. I have some experience with legislative health rights activism in this state, so that's a plus. Anyone out there have success with legislators on Smart Meters/EMF concerns? Any members from Berkeley CA or other jurisdictions that have gotten some politicians to acknowledge EMF dangers? My thoughts on approach at this point are top down--legislators then working on getting this stuff out of schools as much as possible (though since I'm enrolling my kiddo in school this year I'll be working in my home district pretty hard). And yes, I do know this is a David and Goliath kind of effort.

Anyone else in PA, USA want to join me?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-determine-maximum-effective-dose-of-nicotine.19971/post-221061>

I smoked some tobacco as part of a native tobacco ceremony while in Ecuador (We went around the circle and the person who held the pipe spoke to given topics) .

**I really enjoyed the "buzz" that I felt.**

Given all of this talk about the benefits of nicotine, and people who smoke cigarettes in the sun living very long lives, I'm wondering: **does this buzz actually have a benefit?**

****How would one determine the maximum safe amount of nicotine to use _(orally with gum, non-smoking_), before losing the benefit and causing a negative effect?****

Given all the people who smoke it all day long in the sun and live really long and healthily, I am wondering if doing this orally regularly could have a great benefit on health and lifespan.

### karenr — 2017-08-08T02:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laser-vs-ir.19978/post-221142>

What is the difference therapeutically between laser and much less expensive infrared bulbs?

I'm getting treated once a week by a practitioner with an MR4 Activ by Multi Radiance Medical,
Wavelengths 905 nm,875nm,660nm
[http://www.multiradiance.com/products/mr4activcordless](http://www.multiradiance.com/products/mr4activcordless)

Usually several 5 minute programs per session, so 15 min total.

For daily use, I have an Infrared Mini 830 from Redlightman.com, which I use on several problem areas like my spine fracture and belly.

I'm also buying some RubyLux bulbs as per @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) 's recommendation.

I'd like to better understand how these less expensive bulbs compare therapeutically to the professional level laser equipment.. I understand (sort of!) that the laser is superpulsed to prevent accommodation to the wavelengths, but what is the significance of the specific wavenlengths - how do you know which one/s are indicated for specific purposes?

It seems to me that LED lights combined with quantum dots is the near future.
Mostly because they are easily programmable.

There are already TV sets one can buy
QLED (quantum)
OLED (organic)

it is getting wild.

Pretty soon we should have lots of lights available that is programmable.
All one have to worry that the programs would create light that are actually healthy for human consumption.

Next step, I think, would be to think of simulation of franhaufer lines to account for missing light frequencies.

Then polarized or not.

### JanSz — 2017-08-10T16:24:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dutch-test-results-input-needed.19984/post-221355>

@[Jmack](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19672/)
Age: 36 Sex: M Joined JackKruse.com: Jan. 3, 2017
Location: Statesboro, GA, USA
Statesboro latitude ... is 32.3835

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

\==============================================

Ok, you are missing a lot.
First, you are missing on cortisol.
Human can live (for a while) without lots of stuff
but not
without cortisol
body steals everything to just get little cortisol.
Most likely that is why your
DHEAs
pregnenolone
and
progesterone are low

To say it as we say on this @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) board
Your CIRCADIAN RHYTHM stinks.
You must be missing a lots on sunlight even when you have rather plenty of it just outside.

When you look at the top chart (on cortisol) from your report,
think of the area under your actual cortisol levels,
and
the area you should have if your cortisol would follow the top line
you would see that you are living on less than 1/3 of you should have for a good living.
That is why your report says that you told them
that you are fatigued afternoon and evening (but not morning)
your cortisol stores are poor.
Your sleeping habits likely need improvement, look at your low melatonin.

The best way to deal with this problem is to follow (sun=day) (no sun=night) cycle.
You are living on decent latitude, have plenty of natural light available, use it.
Avoid light powered by electricity.

I am not sure if it is a time to think of supplementation.
But what comes to mind is
some small amounts of DHEA pills
and
pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology.

DHEA dose would have to be modified so you would not create excessive E1, E2 or E3
they are at relatively god levels now.

Pregnenolone dose would have to be modified so you would
raise your low progesterone and improve cortisol
depending on your response either progesterone or cortisol will limit your long term pregnenolone dose.
(Your low progesterone is likely the reason that you are complaining of extrogen excess)
Actually your current estrogens are reasonable but progesterone (and cortisol) are low.

At this time your Vit D levels are unknown.
If you would follow and use your available sunlight
you may gauge your compliance by not only following your resultant cortisol levels but also vit D levels.

vit D level goal is:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)

\------------
Your TotalTestosterone is low
your 5a-DHT is high
5aR is working overtime relatively to 5bR
you are reporting
significant symptoms of androgen deficiency
that is not specific
With low testosterone I would expect you to have problems building muscles or exercising
with high 5a-DHT I would expect you to have reasonable sex capabilities.
Which is it?

It would be a good idea to figure out why your testosterone is low.
Testosterone in men is made mostly in testicles.
For testicles to work:
they have to be in good shape
and
need LH hormone to tell them to produce testosterone
and
need FSH hormone to tell them to produce sperm
Lack of ability to make testosterone is called primary reason
Lack LH or FSH is called secondary.
Lack of LH and or FSH may indicate pituitary problems.
Pituitary problems may be due to adenomas (to figure this out pituitary MRI is used)
but more commonly problems are due to pituitary damage (hit to the head, playing football, etc)

Other that changing your life in major way so you would follow
Circadian Rhythm
as nature intended
you may want to do some additional testing.
I do mine once or twice a year as shown on this thread:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717)
and
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-5#post-210075](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-5#post-210075)
note that I am highly fond of this line

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

It guides me on my eating habits and if I want to eat seafood.

also this may be used as (far from ideal) crutch
**Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.**
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)

### Jmack — 2017-08-10T20:04:46+0300

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) Thank you so much for your input. I'm new to all this I just knew I didn't feel right the past few months. My sleep is horrible for the past 8 months I have been living in town (although extremely small). The street lights are horrendous outside of house. I have to sleep with a mask on and also have to wear tru dark glasses during the dark. I workout outside nearly every morning to try and get am sunlight. All this will change in 5-6 months as we are building a house on 100 acre farm and are living in a between house for now. I work in an office nearly all day under horrible lights, I'm trying to figure out how best to hack this currently. You are correct my sex capabilities are 100% it's my exercise and muscle building where I'm really struggling. I developed slight gynecomastia about 3 years ago with a lump in my right breast. My sperm was checked about 3 years ago when my wife was trying to get pregnant and my sperm count was above average in all analysis. I've had some trauma to my testicles when I was younger from sports, martial arts, etc... I've had a few possible concussions but never been checked out. I thank you for your time again!

### drezy — 2017-08-10T20:47:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dutch-test-results-input-needed.19984/post-221372>

My wife had sleep issues for as long as I've known her. It's hard to bring your best self to family interactions when you don't sleep. Worse yet during weekdays often you get home in the evening and are bringing a MORE tired version to the domestic scene. About 5 weeks into light hygiene and cold tubbing it my wife hit a tipping point where the number of bad sleep nights per week became less than good sleep nights until now once in maybe 2.5 weeks she'll have a bad night. Now she usually can trace those nights back to an environmental change or bad decision she made that day.

It sounds like you've got a grasp of some the factors. Did the horrible sleep start around the time of your move?

Since your home is partially finished you may want to find a good nnemf engineer.

Other things I wish I'd done from the beginning in my house:
Ethernet cables instead of wifi
Metal roof
Electrical shut off switch in my bedroom
outlets NOT at each side of the head of the bed
A little sun room for my cold tub (which combined with good light fixed my wife's decades long sleeping disorder)

@[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) 's post here is instructive.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/scompys-journal.16641/page-43#post-209263](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/scompys-journal.16641/page-43#post-209263)

related link
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/seasonal-decline-in-health.8582/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/seasonal-decline-in-health.8582/)

BTW 100 Acre property Nice!

### drezy — 2017-08-11T18:36:09+0300

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=221447)
>
> Any idea for that kind of light:
> I am thinking of low brightness old fashion incandescent light bulbs.
> They could be put on for a 5-10 minutes as the very first light after end of sleeping.

My disclaimer upfront: After absorbing more and more of the science Jack points out, I've become really humble out all this. I think each time you attempt to emulate what otherwise could be true coupling in a system you inevitably will create a different uncoupling.

All that said, I'll imagine some prisoner locked in a room against his will. Few meet that qualification. If I were that person I'd request an incandescent light or 3 and a [rheostat (aka no PWM) dimmer](http://www.ebay.com/bhp/rheostat-control). What uncoupling would I still be hammering myself with? Flicker from the AC line....

Doc Wunsch mentions low power DC lamps. If they had rheostat (resistive/passive) dimmers that would be better. Nobody wants to use rheostats/resistive dimmers because they literally "waste" energy to dim your light and we're all eco-warriors now. Bad news, this system if plugged in and running on an AC to DC transformer would still have something called ripple voltage. Better than flicker but still like baby flicker.

### NeilBB — 2017-08-12T18:25:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dutch-test-results-input-needed.19984/post-221526>

The incandescent "flicker" is more analog than digital. More a fluctuation in intensity of the light, not a true on/off fast cold type flicker. The filament does not have time to get cold (off) because it is relatively inefficient and slow to respond. The LEDs are fast switching and a totally different story, a true on/off strobe effect. And they design the LED's like that intentionally to reduce power consumption. I would think the difference would be biologically significant and that the incandescent flicker seems less harmful than a true strobe, intuitively at least. A natural fire has a degree of what you might perceive as analog "flicker" as well, due to airflow variation, etc...

### NeilBB — 2017-08-12T18:45:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dutch-test-results-input-needed.19984/post-221531>

But of course the "flicker" from a fire's variation in oxygen consumption would be more variable and natural than a fixed harmonic of a 60 Hz duty cycle so I don't mean to imply that they would be equivalent. Also, the optical flicker of a fire could theoretically transmit useful interpretable bits of information on environmentally significant factors such as wind intensity and pO2 of the surrounding air, absent in an incandescent bulb. But I think it's a lesser evil.

### Jmack — 2017-08-12T18:50:49+0300

goto 1:00:00 in and note the studies on melatonin/estrogen in lit areas (aka population density).

Great discussion about lighting in general and DC desk lamps around 1:10:00 ish.

It all depends on the job & office. If plugging in a big long ethernet cable and going outside isn't an option is a DC desk lamp as needed an option? Currently I'm able to work barefoot from under a tree so my "flicker" is at leaf frequency. When inside I turn off as many lights as I am able to. If in an office I try to find an unused conference room and work from there with the lights off.

My only message is that every engineered solution will bring with it it's own problems, hopefully smaller ones. It's just best to be aware.

My engineering dork hack? A hybrid of Wunsch's Case grounded [DC transformer](https://www.amazon.com/Meanwell-Enclosed-Approved-Switching-Transformer/dp/B00GPWLVHI/ref=sr_1_3?ie=UTF8&qid=1502553581&sr=8-3&keywords=meanwell+ac+to+dc+transformer) far away (especially not directly under my colon or junk) and a [dc incandescent bulb](https://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb_sb_noss?url=search-alias%3Dtools&field-keywords=dc+incaandescent&rh=n%3A228013%2Ck%3Adc+incaandescent) or two maybe throw in some a 12v [UV led strip](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B06XWCS4KB/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o03_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1). The hack I'd throw in is a nice fat capacitor to further dimish the ripple voltage that ALL AC/DC bridge rectifiers produce. Being outside is way better though.

### caroline — 2017-08-10T04:42:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-listen-to-this-interview.19986/post-221302>

[http://thewellnesscouch.com/tps/tps-215-the-paleo-lifestyle-for-mental-health](http://thewellnesscouch.com/tps/tps-215-the-paleo-lifestyle-for-mental-health)

Michelle has been a member of this forum for a long time.....

Please have a listen and hear what she is up to.

This is so inspirational ....she is making such a difference

Any thoughts on what she is doing? I suspect she needs all the support she can get.....

### drezy — 2017-08-10T05:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-listen-to-this-interview.19986/post-221305>

I admire her and have became an instant fan.

### caroline — 2017-08-10T05:45:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-listen-to-this-interview.19986/post-221306>

I don't know how Michelle is bankrolling all this - But Tony and I decided to help a little.

Tony actually sat and listened to her whole interview and found it very inspirational. We can't wait to pop over on our travels!

### Da-mo — 2017-08-10T08:36:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-listen-to-this-interview.19986/post-221313>

Michelle mentions people who struggle with anxiety but dont have addiction issues - that is my 23 year old daughter.

I know what she needs but as with other things Dad knows nothing until she crashes and burns and then she might listen to me.

The system treats her with drugs and counselling when what she really needs is to control her light and nnEMF environment. She has a distance relationship with a lovely young man in Tulsa which unfortunately means skyping and video comms late at night for the both of them.

My next move here is to install a wired LAN and ditch the WLAN altogether. Next move is to send some Uvex blue blockers to her significant other and give her a pair for them both to wear when vid-calling. I will ask him to wear them for her sake - I'm sure he will be on board.

I just dropped her at the airport today to go see him - unfortunately for her that means 3 months (VISA limits) living in the city of Tulsa in an apartment and all the nnEMF that goes with it.

Michelle makes a good point about the socioeconomic system - if you are at the bottom and are an addict there is money to throw at you. If you are at the top you can afford the expertise (assuming you know how to find the right experts) but if you are somewhere in the middle you are completely on your own. Thank goodness there are the likes of JK to help us but unfortunately not everyone will find him and, of those, not everyone will have the dopamine to stick with it.

More about the socioeconomic \_ it is expensive to eat right. I know people at the bottom and their shopping list will be made up of the cheapest foods like bread, pasta and beans etc. They will more often be overweight and will tend to buy low fat food thinking that is the right thing for them. FFS soft drinks are cheaper than good water or milk these days.

So long as telecomms has their wicked way the best investments will be in health providers and big pharma.

\[/steps off soap box\]

Hats off to Michelle for trying to make a difference

### caroline — 2017-08-10T09:16:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-listen-to-this-interview.19986/post-221319>

Da-mo ...... so well said - thanks.

Maybe your daughter could meet up with Michelle one day - and lend her a hand?

This would be an awesome adventure for Matty some day......

Oz loves backpackers!

### Da-mo — 2017-08-10T09:23:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-listen-to-this-interview.19986/post-221320>

It is possible Caroline - she is quite the intrepid traveler. She is a horse dentist so can basically support herself wherever there are horses with owners that can pay her.

Great with animals - its the people that get her worried.

Hippotherapy might be a way she could help Michelle. It has helped many others.

### drezy — 2017-08-10T15:44:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-listen-to-this-interview.19986/post-221341>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=221320)
>
> Great with animals - its the people that get her worried.

Lol! Animals are way more honest.

### drezy — 2017-08-10T17:07:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-listen-to-this-interview.19986/post-221360>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=221313)
>
> I know what she needs but as with other things Dad knows nothing until she crashes and burns and then she might listen to me.

That's the way of the world. Give it a decade.

> She has a distance relationship with a lovely young man in Tulsa which unfortunately means skyping and video comms late at night for the both of them.

Pet theory: when someone speaks a different sounding version of English you're forced to become a better listener.

> My next move here is to install a wired LAN and ditch the WLAN altogether. Next move is to send some Uvex blue blockers to her significant other and give her a pair for them both to wear when vid-calling. I will ask him to wear them for her sake - I'm sure he will be on board.

Smart move. Also good indirect way to influence her and bond with someone who may be in your life for the long haul someday. Two conversations I had yesterday within the same hour with two different people both became a discussion about their pregnant daughters and some pretty scary complications that are prompting early C-sections. Young women with their thin myelin really need to know what Doc is laying out as well as the warning that it's going to get worse.
