> Do you use it for CT?

Hi Brent!

I do use it for CT, but only during the late fall and winter months and I only use it indoors because I never cover my head when I am outdoors, as I like to absorb as much uv and IR light as I possibly can and I do CT in lake Michigan and in my CT tub, in the sun. But I love the cold during the winter months and that includes when I am indoors as well, throughout the course of the day. I have to refreeze things. The helmet always melts within and hour on me. I use the cryohelmet in combination with a nice, lightweight cooling vest. It's a great hack for myself and my husband.

### Brent Patrick — 2017-11-02T07:44:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anybody-tried-forehead-grounding.20370/post-227162>

> [Mystic Rose60 said:](/goto/post?id=227115)
>
> Hi Brent!
>
> I do use it for CT, but only during the late fall and winter months and I only use it indoors because I never cover my head when I am outdoors, as I like to absorb as much uv and IR light as I possibly can and I do CT in lake Michigan and in my CT tub, in the sun. But I love the cold during the winter months and that includes when I am indoors as well, throughout the course of the day. I have to refreeze things. The helmet always melts within and hour on me. I use the cryohelmet in combination with a nice, lightweight cooling vest. It's a great hack for myself and my husband.

Thank you Mystic for the valuable information I am going to order so I can use in replace of Face Dunking as experiment and for a hack for sleeping deeper at night ,which I will upload when I am under way

### Tanya — 2018-05-01T19:45:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-phone-model-do-you-use.20386/post-240294>

I stopped upgrading my iPhone at 4s, which is not a 4G phone. This is what is being said on Lifewire about it: "

The iPhone 4S can download data faster—up 14.4 Mbps—than the iPhone 4, which maxes out at 7.2 Mbps. This isn't 4G speed, but some cell phone companies may promote the iPhone 4S as being a 4G phone or close to a 4G phone. Technically, this isn't true. As mentioned above, being 4G requires compatibility with a specific type of cell phone network and specific chips in the phone. The iPhone 4S doesn't have these chips. The phone companies that sell the iPhone in the U.S. have extensive 4G networks, but this iPhone model doesn't take advantage of them."

### Inger — 2018-05-01T21:18:57+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-natural-workout.20402/post-227404>

Hello guys, I just have an issue and I need your help. Somehow when I used to workout before I start the LR protocol, I realised that my left chest size is bigger than left. Now am not leptin resistant and I don't think I ever been. I started doing some physical activity because of my job as pool attendant, like moving a big trolley full of towels or other heavy stuff like tables, chairs and of course always active and walking. What would you recommend me do to to dix this issue? Am gonna start adding some sprints soon. Anyway, after you have awnsered me, you can also tell me if the physical activity I do now is enough so I don't need to go to the gym.

### SlamSlask — 2017-11-26T20:30:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-natural-workout.20402/post-228903>

Can not give you a direct answer but I have asked quiet a few athletes and bodybuilders this precise question:
All of them told me it's completely normal and that it had to do with them using their "main hand" (not sure what the term are in english)

Ones I saw that my bodybuilding friend had the same problem with the right part of his torso same goes for me funny thing is I am right handed and he's left-handed
My research ended with looking into professional boxers (easy to figure out which is their main hand etc.)
Here I did not find a correlation either - Maybe try and start there if your real curious

# Thread: Circadian Rhythm, Spanish Countries, and Degenerative Disease
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-rhythm-spanish-countries-and-degenerative-disease.20412/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### matthewsb77 — 2017-11-07T17:47:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-rhythm-spanish-countries-and-degenerative-disease.20412/post-227534>

I was pondering something I thought you guys might have some thoughts on.

I have a new friend from Spain, who reminded me that a lot of people in Spanish-speaking countries eat dinner very late. (I lived briefly in Chile, so I know it from first-hand experience.) She's typically been eating between 9pm and midnight. Spain as a whole is a bit reversed in this way, overall: that is, their day is shifted more toward afternoon and evening than other European countries.

It made me wonder, **does anyone have any data on whether there are higher rates of degenerative and chronic illnesses -- especially those related to circadian mismatches -- in Spain versus other European countries? I wondered if the data are showing the results of this mismatched lifestyle, or if some other factors I'm not considering are balancing it out (some dietary element, stronger family ties, etc.).**

Once I get stronger, I'd really like to go stay there for 6 months, but the one thing that might stop me is the fact that I've become firmly yoked to the light cycles . . . and Spain is not much for following light cycles .

### drezy — 2017-11-07T17:56:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-rhythm-spanish-countries-and-degenerative-disease.20412/post-227535>

FWIW : It's their Circadian 8PM - 11PM
Blame the Nazis. Yeah really!:

> In 1940, General Francisco Franco changed Spain’s time zone, moving the clocks one hour forward in solidarity with Nazi Germany......After World War II ended, the clocks were never changed back.

from [http://www.bbc.com/travel/story/20170504-the-strange-reason-spaniards-eat-late](http://www.bbc.com/travel/story/20170504-the-strange-reason-spaniards-eat-late)

Like you, I'm also curious if the cultural difference makes any diseases stand out especially if transplanted Spaniards stick to eating at that time in a "truer" time zone too.

### Billybats — 2017-11-07T20:49:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-rhythm-spanish-countries-and-degenerative-disease.20412/post-227537>

Does it really matter what the time is as long as they follow the sunrise and sunsetting? Its hard to do now here at this time of year.

### matthewsb77 — 2017-11-07T22:57:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-rhythm-spanish-countries-and-degenerative-disease.20412/post-227542>

> [Billybats said:](/goto/post?id=227537)
>
> Does it really matter what the time is as long as they follow the sunrise and sunsetting? Its hard to do now here at this time of year.

Point is, they DON'T, and it's more widely practiced, it seems to me, than in some countries -- thus my OP.

### Bettan — 2017-11-08T01:38:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-rhythm-spanish-countries-and-degenerative-disease.20412/post-227547>

I am from Basque Country. North of Spain. We have big issues with time and moving the clocks. The weekend we do it everyone is kind of tired and stressed. You can feel it. During winter specialy in north it's dark at 5pm but nost have dinner at 9p.m., way too late. It gets much better during the summer since sun sets around 22h. The only good thing is that there are so many sunny days, specialy in mediterranean area, in the south.
Answering your question about if there are more chronic illneses than other countries in Europe is hard but we have a lot of protective good things like Mediterranean diet, lot's of seafood, oceans all around, lot's of sunlight, streetlife, siesta and fiesta with low stress levels... specialy outside big cities.

### drezy — 2017-11-09T19:32:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measuring-uv-from-a-black-light.20420/post-227671>

FWIW I put [this one](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B002P4TYVK/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s01?ie=UTF8&psc=1) in my mom's IR sauna.

When measured 3" away with [this meter](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01GT4EF24/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1) it reads between 4.2ish mw/cm2.

Where as [these](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B06XWCS4KB/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1) that I used on the frame of monitors only measure 0.3mw/cm2 3" away from each LED.

### kris90 — 2017-11-15T15:20:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measuring-uv-from-a-black-light.20420/post-228044>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=227671)
>
> FWIW I put [this one](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B002P4TYVK/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s01?ie=UTF8&psc=1) in my mom's IR sauna.
>
> When measured 3" away with [this meter](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01GT4EF24/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1) it reads between 4.2ish mw/cm2.
>
> Where as [these](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B06XWCS4KB/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1) that I used on the frame of monitors only measure 0.3mw/cm2 3" away from each LED.

I've got a bunch of the Blacklight Blue Sunlite bulbs, and I swear by them. It is our source of lighting in the evening as well as red incandescent, and they are super relaxing.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-11-15T16:10:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measuring-uv-from-a-black-light.20420/post-228047>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=227671)
>
> FWIW I put [this one](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B002P4TYVK/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s01?ie=UTF8&psc=1) in my mom's IR sauna.
>
> When measured 3" away with [this meter](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01GT4EF24/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1) it reads between 4.2ish mw/cm2.
>
> Where as [these](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B06XWCS4KB/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1) that I used on the frame of monitors only measure 0.3mw/cm2 3" away from each LED.

Excellent! I have been using some 14 watt spiral blacklights found in canada which I assumed were equivalent to the batteries and bulbs brand which does not ship here. It looks like your brand ships from amazon so I will give it a try. have you tested them for blue light?

### JCDC — 2017-11-15T16:40:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measuring-uv-from-a-black-light.20420/post-228050>

That iPhone one looks like it uses a headphone jack that I no longer have. I wonder if it will work with the lightning to 3.5mm dongle converter...

Wouldn't it be more important to be getting a UV reading at the distance away from the light your skin and eyes are? Hence if I have to be within 3" to get a reading than it is useless unless I personally am 3" from the bulb whenever using it...

### drezy — 2017-11-15T18:06:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measuring-uv-from-a-black-light.20420/post-228065>

> [Sun Disciple said:](/goto/post?id=228047)
>
> have you tested them for blue light?

Great question. I don't have my blue light measurement equipment up yet.\*

> [JCDC said:](/goto/post?id=228050)
>
> Wouldn't it be more important to be getting a UV reading at the distance away from the light your skin and eyes are? Hence if I have to be within 3" to get a reading than it is useless unless I personally am 3" from the bulb whenever using it...

It's all context. I've learned for Doc that UV is very non-linear (aka a little goes a long way). I also have the behavioral template that he uses when stuck indoors on video podcasts. I think on more that one occasion he showed that he had a UV lightweight behind his laptop screen. My mom's sauna is small so she won't be that far away from the light.

"If it's good enough for doc..." maybe isn't scientific but it is a good starting point for me

\* - There are a affordable sensor elements out there (effectively reverse LEDs) that I've been experimenting with. I'm actually having to train myself some new skills to pull this off and I shot my mouth off too early on this but I want a UV and R G B sensor. So, I will make one.

CT seems to make my ideation go off the charts and I've hung this Jack quote as a gentle reminder to keep my ass in gear when I have spare time:

> Ideation, without execution only leads to deletion. Do, act, change, learn, become something new, maybe even find your inner masterpiece.

### drezy — 2017-11-15T23:39:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measuring-uv-from-a-black-light.20420/post-228112>

It seems unnatural.

A child is "supposed" to outlive a parent.
A wife is "supposed" to outlive a husband.

It's mentally and spiritually crushing when this doesn't happen in "proper" order. Few if anyone here can relate at all with how crushed your heart and soul is. My own wife is older but some of my people are short lived. We joke that it's a race with the devil between us, but that only covers up a painful and inconvenient truth.

I just want to let you know that I appreciate your presence here and hope that I am as mentally sharp as you are when I reach your age.

# Thread: Magnet therapy?

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JKLearner123 — 2017-11-12T12:41:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy.20432/post-227833>

I understand from a 2014 JK video on youtube that he uses and recommends a magnetico sleep pad. Is this still the case I wonder? I currently tape a couple of coin sized magnets to my wrists and also feet at night. I understand from a comment made by JK on FB that when using magnets, it's best to use them at night only, and so that is what I do.

Any thoughts on using magnets please?

Also, do I need to observe which way around the magnets are facing - that is, which side they are taped to my skin, and also which "direction" they are pointing? I also use a grounding bed sheet. Thanks a lot for any thoughts/comments.

### Lahelada — 2017-11-12T15:30:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy.20432/post-227849>

Really really careful with that . Yes it depends on the direction the magnets are facing, some even come wrongly labelled. I would not use magnets without extensive study. The magnetico is different as all of your body is within the field , in other words the return field of the magnets does not affect you.

# Thread: Blue Blockers (also, contacts?)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-also-contacts.20445/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kayja — 2017-11-15T20:00:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-also-contacts.20445/post-228082>

I'm having some trouble deciding on blue blockers for both daytime and nighttime and had some questions. I am a comedian so I do care about how I look (I have a pair of the red UVEX but can't wear them out) and have a prescription of -2 diopters in both eyes (well, they're off by .25 but I thought by using the same prescription I might help them balance rather than reinforcing the difference).

I contacted Swannies and they said they block 99% of blue light up to 450nm and 97% up to 500nm. Since it's not 100%, does that make them no bueno for nighttime? How about for daytime, is that too much? Is there a role for my Swannies at any time of day?

I went to an Irlen syndrome diagnostician (now that I get more sunlight I don't notice much difference from my filters) and they said they can make contacts that cover only the pupil and that that would be sufficient to block the light without changing my eye color. Would this work?

I'd be willing to take a hit in the oxygen flow to not look like a Mitch Hedberg ripoff for my short time onstage (I would put them in/take them off ASAP).

Other than making my own blue blockers, what other options are out there for prescription (making my own sounds intimidating but I'm not opposed to it).

Is there a simple guide to blue blockers? I've heard Dr. Kruse say he has 3 pairs (50%, 70% and 100% I believe???). Do I need several pairs and how would I both acquire and use each pair?

Thank you so much for your help
Jamie

### Sun Disciple — 2017-11-15T20:24:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-also-contacts.20445/post-228086>

1\. If you want to have complete control over style of the frame I would reffer you to matt maruca @ RA optics [https://raoptics.io/ He is a member here on the forum as well. He offers a servcice where you would purchase your own frames and lenses and you send them off to be tinted by him. He also offers a variety of styles in house.
](https://raoptics.io/)
2\. _own a pair of swannies but i would not recomend them if you are going for full coverage I wear them during the day some times under junk light but They still alow some of the blue hazard through.

3\. "now that I get more sunlight I don't notice much difference from my filters" I think this tells you alot. listen to the doctor in your head my friend.

4\. see number one

5\. You want different coverage for different situations. A three pair system I think works pretty good. A pair which blocks atleast 435 to 465nm for daytime use in stores and driving . a second between 500-550 for early evening indoorrs and driving and a bedtime pair at 570-580 to stimulate melatonin release and knock you out.
If I had to give you a recommendation I would contact matt and he sells 2 tints one at 500nm and one at 550nm. And for sleep I strongly recomend truedark glasses they seem to be the most effective ones I have N=1._

### shiran — 2017-11-15T20:51:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-also-contacts.20445/post-228089>

[https://medium.com/@jasonlauritzen/...ad60?source=linkShare-347de1621839-1510771838](https://medium.com/@jasonlauritzen/blue-light-and-health-plus-how-to-make-your-own-blue-blocking-glasses-1a3d2694ad60?source=linkShare-347de1621839-1510771838)

[https://spectra479.com/pages/test-strip](https://spectra479.com/pages/test-strip)

# Thread: Have you ever seen an murmuration
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-seen-an-murmuration.20446/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Billybats — 2017-11-15T20:06:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-seen-an-murmuration.20446/post-228084>

Hey,

I felt like I was in the movie " The Birds". My coworker and I were outside chatting and saw all these birds swarming together going back and forth from one section of trees to another.. back and forth.. It was really cool. The were loud too.. We were like what the heck. So as I am I want to know why and looked it up. They say it is a rare thing, cool to be part of rare things in life like that. Just like they say rainbows are rare which I have seen at least five this year and on time it was a double rainbow.

# Thread: Quantum son
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-son.20451/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2017-11-16T03:49:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-son.20451/post-228140>

The following is the testimony of my own son(13 yo):

Salutations everyone! I feel like Doc Kruse and his findings have really provided me insight towards an optimal life. At the beginning of last summer I was 5ft 7in, and weighed about 172 lbs but now after finding out about CT, sun exposure, and a whole lot more I weigh 148 lbs without exercising a day! (excluding gym class) In fact, I was able to eat a pizza and still lose 2lbs the next day.
I am very gratis that Doc Kruse shared his findings with us, and I think that it helped a lot of people.

At the beginning of last summer I was 5ft 7in, and weighed about 172 lbs but now after finding out about CT, sun exposure, and a whole lot more I weigh 148 lbs without exercising a day! (excluding gym class) In fact, I was able to eat a pizza and still lose 2lbs the next day.

### Inger — 2017-11-16T13:16:49+0200

awesome

when I see kids get this, my heart just melts

kids observe, and they are such great learners

### drezy — 2017-11-16T16:06:31+0200

That's the scary part, Inger.

I'm so incredibly lucky to have this guy as my son.

Ladies are so much more connected to the whole child process. As a guy I'm 13 years into all this and honestly still stuck thinking:

Ok I get that there was a nice candlelight dinner.
I remember there was some fun after that.
Wait, there's a whole new person in the universe now? And he came out of where?!?

### kris90 — 2017-11-16T17:02:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-son.20451/post-228193>

That's awesome! I'm trying to get my 3 year old daughter to love CT. I splash cold water on her at the end of her baths sometimes, and its hit or miss. Sometimes she loves it and we both laugh, but other times not so much.

Her diet has been so solid lately. She loves smoked salmon and herring, and eats them daily. It feels awesome to be able to guide our children toward optimal living. Thank you very much @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) !

### drezy — 2017-11-16T17:29:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-son.20451/post-228204>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=228193)
>
> That's awesome! I'm trying to get my 3 year old daughter to love CT. I splash cold water on her at the end of her baths sometimes, and its hit or miss. Sometimes she loves it and we both laugh, but other times not so much.
>
> Her diet has been so solid lately. She loves smoked salmon and herring, and eats them daily. It feels awesome to be able to guide our children toward optimal living. Thank you very much @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) !

I'm pretty sure my son was inadvertently helped along by relatives that gasped at the idea of him getting into the cold tub. The second that he saw it was considered subversive to someone he was in.

There was an old psychology study I wish I could dig up. The scenario was a child is brought into a room filled with all the latest toys. A researcher interviews the kid briefly and then says "I'm going to leave the room for 5 minutes. You can play with any toys you want. The only thing I'll ask is that you just don't press that button over there." 100% of the kids hit it. It was hilarious.

### kris90 — 2017-11-16T17:55:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-son.20451/post-228214>

A researcher interviews the kid briefly and then says "I'm going to leave the room for 5 minutes. You can play with any toys you want. The only thing I'll ask is that you just don't press that button over there." 100% of the kids hit it. It was hilarious.

Lol so true, reverse psychology is big for children. I gotta start trying that, cuz it actually does work.

As a kid, when I played outside in the cold, I always got hot easily so I would take off my jacket, and parents would get so pissed. "PUT YOUR JACKET BACK ON!" If anything, it just made me want to keep it off. My Dad used to think I was cold blooded. Sorry mom if you see this, throwing you under the bus LOL

### caroline — 2017-11-17T07:27:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-son.20451/post-228238>

You have set such a great example for your son - well done Dan!

### drezy — 2018-01-23T23:08:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-son.20451/post-233277>

My son keeps pulling in the high scores. I asked him if 7th grade is more difficult, as was advertised by most people. He said no. My son explained that the students seem more distracted and less focused this year. IS it a case of plain pubescence or pubescence mangled in with blue light and nnemf? Stay tuned....

Also the school sex ed warnings are being sent out in email form as the school gets ready. I thank all the deer and rabbits (especially the rabbits) that live and mate on my land they've made it as simple as a simple "Yep, humans do that too and that's how you're here." It occurs to me only now that some kids might be raised in places where all visible animals are spayed and neutered and maybe sex ed comes as a big reveal. It's a different kind of separation from nature.

My business partner spent years breeding horses and having his daughters help. He swears that girls that breed horses are wayyyyy more discriminating and take their time choosing mates. Their might be something to that.w

### drezy — 2018-02-06T23:33:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-son.20451/post-234261>

This just in from my kid's teacher:

"I want to make you aware of OUTDOOR EDUCATION as an ELECTIVE for next year!!!

Outdoor Education is an EXCITING hand’s on class for students who want to learn skill-based fishing practices, how to load a backpack for hiking trails, pitch tents for camping in state parks, campfire cooking and outdoor Dutch Oven Cooking, xeriscaping and gardening in Texas. Students will ENGAGE in how to track animals and identify their habitats. Students will identify birds and fish and tie fishing knots. **We WILL be outside every chance we get, whether it be casting lures or playing outdoor games!** If you LOVE the OUTDOORS, this is the class for you!"

Classes last 1.5 hours every other day so that's a nice bunch of time outside and a big deal to a mito-parent.

# Thread: Spiders reset body clocks to avoid 5-hour jet-lag every day
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spiders-reset-body-clocks-to-avoid-5-hour-jet-lag-every-day.20466/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Brent Patrick — 2017-11-18T04:04:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spiders-reset-body-clocks-to-avoid-5-hour-jet-lag-every-day.20466/post-228317>

**"[In humans,](https://www.newscientist.com/article/mg23631473-300-sleepwalking-to-disaster/)chronic mismatches in circadian rhythm have been linked to many conditions, from [cancer](https://www.newscientist.com/article/mg23130932-400-body-clock-gene-may-help-lethal-spread-of-breast-cancer/) to [depression](https://www.newscientist.com/article/dn23530-genes-in-the-brain-keep-bad-time-when-we-are-depressed/). Whether the spiders pay a cost for their dissonant clocks is unknown. To understand them better, the team is now measuring how the activity of genes involved in the circadian clock changes throughout the day"

''The normal selective pressures that maintain 24-hour circadian rhythms in most species have loosened in these spiders, says Moore''**

[https://nexusnewsfeed.com/article/c...ody-clocks-to-avoid-5-hour-jet-lag-every-day/](https://nexusnewsfeed.com/article/consciousness/spiders-reset-body-clocks-to-avoid-5-hour-jet-lag-every-day/)

Reading the article noting that the Spider has made adaptations due to its environment for survival and thinking our own mitochondria's real function is a key environmental sensor. Can the spider be compared to an Individual trying to thrive in a world of nnEMF or has nature made the selection for the spider to prosper , surely evolution doesn't make mistakes.

I would be keen to see the end results of this study.

### mbhemming — 2017-11-18T18:04:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-recommendations-for-emf-protective-clothing.20467/post-228357>

Any recommendations for EMF protective clothing?

### Saichi — 2020-01-29T00:44:07+0200

Hello,
My wife sufers from lipedema. A fat tissue sicknes where some fat cells from lipid tissue can not burn fat and they keep acumulating fore ever. There is an input but the ouput doesnt work at all.
Some severe cases are very impresive, my wife is in a first stage after 2 children.

She does a lot of sports, and keto/epi paleo diet (she is not eating good amounts because she is scared)
Does anyone know about how to improve or reverse this lipid cells to make them burn FAT?

I thought of CT. We have not tryied it. Her body is cold all the time due to lipedema and she refuses it. If someone has any experience or knowledge about it, please help.
Sorry about my english (I am Basque)

# Thread: Fish broth DHA+curcum root
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-broth-dha-curcum-root.20516/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shiran — 2017-11-27T15:44:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-broth-dha-curcum-root.20516/post-228943>

Today I went to the port in the old city of Acre I bought a fresh white grouper and fresh shrimp..
The head and the bones of the fish as well as the shrimp scallop with the head and tails plus :
Carrot
Onion
Garlic a lot
Fresh curcum root
Dried Lemon grass
Fresh Herbs
Ginger
Hop pepper
Selery
Black pepper
Zaafran
Fresh Lemon
Bokchoy

And lots of love that is what you see in the pic
I'll filter everything out and to the broth I'll add coconut cream and fish meat
That's our dinner for today
Bon appetite

Can you see the eye in the pic ?

# Thread: what was something you did that surprised you at how well it improved your sleeP/
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2017-12-03T14:16:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229408>

my sleep is still hit or miss because i might get hot or have insomnia. there are a lot of generally accepted ways to improve sleep. what is one thing you did that really surprised you at how much it improved your sleep? also could you be specific as to how much you did of this and how long it took to see results? please don't mention things like blacking out the bedroom, regular bed times, not eating large meals at night, not having a cell phone in your room, as these are things i already do. i also restrict blue light at night, but it seems not to be enough.
thanks.

### shiran — 2017-12-03T14:48:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229410>

I didn't sleep for 7 years because I kept my son alive... I literally kept him alive at night ( read my journal don't ask me why)
I can tell you one thing for sure, sleep is a huge huge deal.
What I learned this 2017 year thanks to Dr jack Kruse is :
Sleep starts at sunrise.

### taiyang — 2017-12-03T16:24:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229414>

you probably had to fight to stay awake, i have to fight to sleep- different. i know sleep is a big deal and i know melatonin is produced in the eye starting a sunrise. i get 30 min of am sun now. that is not enough. i need to increase the amount or most likely do other things in conjunction with the 30 min. i am trying to cut down on screen time so i can only read things of direct relevance to me and my key sleep issue, which seems to be different from your sleep issue. thanks anyway. ps i hope members are not picking up on the habit of being enigmatic in their replies. if you are going to be so, please don't reply to my questions. my screen time is limited. at the same time, i will try to do my due diligence and read up on my issues as much as possible.

### Lahelada — 2017-12-03T17:50:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229418>

Tai, my time is limited, as I want to sleep too.  How do you know what is relevant ? Anything you know already is up for scrutiny.
You say "no large meals at night already doing that". I will mention "**eat in the red** !" So now you could say I eat my latest meal at ... pm and then this would be a very relevant conversation. Maybe you are living under the misconception that eating 4 hours before bed is enough ? CW says so. It is, if bed time is early enough but not if you go to bed at 1pm .

**Are you grounding in your 30 minutes in the sun ? When do your 30 minutes start? Is Uv already present in your location? Maybe you start too late.** You see the forbidden needs to be mentioned for clarity.

You could look into **strength exercises before bed**. **CT ?** Are you doing it? Please give details in your questions so that we can judge if what you do not want mentioned needs to be mentioned. Better still,start a diary showing what you actually do in detail. For example do you get late **pm sun** ? Do you know why you should get it yet ? **"Heal in the red !"**

Radical suggestion 1 : Do more of what you are already doing .
Radical suggestion 2 : Go away to do it somewhere else exactly the way you do it now. It may tell you whether you are in the right place where you live.

### taiyang — 2017-12-03T20:11:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229430>

if anyone is interested in answering my question, I would like to know what worked for YOU specifically re sleep. if no one is interested, no problem everyone wants to limit screen time.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-12-03T22:49:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229435>

What worked for me was simple. I threw away all of my sunglasses and made it a priority to see every morning sunrise and get at least 30 to 60minutes or more of full body(skin surfaces) nude sun exposure, no excuses.This is how you make the sleep hormone, melatonoin and also, dopamine. I also make a point to watch evening sunsets and make more. I also threw away my melatonin supplement that I was addicted to for many years. Within in matter of several weeks, I became a very solid, sound sleeper and to this day, I go to bed when the sun goes down and I sleep for 7 1/2 to 9 hours, averaging right around 8 though. We also have a kill switch to the electricity in our bedroom and that is very crucial for a quality night's sleep.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-12-03T22:59:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229436>

Also, reading might be helpful . While I was adjusting, I would read my favorite book and one that Jack highly suggests too. It helped to change my dispostion and contributed to a more peaceful night's sleep. Here is the free PDF, but you can also order it off from Amazon either new or used. I have the physical copy. The best to you, taiyang!

[http://marketcurry.yolasite.com/resources/The Monk Who Sold His Ferrari- Robin Sharma.pdf](http://marketcurry.yolasite.com/resources/The%20Monk%20Who%20Sold%20His%20Ferrari-%20Robin%20Sharma.pdf)

### Andrea — 2017-12-04T00:47:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229441>

Sleeping in the nature, at a decent distance from civilization, power lines and cell towers. Worked from night #1, and I sleep like a baby most nights out there.

### taiyang — 2017-12-04T02:07:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229445>

mystic, where do you live? if i were to go to bed when the sun goes down, i wuld be sleeping around 6 pm. thanks for the ideas everyone. i have an idea to sleep in a tent in my backyard at least two days a week when the weather is warmer. i wish there were more uv transparent clothes to buy for warmer weather- not just swim suits.

### Sue-UK — 2017-12-04T11:00:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229460>

I'm in the UK and its dark by 5 p.m., winter low light levels generally, and cold. No computer after sunset. I'm in bed by 8 most nights, (5 nights a week minimum) black out curtains. For the first few nights I lay awake in the dark for what seemed like ages (I don't use a clock - clock watching makes my sleep worse), but for trying to get sleepy and if I woke up in the night, I would just try and keep in a calm, meditative state. Within a few days my brain seemed to get used to the new rhythm, and I fall asleep quite quickly now, and very rarely wake up in the night. With sunset so early, I don't eat after 4 pm, some days its around 2 pm. I'm rural, extremely low meter readings, don't have wifi on etc, no street lights. Grounding is critical for me, so I'm using a grounding yoga mat as a runner on the bottom of the bed, so my feet and calves are on it, going out to a grounding rod outside the bedroom window. Before I got the mat, if I woke in the night and couldn't get back to sleep, I would go outside in the dark and ground for a while and then go back to bed. That helped, so I realised how important grounding is to my n=1. When I go to bed I'm using a migraine cap from the freezer to cool the CSF on my head. I take it off either when it loses its coldness if I'm not asleep, or when I start feeling really drowsy. I'm still using summer bedding, difficult at first when the weather suddenly got colder, but I stuck with it and now it seems normal. I'm out for the sunrise, grounding. For the cold I've got a kaftan made out of 100% cotton cellular cot blankets, which gives me some warmth but the holes let in more light. When I can I do my outside CT to later in the morning when its a bit brighter. The water is very cold, but if there's some good winter sunshine, I do a session floating on the surface, then dunking down, and then dry off nude in the sun. The sun feels hotter because my skin surfaces are so cold, and it feels awesome! When the weather is crap and because of what I'm trying to achieve for my own n=1 I do use therapeutic lights some days, and do some specific hacks, (but as treatments not lifestyle choices), but they are add ons. My best sleep in a low quantum yield is grounded, cold, dark and boring.

### taiyang — 2017-12-04T12:09:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229468>

Thanks, Sue. How could I learn to ground with a yoga mat? are there videos on youtube? i read earlier someone's explanation of how they did something similar, and it did not give me a clear picture. i also go to bed around 8 pm and get up at around 4 am. to deal with all the lights in my house (because no one in the house believes in what i am doing), i have to completely cover myself, including gloves, very wide-brimmed hat, long sleeves, turtle neck, pants, socks, uvex glasses. how do you deal with the time from 5pm to 8pm when it is dark, but you haven't gone to sleep yet? do you have special lighting for that time? if so, could you recommend a brand? when doing grounding in cold weather, is it better to be walking barefoot or just sitting, or is really doesn't matter? we have 24/7 wifi with no one but me willing to change. i think this environment is also affecting my 20 year old daughter, so i need to do things like the yoga mat or a magnetico for her or special lights that would allow her to do homework, but be less harmful than regular lighting, because she would probably accept that if she saw some improvement in her sleep and overall health. she has a bucket handle tear of her meniscus and needs surgery on december 19. she had surgery on the same knee 2 years ago. i am concerned her health problems will just get worse and worse. i need all the hacks i can get for this type of person- i cannot force them, but if i do things that don't require them to change their lifestyle that much, it's a beginning. i hope doing hacks for her will let her see improvements, and then cause her to accept more difficult change, like becoming an early bird instead of a night owl.

### Sue-UK — 2017-12-04T13:36:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229469>

I didn't need to learn to ground with the mat, I just make sure its connected, and get into bed. I wouldn't use it connected to house wiring though, for me it has to go out to the earth. (If I lived in a higher non native emf environment I'd probably go for a magnetico). Grounding outside could be standing, sitting or walking, or stretched out on the grass in good weather (belly grounding is great ....), I just have to do it ....

The biggest thing for me for helping family members "see" what I was trying to achieve was getting a meter. There's some that are better than others for measuring different things, I just have a Cornet 88T, but its one of my better purchases this year. It helps to know what I'm dealing with, and DH can't argue the numbers. My youngest left home in September, but when he was living here I did compromise, but only up to a point. Me and DH pay the bills, so we make the rules when it comes to wifi. When the kids are home the wifi is on if they are indoors, but they have to switch it off at night. Two strikes and they are out - if I find its been left on when I get up at sunrise, they lose a strike. Twice and it goes off when I go to bed, and they know I mean it.  I can do all the hacks I want, but if I'm being zapped by wifi 24/7, and particularly at night, I could be limiting the effectiveness of everything else I do, or need to do a helluva lot more to mitigate it, and I'm not gonna do more to stand still .....If I do more its to move forward ..... Lights after sunset are dim and towards the red, and if the tv is on I'm at least covered up and blue blocking. (An n=1 hack on my own blockers for that). Its not ideal, and I've yet to find an ambient light source that I'm happy with using chronically in the evening (even red light) which is why the cold, dark and boring from 8 p.m. is critical. Just enough light to be able to move around the house without walking into things. I don't do any housework or washing up after sunset, if I can see to clean something properly, its too bright ... It can wait. It may sound selfish but I've concentrated on me, not wasted energy trying to convince my adult children .... As my health and mental functioning improved, they started asking questions about what I'm doing ...

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-12-04T14:00:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229470>

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=229445)
>
> mystic, where do you live? if i were to go to bed when the sun goes down, i wuld be sleeping around 6 pm. thanks for the ideas everyone. i have an idea to sleep in a tent in my backyard at least two days a week when the weather is warmer. i wish there were more uv transparent clothes to buy for warmer weather- not just swim suits.

I'm in Michigan @[taiyang](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20771/) . During the long cold and dark winter months, I am in bed oince it gets dark. Once I awaken though, I will just lay there until my wind up clock says 4 a.m. I use red light, candles and the fireplace in the winter and if I get on my computer, my skin surfaces are all covered and I have red and black light behind me and IRIS software that is set to sleep, always.

Sleeping in a tent is a great idea, but make sure you are not sleeping over any nnemf that may be underground or overhead. Do you have the right meter to test this? Also, make sure you are not exposed to blue light (nnemf). I slept in a tent a lot during my first year and it was great! I cut out the floor of my tent and slept on and in a wool sleeping bag during the spring, summer, fall and winter months, away from my home and in an area of our property that we keep wild and the critters come out at night and like it there too. I already was sleeeping in the cold during the winter for many years, as we always keep our bedroom windows open.

It's important to keep experimenting to see what works for you taiyang, Nature being the most crucial and ultimate healer there is though. If you do the work, Nature will not let you down and I say that with great confidence my dear.

### taiyang — 2017-12-06T12:35:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229623>

is there any consensus here on a good honda civic-quality meter? i'm not looking for mercedes level or a ford-pinto one either.

### Inger — 2017-12-06T13:25:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229627>

Cutting the electric breaker for the bedroom

Eliminating the wifi from the neighbors to a great degree through plastering my walls on the side the router was on with 4 fold alufoil

Always getting the morning light/sun on my eyes/body. I sleep right by the open window no curtains, so I wake up and watch into the sky in the direction where sun rises. Every morning. In winter it is so cozy in a ice cold room under a warm blanket.. to still connect with the sky and sun and trees outside.. In summer I go outside as soon as I wake up. I hate to sleep with closed window where I cant see the sky. I need that connection so badly. It is like coming home.

Eating my latest meal as early as possible, the best sleep I get when eating not after 3 PM, usually I eat my latest meal at 4 PM though but I need to do 3 PM more often!

Not drinking alcohol, especially not at night, also no coffee after 2 PM

Being outside nude and grounded in the sun from sunrise forward as many hours as possible

Cutting breaker early at 7 PM and being in the dark and silence with 1 candle reading a book like Osho or other deep, meditative stuff that makes you feel grounded and peaceful and happy. Doing this religiously a few hours before bed

That was a few things that comes to my mind that all changed my sleep to the better

### taiyang — 2017-12-06T13:47:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229631>

i need to find the breaker for my bedroom- great idea. i'm also interested in the alum foil. how did you get the foil to stick?? is the shiny side facing out or in? any other links where i could get more info on on the alum foil?

### Inger — 2017-12-06T13:58:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229637>

I just used this tape one uses when painting the walls, you know? I just taped a little here and there, it sticks well as it is not heavy. I think I did the shiny side out but I am not sure anymore..lol. In the end I covered the whole wall with wallpaper, a light type of it. I just used wallpaper paste to fix it on the alufoil. So now the wall looks pretty normal  Ok a little weird it looks but I do not care.

But I did feel weird a few days after doing that work. My hands got kinda swollen and I felt a little off. I guess I should have used gloves? I wonder if the aluminium can stick to your skin and travel inside your body? At least if doing it again I would protect me better... I just used bare hands you know.. and it was a lot of work as I did the wall 4 times over!
Do not do this if you are not sure where the radiation comes from tho. That could make it even worse! And test with a meter to check that you are not produing a microvawe oven in the process
I used the wifi signals on my laptop to check how much weaker the signal got and it was a significant decrease. But I have only 2 walls plastered, opposite walls, that is where the radiation comes from. Otherwise here is quite a little nnEMF so I felt safe doing it. I would not create a faraday cage, that is too risky I guess. If it is not 100% right done it might make the nnEMF trapped inside and just make it worse!

### philip malone — 2017-12-06T18:21:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-was-something-you-did-that-surprised-you-at-how-well-it-improved-your-sleep.20538/post-229653>

A lot of good info here - I'll just add that on top of low emf and no blu light in the evening, I found an eye mask to help me a lot. You want absolutely no light when you sleep....

I also have the head of our bed elevated by 6" I found bed risers at Target as well

Pilot light kicking in, reckon I slept from about 8.30 pm to around 5 am. Then drank some water and went back to sleep for a couple more hours (which I wouldn't normally do if I go to bed so early). For all the eye rolling and moaning about sleeping in such a cold room, and sliding down the bed, DH didn't need to get up in the night to pee last night. Interesting .....

# Thread: Stuttering
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuttering.20541/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ozbuck — 2017-12-04T01:02:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuttering.20541/post-229442>

Hey,

I'm wondering if Jack or anyone else has any ideas/experience on what causes stuttering and possible tactics to overcome it.

Obviously there's something going on in the neural networks of the brain so I'm interested in any insights people may have in regards to this. It's interesting to note that 99% of people who chronically stutter don't stutter when they talk to a plant, or a dog, or in a room by themselves. It seems that a perceived stress (social anxiety) interacts with the pre-setup neural network to create the stuttering.

Also, many people who stutter who are in a state of full confidence, love, etc find their stuttering greatly diminishes or, even in some cases, disappears completely when in those states.

Any ideas, thoughts, experiences?

Thanks
Oz

### taiyang — 2017-12-16T21:38:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuttering.20541/post-230438>

my husband has Tourette syndrome with stuttering. with age both his vocal and muscle tics have gone down. he can repeat a word like 4 times. it usually is more pronounced in a slightly stressful situation.

### htw — 2017-12-16T22:26:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuttering.20541/post-230439>

my stuttering was huge but now is little, I'm laughing at it now. In my case SCN (dopamine ale melatonin) was issue. Now I love to talk

### nonchalant — 2017-12-16T23:22:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuttering.20541/post-230440>

Ozbuck, following Jack's protocols does reduce anxiety, whether social or physical.

### taiyang — 2017-12-17T01:34:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuttering.20541/post-230456>

my husband was made fun of at his work. then he asked his doctor for medication to control the tics and was prescribed halidol, which stopped the tics but made him feel like a zombie. he decided it wasn't worth it. he just accepts the way he acts and doesn't care too much what others think. he will try to control himself out in public, but then when we get to the parking lot, he will start making sounds like a horse. he used to have an explosive temper and got in streets fights and was beat up himself. that's improved with age too. i like to say that the more his hair falls out, the better he gets

There seems to be something else at play in regards to stuttering... something in the wiring of the brain or how particular chemicals do or don't interact. Maybe someone has further insight into this

### taiyang — 2017-12-17T01:53:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuttering.20541/post-230460>

i have always thought my husband really macho because he had all these tics and still said what he wanted to say. he takes no crap. he is about 5 foot 6 inches. once he was in a train station talking with his friend in Chinese, their native language. this tall white american dude towers over my husband and rudely says that this is america- speak english. my husband curses at him in his chinglish and said- this is american- i have the freedom of speak! i have too many classic stories like that.

his tourette symptoms really took off at puberty and have subsided with age, so i think in his case high testosterone is involved. he also used to be a chain smoker and big drinker, but now he has replaced that with pokemon hunting, so it's all good.

### htw — 2017-12-17T16:31:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuttering.20541/post-230494>

> [ozbuck said:](/goto/post?id=230457)
>
> Was this before and after directly tied to the use of Jacks protocols?
>
> Also, what made you come to the conclusion that it was a SCN issue?
>
> Thanks

I was stuttering all my life, my blue-microwave environment was issue, when I start to realize how biology works:

the first changes began to appear, and my stuttering began to subside.
Currently, my stuttering and skin problems (acne) always appear when I am not sleeping well or I am over-trained and I can easlly fix it

**do whatever you need and it will be fine, you'll see**

### taiyang — 2017-12-17T16:38:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuttering.20541/post-230496>

that was a beautiful video. in a couple of years when my financial responsibility to my children is over, i would love to have a members event at yellowstone.

# Thread: Work Environment
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/work-environment.20542/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2017-12-04T17:38:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/work-environment.20542/post-229481>

I need to pose an important question for a mitochondriac regarding work environment.

Would a mitochondriac choose a job:

1)
\-Work location is close to home (30 min commute) and free parking
\-Office is located outside of downtown core of major city (lower population density)
\-Office is a 2 story building (working on ground level)
\-Atmosphere is more "proper", the people are less fun to work with (energy vampires) and the work is not very exciting
\-Strict telecommute (work from home) policy (only as a case-by-case need)

2)
\-Work location is farther from home (45 min commute) and parking would be $100/month minimum
\-Office is located in the centre of downtown core (very high population density)
\-Office is in a high-rise building, likely between floors 5 and 16
\-Atmosphere is super chill, the people are a blast to work with (used to work with them, energy boosters) and the projects are super exciting and engaging
\-Flexible telecommute (possibility to work from home 1-2x per week, or as needed)

Salary is the same, but of course 2) requires more $ for gas and parking.

### WalterNL — 2017-12-04T18:09:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/work-environment.20542/post-229482>

Interesting choices. You have to grade all factors as to how bad/good they really are. Maybe both offices are equally bad in terms of nnEMF, and working home cuts down the total time spent commuting for the second job compared to the first. If there is no real benefit to the first office I'd go with the second. And given that the group are energy vampires, I assume their office isn't good at all.

### kris90 — 2017-12-04T18:19:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/work-environment.20542/post-229483>

Thanks Walter, I have a similar analysis: an office job is an office job. Poor lighting, and nnEMF regardless. Working from home is a huge plus since my home environment is literally as optimal as can be, so I think I need that flexibility.

Also, the work I do and the people I work with seems to have a major impact on my mood, energy and wellbeing. Working on boring shit all day with boring people = exhausted by the end of the day. Working on cool shit with hilarious people acting like their true selves = feeling energized by end of day, and have funny stories to share with my wife over some malbec when the kids are in bed. If it means a little more EMF, I think I can biohack that.

### shiran — 2017-12-04T18:24:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/work-environment.20542/post-229484>

Remember dopamine it's a huge issue looking into someone's eyes is so important for wellness.
I trying to look for the ideal work place in nature with nice people... it's not that easy...
I would like to work in a place like the farm Jack is creating...

### kris90 — 2017-12-04T18:29:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/work-environment.20542/post-229485>

Yes I would love to get out of office/desk job and out into nature. I do take a morning and afternoon walk and spend an hour on my lunch outside, so atleast I'm providing myself "recess" like a child gets in school. But until I figure out a new career path, I'll just have to make the best of it.

Working with the right people has made a HUGE difference for me. You are right, the dopamine yield or decline from people is important. I can boost my own dopamine easily, but I think we all need human interaction. I was happy having fun with my old work buds, and unfortunately I got blindsided with the work location and convenience that I threw that away.

I've never felt my dopamine levels soar higher than when I spent a weekend in Vermont with Jack. Holy shit, I felt like I was on a crazy high for 48 hours, and it even continued after I returned home that weekend.

### kris90 — 2017-12-04T18:41:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/work-environment.20542/post-229486>

Another major thought to consider: at my current work location, I'm about 15 min drive from a beach so I can soak up some sun to get my Vit D up in the summer.

Unfortunately, working downtown, I would not have such benefit, because I would have pay-as-you-go parking, so it would be way to expensive to drive there on my lunch everyday. My Vit D levels would likely suffer.

### Pogo — 2017-12-05T04:00:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/work-environment.20542/post-229518>

I would do whatever I could to make that:

> 2)
> \-Atmosphere is super chill, the people are a blast to work with (used to work with them, energy boosters) and the projects are super exciting and engaging
> \-Flexible telecommute (possibility to work from home 1-2x per week, or as needed)

work for me. Given the alternative, what is the point of any of it?

Get your vit-D in the mornings, on your telecommute days (work at the beach?), and the weekend.

# Thread: From Dr. Lazlo Boros , Re: Deuterium Testing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-dr-lazlo-boros-re-deuterium-testing.20547/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2017-12-06T07:02:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-dr-lazlo-boros-re-deuterium-testing.20547/post-229615>

After listening to Jack w/ Nathan Walz on Facebook last night, I sent Dr. Lazlo Boros an e-mail to find out more about where to get the deuterium tissue fractionation testing that Jack mentioned, and he sent me a lot of information that may be of interest to people on the forums.

I've attached his response below:
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

From Dr. Boros:

"
The easiest and most reliable whole body tissues deuterium depletion test is our Breathy(R) assay that you can fully explore and order at [www.cignaturehealth.com](http://www.cignaturehealth.com/)

I also developed a tissue based indirect surrogate deuterium test using certain sequences in connection with nuclear magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). You can review this method by listening to my talk below. You can also order this test via our CignatureHealth website!

The precise biochemical mechanisms how deuterium depletion occurs in cells can be found in [Med Hypotheses. 2016 Feb; 87: 69–74. doi:](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/elink.fcgi?dbfrom=pubmed&retmode=ref&cmd=prlinks&id=26826644) [10.1016/j.mehy.2015.11.016](https://dx.doi.org/10.1016%2Fj.mehy.2015.11.016)

Neuro-Oncology, Volume 19, Issue 4, 1 April 2017, Pages 595–596, [https://doi.org/10.1093/neuonc/now284](https://doi.org/10.1093/neuonc/now284)
[https://www.academia.edu/21057478/C...quilibrium\_and\_Water\_Pools\_of\_Mammalian\_Cells](https://www.academia.edu/21057478/Compartmentalized_NADPH_Synthesis_Intramolecular_Deuterium_Disequilibrium_and_Water_Pools_of_Mammalian_Cells)

Aging related excellent deuterium depletion related experiments have also been published:

[Toxicol Lett. 2012 Jun 20; 211(3): 319–324.](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/elink.fcgi?dbfrom=pubmed&retmode=ref&cmd=prlinks&id=22561170)doi: [10.1016/j.toxlet.2012.04.014](https://dx.doi.org/10.1016%2Fj.toxlet.2012.04.014)

Please let us know if you have questions.

Best Wishes, László G. Boros"

### Billybats — 2018-09-07T21:29:35+0300

# Thread: nnemf product testimonials
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-product-testimonials.20548/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2017-12-06T12:59:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-product-testimonials.20548/post-229624>

# Thread: turned off circuit breaker in bedroom- morning fasted blood sugar relatively lower
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turned-off-circuit-breaker-in-bedroom-morning-fasted-blood-sugar-relatively-lower.20552/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2017-12-07T12:44:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turned-off-circuit-breaker-in-bedroom-morning-fasted-blood-sugar-relatively-lower.20552/post-229717>

this is probably too early to be happy about- but i still want to put it out there and maybe get some responses. i have been getting all sorts of great ideas from this site and also from watching youtube videos of nathan walz and matt maruca. yesterday i turned off the circuit breaker to my bedroom. i had worse hot flashes than i've had in a while. could have been from the spicy chili i ate. i took an aleve and was fine. the thing that was surprising is that my fasting blood sugar was 79/80. that's not a huge drop from 84- 90, which is what i have been getting with low carb and attention to blue light. but the really surprising thing is that i had carb craving last night before i went to sleep and had a wrap, tangerine, and hummus. usually that would push my blood sugar up the next morning to 90 plus. so it is very unusual for me to be 79/80. in the past when i've had hot flashes at night, my morning fasting blood sugar was not affected. i've been taking my morning fasted blood sugar for years (was prediabetic at 5.9 in 2009), so i know my patterns- and this morning's result was truly unusual. i also did 15 minutes of morning fast walking with no coat in the 40's F, so maybe that also had an effect?

# Thread: Kidney dynamics, a few initial thoughts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-dynamics-a-few-initial-thoughts.20561/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Leger — 2017-12-09T18:14:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-dynamics-a-few-initial-thoughts.20561/post-229879>

This post is a continuation of the thread started by @[Technosmith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20046/) :
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...edicine-kidney-yin-and-chi-deficiencey.20468/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/traditional-chinese-medicine-kidney-yin-and-chi-deficiencey.20468/)

From the CCM (Classical Chinese Medicine) perspective, the kidneys are the batteries of the body. This has dramatic implications for the new findings in water and how it functions in our body. A discussion I had with a friend - Dr. Robin Murphy (ND) - on this subject - he was of the opinion that the kidneys main function is to structure water. I was just getting into water structuring at that point in time, but his statement continues to echo through the years. It offers a possible starting place for how water gets structured in our bodies and a way of looking at the kidneys from an energetic or dynamic perspective. The Western perspective is mired in (bio) chemistry. Looking at organ dynamics - their energetic function - possibly informed by some understanding of the CCM perspective - is the way forward.

Relatedly, Biology is the foundation of Reality, physics is a subset. Physics is a way for us to study dead matter, the 'corpses' of Life; basic arithmetic so that we can get our thoughts straight before tackling the advanced 'math'. The discovery of quantum mechanics, etc, is the beginning of a bridge to Life. Quantum Biology extends the bridge, offering tantalizing clues to a much better understanding of Life.

I was just getting into water structuring at that point in time, but his statement continues to echo through the years. # Thread: protecting yourself at work: reducing blue light from screens vs reducing nnemf exposure from devic
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protecting-yourself-at-work-reducing-blue-light-from-screens-vs-reducing-nnemf-exposure-from-devic.20570/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2017-12-12T12:15:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protecting-yourself-at-work-reducing-blue-light-from-screens-vs-reducing-nnemf-exposure-from-devic.20570/post-230085>

As a teacher, I work in an environment where the wifi signal keeps being ramped up in my school building. people complain about the weak signals (as a side note, the signal seems to go out when it is raining for some reason), and then more antennas or whatever they use are installed to make the signal stronger. i want to get some type of blue-blocking eye glasses, but those type of glasses don't reduce radiation exposure to the wifi signals, correct? what do members use to reduce their overall radiation exposure in the workplace? i am reading scott compton's journal, but am only on page 9, so i'm just at the parts where he is making homemade hats by sewing in some sort of material and he is about to talk to his child's school principal. i am doing reading, so please don't think i am just another lazy newbie.....has anyone found a meter-verifiable way to reduce their radiation exposure to wifi in the workplace???

### WalterNL — 2017-12-12T13:08:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protecting-yourself-at-work-reducing-blue-light-from-screens-vs-reducing-nnemf-exposure-from-devic.20570/post-230088>

True, glasses don't help against all nnEMF, but they do help against the worst of it which is artificial blue light. But the main thing you can do is avoidance, so limit exposure and move yourself away from the source of nnEMF. If you don't, all other things proposed on this blog help some way or another to mitigate nnEMF damage.

As far as I know there is no way to stop microwaves from influencing your biology if you are within range.

### SlamSlask — 2017-12-12T14:03:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protecting-yourself-at-work-reducing-blue-light-from-screens-vs-reducing-nnemf-exposure-from-devic.20570/post-230091>

Turn off what you can - after that use the inverse square law to your advantage (get as far away as possible)
After this consider if the radiation is worth your paycheck

# Thread: A success story overcoming fatigue
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mbhemming — 2017-12-13T06:39:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-230202>

I'm now about 9 months in to following Dr. Kruses suggestions. I'm happy to report a success in overcoming fatigue. I have been keeping data on my fatigue issue for 209 days now. The attached graph shows the decrease in my fatigue symptoms.

Interpreting the scatter plot:
Each data point in the plot represents an average of the 90 days. So the first data point is my average amount of fatigue from day 1 to day 90. The second data point is the average from day 2 to day 91.

The red line is linear model that was fit to data to clearly show the decrease.

I'm happy to report that I have had no symptoms for the past 48 days!!!

I would like to personally thank Dr. Kruse!

Am I totally better yet? No, I still have some cognitive impairment symptoms that come periodically.

### Susie Cahill — 2017-12-14T09:21:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-230274>

Wow, that’s awesome.
I didn’t keep a diary but after finding JK in May this year I’m a new person. I had stage 3 adrenal fatigue and really only did a loose version of the diet but added heaps of oysters. I had 3 holidays in the tropics of 10 days duration on his advice, religiously saw the sun rise and wore blu blockers, went to bed at 9 pm and tried to stay out in the am light as much as possible and totally rearranged my social life; no dinners only lunches. The game changer for me was the Novothor light pod. I did it 2-3x/week and in a 12 sessions I had more energy than I’ve had in a decade. Just need to check my cortisol levels now and I can’t imagine they haven’t gone north. I’m one happy camper. Keen to start CT.

### taiyang — 2017-12-15T12:09:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-230342>

congratulations mbhemming! can you give some specifics of what you did?
susie, how did you find a place offering the light pod? i googled my state and the name of that pod and came up with nothing.

### mbhemming — 2017-12-16T02:33:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-230400>

@[taiyang](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20771/)
I didn't do everything perfectly, I just did what I could.
\-eat seafood approximately every second day
\-kept reducing EMF as much as I could. I bought a gaussmeter and RF meter. I haven't been able to get rid of my cell phone but I reduced the use as much as possible.
\-I starting doing CT in september but took a really non-aggressive approach to it. I was doing around 2 or 3 times per week in the cold tub. But then it started getting cold where I live so I stopped doing the cold tub and just didn't turn on the heat
\-moved out of the city in a little house that is at ground level, has a well, is in the woods, is 100m to the ocean, and has a fireplace
\-I was doing paleo at first but I had a hard time keeping a healthy weight so now I just eat relatively natural
\-stopped alcohol and caffeine(I started them again recently because I haven't had fatigue symptoms)
\-I also got a tonne of sun last summer. I tried to get 5 hours/day for each day that it was sunny
\-took over a gram of magnesium per day
\-did a lot of walking for exercise
\-I did a lot of circadian synchronization
\*you can check out my health journal for more info. I just tried to summarize it here

### taiyang — 2017-12-16T12:55:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-230416>

wow- that's great that you moved to the woods. that's always been a dream of mine. i was lucky to have grown surrounded by woods.

no- i'm in the us. i'm going to look around some more. i live near a big city, so there should be something. thanks for your reply. i just saw from novothor.com that the nearest location to me is two states away. i will have to research other options in addition to what i am already doing.

### mbhemming — 2018-02-24T19:54:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-235626>

More time has past and I've continued to make improvements.

Interpreting the attached graphs:
\-All three graph are on a 90 day window. This means that each data point is the average of 90 consecutive days. So the first data point is the average from day 1 to day 90, and the second data point is the average from day 2 to day 91.
\-The sequence of events are as follows. I started implementing Dr. K's ideas, then a couple months later I started collect the 'fatigue' data, after 3 months of collecting the 'fatigue' data I started collecting the 'energy' and 'cognition' data.
\-the red line is a linear model that fits the blue data

Congratulations on your improvement!

### Susie Cahill — 2018-04-09T10:46:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-239027>

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=230342)
>
> congratulations mbhemming! can you give some specifics of what you did?
> susie, how did you find a place offering the light pod? i googled my state and the name of that pod and came up with nothing.

Taiyang,
I’ve had a breakthrough with maintaining my energy levels without the Novothor light pod. When I was really bad with adrenal fatigue I was doing Novothor sessions 3x/week. I then got to a stage of backing it off to 1x/week which I maintained for about 2 months. I always wondered how much emf’s the machine gave off and when I saw a Jack Kruse comment somewhere, maybe here or Facebook, suggesting a guy go swimming rather than purchase a Joove lamp, I thought maybe I should try swimming too. The bay is the same travel time from my home as the Novothor so I started swimming. It’s not too cold here yet hovering around 60-62 degrees but for me, who’s always felt the cold it a big thing but I’m past dreading it now and I feel great. I get the same energy kick I was getting from the light pod. I also do a sauna then get back in the cold water again. I’m now swimming .75 kilometre, when I started I was swimming about 100 metres.
Apparently the water temp gets down to 46 or 8c in winter. The club is over 120 yrs old and the swim group is called the Iceburgers. I been pick the brains of the veterans and they say things like, this is our flu injection, we never get sick, I don’t feel right if I don’t start my day this way. Their mean age would probably be around 70 yrs.
Are you close to the beach?

### taiyang — 2018-04-09T13:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-239032>

I am 10 minutes walking distance from a big river. I am very cold intolerant. Even on a super hot day in the summer, I won't go in the ocean past my calves. Right now i am in one of my cyclic bouts with anemia due to heavy periods. i am being more aggressive with stopping the flow of blood with norethindrone pills since i am getting weak and a sore throat from blood loss. i always hold off on taking pills unless absolutely necessary. i should have started supplementing with iron earlier, but i got scared off by reading about the dangers of iron overload. i am definitely not going to be at that point for a long while, so i am supplementing. maybe as my iron/ferritin stores fill up, i will be less cold intolerant by the summer and i will try swimming in the ocean (1.5 hours a way) like 2x a week and the river everyday. thanks for the update!!

### Susie Cahill — 2018-04-10T13:51:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-239122>

Good luck with that. jK also said your omega 6 :3 ratio should be below 10. If low on omega 3 you’ll feel the cold more.
Best wishes.

### JanSz — 2018-04-10T15:58:34+0300

> \-kept reducing EMF as much as I could. I bought a gaussmeter and RF meter. I haven't been able to get rid of my cell phone but I reduced the use as much as possible.
> \-I starting doing CT in september but took a really non-aggressive approach to it. I was doing around 2 or 3 times per week in the cold tub. But then it started getting cold where I live so I stopped doing the cold tub and just didn't turn on the heat
> \-moved out of the city in a little house that is at ground level, has a well, is in the woods, is 100m to the ocean, and has a fireplace
> \-I was doing paleo at first but I had a hard time keeping a healthy weight so now I just eat relatively natural
> \-stopped alcohol and caffeine(I started them again recently because I haven't had fatigue symptoms)
> \-I also got a tonne of sun last summer. I tried to get 5 hours/day for each day that it was sunny
> \-took over a gram of magnesium per day
> \-did a lot of walking for exercise
> \-I did a lot of circadian synchronization
> \*you can check out my health journal for more info. I just tried to summarize it here

What kind of MG did you take?

I've looked around the forum for the answer to this question and have seen tid bits here and there. But my question is, would sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) be "good" to consume daily in small doses?

I've been reading that it increases cell voltage by being an electron donor and that it also increase cell oxygen as well. I would think both of these things would be beneficial in today's nnEMF world.

Thanks for any replies and help!

I'm a huge an of it - but I use potassium bicarbonate instead - I rub baking soda all over my body after the shower and after I rub epsom salts and mag chloride - my skin gets hot - then I blast it with cold water and it hydrates my skin - JK has said previously that baking soda increases the exclusion zone of water...

But, to pick something like this alternative up (possibly second hand for $300) is a good proposition I think.
