**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-ct-in-a-freezer.20584/post-239734>

this is how i ct at home. i love it man! i change out the water every couple of weeks and dump a bottle of hydro-peroxide in and never have any issues with bacteria that i can tell. you can pick these guys up for 100 bucks on craigslist easily. i drop piece of copper in there that's connected to a grounding rod and a chunk of activated charcoal go into the cold!

i am rearranging the ct chest freezer and i was wondering if i would be risking electrocution by using an immersible pump while i'm inside. what do you guys think?

### drezy — 2018-04-24T18:34:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-ct-in-a-freezer.20584/post-239836>

> [Benny Hammond said:](/goto/post?id=239734)

>
> I am rearranging the ct chest freezer and i was wondering if i would be risking electrocution by using an immersible pump while i'm inside. what do you guys think?

If you have a Cornet or any other EM meter do yourself a favor and turn the pump on while holding the meter near it to see why I never run my pump and even have a shutoff for power to the whole circuit when I'm in my tub.

A cheap outlet timer will work well once you figure out about how much flow per day you need.

### Benny Hammond — 2018-04-24T22:31:48+0300

you believe i would be risking electrocution with a submersible pump running while doing ct? i always be sure the freezer is unplugged before i go in.

### drezy — 2018-04-24T23:06:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-ct-in-a-freezer.20584/post-239865>

That's not what I said at all. As an electrical engineer I know that if you get a GFCI plug in your circuit and there will be zero chance you'll electrocute yourself.

My point is that if, for some reason, you like the idea of straddling your prostate up on top of a strong alternating magnetic field that would make a cornet meter max out then by all means go ahead if it's important to run your pump exactly while you're also in the tub. Otherwise just design around it.

PM me and we can talk. I love knowing others out there are coming up with different cool systems.

FWIW I circulate my water in between sessions and have rarely seen my tub temp increase 2 degrees during 45 minute use.

### drezy — 2018-04-26T15:58:49+0300

In the context of a forum, when I answer someone I am on the record for all future people with similar questions that find that answer.

If you concern yourself with increasing longer term colon and prostate cancer then you'll want to not be in a cooler with a pump running anywhere near your bottom.

If you care only about short term electrocution then you probably want to ask on an electrician forum. There will be many mothers worried about their pool pump electrocuting their kids that keep dipping in the landscaped koi pond.

### Billybats — 2017-12-19T15:03:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-my-florescent-bulb.20595/post-230660>

Hi Everyone,

Where I rent, my kitchen has a florescent light. Can I replace the bulb to make it non florescent. This may be a dumb question but are they made only for florescent bulbs or is the fixture what makes it a florescent bulb. If I can replace the bulb can I use a reptile bulb.

Edit: actually I think if I can just put in an incandescent bulb would be better?

### JanSz — 2017-12-19T15:30:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-my-florescent-bulb.20595/post-230661>

You have to get another lamp.

The is variety of incandescent bulbs, when shopping for new lamp have with you the bulb in question.

...

### Billybats — 2017-12-19T17:10:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-my-florescent-bulb.20595/post-230677>

That's what I figured I will have to do is get a different light source. So I guess just a regular lamp with an incandescent bulb. Then maybe a full spectrum lamp and bulb for the living room area. Thank you JanSz.

### DavidL — 2017-12-19T17:21:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-my-florescent-bulb.20595/post-230679>

Incandescent is better because it contains IR. Some incandescents have measured in the UV range. Halogen are good too because they have infrared. My incandescents are soft white, red, or orange. I used to use the daylight bulbs but that was before I learned about light.

### Billybats — 2017-12-19T19:28:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-my-florescent-bulb.20595/post-230689>

Thank you.. I have all incandescent except for the kitchen. I wasn't sure if I can just put a incandescent bulb/tube in that fixture but I guess not. I have been thinking of putting a lamp in the kitchen for a while now with an incandescent bulb and taping the switch for the florescent light so we don't turn it on...lol So it's going to be incandescent and candles which we have been using more. Thank you guys.

# Thread: Starting the $100,000 2018 transformation Jack Kruse way.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-the-100-000-2018-transformation-jack-kruse-way.20600/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 29

### Billybats — 2017-12-21T16:31:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-the-100-000-2018-transformation-jack-kruse-way.20600/post-230793>

Hello All,

Well I wasn't going to tell anyone I was going to do this transformation through bodybuilding site. I wanted to keep kind of a journal to keep me accountable for myself, whether I make it or not. I want to see if I make it the whole way because I didn't last year. Last year I feel really crappy and wasn't physically in the right mind..basically I was ill still overcoming my near death experience. I am going to do it in Jack Kruse guidelines. I will post before pictures..yep you heard me... and no laughing.. lol. I want to do this for myself and I can use the money and who knows where it will go from there. I said the only things that I have changed are the blue blocker glasses and what made a big difference is putting a blue blocker on my tv that never comes off, since doing that my sleep has changed soooooooo much. I also have added a basic multi vitamin and 1/2 dose of b6 I got rid of the other supplements (coq10 and L- gutamine) I know supplements, but they are helping until I can get more morning sun. Overall I am feeling not to bad. So just those changes helped me feel more sure I can to this and feeling fed up with feeling crappy. The changes I am in the process of making are:

My environment- Changing my job soon so I am not on computers as much or at all and my schedule will be later to get my morning sun. I maybe working with toddlers at a private preschool, well see.
My light in the kitchen.
Moccasins with leather soles so I can workout with them outside.

CT- I have been doing some but going to really put my all into it just to see what happens with doing minimal weight lifting.

My diet- Eat optimal which this I haven't done in while, just being honest.

I know there are other things but drawing a blank at the moment. So I am going to try and come to this thread and keep updating.

### Lahelada — 2017-12-21T16:42:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-the-100-000-2018-transformation-jack-kruse-way.20600/post-230794>

Power to your elbow.

### Billybats — 2018-01-03T16:37:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-the-100-000-2018-transformation-jack-kruse-way.20600/post-231500>

Hey everyone,

I don't know if its this cold weather or what. I don't think its the cold because I like how I feel out there and tomorrow we are suppose to be getting snow and Friday 0 degrees with minus wind chill 40-50 gusts. How do I cold adapt outside, should I be wearing a hat, would it be the same as sitting in the tub with gloves and a hat. The other thing is at work my area doesn't get any warmer the 65 degrees so its colder the any other area of the building and home, I guess that is good but not sure.

I started the Epi-paleo diet and I know I need a lot of healing or is it normal to feel funky in the head and upper back area and my ringing in my left ear is worse. Sooooo I am thinking my body is borderline shot but way better then it was 3 years ago. The feeling in my head is not bad but weird and annoying. It feels like there is just slight swelling if that is possible in the hypothalamus/pituitary area or it's my back muscle being stiff. This gets worse when I try to go keto and not eat sugar/bread. Is this a detox. I can focus okay but last night I had to take aleve and ice pack to my upper back to go to sleep so I didn't wake up with a migraine so I didn't lose work. I feel better and slept ok, not too bad. Now back to the cold weather,

As for my pictures they should be coming this weekend or before the 14th deadline.

As for the diet that I only started yesterday I had eggs for breakfast, tuna with mayo and pickles but a few hours late had some nuts and a few m&ms I know not perfect, then dinner had raw oysters, cooked shrimp with broccoli and mushrooms. I had a craving for sweets alot but I had glycine that was sweet and took some of that. I did stop my multi, l glutimine and coq10. I may start the multi back up not sure. This morning I made a drink with water, turmeric, cinnamon, and lemon to see how that helps my head.

Waiting on that job interview for a teachers assistant for toddlers, even if they have wifi at the school it bets sitting all day at work in front of a computer and not enjoying my work.

# Thread: hack morning hormone cascade?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-morning-hormone-cascade.20604/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2017-12-22T19:59:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-morning-hormone-cascade.20604/post-230861>

1\. I need to eat within 20 minutes of waking.
2\. I need to get red light with the morning sun and eat when the sun is up.
3\. for my job (on which i support a family of 4 including supplying dental and health insurance), i wake up in the dark in the winter months (only).

Question- can i make a fake morning sun environment within which to eat my high protein breakfast and align my hormone signalling? or do i just take the damage of circadian mismatch until spring? any morning sun devices out there???

thanks.

### JanSz — 2017-12-22T23:21:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-morning-hormone-cascade.20604/post-230881>

**Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.
**
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4)

### Ted — 2017-12-23T01:15:15+0200

>
> Question- can i make a fake morning sun environment within which to eat my high protein breakfast and align my hormone signalling? or do i just take the damage of circadian mismatch until spring? any morning sun devices out there???
>
> thanks.

I'm sure there are other ways, but what I do is to put on an incandescent lamp. This has a little blue and a lot of red, so it should be similar to the sun just rising.

### taiyang — 2017-12-24T13:11:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-morning-hormone-cascade.20604/post-231005>

Hi Jan- I saw your high morning cortisol on your graph. Do you have any blood sugar issues? i am wondering if high cortisol could be reflected in higher morning blood sugar readings. my morning fasted blood sugar readings have gone down with mitochondriac hacks, and i am wondering if this phenomenon is related to lower cortisol.

For me, paying attention to first- blacking out my sleeping environment, second- wearing blue blockers in the evening/avoiding screens in the evening except for the TV from a distance, third- taking off my glasses when out in the sun, and fourth- turning off the circuit breaker to my bedroom has led to lower morning fasting blood sugar. i don't monitor my blood sugar throughout the day since i'm a little lazy and i am just trying to prevent going back to being pre-diabetic.

here is some data:
in november 2014
age- 49 and suffering from hot flashes at night, poor sleep headaches
average morning fasted blood sugar - \*\*\*106\*\*\* from 22 mornings (forgot to take my bg some mornings)
highest value 119
lowest value 94
weight- around 133 lbs.
in november 2015
age- 50 still suffering from poor sleep/headaches due to hot flashes
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*93\*\*\*from 28 readings
highest value 103
lowest value 82
weight- around 135-139 lbs.
in november 2016 (first summer i spent walking in the city for hours, but with glasses on and a visor. no sunblock)
age 51- still suffering from hot flashes. started playing around with intermittent use of 5 HTP. sometimes it helped with sleep, but not if i had bad hot flashes. then only ice on my head and aleve worked for the head aches and hot flashes. i took it rarely, though.
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*102\*\*\* from 29 readings (at this point i sometimes would take two readings consecutively and saw my meter could read 86/91 0r 84/90) I was also getting more and more desperate because of poor sleep and was also rating my hot flashes as well as my blood sugar. i saw that i might have a high morning blood sugar reading of 84-107 and no hot flashes the previous night or extreme hot flashes and a morning blood sugar of 96-106. so my hot flashes did not seem to track with my blood sugar.
highest value 109
lowest value 86
weight around 135-138 (I stopped caring about my weight and hardly have any notes about it. I was really focused on my sleep since my memory was going).
in november 2017 (second summer i spend walking around the city for hours. still have glasses on and a visor. still no sun block)
age 52- i started completely blacking out my bedroom, but forgot when that began. the most important changes aroune october 19 2017 are the uvex glasses and no screens except for tv at a distance after sunset. i start to not wear glasses outside and crack my windows when driving. no supplements at all.
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*88\*\*\* from 19 readings
highest value 96
lowest value 81
weight around 137-140 lbs (still not noting it a lot since sleep and cognitive function are my priorities. also fewer notes on calendar because i started sleeping better so not as desperate)
\*\*\*i turned off the circuit breaker to my bedroom on Dec. 7. I had a hot flash that night, but now is the first time I'm seeing a blood sugar in the 70's. from Dec. 7 to Dec. 24 my average blood sugar is \*\*\*82\*\*\* from 20 readings (some days I forget to measure, some days I take two readings consecutively and get results like 78/80 or 77/87). i am going fuller into perimenopause at 52 as i get my period on dec. 5 at day 60 of my cycle. i am starting to eat more carbs because i feel protected because of the drop in my morning fasted readings. this is probably a mistake, but i am still getting lower fasted readings even with eating more carbs. my weight hasn't gone up and is still around 138 lbs.
i also posted this in the success stories forum.

> lowest value 82
> weight- around 135-139 lbs.
> in november 2016 (first summer i spent walking in the city for hours, but with glasses on and a visor. no sunblock)
> age 51- still suffering from hot flashes. started playing around with intermittent use of 5 HTP. sometimes it helped with sleep, but not if i had bad hot flashes. then only ice on my head and aleve worked for the head aches and hot flashes. i took it rarely, though.
> average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*102\*\*\* from 29 readings (at this point i sometimes would take two readings consecutively and saw my meter could read 86/91 0r 84/90) I was also getting more and more desperate because of poor sleep and was also rating my hot flashes as well as my blood sugar. i saw that i might have a high morning blood sugar reading of 84-107 and no hot flashes the previous night or extreme hot flashes and a morning blood sugar of 96-106. so my hot flashes did not seem to track with my blood sugar.
> highest value 109
> lowest value 86
> weight around 135-138 (I stopped caring about my weight and hardly have any notes about it. I was really focused on my sleep since my memory was going).
> in november 2017 (second summer i spend walking around the city for hours. still have glasses on and a visor. still no sun block)
> age 52- i started completely blacking out my bedroom, but forgot when that began. the most important changes aroune october 19 2017 are the uvex glasses and no screens except for tv at a distance after sunset. i start to not wear glasses outside and crack my windows when driving. no supplements at all.
> average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*88\*\*\* from 19 readings
> highest value 96
> lowest value 81
> weight around 137-140 lbs (still not noting it a lot since sleep and cognitive function are my priorities. also fewer notes on calendar because i started sleeping better so not as desperate)
> \*\*\*i turned off the circuit breaker to my bedroom on Dec. 7. I had a hot flash that night, but now is the first time I'm seeing a blood sugar in the 70's. from Dec. 7 to Dec. 24 my average blood sugar is \*\*\*82\*\*\* from 20 readings (some days I forget to measure, some days I take two readings consecutively and get results like 78/80 or 77/87). i am going fuller into perimenopause at 52 as i get my period on dec. 5 at day 60 of my cycle. i am starting to eat more carbs because i feel protected because of the drop in my morning fasted readings. this is probably a mistake, but i am still getting lower fasted readings even with eating more carbs. my weight hasn't gone up and is still around 138 lbs.
> i also posted this in the success stories forum.

Hi taiyang;

In you glucose story, not even once I see fasting insulin being mentioned.
Think of 2-3 as desirable number.
That usually leads to once/day meals.

Unless there is something else as a reason for my very high cortisol from wakeup till early afternoon,
currently I ascribe shape of my cortisol curve to possibly extreme way I deal with day and night lighting.
Other that using natural sun, I do this:
**Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.**
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)

Consider doing
DUTCH
and
Spectracell Micronutrient analysis
post (complete files) of results here.

and drink excessive amounts of Lugols

.......

Hi JanSz

### taiyang — 2017-12-26T11:38:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-morning-hormone-cascade.20604/post-231088>

thanks for answering my questions, jan. i see you have been a member since 2012 and since i have been coming here since the fall of this year, i see that you are helpful to everyone. member participation is key as jack is a busy man. i have a few questions if you have time

\* jan, have you read posts by members who had cancer and had good outcomes with jack's protocols? if so, can you tell me their names so I can read their journals?
\*also, i didn't see CT on your list. do you use it?
\*i am assuming you optimized fatty acid profile by eating more seafood?
\*how did you optimize micronutrients? jack talks a lot about dha being THE food story, but i always felt confused by this as the body needs certain substrates derived from nutrients to function, leading me to think DHA **plus** micronutrients to build substrate are the food story.
I know jack's writings are organized by topic, but i think it would be useful if the members could organize their successes by topic. search doesn't always help or it leads to posts that are only very slightly related to the topic i am searching.

### JanSz — 2017-12-26T16:44:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-morning-hormone-cascade.20604/post-231096>

> [Susie Cahill said:](/goto/post?id=231081)

> I’m excited to hear about the Preventa progress. Did you do a baseline test of your deuterium with cignature and if so what test did you do? Also, are they managing your DD in any way? And do cignature require you to be a ‘ patient’ of theirs before selling you Preventa?
>
> I am thinking of you this Christmas JanSz as I know it is the first since your wife has passed. I hope you can draw on all the memories of all the Christmases you shared. Thank you for taking the time to share your knowledge and experience here. Be blessed.

Merry Christmas @[Susie Cahill](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20565/) and @[taiyang](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20771/)
and to everybody else who reads this post.
Yes tough Christmas for me but my kids are helping me great deal.
Best wishes to everybody.

When I first learned about CingnatureHealth (from @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) ) I ordered their Cholesterol test.
In return I got from them numerous helpful e-mails with explanations.
Eventually I have send out for testing my breath condensate and urine.
The process takes about 4 weeks, so I do not have results yet.
But that is going to be my baseline.
The initial goal is to get to 130ppm on breath condensate (I think for everybody), or lower depending on ones health condition.
The thought is that increased D promotes growth (good for growing bodies), (not good for those who completed their growth).
I have seen written a 30yo as a breaking point between growth and no growth.

At about the same time I have send my coconut water for D testing.
While doing it I found that the coconut water is in their food category.
That may be significant in the future,
since the deuterium management project consist of three parts.
drinking DDW water
breathing in a way that helps body to manage D
diet using foods low in D

I am assuming that CignatureHealth would not want call me their patient.
Customer would be better description.
They always underline that person seeking medical help should ask doctor for advice and do not change what doctor ordered.
They are working hard to not to cross Big Pharma.
Practically, the way I see it, CignatureHealth works on implementation and further research of dr. Laszlo Boros ideas.

I was not pushed into buying anything, either tests or DDW.
At first I thought that I will wait for test results, but couple days latter ordered 4 months worth of Preventa 25.
Each month worth of water is in one box.
Box have 12 bottles, each 1.5liters.
Due to shipping costs, the 25ppm version is used, weights least.
For drinking I was advised to use 105ppm.
To get 105ppm I have to mix one part of 25 with two parts of any other water.
Any other water, usually tap water, is assumed to have 150ppm D.
(25+2\*150)/3=108.3 close enough
Latter with help of @[WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/) (and a helpful kick from @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)) I found that (very likely) RO water have 140ppm or there about. (Still waiting for someone to test it and publish, maybe you can do it?)
(25+2\*140)/3=101.67ppm D
That made me drop the Poland Spring water that I was drinking and changing to the cheapest water available in Costco, which happens to be RO water (and saying it on the label).
Most cheap waters have RO in their process, but not all advertise that.

I was advised to use DDW as my water everywhere, drinking and cooking.

Note, when boiling DDW the steam is a light water, that is, long boiling/cooking increases D content.
That is not really significant but worth keeping in mind.
One step in rectification column (under atmospheric pressure) changes D content by about 4%.
8% under vacuum.

Long way to say that CignatureHealth have not pushed me this way or the other.

\========================================

@[taiyang](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20771/)

\[qu ote="taiyang, post: 231088, member: 20771"\]thanks for answering my questions, jan. i see you have been a member since 2012 and since i have been coming here since the fall of this year, i see that you are helpful to everyone. member participation is key as jack is a busy man. i have a few questions if you have time

\* jan, have you read posts by members who had cancer and had good outcomes with jack's protocols? if so, can you tell me their names so I can read their journals?

I do not remember such cases.
I have a dear friend in Poland.
Hi have metastatic prostate cancer.
12 years ago his prostate was removed.
Now cancer got to his sacral bone.
I would love if he tried DDW, I am trying to convince him to do it.
He would be a great candidate. If he would use DDW, then monitoring his breath condensate ppm of D would be just to see the D part. More important would be to see how his PSA reacts. That is where the action is.

\*also, i didn't see CT on your list. do you use it?
I am relatively light on CT.
I take daily cold showers, walk bare foot over snow, have a light clothes.

\*i am assuming you optimized fatty acid profile by eating more seafood?
Number #1, for many years I am testing my fatty acids using
22 ------- Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)
doing that I found that I have a lots of DHA and even more EPA
note that seafood comes with tons, and tons, and tons, and tons,
of EPA.
That super excessive amounts of EPA kill AA that we need.
Another words,
discussing fatty acids without actual testing them is rather useless, nobody gets convinced.
It is even worst.
There are laboratories (likely own by fish oil companies), they have pre-aranged setup, one can newer be too high in DHA or EPA. Every test ends up with advice, eat more fish oil.
It is even worst than that.
On my last test, rather than using LabCorp I went with another laboratory.
Just in time when @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) and Laszlo Boros
started talking about importance of
Tetracosanoic Acid, C24:0
by another name
that new lab, conveniently skipped that one.

\*how did you optimize micronutrients? jack talks a lot about dha being THE food story, but i always felt confused by this as the body needs certain substrates derived from nutrients to function, leading me to think DHA **plus** micronutrients to build substrate are the food story.
I know jack's writings are organized by topic, but i think it would be useful if the members could organize their successes by topic. search doesn't always help or it leads to posts that are only very slightly related to the topic i am searching.\[/quote\]

I use
Spectracell micronutrient analysis
it checks about 34 of them (except for lugols that should be drank to excess).

/////////////

breathing

### Cpt.Tired — 2017-12-23T05:09:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-dha-biohack-to-try.20606/post-230914>

Just thinking out loud here:

Has anybody ever tried chewing their seafood for a real long time and let the nutrients absorb through our mucosa?

It makes sense to me. Not only do you get the benefit of getting DHA right into the blood, anything left over

will get absorbed better (if at all) in our guts since they are usually trashed anyway..

I've been getting great results lately and this is one thing (among many others) that I have been doing.

Thoughts anyone?

# Thread: much improved sleep; no headaches; much reduced hot flashes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2017-12-24T13:15:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/post-231006>

i am sharing my story with the diabetes and apoe4 online communities.

i am wondering if high cortisol could be reflected in higher morning blood sugar readings. my morning fasted blood sugar readings have gone down with mitochondriac hacks, and i am wondering if this phenomenon is related to lower cortisol.
For me, paying attention to first- blacking out my sleeping environment, second- wearing blue blockers in the evening/avoiding screens in the evening except for the TV from a distance, third- taking off my glasses when out in the sun, and fourth- turning off the circuit breaker to my bedroom (i see a reading of 79 in the morning for the first time ever) has tracked with/been correlated with lower morning fasting blood sugar. i don't monitor my blood sugar throughout the day since i'm a little lazy and i am just trying to prevent going back to being pre-diabetic.

here is some data:
in november 2014
age- 49 and suffering from hot flashes at night, poor sleep headaches
average morning fasted blood sugar - \*\*\*106\*\*\* from 22 mornings (forgot to take my bg some mornings)
highest value 119
lowest value 94
weight- around 133 lbs.
in november 2015
age- 50 still suffering from poor sleep/headaches due to hot flashes
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*93\*\*\*from 28 readings
highest value 103
lowest value 82
weight- around 135-139 lbs.
in november 2016 (first summer i spent walking in the city for hours, but with glasses on and a visor. no sunblock)
age 51- still suffering from hot flashes. started playing around with intermittent use of 5 HTP. sometimes it helped with sleep, but not if i had bad hot flashes. then only ice on my head and aleve worked for the head aches and hot flashes. i took it rarely, though.
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*102\*\*\* from 29 readings (at this point i sometimes would take two readings consecutively and saw my meter could read 86/91 0r 84/90) I was also getting more and more desperate because of poor sleep and was also rating my hot flashes as well as my blood sugar. i saw that i might have a morning blood sugar reading of anywhere from 84 to 107 and no hot flashes the previous night or extreme hot flashes and a morning blood sugar of 96 to 106. so my hot flashes did not seem to track with my blood sugar.
highest value 109
lowest value 86
weight around 135-138 (I stopped caring about my weight and hardly have any notes about it. I was really focused on my sleep since my memory was going).
in november 2017 (second summer i spend walking around the city for hours. still have glasses on and a visor. still no sun block)
age 52- i started completely blacking out my bedroom, but forgot when that began. the most important changes around october 19 2017 are the uvex glasses and a hat and no screens except for tv at a distance after sunset. I brush my teeth in dim light or in the dark. i start to not wear glasses outside and crack my windows when driving. no supplements at all.
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*88\*\*\* from 19 readings
highest value 96
lowest value 81
weight around 137-140 lbs (still not noting it a lot since sleep and cognitive function are my priorities. also fewer notes on calendar because i started sleeping better so not as desperate)
\*\*\*i turned off the circuit breaker to my bedroom on Dec. 7. I had a hot flash that night, but now is the first time I'm seeing a blood sugar in the 70's. from Dec. 7 to Dec. 24 my average blood sugar is \*\*\*82\*\*\* from 20 readings (some days I forget to measure, some days I take two readings consecutively and get results like 78/80 or 77/87).
highest value 90
lowest value 77
even with the inaccuracies of glucose meters, i can see a clear trend of better blood sugar. i have been using the walmart relion meter for all my readings throughout the years.
i am going fuller into perimenopause at 52 as i get my period on dec. 5 at day 60 of my cycle. i am starting to eat more carbs because i feel protected because of the drop in my morning fasted readings. this is probably a mistake, but i am still getting lower fasted readings even with eating more carbs. my weight hasn't gone up and is still around 138 lbs.

### taiyang — 2017-12-29T20:15:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/post-231244>

the apoe4 community is very interested in my results, but no one in the diabetes online community seems interested. i just told them to did what i did for one lousy night- just turn off the circuit breakers to their bedroom to see if their was any difference in fasted morning glucose- but so far no one has taken me up on my idea. it might've worked for someone- maybe not everyone.
oh well...

### Inger — 2017-12-29T20:42:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/post-231247>

Cool stuff Taiyang! I sure know I feel a difference when I turn off my circuit breaker - it is just more relaxing and feels better, sleep is better, everything is better

Hard to believe how deep people sleep.... even if you rub it right under their nose and there are clear results, they just are too tied to their old believes...they cant break it
The daughter of my ex has diabetes 1 and as me and him tried to cut carbs and measured bloodsugar and saw how big difference just witrh thsi one thing we got, he suggested her to drop carbs and she did.. and she got awesome results, could drop insulin use huge!.. But soon enough she was back to her old habits.. she could do it just a few months and then she started eating bread and sugars again.
You have to be real brave and curious to totally break with old habits and jump into the unknown, and cange your lifestyle for sure! Maybe you have to be wild  to a certain point that our civilisation/culture today do not support....

### taiyang — 2017-12-30T14:51:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/post-231288>

another morning of excellent fasted blood sugar even when eating more carbs than usual- 77. if this is placebo effect, it sure is lasting a long time. with lifestyle changes of varying degrees, here is what i have noticed:
1\. better sleep. even if i wake up for a period of time, i can fall back asleep, which i couldn't do before.
2\. no headaches. i would often have headaches during the day and even at night if i had especially bad hot flashes.
3\. fewer to no hot flashes. i may now be able to do heavier exercise- like weight lifting. before doing heavy exercise would lead to awful hot flashes/headaches at night. yesterday i did some weight lifting and had a slight warming/insomnia. i was able to fall back asleep.
4\. the 'volume' on my attention has been turned up. if you can imagine needing the sound of the tv turned up- if the sound is a little too soft, you can catch things, but you miss a lot even though you concentrate harder. yesterday i noticed that i was able to 'multi-task' in perception in a way that i haven't been able to in recent memory. for example, my daughter is crazy about the tv show grey's anatomy, and she wants me to keep her company. that means that i have watched some shows multiple times via netflix. yesterday i noticed that even though i had seen a show a while a go, i was able to pick up on a lot of nuances in the actors' expressions that i hadn't seen previously. i noticed more of everything, like the fact that christina's pager lit up with a bright light. before i wouldn't have had the mental energy to focus on incidentals like that. and it wasn't just the fact that i had seen this particular show before either. i have watched a lot of reruns and never had the experience of being able to focus on multiple points like i did yesterday. it reminds me of the experience i had of watching a star trek episode while i was in college. i used to watch star trek with my family when i was little, but had not seen any shows for years and years. then when i watched that one star trek episode in college, i was able to catch all the nuances/sub texts/ in between the lines kind of stuff conveyed by the actors' facial expressions that i wasn't able to catch when i was young and had less insight into facial cues. so anyway, i hope my brain keeps healing.

### drezy — 2017-12-30T23:16:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/post-231321>

That's an amazing way to go into the new year. Congratulations.

Keep going. Having only spoken up here a month ago, you stand great odds that your brain will not only "keep healing" but improve beyond what you were used to having access to up there. That's been my experience at least.

# Thread: Flicker-free computer monitors?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-12-24T14:43:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/post-231012>

Hi, does a flicker free computer monitor actually exist? I understand it is advertised as existent but is the flicker-free that is being advertised, equal to what, I suppose, jack kruse would consider absolut-non-existing flickering?

Is jack kruse referring to the flickering caused by the PVM (pulse width modulation) _PWM is used to regulate the brightness of LCD and LED displays._ Or is he referring to the "flickering" caused by the refreshal of the monitor's display?

If, the latter, then flicker-elimination should not exist?

### drezy — 2017-12-24T17:00:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/post-231014>

Nice. You dig deep. It's part of getting to know the risks you are willing to accept.

Yeah I don't think your SCN or topological receptors bother making the academic distinction between ppm and refresh rate/pixel changes.

For your reference Brent Next's solution might be the best comercially available:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/page-3#post-225708](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/page-3#post-225708)

I keep putting in cheap low bids for used kindle units but nobody wants to sell them to me for cheap to make a "monitor" setup like below:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/outdoor-office-in-the-sun.19840/#post-222100](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/outdoor-office-in-the-sun.19840/#post-222100)

Even that would have a flicker-like effect for every pixel that changed as I typed but that's good enough for my risk tolerance & blue light requirements. The refresh rate won't be high but that's sorta the point...

If any of you young dudes beat me to it here are some instructions (google translate will be your friend):
[https://github.com/nahtethan/dxg-display/blob/master/DXG.md](https://github.com/nahtethan/dxg-display/blob/master/DXG.md)

Hacking, rooting, and jailbreaking are all a part of the mitochondriac toolkit.

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-12-24T18:47:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/post-231019>

I appreciate the information, but the monitor u are referencing is simply too low performance... I need at least 100Hz refresh rate and 1080p...

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=231014)
>
> Yeah I don't think your SCN or topological receptors bother making the academic distinction between ppm and refresh rate/pixel changes.

I really hope the above isnt true...  That is really the QUESTion of this post to figure out. Lord have mercy.. It does seem logical, but I am still not sold that refresh rate = flicker effect???

### drezy — 2017-12-24T19:38:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/post-231024>

> [Nicolaj Sølvsten said:](/goto/post?id=231019)
>
> I appreciate the information, but the monitor u are referencing is simply too low performance... I need at least 100Hz refresh rate and 1080p...

We're bound in opposite directions on that one lol.

I'll split the difference on perspective... The portions of the screen that are changing (color/brightness) quite a bit are flicker-like:

If you mainly just watch documentaries on turtles and pygmy three-toed sloths I think you'll be safer

# Thread: Violettglas
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/violettglas.20618/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Leger — 2017-12-27T17:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/violettglas.20618/post-231137>

I've been experimenting with water for quite a bit of time. A friend recommended I look into Violettglas. I've been playing with it for about a year and a half. From the miron-glas.com website:

> Miron Violet glass blocks the complete spectrum of visible light with the exception of the violet part.
> At the same time it allows a certain part to be permeable for radiation in the spectral range of UV-A, and infra red light. This unique combination offers optimal protection against the ageing processes that are released by visible light, thus lengthening durability and potency of products.

I purchased some of their water bottles. I place the bottles in a light box I built and leave in the sunlight for some number of hours. I then put the bottles in the fridge and chill to ~40f. The water I'm currently using is filtered (Woder 2 stage) tap water. I then pour the water through a Natural Action portable water 'structurer' - [https://naturalactiontechnologies.com/project/portable/](https://naturalactiontechnologies.com/project/portable/). This is my optimal water. It is silkier, denser and has a certain freshness. It's been awhile since I've had fresh glacier water, but this is the closest I've gotten to that water.

### WalterNL — 2017-12-27T18:30:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/violettglas.20618/post-231139>

Cool find, I'm going to get a few of those bottles.

# Thread: Lets have fun- science jokes and other jokes too.

### Billybats — 2017-12-28T00:46:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-fun-science-jokes-and-other-jokes-too.20619/post-231153>

I love this one:

Two atoms are walking along. One of them says:
“Oh, no, I think I lost an electron.”
“Are you sure?”
“Yes, I’m positive.”

And this one:

Never trust an atom… they make up everything!

Now some of them I didn't get where I found those jokes. .lol

### Billybats — 2017-12-28T00:54:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-fun-science-jokes-and-other-jokes-too.20619/post-231154>

Okay a couple more:

A photon checks into a hotel. When asked if it needs a bellman, it responds “No, I’m traveling light”

A proton, a neutron, and helium walk into a bar and order three beers.
The bartender appears with 3 beers and asks the proton, “Are you sure you’re over 21?” The proton replies, “I’m positive.” So the bartender gives him the first beer.
He gives the second beer to the neutron and says, “For you, no charge.”
He throws the third beer in helium’s face. Helium doesn’t react.

### Billybats — 2017-12-28T01:00:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-fun-science-jokes-and-other-jokes-too.20619/post-231155>

This is the last one for now. Lol.. my boyfriend and I always say "there's that word again" when we say the word heavy

### Lahelada — 2017-12-28T04:20:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-fun-science-jokes-and-other-jokes-too.20619/post-231160>

A biochemist walks into a student bar and says to the barman: “I’d like a pint of adenosine triphosphate, please.” “Certainly,” says the barman, “that’ll be ATP.” ( Uk centric joke  )

Why are quantum physicists terrible in bed? Because when they find the position, they can’t find the momentum, and when they have the momentum, they can’t find the position.

Never trust an atom. They make up everything.

When I heard that oxygen and magnesium hooked up I was like OMg.

### Billybats — 2017-12-28T17:07:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-fun-science-jokes-and-other-jokes-too.20619/post-231169>

I decided to make my password "incorrect" because if I type it in wrong, my computer will remind me, "Your password is incorrect."
\---------------------------------------------------------

**A Man Walked into an EMF Consultant's Office ...**

**A man walked into an EMF Consultant's office and inquired about the rates for undertaking an EMF investigation and remediation.

"Well, typically we scope, structure and plan the assignment in advance, and charge $150 an hour (plus sales tax and expenses) for three questions", replied the consultant.

"Isn't that ridiculously expensive?" asked the man.

"Yes," the EMF Consultant replied, "and what was your third question?"

**
\--------------------------------------------------------
**One day an EMF Consultant was walking in the woods ...

An EMF Consultant was walking through the forest one day when a frog called out to him from the side of the path...

It said "If you kiss me, I'll turn into an enchanting, beautiful prince/princess". She/He bent over, picked up the frog, put it in his pocket and continued with his walk.

The frog chimed up again and said, "If you kiss me and turn me back into an enchanting and beautiful princess, I will stay with you for one week." The guy stopped, took the frog out of his pocket, smiled fondly at it, returned it to his pocket and resumed his walk.

The frog then cried out, "If you kiss me and turn me back into an enchanting, beautiful princess, I will stay with you forever ." Again the guy took the frog out, smiled at it and put it back into his pocket.

Finally, the frog asks in exasperation, _"What is wrong with you?_ I've told you I'm an enchanting, beautiful princess; that I'll stay with you and be your girlfriend forever. _Why won't you kiss me?_"

The guy said, "Look, I'm an EMF Consultant. I am so busy keeping my clients safe in these times of rampant electrosmog I don't have time for a girlfriend, but a talking frog – How _Cool_ is THAT!! "

**
\------------------------------------------------------

in some neurodegenerative diseases, there is dysregulation of the quality control system. either the autoimmune system is overactive and destroying healthy neurons before their time or underactive, and organelles- lysosomes primarily?-get clogged with cellular debris. also, with a leaky blood brain barrier (from nnemfs and/or chronic high blood sugar degrading pericytes at the BBB), there may be an overload of toxins that the quality control system cannot keep up with. valter longo and (borrowing from Longo's research) de vany emphasize strengthening the quality control system through fasting and refeeds and weight lifting.

# Thread: Happy New Year everyone!

### Inger — 2017-12-31T14:45:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-year-everyone.20625/post-231337>

I love these winter holidays, Christmas is so awesome because it is the time when dark turns into light again..slowly and steadily...spring will soon arrive   And New Year is so exiciting, because that is when one year has passed and I think about all the things I can improve, and what I have all learned in the past year. Wow.. I am excited like child, huh!
I have cleaned my home nicely, watered my flowers, having to leave for work soon at a huge new year party.. everything feels good and I am ready for 2018 to come with more amazing challenges and more to learn and beautiful things to experience

2017 truly was a crazy year for me! I learned a ton.. with all its ups and downs

I am so grateful for you all, being on this journey together is for sure something special
2018 will be a deuterium depleting year for me, I am so all in
It is rainy and stormy here..all gray.. but I feel high... whats up with this deuterium depletion magic...lol Dipped in my rainbarrel today morning as always, and I am so grateful for it! My deuterium depleting machine, all for free..   Drinking glacial water... diving into the darkness...and enjoying the light even more that now increases every day

Wishing you all a magic 2018 filled with love, health, happiness and and an always expanding awareness.. what a beautiful journey this is!

# Thread: haolgen light on dc current used by dr wunsch for reading at night?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haolgen-light-on-dc-current-used-by-dr-wunsch-for-reading-at-night.20650/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2018-01-05T13:51:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haolgen-light-on-dc-current-used-by-dr-wunsch-for-reading-at-night.20650/post-231667>

i was listening to dr mercola interviewing dr wunsch. dr. wunsch was saying that when he read at night, he used a halogen light running on a dc current. does anyone know more about this?

### Lahelada — 2018-01-05T14:33:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haolgen-light-on-dc-current-used-by-dr-wunsch-for-reading-at-night.20650/post-231669>

DC current to avoid the 60hz oscillation of the European network.
Halogen to avoid the flicker effect inherent in LED lamps.
You need to think about colour temperature and how many lux you allow at night ,too.

Dr Wunsch has a vimeo site with tons of good videos .

### drezy — 2018-01-05T14:43:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haolgen-light-on-dc-current-used-by-dr-wunsch-for-reading-at-night.20650/post-231670>

The keys to that kingdom are here:

[https://bengreenfieldfitness.com/podcast/biohacking-podcasts/what-is-the-healthiest-lighting/](https://bengreenfieldfitness.com/podcast/biohacking-podcasts/what-is-the-healthiest-lighting/)

53:20 - 1:03 ish but there are more goodies beyond that point.

### Sue-UK — 2018-01-05T15:02:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haolgen-light-on-dc-current-used-by-dr-wunsch-for-reading-at-night.20650/post-231672>

I've got one of DH's trailer lights (incandescent) that runs off DC to avoid flicker, I sometimes use it in the early evening rather than put the overhead incandescent lights on, but its small, on top of a bookcase and it only gives enough light to move around safely. It doesn't give the eye irritation I get from standard incandescents. I have a right eye problem that I'm hacking, but I have to do more, and give up reading time after sunset, to protect what vision I still have in that eye, and also protect my left eye. I can't read in dim light, but I've not tried bringing the light closer and trying to read by it, or getting a (bigger) halogen, because of my n=1 risks. My eye hack includes evening frequency light blockers (trying to match the -3 and -6 solar elevation frequencies) which in dim light I can move around with, but I can't read with them on, even under bright light. I'm looking forward to the days getting longer .....

### taiyang — 2018-01-06T13:23:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-send-a-private-message.20655/post-231769>

how do i send a private message?

### Inger — 2018-01-06T13:37:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-send-a-private-message.20655/post-231770>

On the top of this site on the right are 3 buttons (in the same line where is "home" "forum" "members" but they are on the left), "your name", "inbox", and "alerts"
Just click on the "inbox" and you will enter the message page, and then just message who you want per their forum name . It is very simple and easy

### Inger — 2018-01-06T13:39:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-send-a-private-message.20655/post-231771>

you can also click on a member name, and then click on "start conversation"

# Thread: Hypnagogic Hallucinations
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypnagogic-hallucinations.20688/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HopingMom — 2018-01-12T04:12:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypnagogic-hallucinations.20688/post-232292>

Anyone else have experience with these hypnagogic hallucinations? I would describe as "mini dreams" experienced just at on set of sleep or when laying down. I've actually been experiencing this for years and thought everyone else did too, but I guess not. Usually I experience image flashes (like a dream/picture image), as of late it has changed into someone saying words-small phrases or names. It will happen to me anytime I lay down/relax, anytime of day. Before bed it is generally vivid textured/colored images-these don't make sense, but are extremely detailed and beautiful. It is not scary or anything like that.
Is this good/bad? What's the deal? Any info would be greatly appreciated. Thanks!

### av8r — 2018-01-12T11:21:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypnagogic-hallucinations.20688/post-232308>

I experience that, I also experience audible hallucinations at the threshold between awake and sleep that can be frustrating to say the least. Any sporadic noise, including any snorts or snores I create is amplified in my head and startles me awake. Years ago it drove me to the point of getting a sleep study done… results was repairing my deviated septum… it helped.

# Thread: Happily overwhelmed!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happily-overwhelmed.20689/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BiuTze — 2018-01-12T04:54:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happily-overwhelmed.20689/post-232293>

Hello all, this is my first post!
This post is meant to be a friendly introduction, a way for me to focus my attention, and maybe get a little direction. I kind of just want to get the past week off of my chest too, I've been doing a lot of things and I'm very excited about a lot of it.
I've been in the middle of a really good healing process for a few solid months, with this last month really making me feel like I'm on fire. I got back from a really cool retreat about 40 days ago, and I've been doing LOTS of research since then. It started off with a documentary about the kogi people of Columbia, which I watched on youtube. I saw a link to Gerald Pollacks Tedtalk the 4th phase of water from there, got curious about NIF and IF lighting to try and produce more within mysef, and eventually I found my way here!
I've made a lot of purchases I am really excited about, one of which is a magnetico which arrives Saturday morning! (super excited about that puppy!) And these lovely blue blocking glasses I am in love with!
Anyways, I have the epi paleo book, among 20 other books, I've been watching podcasts with Jack Kruse for many hours a day.. and I'm a little overwhelmed by it all.
I was hoping someone could maybe point me in the direction of how to proceed.
I live in Minnesota, in the City, I work from 6am-2pm, but I can take breaks pretty much whenever I want as long as it doesn't exceed 30mns, so when the sun is out, I have been trying to get some of it as best I can...
Anyways, I guess I was wondering if anyone from my general area would like to give me some dieting tips, some ways to get around the fact it's almost always cloudy.. that type of thing.
I work in an RG Cooler, so I was thinking of trying to use that to my advantage and try to survive in there in a tshirt and shorts in an attempt to get some CT going..
I'm going to cut this short for now so I can focus my shorts a little more concisely later, thanks for listening!
I'm very excited to continue to grow and learn, I feel infectiously positive, and everyone in my life can tell..
I know this is the right thing for me to be doing, and my motivation levels hasn't been so high in forever!

### caroline — 2018-01-12T05:16:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happily-overwhelmed.20689/post-232295>

Hi ...and welcome! Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. some health history - also your Mom's and Gran's

Have you read the easy start guides? leptin reset? C/T?

what are you eating? what water are you drinking?

do you have blueblockers?

how is your sleep? libido?

if you can manage it - become a gold member and you will have access to all Jack's blogs and the monthly live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything.

Have you watched Jack's Vermont talks? These are particularly gold!

The youtube event with Jack and Jeremy?

Jack's talk from Mexico at his optimal event?

Have you thought of moving? You need sun! at least are you able to go to someplace like the Yucatan several times a year?

Jack is all about the three legged stool ...... water, light and magnetism

enjoy!

> Or are you saying the sun where I live is pretty much useless?
> This is my long/lat: 46.7296° N, 94.6859° W

Opinions vary.
Today the emphasis is mostly on deuterium.

But you cannot go much wrong if you
find out your haplogroup (23andme.com)
get latitude were your 5000-10000 years ago grandmother lived (that also comes with consideration for altitude)
stay there if you are healthy
go closer to equator if not

frequent trips to cenotes in Mexico are helpful
cenotes---> magnetic field, sort of better water but not really (but good to make RO water out of it).

..

///

### drezy — 2018-01-12T21:48:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happily-overwhelmed.20689/post-232380>

Welcome .

Good job with the kiniki bravery.
I think there is a point to get outside if it's, say, brighter than night.

Year round I soak up as much morning light as possible. In the winter I stay outside pretty much all day with an uncovered surface. When I venture into the grocery store I put on as little as the store requires(shirt and shorts). People gawk a bit but there's no point to caring about that.

# Thread: cheap blue blockers for elementary schools
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2018-01-13T13:50:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232409>

laptops are being used more and more in the elementary schools. kids there are not as concerned about peers as the upper grades, so i thought it might be a good place to start with them wearing cheap blue blockers when they are on laptops. i'm thinking of something like the cheapie 'glasses' handed out for free when we had the solar eclipse. couldn't they be produced and sold in bulk to schools? do these type of glasses already exist? maybe it could start out with teachers handing them out to students with attention problems (which is becoming a greater portion of the student population based on my experience). if teachers saw that the kids were better rested, more focused, acting out less, you can bet that teachers would start using and promoting them to parents like crazy. whatever reduces teacher/parent stress will catch on like wild fire.

### shiran — 2018-01-13T15:35:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232412>

The nnemf in schools is a huge deal , it must come from the bottom , from the home the family's , it won't come from the top of the pyramid, to much ignorance. I totally agree with your premise . We should lecture and spread this knowledge out to as much parents as possible to as much pregnant woman , teenagers

### taiyang — 2018-01-13T17:02:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232424>

shiran, isn't it true that in your country of israel there are restrictions on use of wireless in the schools? i'm looking for youtube clips and documentaries/news reports of how wireless use is being restricted in schools overseas. i'm going to put together a short google slides presentation with a focus on what is being done in other countries.

### Janet Shold — 2018-01-13T18:34:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232439>

We as parents need to work hard to educate each other about the dangers of blue light and nnemf. You are right, changes need to come from parents because school boards aren’t going to do it on their own. It’s going to take some work. Cheap blue blocking glasses would be amazing in schools.

I also think that wifi has no place in schools, and all internet should be connected via Ethernet. The problem is wifi is a lot cheaper, so budget conscious school boards have installed that in most schools.

My daughter has been struggling for years with hashimotos and Lyme disease. We finally got her back into school for grade 9 and she was rocking it for one month in the fall of 2016. She attended a robotics and engineering program, were there was a laptop using wifi for every student in the class. After a month of school she collapsed and hasn’t been well since. I looked at a map of cell towers and that school is surrounded by a high density of them. I’m just clueing in now about eemf dangers - thanks to finding Jack Kruse and this community. We just recently hardwired our home and disabled wifi.

Of course, the school is lit with fluorescent lights, as well.

### taiyang — 2018-01-13T18:37:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232440>

now we need to find a company that already makes cheap paper blue blockers. if these glasses don't already exist, no company will make them unless they think there is a good market for them. we also need to stop the onslaught of online testing.

### shiran — 2018-01-14T08:22:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232511>

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=232424)
>
> shiran, isn't it true that in your country of israel there are restrictions on use of wireless in the schools? i'm looking for youtube clips and documentaries/news reports of how wireless use is being restricted in schools overseas. i'm going to put together a short google slides presentation with a focus on what is being done in other countries.

Unfortunately our country is one of the most bombarded in nnemf due to military reasons and the Israelis are pioneers in the tec industry and addicted to tec , so do you think the government cares about emf in schools or fluoride in our water? The blue light is not in their top concern at all. There are some regulations in kindergarten regarding wireless but as the kids grow at schools it worsens . In my kids schools they use cord internet and wireless wifi with some regulations I can check for you the exact program for that. What I did in my sons class regarding the blue light in cooperation with the teacher, as much as she can use sunlight as here main lighting and we covered the fluorescent with a blue blocking, I think the blue blockers for all the kids is a great idea I'm not sure about the Responsiveness of the kids if it dost come from home..it will be a challenge

### taiyang — 2018-01-14T12:27:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232512>

if you could find and post links to any news clips or documentaries about what it took to get whatever restrictions on technology in your county, i would appreciate it. i'm doing searches too, but you as an insider may be able to find something that i wouldn't think of to search.

# Thread: Connected movement in nature
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2018-01-15T13:41:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-232593>

How do you guys feel when moving in nature? I there a difference if you are grounded or not, if you have you solar panel covered or not?
Yesterday I felt this weird presence of something guiding me. We were just short time outside at 0°C barefoot and upperbody naked in beautiful sunshine. As I would running over the grass it felt something was guiding me and movement became so instinctive and natural - fluid. We had a indoor course before with movement (lucky me not artificial lights on) and I could notive a difference right away. Completely other feeling in movement. Also if nature wants you to to do something. So strong I never felt it before.

### Inger — 2018-01-15T16:17:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-232609>

My most wonderful moving in Nature is when picking oysters naked on the ocean floor...

I feel just so in the here and now, such a joy and happiness, so perfectly happy and thankful being in the moment... I just cant describe it.... but it is as.. everything is just... perfect!
For me to be grounded and naked in nature is the best!
Somehow it shuts my brain down. I am breathing, my whole body is breathing in sync with everything.... There is no needs anymore, nothing you want or need or miss.... Pure magic
Freedom    Innocence... like a Child.. curous what beautiful looking oysters I will find the next, what crabs or fishes or birds or creatures I will meet...
Barefoot, connected, little or no clothes.. in the wild... That is feeling like... coming home
I am alone, but I do not feel alone, not one bit!

wow thats exciting !

### Christine_L — 2019-03-20T16:53:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-266009>

Has anyone done the MovNat workshops that they can share? The new weekly MAPS emails are great. I am doing a few exercise "snacks" daily rather than crossfit. I bought myself a jump rope and a kettlebell and use that at home outside.

### countingstarsx — 2019-03-22T03:35:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-266171>

> [Christine\_L said:](/goto/post?id=266009)
>
> Has anyone done the MovNat workshops that they can share? The new weekly MAPS emails are great. I am doing a few exercise "snacks" daily rather than crossfit. I bought myself a jump rope and a kettlebell and use that at home outside.

I love Movnat! Are you talking about the new series that just came out with Katy Bowman? Ive heard good things about it. There are a lot of Movanat videos on YouTube that I use. Katy Bowman's books are also phenomenal.

### Christine_L — 2019-03-25T21:35:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-266420>

I havent seen the new series! I am using the Youtube videos for now but want to take an in person class for tips and pointers. I feel like a damn turtle trying to get up from the low squat to standing!

### LieselK — 2019-03-25T22:49:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-266429>

> [Christine\_L said:](/goto/post?id=266420)
>
> I havent seen the new series! I am using the Youtube videos for now but want to take an in person class for tips and pointers. I feel like a damn turtle trying to get up from the low squat to standing!

You may like the BirthFIt videos. My chiro is the regional director in NC and she has some great videos on it. I do the functional progression outside when I think of it. It mimics how babies build the correct muscles to go from laying to standing. Super cool stuff especially because women are not supporting their cores properly for uterine strength and pelvic health. The functional breathing and just the first couple of movements have been all I've worked on for months and it's astounding at the difference in my core strength! I think a lot of the other types of fitness regimes do not honor womens bodies well- pre, during and post pregnancy. It's very empowering to embrace your body as a women and also build strength! I sometimes do heavy lifts too, but outdoor movement and BirthFit is a great foundation.

### Christine_L — 2019-03-25T23:21:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-266439>

> [LieselK said:](/goto/post?id=266429)
>
> I think a lot of the other types of fitness regimes do not honor womens bodies well- pre, during and post pregnancy. It's very empowering to embrace your body as a women and also build strength! I sometimes do heavy lifts too, but outdoor movement and BirthFit is a great foundation.

I totally agree! Will check out BirthFit, thanks for suggesting!

### Karen & Glen C. — 2019-03-25T23:51:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-266443>

My take on natural movement is 'your way' up to your limits and keep going.
I teach martial arts and the weirdest thing I see on the net is male instructors teaching women how to move like a male.

Its rediculous and completely takes away from the power of natural in motion.

Yeah sure take some tips and ideas but in the end its really just your gender and nature... 'shin-do' (let nature guide you).

"Letting go isn't scary. Holding on is".

### countingstarsx — 2019-03-26T19:30:53+0200

Have you looked for MovNat trainers in your area? Maybe they have some classes they teach! [https://www.movnat.com/find-a-trainer/](https://www.movnat.com/find-a-trainer/)

I have thought about getting a level one certification from them, and if I like it level two. I feel like it would be really interesting and be another way to have a cash flow doing something good for myself and others. The new series was just released a few weeks ago. I think it is $300 for 6 hour long classes. I have heard it said from those who have bought it that there are movements in it and correctives that are not found in any of MovNat or Katy Bowman's other works.

# Thread: Awesome movie for what doctor Kruse talks about

### Billybats — 2018-01-16T22:29:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-movie-for-what-doctor-kruse-talks-about.20710/post-232686>

Here is the trailer. Most of the talking is in the beginning and at the end and it isn't much talking at all. It is mostly if not all about him surviving his injuries. You have to watch it to appreciate it. You can't really explain it. I enjoyed watching how he experienced survival and life. My boyfriend was waiting for the revenge part of it. It was for me a really relaxing movie. Its about 2 hours long but very worth it.

You will enjoy it if you enjoy how they survived back in the day.

### Billybats — 2018-01-24T19:31:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-movie-for-what-doctor-kruse-talks-about.20710/post-233376>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/almost-any-mito-disease-changes-in-the-retina-first.20719/post-232816>

I was reading Jack's latest patreon blog where i believe he stated that almost all disease are tied to changes in the retina. if so:

1\. do those kind of changes in the retina always lead to vision changes?
2\. if the answer to #1 is yes, how bad to the vision changes have to be for disease to become apparent? since i've worn glasses from 5 years old and my vision usually gets worse every time i see the eye doctor, i'm particularly interested in this answer. on the other hand, there are people with better vision than me who already have disease. so how is that possible? it seems their retinas are more functional than mine, yet i am healthier than they are????

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shiran — 2018-01-20T14:46:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-cut-off-switch.20727/post-232957>

[https://en.geovital.com/products/electronic-pollution/circuit-cut-off-switch/](https://en.geovital.com/products/electronic-pollution/circuit-cut-off-switch/)

What do you think about this product?

### drezy — 2018-01-20T19:42:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-cut-off-switch.20727/post-232975>

As a (mostly) reformed Tech & Automation dork I'd stick to the simpler system of just hitting the breakers or setting up a master switch unless:

1.  The breakers are very difficult to access AND wiring a master switch is a pain/expensive/impossible
2.  You like the idea of having the circuits off even during the day (which is nice ) without having to manage it yourself

Automated systems bring a little more complexity and wiring/circuit prerequisites which may or may not be an issue for your home.

### shiran — 2018-01-21T15:19:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-cut-off-switch.20727/post-233045>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=232975)
>
> As a (mostly) reformed Tech & Automation dork I'd stick to the simpler system of just hitting the breakers or setting up a master switch unless:
>
> 1.  The breakers are very difficult to access AND wiring a master switch is a pain/expensive/impossible
> 2.  You like the idea of having the circuits off even during the day (which is nice ) without having to manage it yourself
>
> Automated systems bring a little more complexity and wiring/circuit prerequisites which may or may not be an issue for your home.

We are building a new home and therefore we thought to combine a convenient and easy solution. Do you recommend it anyway when there are no limitations? thanks Dan

### drezy — 2018-01-21T18:02:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-cut-off-switch.20727/post-233066>

Congratulations on the new home.

It sounds like the company may have field experts that check out your design and use a meter to check. If it all measures clean then it looks cool.

# Thread: Quick mods to a great pork belly recipe
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2018-01-20T20:54:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-232985>

In a previous thread elsewhere on the forum ( [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...erium-depleted-water.20394/page-4#post-232020](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-to-make-deuterium-depleted-water.20394/page-4#post-232020) ) I mentioned a great pork belly recipe I found online ( [https://deadspin.com/how-to-cook-pork-belly-which-thoroughly-kicks-bacons-a-1619788169](https://deadspin.com/how-to-cook-pork-belly-which-thoroughly-kicks-bacons-a-1619788169) ).

One modification to that recipe that makes it lower maintenance is to NOT brown the fat layer at first but do it at the end if needed. My oven may need temp calibration, but I kept getting semi-burnt and burnt top fat layers. So the mod looks like this:

1.  Throw some dry brine (salt, sugar if desired, and spices) on the pork belly
2.  Let sit in fridge for 1h - 48hr
3.  Put on a rack(to let dripping fall below) in a broil pan (image below)
4.  Place the broil pan on the middle rack in 325F/160C/gas mark 3 oven (I still use a tin foil hat on top of it for now)
5.  Forget it even exists for 90 min and don't even look it's direction
6.  Pour 12oz/350mL of water in the bottom the broil
7.  Forget it even exists for 50 min and try not to even smell how great it smells
8.  Hit it with the broiler at the end and watch to get it as dark or light as you like

Should look like this at the end:
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### Foxglove — 2018-01-20T23:49:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-233004>

Omg thank you! I just picked up a nice thick pork belly at WF today, and I was just about to ask for your recommendation on the best way to cook it! Can't wait to try this.

### drezy — 2018-01-21T00:52:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-233008>

> [Foxglove said:](/goto/post?id=233004)
>
> Omg thank you! I just picked up a nice thick pork belly at WF today, and I was just about to ask for your recommendation on the best way to cook it! Can't wait to try this.

The 2 hour 30 minute cook time is long but it's worth it.

I'm not sure if my oven is running too hot, but "crisping" at the end just makes it so much easier to make it taste as though you cooked it like a boss. Simplicity is something I've come to appreciate more and more over time.
