### Sheddie — 2018-01-21T15:23:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-233047>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=233009)
>
> @[Sheddie](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20386/)
> You tried the recipe from the site. Did your pork blacken a bit much? Help me be lazy and not have to calibrate my oven...

Hi. First time to do a recipe I follow it to a T. Except, I had pre-sliced pork, the long side, and had to stack it with the 'streak o' lean' meat showing on top. No, it didn't blacken, had a good browning on it's top; still not not crispy enough for me and next time I'll broil at the end, or drain off some fat early. Maybe your oven is running hot for the initial 500deg start? (Or my oven runs cool?!) The Mod edit looks fabulous -- and especially the portioning for more surface exposed to crisp! Good to know the beer isn't essential.

### drezy — 2018-01-21T18:45:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-233071>

> [Foxglove said:](/goto/post?id=233044)
>
> Do you cut the slab into sections first? Mine is almost 3.5 lbs. Last time I cooked one I did it whole, but it could have been a bit crispier.

I do, but admit that it might not be optimal. I like some crisp but haven't "done the math" on how much good (low D)fat I lose. On my next iteration I think I'm going to try to pan sear it first hopefully cauterizing in all but the top fat layer.

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=233047)
>
> Good to know the beer isn't essential.

Yeah I never bothered with that. Beer has always been a bloat promoter for me. Total beach body killer.

### River — 2018-01-22T16:48:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-233131>

Do you drizzle the drippings over the finished pork belly, or save it for later use?

### drezy — 2018-01-22T16:59:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-233134>

> [River said:](/goto/post?id=233131)
>
> Do you drizzle the drippings over the finished pork belly, or save it for later use?

I found that right then and there is a great opportunity to reduce the drippings and strengthen the flavor.

Keep in mind that D2O has a higher boiling point (aka bye bye H2O first) so reduction all the way down to just well flavored fats might be a wise strategy. Water can always be added last minute.

### caroline — 2018-01-27T06:06:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-233606>

What I do is even easier ........put the pork belly on baking parchment in a pan. Add salt and pepper and bake in preheated oven 400f for 30 minutes.
Turn and pour off the fat - add salt and pepper and bake for another 20 - 30 minutes. Turns out perfect and crispy everytime!

I just opted for stainless steel slotted baking sheets because they are a breeze to clean in a dishwasher. Maybe being a guy I have a lust for easy to clean steel.

Unless my son is helping I'm the only one that works in our kitchen. I once saw my wife chop an onion 16 years ago and let her know that I'd indefinitely be tackling kitchen duty (because I didn't want to age significantly before the first onion was fully chopped).

That allows me a bit of freedom of equipment choice. My new favorite is the sausage stuffer (as you'd expect it's a topic of endless jokes in my house now). Cascades of extremely immature innuendos aside, Jack answered a Q&A question for me that has me favoring house made sausage to protect the fats (versus house made bacon). I also get to choose my fat ratio. Obviously I push the limits.

Are you satisfied with your cooking tools in your new residence @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) ?

### caroline — 2018-02-17T02:50:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-234954>

### caroline — 2018-03-30T05:56:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-238296>

### freesia — 2018-03-30T07:44:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-238299>

Agreed it really doesn't need anything more than salt and pepper!!

Recently I've used thyme as well, because it's plentiful in my garden at the moment - and I love the smell of thyme cooking.
I also made one batch with ginger (juiced/grated ginger root), cayenne pepper, salt and lime zest, and one time I added sweet paprika along with the salt and pepper. I've been using a low, slow cooking method. As well as nicely cooked pork you get an amazing gelatinous broth with a good layer of lard.

### caroline — 2018-04-01T04:16:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-238429>

yes - I add tumeric too.

# Thread: Deuterium
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 63
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jamie Ward — 2018-01-23T21:59:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-233274>

levels came back 146 & 147 for Kate and I. Yet to see anyone with 'optimal' levels... makes me question the cignature health test when they are selling depleted water... has anyone had anything below 130?

### Susie Cahill — 2018-01-24T07:44:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-233321>

Not yet but my Breathy test from cignature arrived today along with my breathing mask from cignature that I paid US$130, plus shipping for. It’s got the brand name Humidiflyer on it so I thought I’d have a Google. Ironically it’s made in my home city Melbourne, Australia, and I could have purchased it for AUS$42!

### Tanya — 2018-01-24T20:59:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-233384>

> [Jamie Ward said:](/goto/post?id=233274)
>
> levels came back 146 & 147 for Kate and I. Yet to see anyone with 'optimal' levels... makes me question the cignature health test when they are selling depleted water... has anyone had anything below 130?

Yes.... Makes one wonder.... One way of figuring this out would be to send in a sample from a child age 10 or 12 (that is the the level of deuterium is ideal), but instead of indicating his/hers actual age, write one as - let's say 50 or 60. Kind of sneaky way of approaching it, but would be interesting to see what the test result would be

### JanSz — 2018-01-24T21:03:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-233387>

> [Tanya said:](/goto/post?id=233384)
>
> Yes.... Makes one wonder.... One way of figuring this out would be to send in a sample from a child age 10 or 12 (that is the the level of deuterium is ideal), but instead of indicating his/hers actual age, write one as - let's say 50 or 60. Kind of sneaky way of approaching it, but would be interesting to see what the test result would be

10 or 12 yo are still growing, their deuterium is supposed to be high.

30yo was said to be finished with growing.
Healthy, but "normal, average" 30yo should be tested.
"normal, healthy average" is to be determined, but it is not a gold medal winner in anything, not pregnant, not menopausal or post menopausal, male or female, nothing in between, ....
.....

### Tanya — 2018-01-24T21:18:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-233389>

>
> 10 or 12 yo are still growing, their deuterium is supposed to be high.
>
> 30yo was said to be finished with growing.
> Healthy, but "normal, average" 30yo should be tested.
> "normal, healthy average" is to be determined, but it is not a gold medal winner in anything, not pregnant, not menopausal or post menopausal, male or female, nothing in between, ....
> .....

I was basing this on a research done on body water turnover. Here is a link: [https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/jpa/19/5/19\_5\_207/\_pdf](https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/jpa/19/5/19_5_207/_pdf)

Until at least age 15, children show higher body turnover than adults.

### JanSz — 2018-01-25T15:27:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-233454>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=233442)
>
> I would say more accurately that some may be doing it right but not testing.
>
> Personally, I do not run tests but gauge how I am going by:
> 1\. How I feel (energy, sleep quality, brain fog or lack of such etc.+
> 2\. Sexual function (including night wood)

I am still waiting for my Breathy test indicating my status after 35 days of DDW use,
but after 22 days of DDW use I got significant break thru, that (more or less) holds to this day.
Today is my day #46 on DDW
\-----------------

I am trying to figure out some goal for my self.

As far as I can figure, CignatureHealth's goal is
130 ppm (D/H) on breath condensate (but this looks like a secondary goal to me because)
they also have and test for ATP-RPM and on my breath condensate my
ATP-RPM=16.4 (sub optimal)
but I was told that their goal for me is to reach 40.
I am 78yo, and likely they set a goal for me according to my age. But in my numerology over last 20 years, I was always trying to measure up to 25-30 yo healthy men.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) posted his results as 132 , but I would love to also know his ATP-RPM, actual and his goal.
Jack also said that he is on DDW for 12 years.
Would love to get some details on that.

In their introductory video, I think dr Q says that after reaching 130ppm body should (be able) to take over D management and only occasional DDW use is, or may be, needed (one month out of the year).
So it looks like at least one or two tests over the year are useful in gauging the D status.
\---------------
Breath condensate represents status of a whole body (composite).
As far as I can tell, CignatureHealth distinguish (liquids-water), (solids), and composite.
Liquids or solids vary on deuterium content.
I got burned with Coconut water (which I thought should have low ppm, thinking of coconut oil that is low)
When I tested (my) Coconut Water it came (very high).
When I asked what happened, got as answer: solids are low there, water high, combined high.
Do I really care that solids in Coconut Water are low.
\---------------
From @[WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/) I got:
Metabolic water∼115 ppm
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/deuterium-in-foods.20423/page-7#post-229480](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/deuterium-in-foods.20423/page-7#post-229480)
[http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/74/19\_Supplement/1426](http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/74/19_Supplement/1426)
names there László G. Boros, Gábor Somlyai
Metabolic water by complete fat oxidation, coupled with cytochrome-c, contains low average ∼115 ppm (parts per million) deuterium due to deuterium discrimination by plant lipogenic enzymes during photosynthesis.
but
they studied kidney tumors
so not sure if that applies to healthy body

\-------------------
Jack mentioned egg yolk as low in D.
Implies that egg white is high.
Eventually I would like to have that tested (on the cheapest eggs that I can find) (good ones have no choice but have to be better).
\------------------
.

### JanSz — 2018-01-29T19:28:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-233744>

> [Valerie said:](/goto/post?id=233719)
>
> @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) what are your break throughs you have observed?

I have BHP, Benign Prostate Enlargement.
8 years ago had Green Laser Prostate surgery.
They drill bigger hole.
After surgery first year was a hell, peeing every 10-15 minute, great urgency, few times did not made to the bathroom, wet pants.
Now I am better but ended with very small volume bladder and occasional but relatively frequent urgency.
I measure often what I pee, most often max is 250cc, but sometime 275cc.
So I have frequent nightly bathroom trips, usually 2-3.

Started on DDW 12/11/17/Mon
22 days latter 01/01/18/Mon I slept 11 hours, first time ever after prostate surgery
01/02/18/Tue
01/03/18/Wed
01/04/18/Thu --320cc
01/05/18/Fri---350cc
01/06/18/Sat---350cc
01/07/18/Sun---310cc

few days ago, after @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) posted video on salt I started eating 2 teaspoons of salt daily
01/27/18/Sat --- had to pee twice at night 325cc and 350cc
01/29/18/Mon---- today, peed 3x, 325/335/350

I hope that after some time my peeing will get to normal.

Overall I feel little more energy.

....

### Joseph Thia — 2018-02-21T05:58:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-235260>

My breathy test results came in at 148.8ppm. My version of CT is being in a swimming pool (about 27 degrees Celsius or 80 degrees Fahrenheit) and hopefully under strong sun during noon time. Waiting for the February webinar Q&A to learn more about DDW.

Jack mentioned before that if the results of other tests were ok but the breathy test results sucked, it told him that the environment sucked (which is the case for me in Singapore, one of the top worst cities to be in, based on Jack’s experience).

### karenr — 2018-02-21T15:37:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-235278>

Interesting that Breathy test results don’t seem to correlate much with overall health status - my baseline was 148.7, with other labs out of whack, and in a debilitated condition...while others who seem to be in better general health have similar Breathy results.
Thoughts on that?

### seanb4 — 2018-02-21T18:35:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-235295>

> [karenr said:](/goto/post?id=235278)
>
> Interesting that Breathy test results don’t seem to correlate much with overall health status - my baseline was 147.8, with other labs out of whack, and in a debilitated condition...while others who seem to be in better general health have similar Breathy results.
> Thoughts on that?

Yes, I would have thought that I would have relatively higher D since I'm ill and others who are healthier would have had more ~130.

I think because most waters are around 150ppm, and most people on this forum have not been drinking truely DDW, then the breathy / saliva is just a reflection of the water we are drinking and maybe the sun we are getting (people getting test in winter so not as much DD)?

Perhaps keto is DD TCA substrates but not the water that ends up in saliva / breath tests?

### Allin — 2018-03-05T07:10:41+0200

_So far I have only seen test results between 145-149 ppm. Remember different organs have different energy levels or requirements, different quantity or numbers of mitochrondria in them and different heteroplasmy rates or percentages._ _I wonder what your resting rate of ATP or efficiency is?_

_The food you eat, the water you drink, the quantity and quality of sleep you get, the amount of Blue light you are exposed to and the quantity and density of the various wave forms your body is subjected to and that includes when and how much sun you get. These are listed in the order of least to the most important._

### Ted — 2018-03-09T01:34:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-236732>

I'm not sure if this has been answered. Since many of us live in northern latitudes and have snow, would collecting snow and let it melt be DDW?

### WalterNL — 2018-03-09T12:37:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-236747>

> [Ted said:](/goto/post?id=236732)
>
> I'm not sure if this has been answered. Since many of us live in northern latitudes and have snow, would collecting snow and let it melt be DDW?

Relatively speaking yes. But it depends on your latitude and altitude to what degree the water is deuterium depleted. Higher latitude/altitude = lower deuterium.

### Ted — 2018-03-09T13:57:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-236754>

I'm at the 41st latitude. So, would drinking melted snow be a good idea? Are there any problems with it since it hasn't been filtered from the ground? Are people doing this?

### JanSz — 2018-03-09T14:13:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium.20749/post-236755>

> [WalterNL said:](/goto/post?id=236747)
>
> Relatively speaking yes. But it depends on your latitude and altitude to what degree the water is deuterium depleted. Higher latitude/altitude = lower deuterium.

> [Ted said:](/goto/post?id=236754)
>
> I'm at the 41st latitude. So, would drinking melted snow be a good idea? Are there any problems with it since it hasn't been filtered from the ground? Are people doing this?

This type of questions may drain energy and prevent meaningful activities.
They are used by advertisers pushing their (mostly worthless) products.
Indeed water from snow that melts on the roof should have a one atom of deuterium less than the water on the ground.

Because of that people may be chasing other waters that have 3 deuterium atoms less.

From discussions on deuterium my take is that the first water that is worth drinking when deuterium level is used as criteria is**
125ppm(D/H) and less.
And that is for people that are currently healthy.
ppm(D/H) goes down from there.**

Anything else makes no practical difference.
With that mind set, Icelandic or glacial or VOSS or Evian or similar are just as good as common tap water (except that they cost arm and a leg) when price is compared to similarly diluted DDW-25 ppm(D/H).

Yes, vacations, should be planned to places known for their low deuterium content.
But that is usually one two or three weeks out of 52.

...............
......

# Thread: Child started to stutter at 12 years old ... maybe after vaccine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/child-started-to-stutter-at-12-years-old-maybe-after-vaccine.20753/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sandra — 2018-01-24T04:43:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/child-started-to-stutter-at-12-years-old-maybe-after-vaccine.20753/post-233312>

I am wondering if there could be a potential link between a child developing a stutter and a vaccine. I have read the other threads here about stuttering and some comments seem like they could be of use but most seem to be for quite different stuttering situations.

My son was 12.5 when he received the following 2 vaccines through the school program.

-   Human papillomavirus (HPV)
-   Diphtheria, tetanus and acellular pertussis (whooping cough) (dopa)

He is now 13.5 and I have noticed that in recent months he has started to stutter. I do not know exactly when this started to happen, but I wonder if it may have anything to do with the vaccine. At the time of the vaccine, he did not develop any other noticeable side effects like fever, vomiting, swelling, etc.

He is seemingly normal in every other way ... sleeps well, does well at school, has lots of friends, is active (rides his bike to schools, swims a couple of times a week and plays club soccer), is confident, not allergic to anything.

When he stutters, it doesn't really seem to bother him that much but he does get a little frustrated at times, but more 'why is this happening' rather than 'I'm not going to talk anymore'. He does not stutter all the time but I've not really been able to correlate it to when he's over-excited, anxious, etc. I also wonder if it could just be a result of him growing and his mouth and brain not quite lining up exactly?

In any case, is there anything I could look at to remove the stutter? I'm wondering if I should take him to see a speech pathologist but if it may be a result of the vaccine, perhaps he needs some kind of detox.

Any comments or advice would be great, thanks


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: heavy bleeding in perimenopause- help!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heavy-bleeding-in-perimenopause-help.20769/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2018-01-27T16:56:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heavy-bleeding-in-perimenopause-help.20769/post-233625>

I have had heavy menstrual bleeding leading at times to iron deficiency anemia since age 42 (I am now 52 yo). i have controlled it with taking supplemental iron at times and taking aleve to cut down on heavy flow about 3 times a month. now that i am closer to menopause, i am having irregular periods and bleeding for like 2 weeks straight. i am going to make an appointment with my ob/gyn as soon as possible. i know what she will do- she knows i don't want the usual remedies like an uterine ablation/mirena iud/hysterectomy, so she will give me a hormone pill, which i also don't like. but since i am either on my way or already in an anemic state again, i will take the pill unless anyone has other suggestions. i don't want to take aleve every day as that has its own risks. searching the forums, all i found was one post that said:
"N-acetylcysteine
Calcium D-Glucarate
Pregnenolone 50 mg
These latter 3 have helped me completely cure my heavy bleeding (menorrhagia)."

### Penny — 2018-04-20T22:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heavy-bleeding-in-perimenopause-help.20769/post-239581>

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=233625)
>
> I have had heavy menstrual bleeding leading at times to iron deficiency anemia since age 42 (I am now 52 yo). i have controlled it with taking supplemental iron at times and taking aleve to cut down on heavy flow about 3 times a month. now that i am closer to menopause, i am having irregular periods and bleeding for like 2 weeks straight. i am going to make an appointment with my ob/gyn as soon as possible. i know what she will do- she knows i don't want the usual remedies like an uterine ablation/mirena iud/hysterectomy, so she will give me a hormone pill, which i also don't like. but since i am either on my way or already in an anemic state again, i will take the pill unless anyone has other suggestions. i don't want to take aleve every day as that has its own risks. searching the forums, all i found was one post that said:
> "N-acetylcysteine
> Calcium D-Glucarate
> Pregnenolone 50 mg
> These latter 3 have helped me completely cure my heavy bleeding (menorrhagia)."

I used to bleed like a stuck pig... I mean it was ridiculous - so I went to the OB/GYN for what you are describing and while there, they took an x-ray - the x-ray teck uttered those magic words "I have no attachment to my uteris" and there it was... all of a sudden I had no attachment to mine either... so I asked the doctor if she could take it out - she said yes - then I asked her if she could take it out by Friday because my insurance was about to end - she checked, there was an operating room on Wednesday - I checked in, she took out just the uteris and left the ovaries - keep your ovaries - I brought Harry Potter book 7 to the hospital, I read it and walked out the next day - I don't even think that was legal - it became my new religion - it was the most freeing thing in the world for me - I'm a swimmer so you can imagine what a pain in the ass it was - I don't know where you live or how your health is, but I seem to do fine having surgery - recently I read the Cortisol post and the DHEA post and it was another eureka moment for me - I started experimenting with DHEA vaginally - I just use this expired DHEA PLUS from Life-flo and it has been amazing - I mean brain fog gone - so much more energy - as for sleep, I use a dab of progestrone cream - I don't even think it matters which one you use - all of a sudden I can remember my dreams - sleep is so much better - by the time you're my age (59), I don't think we're making anything - if you put the DHEA in your vagina rather than on your wrists etc, it will make however much of estrogen/testosterone you need - you can do between 3mg and 12 and it seems like you get the same result - there is a youtube video from I think it's ZRT labs where they chat about it - I also just bought some pregnenolone - but they give you 50 mg - I have read the dose should be like 2 mg and you should only do it for 5 days straight before taking a "hormone holiday" - Ray Sahelian writes a lot about it - I'm going to have lots of fun trying to not overdo it on a 50mg capsule - the other thing that has been very cool lately is salt - in fact, I'm hacking an Ayuvardic black salt at the moment that has sulfur in it - needless to say, it's a tough hack...  Just my 2 cents - I also put the DHEA cream on my face - it's all in the blogs - just read one a day -

### taiyang — 2018-04-21T00:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heavy-bleeding-in-perimenopause-help.20769/post-239607>

thanks for writing penny, i appreciate it.

# Thread: Structuring The DDW
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2018-01-28T00:16:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/post-233649>

I have some DDW coming from Dr. Boros' clinic --

Are there some simple ways to structure and enhance the DDW?

Out in the sun with a Tibetan singing bowl to carry to blast it with sound waves and then to a dark steel container for the rest of the day?

### Allin — 2018-01-28T00:35:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/post-233650>

On the bottles of DDW you will receive, it states store away from direct sunlight. I do have the other Preventa DDW bottles (which are plastic), not in the sun. I have put some of this DDW into a glass VOSS bottle, put in the frig to get cold and then drink the DDW water while outside getting sun and yes the glass bottle did experience some direct sunlight. I drink some cold DDW and then jump in the cold pool for 40 minutes, then get into the sun for an hour while drinking some more cold DDW. That is pretty much how I structure my DDW.

### TANN — 2018-07-19T18:17:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/post-246204>

> [Allin said:](/goto/post?id=233650)
>
> On the bottles of DDW you will receive, it states store away from direct sunlight. I do have the other Preventa DDW bottles (which are plastic), not in the sun. I have put some of this DDW into a glass VOSS bottle, put in the frig to get cold and then drink the DDW water while outside getting sun and yes the glass bottle did experience some direct sunlight. I drink some cold DDW and then jump in the cold pool for 40 minutes, then get into the sun for an hour while drinking some more cold DDW. That is pretty much how I structure my DDW.

Hi Allin,
long time since we talked and i recall you were very helpful when i first joined the forum.
I trust you are doing well? I am very interested to know how you feel about the DDW and wondered how much you rate its performance etc?
Best Regards
TANN

### Allin — 2018-07-19T19:54:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/post-246209>

> [TANN said:](/goto/post?id=246204)
>
> Hi Allin,
> long time since we talked and i recall you were very helpful when i first joined the forum.
> I trust you are doing well? I am very interested to know how you feel about the DDW and wondered how much you rate its performance etc?
> Best Regards
> TANN

_Hey Tann, Good hearing from you. I would rate DDW a must in todays world. I would like to see your levels close to or below 130 ppm of Deuterium. Mine was 147 ppm. I drank the DDW, along with spring water for 4 months and my level dropped to 130 ppm. I had increased mental clarity, more energy and less joint pain. DDW is not a magic pill but a tool to help you on the road to optimal. Sunrise sun and grounding, more sun in morning, blue blockers at night, etc are also needed. The BIG issue I have is population density and I am looking to move to east Texas. For example; a couple years ago I was in Studio City and out in the sun and had a great tan and my Vitamin D level was 19. I said what?...19...Now it is in the low 30s but I doubt if I will ever seen 40 or 50 here in West LA._

### TANN — 2018-07-24T08:35:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/post-246562>

Good to hear from you Allin. Its very expensive so i dont think im going to be buying the DDW. It sounds very impressive what you achieved but its a shame that levels would raise again so quickly without continued use. I know people talk about re-freezing water as well but it seems that it doesnt achieve a considerable drop in dueterium.
All the best with the move to East Texas!
TANN

This may have been brought up in a prior thread, but I came a cross an ad about the HumanCharger. UV free full spectrum light to give the body a boost. Does anyone have any experience with it? [www.HumanCharger.com](http://www.HumanCharger.com)

### Sheddie — 2018-01-28T16:16:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-charger.20773/post-233676>

IF this passes muster, I'd have to be seriously intrigued since having implanted UV-blocking intra-ocular lenses (IOLs) after cataract extraction just 4 months before I discovered this site and began my journey with the 'new paradigm!' (Talk about 'timing!')

One of the first articles I found after becoming introduced to _Light, Water & nnEMFs_ indicated there is some clinical research on blue-blocking IOLs for older patients, especially when autoimmune conditions are a factor. (Too bad my, otherwise world-class, eye surgeon didn't 'see' that when he saw my medical records! I wrote him about it but got no response. I can only hope he considers this for future cataract patients.)

This quote is found on the How It Works page: Notice the light is 'UV-free.'

Start your bright light therapy

Simply push the power button and the UV-free, calibrated white light will start flowing through your ear canals to the light-sensitive regions of your brain.

I went to the Human Charger research site but I don't have the background to determine if the cited publications/references match to support the marketing claims. Thanks for bringing this up, Michael. I look forward to more consideration on this device.

### taiyang — 2018-04-21T16:23:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/billy-bats-susan-labs.20785/post-239641>

Hi Billy. non- doctor here. your c-reactive protein was high. were you fighting a cold/infection at the time of the blood draw? i once was fighting an infection when i had a blood draw. it is counter-productive to test c-reactive protein when you are sick. mine was 10. i tested a month later after i was't sick anymore and it was back to normal. a low carb diet/high protein can dehydrate you- that is my comment on the BUN/creatinine ratio. ALT/AST is liver function. one of yours was high. mine is usually great. it only went up the year i religiously took vita d and iron supplements. unless you know for sure a supplement is helping you, i would not take ANY. that action may improve your liver function.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-seasonally-for-my-lattitude-with-my-ancestors-indeginous-diet.20792/post-233881>

So my ancestry according to 23 and me is primarily of the british isles. I live now in Canada at the 45th lattitude and my celtic ancestors lived between 50 and 60th. So I would imagine it would be wise to pay attention to whats available in both locations. I found an interesting essay on the Dietary history of the Celts pre and post Roman intervention.I especially like the bit about seafood and seaweed. Enjoy!

### JohnnyAZ — 2018-02-01T16:22:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ehs.20795/post-233908>

i just finished the power grid webinar and the Q&A. If I am understanding, EHS (can’t remember exactly what it stands for but I think it’s electric hyper sensitivity) is really just a symptom of low electrons? Or is EHS a sign you have screwed yourself up beyond repair by being low on electrons for too long?

### brandie — 2018-02-01T16:37:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ehs.20795/post-233909>

> [JohnnyAZ said:](/goto/post?id=233908)
>
> i just finished the power grid webinar and the Q&A. If I am understanding, EHS (can’t remember exactly what it stands for but I think it’s electric hyper sensitivity) is really just a symptom of low electrons? Or is EHS a sign you have screwed yourself up beyond repair by being low on electrons for too long?

I don't believe there is "beyond repair" in most situations. I am/have been EHS, it's been so bad before I almost passed out in a book store that must have had the strongest wifi ever. Other stores have caused massive anxiety. My daughter often felt weak, low blood sugar feelings in stores. I don't know if it's a wifi or electrical problem.
Hydration was HUGE. I improved most when I started using red light therapy, before Dr K talked about morning red/sunlight.

# Thread: mito-job FYI...Census Enumerator--walk around outside and get paid!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-job-fyi-census-enumerator-walk-around-outside-and-get-paid.20797/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ValerieBee — 2018-02-01T20:16:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-job-fyi-census-enumerator-walk-around-outside-and-get-paid.20797/post-233924>

Census bureau is starting to hire temporary workers for field work. I did this last census. Pay is really good for the job. As an Enumerator I walked door to door trying to complete census surveys that had been unanswered or incomplete. People on this forum are looking for optimal and mitochondriac type jobs frequently, so I wanted to share. FYI the post says you use laptops, tablets and smart phones for the data. That is new since the 2010 census, but still worth checking out even if it's not a totally screen free job anymore. Mitochondriacs know how to maximize the safety when using devices.

[https://www.census.gov/2018jobs](https://www.census.gov/2018jobs)

### ValerieBee — 2018-02-01T20:24:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-job-fyi-census-enumerator-walk-around-outside-and-get-paid.20797/post-233925>

By [Erin Huffstetler](https://www.thebalance.com/erin-huffstetler-1387607)
Updated October 05, 2017
Every 10 years the government conducts a census of the US population to get a count of how many people live in each state, and to get a better understanding of American demographics. The results of the census help to determine each state's congressional representation and how much federal money each community receives.

The next census is scheduled for 2020 and will require thousands of census takers to complete.

Apply to be one of them, and you could enjoy a temporary boost to your income.

**What a Census Taker Does:**
Census takers go door to door to verify the residential addresses in their community before the 2020 census is mailed, and then later return to the streets to interview any residents that haven't responded to the census mailing.

A census taker must ask a wide range of questions which some people find invasive. For example, they must ask about each individual's ethnic background, marital status, income, and place of birth. Each of these questions helps the federal government to make policy and budget for needs. While the individual answers to these questions are confidential, there are a variety of situations in which it might be difficult to elicit answers.

**Who's Eligible:**
To be a census taker, you must:

-   be a US Citizen (or a legal permanent resident or non-citizen with a work visa and a bilingual skill that no available citizen possesses)

-   be at least 18 years old
-   be able to read, write and speak English
-   have a valid Social Security card
-   pass a written test
-   have a valid driver's license (if you'll be working in the field)
-   pass a background check
-   complete four days of training

**About the Census Taker's Test**
The Field EmployeeSelection Aid is a test consisting of 28 questions designed to measure the knowledge, skills, and abilities, required to perform census jobs.

The test is multiple choice and lasts for 30 minutes.

The questions you answer allow the Census administration to assess your ability to read and follow a map, accurately record information, do simple arithmetic, complete basic clerical skills such as alphabetizing and reading for information. You'll also be asked questions that check your understanding of concepts included in the Census questions.

While the test isn't extraordinarily difficult, it is challenging. Even college graduates can benefit from reviewing a practice test. Each state has its own testing procedures, so you may want to contact your state Census office for the most relevant practice test.

**Hours and Pay:**
Census takers generally work between 20 and 40 hours a week for 5-10 weeks and are paid on a weekly basis. You're paid for travel and training, but you must be willing and able to work evenings, weekends, and in poor weather conditions. According to the Census website:

_When you have successfully completed your training, you will be given several neighborhood blocks called "Assignment Areas". Normally, your first assignment will be the area in which you live. You will be provided a map of each Assignment Area and the necessary census forms and materials to do the job. Most of the jobs are in the field and require you to locate and interview households and record information about the residents. When you accept your appointment, you assume an obligation to stay with the job until your assignment is completed._

The pay rate varies by location, but it is safe to say that even a beginning Census taker will make more than minimum wage. To determine the hourly rate for your area, visit [Field Job Opportunities by State](http://www.census.gov/about/census-careers/opportunities/location/regional-office-careers.html). Then, select your state from the drop-down list.

**How to Apply:**
Call the US Census Bureau job line at 1-866-861-2010 to schedule an appointment to take the employment test. Then, print out a copy of the [Census Employment Application](http://www.census.gov/search-results.html?q=JOB+APPLICATION&search.x=0&search.y=0&search=submit&page=1&stateGeo=none&searchtype=web) and Form I-9.

Be sure to bring both forms to your appointment, along with all required forms of identification.

**Tips to Land the Job:**

-   Apply early. Hiring starts February 2019, and most positions will be filled by May 2019
-   Download and take the [Census Practice Test](http://www.census.gov/search-results.html?page=1&stateGeo=none&searchtype=web&q=PRACTICE+TEST) before your scheduled appointment
-   Clear your schedule for the period of time during which you'll be working, as a Census taker who's not available will almost certainly lose his or her job.

### ValerieBee — 2018-09-05T20:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-job-fyi-census-enumerator-walk-around-outside-and-get-paid.20797/post-250272>

General applications across the country open now: [https://recruitment.2020census.gov/Census/Account/ZipCode](https://recruitment.2020census.gov/Census/Account/ZipCode)

# Thread: Food for thought
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-for-thought.20798/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tanya — 2018-02-01T20:38:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-for-thought.20798/post-233926>

I am reading a book “Enviromedics” written by two doctors - Jay Lemery, M.D. and Paul Aurbach, MD. It provides so much information regarding the impact of climate change on human health. Let’s just say that book is very compelling. But when I got to chapter 11 - Harmful Algal Blooms - I wished I never came across this book. It kind of put a damper on my usual enthusiasm for a seafood. I guess it can do to you when they tell you about presence of cyanobacteriacteria that produce cyanotoxins that are highly poisonous to the human nervous system, liver and kidneys. It can accumulate concentrations potent enough to paralyze someone. They listed various shellfish poisonings as well. Got to go - my oysters are waiting for me.

### drezy — 2018-02-01T22:39:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-for-thought.20798/post-233929>

My wife has requested oysters for dinner 95% of the time over the last 4 months and said she feels great, has better sleep, more even stress response, and is now regularly asking me when we're going to arrange a little time alone.

The oyster farmers(?) here in Texas will discontinue harvesting while the algal bloom is in session on their own man made reefs. It turns out that they are experts and dead customers don't pay so well. Interruption is a pretty short period of a couple weeks here.

I hope you enjoy your oysters. I know we will this evening.

### Jonathin — 2018-02-01T22:46:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-for-thought.20798/post-233930>

Toxic cyanobacteria are fairly common in fresh water with high nutrient loading.

### Alison N — 2018-02-02T00:57:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-thoughts-on-my-genetic-genie-results.20801/post-233939>

In regards to your VDR snps - I recently stumbled upon Jacks thoughts... See below.. There may be much more on this topic but I'm still searching these forums.

_VDR Taq I polymorphism may be associated with susceptibility to Oral Squamous Cell Cancer......so avoid booze and tobacco and you should be OK. This SNP links VDR to NK T cell activation. I would also say I would pushes curcumin and K2 with this SNP. It is also associated with periodontal disease......so it is linked to heart disease too......if their HCY is up make sure it is not above 12. If it is they need expensive methylation testing done. Why the oral cancer link? Viral and heavy metal toxicity because when VDR Taq 1 is +/+.....it changes how COMT enzyme behaves. COMT is all about dopamine clearance. These two genes work together so the status of one will affect the other. When you combine the SNP's you can actually act more like a COMT -/- (not good at all). This is why further testing is needed.

When COMT is double negative or there are sequential SNP making it behave like you are COMT -/- it implies that the enzyme is working very well and is rapidly breaking down dopamine. This means you are using up methyl groups (BH4) rapidly and there is less available for other physiologic needs. This drives your HCY higher and it can down regulate immunity, especially T cell function. You might have to add methyl donors in supplement form for optimal dopamine and serotonin balance and for optimal methyl balance in the body.

In addition, since phase two detox requires methyl groups for heavy metal clearance you are more susceptible to toxic heavy metals in the mouth or diet and your viral/bacterial immunity will be altered. It will take longer to excrete metals and clear infection and this chronic immunity down regulation also leads to cancer/infections.

VDR Fok ++ = VDR (Fok-I) polymorphism is associated with the risk of bladder cancer and SLE (lupus) for many of the same reasons above. VDR gene Fok I polymorphism has been associated with SLE in the Chinese population. The SLE patients carrying F allelle Â± are more likely to have serositis and produce anti-dsDNA antibody, anti-Sm antibody and anti-Histone antibody, presumably as a result of down-regulation of VDR mRNA. So here you would need to see if you are making AI antibodies which are also directly tied to T Cell function. Look for lowered CD4/8 ratio's and elevated HCY above 12 or below 7. This SNP seems to have very different expression in different populations. It is bad in Asians but not so bad in Indians and we do not know why as yet. That is all I know........._

### drezy — 2018-02-02T01:14:26+0200

FWIW, I've been running an experiment on my wife and myself.

Instead of calling it a "defect" I'm temporarily calling it a subprocess of the sun's. It's just like when you send a process to a main processor then it instead decides it is best sent to a sub-processor.

Treating the sun as my sub-processor (to my "defects") has been very advantageous to me. My wife has begun to see it too and has arranged way more work from home (in the sun almost nude) days too. At some point I think there is the surprise that it's not "sub" but main...

### Mommy3times — 2018-02-03T00:09:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-win-emf-antenna.20804/post-234016>

My [**Tinnitus** always gets loud after I go into the city.](https://41012569.r.bat.bing.com/?ld=d3hl3sn3tP9ejuJvLhcBw2BTVUCUzW2A7JvOa6jOPcq6d2JWg4BvdMxOiIGk5DOpiCZH-tRiOBecyPEUAy_KQXY-nnRJAm-8OvF0lImEhM2REsho-HO5JmV3o8nSSIem4JQggp09ByOln5OeXawScEtYP4SxBPphUh6mI6CqPMV-i-zZUI&u=https%3a%2f%2flipoflavonoid.com%2fcontent%2f13-what-is-tinnitus%3fmsclkid%3d%7bmsclkid%7d%26utm_source%3dbing%26utm_medium%3dcpc%26utm_campaign%3dBranded%26utm_term%3dlipo%2520flavinoid%26utm_content%3dBranded) Now I avoid the city as much as possible and wear my hat lined with a thick tin foil..
\-I forgot the tin hat... bed time came and it sounded like I had a raddle snake in my ears. Waking 2 hours early, I laid my Left ear on 2 Magnetico pads with only 3 inches of height and I could hear the ring change pitch and gradually reduce about 75% to a steady hum (still louder then usual). Right ear, still loud and laid on Magnetico with success.
\- Last night Ear Left grounded 3" from magnetico again now its lower and more consistent then it's ever been.
\-I always have some hum at night in spite of grounding outside daily for 3 mos in very low measured EMF for about 6 hours/ day. (Meters show less then .02 RF, 0.3 magnetic field and cell tower 1.5 miles from house.)

Past 2 mos:
\*Using Vielight in my nose & randomly in ears(since it just makes the ringing louder).
\* Lay on the magnetico for 45 min with vielight in my nose, I often feel electrical wave behind my neck going to my spine. Is this a good thing? No EMF's measured and no electricity where I sleep.
\*Redox improving as my eyes have been too dry to even cry for over 15 yrs. No more sinus congestion

+3 mos prior- living in apt with 16 smart meters - 30 ft away
\* After CT, several times, I had the same electrical sensation, while shivering when walking barefoot outside.
\*New home- no "electrical" feel with shivers

+6mos-1yr prior- 1 smart electric meter , 2 smart water meters- 15 feet away
\*2x's on my magnetico, I had a similar, but more intense electrical sensation going from neck to spine for about a minute, couldn't move my body, wondered if it was a seizure. Intensified /painful, when I went to bed wet. Sadly, that spot measured the best in my house, guessing it was the meter pulling.
\*Promptly moved into a backyard tent. a foot of snow. Cold. No more Problems.
\*[**Tinnitus** disappeared on long hikes in Glacier National Parks. I'd be exhausted on the same trail, until it was muddy day. Inspired by Jack- I went barefoot and ran most of the way back. A week later, dry soil with shoes, exhausted...](https://41012569.r.bat.bing.com/?ld=d3hl3sn3tP9ejuJvLhcBw2BTVUCUzW2A7JvOa6jOPcq6d2JWg4BvdMxOiIGk5DOpiCZH-tRiOBecyPEUAy_KQXY-nnRJAm-8OvF0lImEhM2REsho-HO5JmV3o8nSSIem4JQggp09ByOln5OeXawScEtYP4SxBPphUh6mI6CqPMV-i-zZUI&u=https%3a%2f%2flipoflavonoid.com%2fcontent%2f13-what-is-tinnitus%3fmsclkid%3d%7bmsclkid%7d%26utm_source%3dbing%26utm_medium%3dcpc%26utm_campaign%3dBranded%26utm_term%3dlipo%2520flavinoid%26utm_content%3dBranded)

Finally a WIN until 5G arrives

​

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-grass-fed-meat-in-australia.20806/post-234026>

Just catching up on the deuterium posts & wondering why it was recommended not to eat grass-fed meat here?

### Brent Patrick — 2018-02-04T06:01:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-grass-fed-meat-in-australia.20806/post-234083>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-grass-fed-meat-in-australia.20806/post-234084>

### Brent Patrick — 2018-02-04T06:15:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-grass-fed-meat-in-australia.20806/post-234086>

> [Jenny S said:](/goto/post?id=234084)
>
> Read it on one of the deuterium blogs yesterday - can't remember which one

Unless we have context it is hard to make opinion other than Grass fed beef is superior to Grain fed as wheat is a C3 Plant and full of Deuterium Grasses are C4 and low deuterium.
Cattle that are raised on pasture are low in deuterium and higher in DHA but not at the levels you would find in seafood

**"Sinclair and co-workers were the first to show that beef consumption increased serum concentrations of a number of n-3 fatty acids including, EPA, DPA and DHA in humans \[[40](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2846864/#B40)\]. Likewise, there are a number of studies that have been conducted with livestock which report similar findings, i.e., animals that consume rations high in precursor lipids produce a meat product higher in the essential fatty acids \[[53](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2846864/#B53),[54](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2846864/#B54)\]. For instance, cattle fed primarily grass significantly increased the omega-3 content of the meat and also produced a more favorable omega-6 to omega-3 ratio than grain-fed beef \[[46](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2846864/#B46),[55](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2846864/#B55)\-[57](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2846864/#B57)\]."

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2846864/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2846864/) ​

**

### Sunny Cowgirl — 2018-03-04T06:50:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-grass-fed-meat-in-australia.20806/post-236242>

> [Brent Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=234086)
>
> Unless we have context it is hard to make opinion other than Grass fed beef is superior to Grain fed as wheat is a C3 Plant and full of Deuterium Grasses are C4 and low deuterium.
> Cattle that are raised on pasture are low in deuterium and higher in DHA but not at the levels you would find in seafood

My understanding is that deuterium in concentrated in the grain, moreso than the grass plant itself. Feedlots also feed a great deal of C4 grains, eg corn, sorghum.
A lot of pasture raised cattle graze on C3 grasses, think ryegrass, cocksfoot, phalaris etc. So the feedlot vs grass fed is not really a C3 vs C4 story. It's more of a grain vs green leaf story.

### Sunny Cowgirl — 2018-03-04T06:58:12+0200

Grass fed will still be a far better choice than grain fed anything in my opinion. Perhaps the secret is to get really selective about where the grass fed cattle are raised as, even in Australia, some areas have water with naturally lower levels of deuterium.
And choose the fattier cuts of meat more often so that we can do the depletion ourselves

### Jenny S — 2018-03-08T08:50:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-grass-fed-meat-in-australia.20806/post-236658>

> [Sunny Cowgirl said:](/goto/post?id=236245)

> Grass fed will still be a far better choice than grain fed anything in my opinion. Perhaps the secret is to get really selective about where the grass fed cattle are raised as, even in Australia, some areas have water with naturally lower levels of deuterium.

Where does are water have lower levels of deuterium?

### Sunny Cowgirl — 2018-03-18T23:56:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-grass-fed-meat-in-australia.20806/post-237467>

> [Jenny S said:](/goto/post?id=236658)
>
> Where does are water have lower levels of deuterium?

Cattle drinking water from glacial melt river sources would be one place. Geography and geology can influence but the only way to know is by testing the source water. Not much for any of us to go by yet but over time that information may be helpful. Depends how long it takes for the wider population to become aware of the deuterium issue.
Personally, I think it's something to learn about in time but there are probably more effective ways to help ourselves in the meantime as JK has been outlining.

### JohnnyAZ — 2018-02-09T04:23:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-panel.20840/post-234391>

I have taken regular hormone panels since I started following the advice of Jack and the forum members. At the outset my results were horrible...however (thanks to Epi-paleo and emf reduction) this time almost everything was within range...except progesterone and honestly I don’t know why. When I did a salivary test in August of 2017 I was 14 pg/ml (recommended scale of 12-100) and this time I did a blood test and I am .19 ng/ml (recommended scale of less than .15).

DHEA is in line and I do take DHEA before bed. I know DHEA can convert into other hormones but not sure if progesterone is one of them. Testosterone was actually up on this test, but not drastic and on the middle of the scale still.

Any ideas?

I realize progesterone is the hormone I know absolutely nothing about. I fell off Epi-paleo over the holidays so not sure if this anomaly is dietary.

### JohnnyAZ — 2018-02-09T04:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-panel.20840/post-234392>

If this is a dumb question...please just point me to the blog I can read for this.

### caroline — 2018-02-09T09:11:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-panel.20840/post-234397>

Don't forget that a test is just a moment in time .....how are you feeling? What does the Doctor in your head tell you???

### JohnnyAZ — 2018-02-09T15:17:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-panel.20840/post-234408>

I am feeling pretty good overall. I still get the brain fog in certain situations but definitely I am improving.

# Thread: Goodbye Akaloids
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-akaloids.20841/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2018-02-09T06:17:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-akaloids.20841/post-234395>

Hey all

I had an operation on my shoulder back in December and Jack told m to go full keto and have been doing that. I start my day by drinking lime water and continue this till about 4.30-5pm. I have been drinking San Pelligrino which i buy in bulk through a friend who has a restaurant, and add the lime to that. At first I was drinking too much lime juice (my staff were juicing 500ml's a day ) and all my lips ulcerated. But now i have toned it back to 3-4 dessert spoons in a litre of water and that had been working well. Occasionally I will also add a teaspoon of rosehips to a litre of water as well and sip that.

I have also begun spraying myself with a super saturate solution of Magnesium Suphate before i go out in the morning (6am) and go for a run and do exercise. This crystallizes on me and looks like i have dried sea spray on me, but once i get a mild sweat up (i run barefoot in shorts only) this then deliquesces.

In addition I have to give shout out to "Janz" and his heavy fat pork he had pictured on one of his posts. You inspired me to try Bacon after not eating it for decades. I have told myself that pork doesn't agree with me.....but ....after 3 days of feeling a little uncomfortable ...I have been totally wrong all this time. I now get a 'Guancialle' from a farmer in the mountains that lets his pigs run free and they don't eat grains. Literally there is hardly any meat on the bacon....its all fat! (see pic). So for breakfast I have 4 lightly boiled eggs and three to four strips of bacon (this is more warmed rather than cooked...i don't have it with any burnt bits). Holy Moly....I have to say this has been rocket fuel for my sex drive since starting eating this bacon. Thanx Janz

In addition i covertly sit outside and spread my legs when i read my morning information at about 8-8.30am and get the UVA sun on my 'jewels' and this has also been rocket fuel for the above mentioned. I covertly do this as I don't want to shock my poor house staff too much.

Also I have made a cool ice bath outside down the side of my house to do ice baths and also take full advantage of the Fournier effect. (See pic)

In addition when the suns not out i get 15 mins front and back (usually reading one of Jacks blogs )in front of my home made JOOV /IR light set up. I utilized the 630, 660, 760 and 820 5w LED's (sourced thru my office in china) and placed them in the array as pictured (you will notice the grouping of 620, 660 around the 'nether' region ; )). I can stand approx 30cm from the lights and due to beam radius i get a mix of all. I generally use this on heavy cloud/rain days or days when i have had long periods on the computer or in the factory to mitigate the blue light that is abundant there. I really notice the effect on my eyes (i keep them shut in front of the light panel...but i know the red penetrates my eye lids) as it totally rejuvenates them after getting zapped by the factory lights or screen time.

In addition I wanted to say a huge thanks to Jack for when he made the point about the pen light to the back of the knee shutting down melatonin in his webinar (re: melanopsin in adipose tissue). After this i TOTALLY blacked out all in my bedroom....not a pinhole of light anymore. Holy shit.....I have never slept like this in my life !!!...I now get 2 nights of sleep in one! I HIGHLY recommend this to everyone.

Anyway with all of the above new routine in my new house there is no need for alkaloids to keep me pumping through my rather demanding schedule during the day.

s

### caroline — 2018-02-09T09:15:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-akaloids.20841/post-234398>

great success story Simon - thanks for posting. How is your shoulder?

### Simon Wright — 2018-02-09T10:34:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-akaloids.20841/post-234401>

on the mend thanks Caroline...the ice baths and sun are prolific....each day a very noticeable difference thanks.

### JanSz — 2018-02-09T17:35:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-akaloids.20841/post-234427>

San Pellegrino when deuterium is concerned is as good as tap water.

Much cheaper Aquafina (made by Pepsi) is (supposedly) the winner (I will test it and post results).
But right now I have seen it posted by CignatureHealth.com as Aquafina (139.5ppm) (D/H)
No known to me natural water is even close to that.

Reverse Osmosis water that I was buying at Costco, because I thought that all RO's have an edge, turned up not so hot.( 149.4 ppm (D/H) Not bad, still better than Pellegrino, but not by much.

Stay away from Coconut (water) (156 ppm (D/H), it is deuterium (nuclear) bomb. Just the opposite to what Coconut oil should be.

Solids are often low in deuterium.
Water is often where organism dumps deuterium. (may be except some seeds/fruits).

Looking at ppm numbers does not give good feeling about differences.
They are much better seen when presented as δX values.

In your part of the word it is likely that you should thoroughly cook pork.

Why one needs artificial lights when he/she resides at equator??
But in your setup you are still missing the vital the 1538.5nm (almost pure heat)

lime juice ----(chirality)---- ascorbic acid

### shiran — 2018-02-09T21:47:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-akaloids.20841/post-234444>

That's amazing great to hear, can you please link that light pen thanks

Hi Janz

Noted on coconut water....thats a disappointment as I love them. The only point outside of this is that i get loads of sun.....like an excesive amount. I live on the beach and am there alot. I see every sunrise and sunset and come home three days a week for a ice bath at lunchtime.

As for the San Pelligrino....its a known quantity to me. The water that is available here isnt (and the testing capacity is not present here) . In addition i feel very good on it and have had positive physical responses when i add the lime juice.

I need the artificial lights because i own a garment factory.....it is fitted with CFL's, I work in town and I know the place is not the most healthy . Whilst we have lowered curtain heights to let more natural sunlight come in and also leave some windows ajar.....I used to leave work feeling frazzled. I do not accept the that after working a full day at work i should feel physically tired when i have only applied myself mentally. Since using filters on screen, blu blockers (i gotta say the new Ra-optics daytime ones are very good) and also the light panel my general energy has been impressively increased. I get what Jack says when he wants to mitigate his work place......this is what i do with the principles I have learnt here.

### Simon Wright — 2018-02-10T06:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-akaloids.20841/post-234475>

HI Shiran, It is mentioned in the January webinar I am pretty sure.

After a quick search on web...this came up on Ben G/fields site:

Ben: Yeah. Yeah, it’s really interesting. It’s the same reason like your skin has photoreceptors and if some, if there’s even like a little bit of light in the bedroom, it can disrupt your circadian rhythm. That’s why when I’m at like a hotel, my bedroom is already pretty good, but when I’m at a hotel, I unplug the TV and I unplug the alarm clock, and I just limit as much light as possible because they’ve shown that even something as small as a pen light on like the back of your knee can stimulate the photoreceptors on the skin and disrupt sleep, and it’s specifically artificial light, right.

# Thread: Hot versus cold water
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-versus-cold-water.20862/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Technosmith — 2018-02-12T19:23:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-versus-cold-water.20862/post-234622>

Hey folks,

In terms of striving for optimum and effects on EZ, how important do you guys think it is to drink your water unheated?

Certainly in a cooler climate, such as where I live in the UK, heated drinks/fluids are the norm and have been for years (as far as I'm aware).

Also, I've read some opinions that heated water may be more beneficial for healing, perhaps relating to the avoidance of the need to heat up the fluid in the body or due to it removing impurities during the heating process.

Opinions and experiences would be interesting, alongside the sciency bit of course!!

Thanks,

Phil

### brandie — 2018-02-12T19:46:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-versus-cold-water.20862/post-234628>

Water expands with heat.

Water shrinks with cold.

When you're talking quantum, smaller is what functions in our bodies best.

# Thread: supplements vs sun-pplements
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-vs-sun-pplements.20865/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2018-02-13T00:57:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-vs-sun-pplements.20865/post-234644>

i am confused about jack's view on supplements. on the one hand he says to be wary of the pill pushers- ie supplement sellers and that light water magnetism is what we most need, with dha from seafood for the battery..on the other hand he sells supplements and sometimes talks about needing supplements for certain conditions.

so i am confused. for members who have been around here longer, how do you sort this apparent contradiction? right now i am supplementing with iron since i could feel my body dropping into anemia again after many weeks of bleeding due to perimenopause (i have stopped bleeding now). when i drop into anemia, my tongue gets sore. now after supplementing for a week, it's feeling more normal, so i caught it early. my regular doctor won't give me a test for ferritin because he says testing once a year is enough the dummy.

### Penny — 2018-02-13T01:53:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-vs-sun-pplements.20865/post-234648>

You have a low redox...
[https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/)
[https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-24-are-myopia-light-and-aging-linked/](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-24-are-myopia-light-and-aging-linked/)
So if you read Time #21 and you wondering how ferritin works as a semiconductor you are in luck. Iron is an essential trace element that is used to form molecules in the body, such as hemoglobin. Ferritin is the major storage protein for iron. Free iron is toxic for cells because of its reactivity. So what controls its release? Its charge within the ferritin protein controls its release. Since iron is very reactive to sunlight becuase of its large amount of D shell electrons. Iron has a higher rate of emissivity than other atoms in cells and atoms with this ability readiate more energy than expected. When their emissivity is in the IR range of light, the proteins containing iron seem to be more energized. Emission of light energy reflects the intensity of charge movement in a protein. Ferritin is a solar active protein. Sunlight wavelengths can change when light travels through the atomic material in a cell or in blood. This change in incident light frequency is called a Stokes shift. The reason for this is when sunlight energized blood plasma solar radiation drives the charge movement which generates new electromagnetic waves that can also generate other electromagnetic waves that change the charges in their local environment. So when ferritin rises it is usually because people are not spending enough time in the sun. The body up-regulates ferritin production in this case awaiting its activation. Many people think that ferritin elevation is a sign of inflamamtion but it is actually a sign that these people live a solar spectral deficient life. If you lack sunlight, you have less electrons and more protons in tissues and this lowers the pH and this decreases the exclusion zone (EZ) in water. Ferritin is the protein within the body that stores iron and releases it through channels in a controlled fashion. The unique structure of ferritin forms a spherical shell in which the iron is "stored" as Fe(III) in a crystalline mineral. Ferritin consists of 24 peptide subunits that form two types of channels where these subunits intersect; the 3-fold channel is polar and the 4-fold channel is nonpolar. The residues that line the channels determine the polarity of the channel. Polarity refers to significant differences in charges due to the electronegativity between adjacent atoms in a molecule. The polarity of the channel link to the size of the EZ in blood plasma. The iron in the ferritin core is stored as Fe(III) in a crystalline solid. This crystal acts as a semiconductor inside of ferritin. The smallest repeating unit of the ferritin lattice is called the unit cell. The unit cell for iron is ferrihydrite. In the mineral ferrihydrite, every Fe(III) ion is coordinated to six O(II) ions. Oxygen is very electronegative compared to Fe(III). However, in ferritin, the mineral core has approximately 10% of the Fe(III) ions coordinated to five O(II) ions and 1 phosphate group. Phosphate is very active photosynthetically with sunlight. Most of the phosphate groups that are coordinated to the iron ions lie on the outside of the crystalline structure in ferritin, and are used to bind the mineral to the residues on the inside of the ferritin protein shell. When the Fe(III) in the crystalline mineral is reduced (added electrons from phosphorus or the charge separation of water by sunlight) to Fe(II) state, the iron becomes solvated in blood plasma and ferritin releases the solvated iron to tissues, in this form Fe(H2O)62+, through ferritin's 3-fold polar channel. In order for iron to be released from ferritin, the mineral lattice must be dissolved (i.e., to allow the iron atoms to break away from the lattice structure deep inside ferritin). This removal is accomplished by reducing iron from the Fe(III) (ferric) oxidation state to the Fe(II) (ferrous) oxidation state. In the Fe(II) state, iron breaks away from the lattice as the Fe2+ ion. The POSITIVE CHARGE of the Fe2+ ion attracts the electronegative oxygen atoms (ELECTRONEGATIVE) in water, and so a water "cage" forms around the ion. That cage is a Farady cage which shelters the iron from other electromagnetic waves. Hence, ferritin can control the amount of available iron in the body electrostatically, preventing iron disorders like anemia and iron overload. If someone's environment ruins the electrostatic mechanism expalined above for any reason it will effect iron status and ferritin levels in the blood. This mechanism is linked to presence or absence of charge in blood plasma or in phosphorus as a dopant. The EZ potential energy holds the key to what your iron status ultimately will be. Anemia is becoming epidemic in women and children these days for a deep reason: humans have created a low quantum yield world and this alters the charge of blood plasma which directly impacts the release of iron from ferritin.

The three-dimensional structure of ferritin is crucial to understand its quantum function within the body. In fact, the three-dimensional stucture of any molecule is critical in determining a molecule's properties and function optically. Hence, to better understand ferritin's role in the body, we must familiarize our self to different types of molecular representations to study ferritin's three-dimensional stucture. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3105608/](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpmc%2Farticles%2FPMC3105608%2F&h=ATPuubupVSAldE74JLl95g5n1c1ReQP7hZw9ItMpLm2TAn-1n6MoxP0ceEyqQ599ZStT7KHkrF94LUjiCDPwbfhcaV92JqakpLLocJD9m73mju8U_6dlbJassjQ_aFVnAaANVmg8-Ui3nMfZoJBq28BK0u2zb1pMLXz_xKZNEdGoXagzCYzVqXhLvG1m7R_pdKRJnHp7nZkagJyafAPproRRpElMBT9VhAHSbcNYihHAH-UxWIilgAQLEnWL9XEPdC38)

### taiyang — 2018-02-14T12:26:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-vs-sun-pplements.20865/post-234721>

Thanks for replying, Penny. "Hence, ferritin can control the amount of available iron in the body electrostatically, preventing iron disorders like anemia and iron overload..." This statement is the crux of my confusion. ferritin requires iron, no? if you lack iron, your ferritin will be low. am i correct? in order to boost ferritin, you need to not lose iron or you need to supplement iron. are you saying that i should not supplement with iron when i am anemic due to blood loss from heavy, prolonged periods? do you think being in the sun, grounding, and drinking deuterium depleted water is the only thing i need to rebuild my iron stores to their optimal level?

Some of the butter out there that many thought was 100% grass fed, is now being reported as not 100% grass fed... and may even have GMO's.

In that light, I'm wondering:

1\. What brand of 100% grass fed raw butter is everyone eating these days?

2\. Where/whom do you purchase it from?

3\. Why do you like this brand over others available?

Thanks for your participation!

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-poll.20875/post-235224>

### Martha Ray — 2020-12-20T03:20:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-poll.20875/post-293956>

I have not yet found a butter made 100% grass fed where I am in Central VA. The Amish near here may be a source but I've not found one to buy _**yet**_.

Michael McEvoy of _Metabolic Healing_ suggested _**Organic Valley**_ **Pasture Butter, Cultured** years ago and it was a past favorite. It's organic but not sure it is 100% grass fed.

Favorite now and I really like the taste is **_Lewis Road Creamery Butter w/ Sea Salt_** [https://lewisroadcreamery.com/pages/about-our-butter](https://lewisroadcreamery.com/pages/about-our-butter) It is from New Zealand and states 100% grass fed.

I also like _**Goddess Ghee**_ their plain Ghee, [https://www.goddessghee.com/shop/](https://www.goddessghee.com/shop/)ghee out of Asheville, NC.
And their _**Brown Butter Ghee**_ but it tastes too good to be healthy [https://www.goddessghee.com/shop/brown-butter](https://www.goddessghee.com/shop/brown-butter)

Before I found Goddess Ghee _**[4th & Heart](https://www.vitacost.com/4th-heart) Grass-Fed Ghee Butter**_ Himalayan Pink Salt [https://fourthandheart.com/product/himalayan-pink-salt/](https://fourthandheart.com/product/himalayan-pink-salt/) was the best tasting and easily available--says Grass Fed but does not say 100% grass fed and I've gotten away from Himalayan Pink Salt due to possible higher fluoride content

The subject of butter always reminds me of this tongue-twister thatI learned in college speech class. [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty\_Botter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty_Botter)

****"Betty Botter** is a** [**tongue-twister**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tongue-twister) **written by** [**Carolyn Wells**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carolyn_Wells)**.**[**\[1\]**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty_Botter#cite_note-:0-1)[**\[2\]**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty_Botter#cite_note-2) **It was originally titled "**_**The Butter Betty Bought.**_**" By the middle of the 20th century, it had become part of the** [**Mother Goose**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mother_Goose) **collection of nursery rhymes.**[**\[3\]**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty_Botter#cite_note-3)"

"Betty Botter bought some butter,
"But," she said, "the butter's bitter;
If I put it in my batter,
It will make my batter bitter;
But a bit of better butter,
That would make my batter better."

So she bought a bit of butter,
Better than her bitter butter,
And she put it in her batter,
And the batter was not bitter;
So it was better that Betty Botter
Bought a bit of better butter."

**Here's to Better Butter!**

### Dan2 — 2020-12-20T11:03:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-poll.20875/post-293959>

[@Martha Ray](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22506/)
Martha, look here
[https://www.realmilk.com/real-milk-finder/virginia/#va](https://www.realmilk.com/real-milk-finder/virginia/#va)
and if you find good cream (which might be more common than the farm making and selling butter), that can be made into good butter with a hand-crank or electric butter churn (or just put about half-full in a jar and shaken for a while). Butter made from raw cream especially is delicious, better than any stick butter I've tasted; if the cream is pasteurized before becoming butter or if the butter from raw cream is frozen it loses a taste that raw butter has that's especially good.

Butter made from raw cream especially is delicious, better than any stick butter I've tasted; if the cream is pasteurized before becoming butter or if the butter from raw cream is frozen it loses a taste that raw butter has that's especially good.

Better Butter is a goal of mine Dan2...Thank you for the encouragement.

# Thread: Brand new "super B" bulb just released From Solacure

**Subforum:** Biohacking 101

### Hbuttel — 2018-02-17T17:27:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-super-b-bulb-just-released-from-solacure.20891/post-234985>

The company is called "Solarcure", and their market is geared towards making light bulbs that help the marijuana growing and curing industry, wood curing, nail polish drying in salons, etc..

They have a brand new bulb release that seems to be a tad bit safer than their older version called the "flower power", which cuts out at around 275nm, and this new "Super B" drops out at 280nm.

"Super B" 34% UVB/66% UVA F32T12 bulb.

He is currently running a really great deal if anyone is interested, check out their Facebook page.

"Arriving today, and I'm feeling generous. Our "Super B" 34% UVB/66% UVA F32T12 bulb. These are on sale for $30 ea, but for our followers that wants to try them and give some feedback, I will make you a deal too good to pass up: a 4 pack for $80 and I will eat the shipping (normally $20-30), but only for US 48 states. Limit of 16 total bulbs per customer at this price. Msg me and I will give a special paypal account to send to. This is like buying for $15 each (half of my best sale price, 1/3 of normal), and I'm only going to do this for a couple hundreds lamps, so when they are gone, they are gone. Basically, I want the feedback and willing to put my money where my mouth is on these new lamps."

Here is the link - [http://www.solacure.com/superb.html](http://www.solacure.com/superb.html)

And a link to the spectrum chart -

### Hbuttel — 2018-02-18T02:49:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-super-b-bulb-just-released-from-solacure.20891/post-235035>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=234997)
>
> [View attachment 6736](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/6736/)
>
> Compare to Red Sperti $319

> [View attachment 6735](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/6735/)

Nice, I like the red sperti but in comparison it is a bit pricy for its size. It is 30%uv-b and 70%uv-a.
The total size is 25" x 14" and 100 watts.
Price 319

The solacure is 34% uv-b and 66% uv-a.
The bulbs are 4 foot long compared to 15" so you get much more coverage.

If you wanted to mount 4 of those on your wall,
That's $80 for the bulbs and $160 for 4 ballasts = $240
128watts spread over a 25" x 48" area
You would save $80, gain 33" in length, plus 28watts in power.

### Aaron Kelly — 2018-02-18T23:01:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-around-zinc.20900/post-235071>

So I have noticed a few times when I take Zinc that I get a bit disoriented, dizzy and anxious.

I thought it might have been just when taking Zinc alone as I had a few lozengers around Xmas and after 3 or 4 days I noticed the symptoms mentioned above. Today and yesterday I consumed a few fresh raw oysters but only like half a dozen and within 15 mins today I get the same feeling.

I would have thought the copper in the oysters would balance the zinc but is it possible to be very zinc sensitive. Anyone with anything similar or what to test would be great to hear back.
