# Thread: Just moved, slow adaptation to new light cycles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-moved-slow-adaptation-to-new-light-cycles.20994/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Imogen Skye — 2018-03-05T23:58:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-moved-slow-adaptation-to-new-light-cycles.20994/post-236369>

We were living in Yukon at 60\*N for ~9 yrs, and we moved to 48\*N 3 months ago. In doing so, we gained hours of twilight that do not exist in Yukon, and of course sunlight during winter that we didn't have (right now the sun time is approx equal to there, although it is getting to the 24 hr sun in Yukon, so it changes quickly). My 5 children and I have spent nearly a decade in a very extreme light cycle that messed up our circadian rhythms very obviously. Since relocating, we only need to eat about half of what we did before, have dropped nearly all of the supplements we needed there just to remain stable health-wise, and my children have been in a growth spurt since about two weeks after arriving here, all outgrowing their sizes within two months and now about to go up another size. They are already tall (and slender), but this rate of growth is unprecedented since they were infants. My eldest is 14, so they have all been at 60\*N for most/all of their childhood. I have a traumatic brain injury from a collision 3 yrs ago and still have symptoms of this that are complex, so I may include these in responses if it seems relevant.

My question is about sleep times, given whatever clues you can glean from what I shared above. We are 3 hrs ahead of Yukon now. It has been 3 months. My 12-14yo children are having a very hard time getting to sleep before 11pm, which has come down from our arrival of 2am sleeptimes But, 3 hr time difference to consider)! We still observe all the nighttime protocol for no screens and low-no light after sunset. My 7 and 10yo and I can all fall asleep easily between 9:30 and 10:00pm. But NONE of us can wake up before 9am- at all. Some days we even sleep right through the morning alarm until 10am! If we happen to get up, we're sleepy and sleep-drunk for an hour or two before we're really alert. I don't know if this is an adjustment period, if this is actually a normal developmental sleep time, or what to think about it. I get concerned because I would like us to be up at sunrise, but this is so impossible for me or them. I noticed that if I am woken at 6am, I feel fine to be up for the day, but if I do this, I need a 2-3hr nap in the afternoon. I don't know if this is healthy and just inconvenient, or a problem.

Some more info is that we have no other symptoms of unwellness that are obvious. The circulation problems we had in Yukon are resolved or in the case of my 14 yo, nearly- looks like he is about to done with toe infections for the first time in years in the next few days. I have been sunbathing daily for the past few days down to -3C. My children are outside everyday for at least an hour but usually several hours, and our windows are mostly very old single pane- atrocious for insulation and great for sunlight, or old double-pane also letting in lots of light and heat. There are streetlamps here, the closest is about 100 metres and it is a low-lumen LED. There are two lamps visible from my house. I live in a remote town, north of Lake Superior, cell service is spotty because the towers are far, and there is no industry here. I do not have wifi- only ethernet wired in (no gaming systems, just one computer and one cell phone in the home), but we do have a smartmeter outside our kitchen, and the mast for our electricity comes in the second floor in a bedroom, opposite the beds. I am suspicious of these and hope to figure out a way to deal with these asap.

Any insights very welcome and I appreciate them very much!

### Erin Gaiser — 2018-07-22T11:00:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-moved-slow-adaptation-to-new-light-cycles.20994/post-246400>

Im new here, but smart meter is terrible.....

Right now my kids are out everyday in just a swimming suit - but Im trying to figure out what to do when winter comes. How are you doing the sunlight in winter with the kids?

### JanSz — 2018-07-22T14:54:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-moved-slow-adaptation-to-new-light-cycles.20994/post-246408>

We were living in Yukon at 60\*N for ~9 yrs, and we moved to 48\*N **3 months ago.**

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Imogen Skye — 2018-07-23T17:55:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-moved-slow-adaptation-to-new-light-cycles.20994/post-246522>

Erin, we just made sure we were outside every day that it was possible, and wore as little clothing as possible. Then once it was tolerable, we started going out with our skin exposed. I started sunbathing at -3, by carving a "chair" out of the snow on my deck. I sunbathed everyday until it was warm, and now I do several times each week, but I am outside nearly everyday for many hours. Exceptions are rainy days that are also cold. If it is warm and rainy, I'm out still.

Of note: My children are home-educated, and I am not employed outside the home, so we have a flexible schedule.

### Imogen Skye — 2018-07-23T18:03:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-moved-slow-adaptation-to-new-light-cycles.20994/post-246523>

JanSz, it has now been 8 months and we're still adapting, haha. But much better than we were before. I realised that I expect adaptation to new things to happen much faster than is likely or even possible at times. Thanks for the reminder/confirmation! I was talking with a friend about my unrealistic expectations being fixed by this experience just before I saw your reply!  Takin' it easy now....

### kris90 — 2018-03-07T17:18:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trouble-with-blacklights.20999/post-236564>

> **[Blacklight Blue Bulbs](http://www.lightingsupply.com/blacklight-blue-tubes.aspx)**
> A blacklight blue bulb is a light bulb that purposely blocks almost all visible light from leaving the bulb, so you're left with only a dim purple (or violet) glow coming from the bulb. The rest of what comes from the bulb is beyond violet, or what we call "ultraviolet" -- wavelengths that are too short for us to see with our eyes. But they can cause items to fluoresce, or glow, and this is why we get a fluorescent effect from these bulbs, which are often used in night clubs or at parties especially during Halloween.
> So if you remember seeing your shoe laces glowing from a black light, this is why. And now you know that was really a blacklight blue light bulb.
> Since ultraviolet or UV light is infamously associated with skin cancer and potentially other health problems, it's worth mentioning that there are three types of UV light: UVA, UVB, and UVC. UVA, which is closest to the visible light spectrum, is the safest of the three, and this is primarily the type of UV light emitted by a black light. So occasional exposure to a blacklight blue bulb is not expected to cause any health problems.
>
> **[Black Lights](http://www.lightingsupply.com/blacklight-tubes.aspx)**
> While we typically refer to blacklight blue bulbs as "black lights," now you know that's not the correct term, at least in the lighting industry. Black lights, ironically, not only produce more visible light than blacklight blue bulbs, but the light from a black light is actually bluer. (The blacklight blue bulb's light is more purple, or violet.)
> The light from a black light is much brighter than that from a blacklight blue bulb, because it doesn't attempt to block all visible light from the bulb. These bulbs are perhaps most famously used in bug lights, or bug zappers. You may recall the bluish glow coming from these devices. Insects can see the emitted UV light and are attracted to it.
> Of course if you're looking to avoid bugs, [yellow lights](http://www.lightingsupply.com/catalogsearch/result/index/cat/951/lsc_base/Medium%20\(E26\)/?q=yellow) work best. So you can keep your bug zapper at one end of the yard while you and your friends sit closer to a yellow or "bug repellant" light. (These are just harder for bugs to see.)
> We hope this helps you to understand the difference between black lights and blacklight blue bulbs -- a somewhat confusing topic until you've seen the facts! Of course if you need black lights or any kind of lighting at all, we're here to assist by answering your questions and helping to take your order.

[http://www.lightingsupply.com/articles/black-lights-blacklight-blue-bulbs/](http://www.lightingsupply.com/articles/black-lights-blacklight-blue-bulbs/)

# Thread: Maybe this videogame plotline will become truth someday...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maybe-this-videogame-plotline-will-become-truth-someday.21025/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dbaverstock — 2018-03-10T12:07:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maybe-this-videogame-plotline-will-become-truth-someday.21025/post-236827>

**Second Disaster**

[EDIT](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster?action=edit)
[SHARE](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#)

_"I had hoped you might find this place – and finish our work. But it is too late. You and the Templars have squabbled over our refuse. You have wasted centuries. And so you have lost your chance. You cannot hope to stop the end now, Desmond. Only to survive it."_
―Minerva, to Desmond Miles.[\[src\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Assassin%27s_Creed_III)
[DIA satellite accident](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/DIA_satellite_accident)
[](https://vignette.wikia.nocookie.net/assassinscreed/images/5/55/2012_cme.png/revision/latest?cb=20131029224540)
**Second Disaster**
**Date**
21 December 2012
**Place**
Global
**Outcome**

-   [Earth](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Earth) protected:
    -   [Human civilization](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Humans) largely unaffected
    -   Death of [Desmond Miles](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Desmond_Miles)
    -   [Juno](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Juno) released from the [Grand Temple](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Grand_Temple)

**Affiliations involved**
[Assassins](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Assassins)
[Isu](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Isu)
**Participants**
Desmond Miles
Juno
[Minerva](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Minerva)
The **Second Disaster**, so named because it was a recurrence of the [Toba catastrophe](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Toba_catastrophe) that nearly destroyed the [Isu](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Isu) civilization, was a would-be cataclysmic event on 21 December 2012 triggered by a [coronal mass ejection](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/coronal_mass_ejection)great enough to devastate the entire Earth. Predicted, but ignored for much of recorded [human](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Humans) history, it promised to see the near complete destruction of mankind.

Ultimately, the calamity was prevented through the efforts of the [Assassins](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Assassins), who, guided by messages left by the [Capitoline Triad](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Capitoline_Triad), journeyed to the [Grand Temple](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Grand_Temple) that housed a device called [the Eye](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/The_Eye) capable of generating a planetary shield. On the day of the disaster, the Assassin [Desmond Miles](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Desmond_Miles) used the Eye to protect Earth from the CME, at the cost of his own life, and the release of a vengeful [Juno](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Juno) from the Grand Temple.

**Contents**
\[[show](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#)\]
**History**
**Predictions**
Following the [Toba catastrophe](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Toba_catastrophe), which saw the near complete extinction of both the [Isu](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Isu) and their human [enemies](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Human-Isu_War), the three known as the [Capitoline Triad](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Capitoline_Triad): Juno, [Jupiter](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Jupiter), and [Minerva](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Minerva), sought to help protect the world from a predicted second event. To this end, the three developed the [Vault program](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Temples), and built the Grand Temple to store their research.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)[\[2\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-Revelations-1)

After much experimentation, the three settled on one idea, but before they could bring their plan into action, Minerva and Jupiter learned of Juno's treachery and sealed her within the Grand Temple. Following the eventual extinction of the First Civilization, humanity passed on the warning through word-of-mouth, though soon it became mired in myth and legend – ignored by many as superstition.

Ezio informing his allies of the disaster Minerva predicted

In 1499, Minerva appeared to [Ezio Auditore da Firenze](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Ezio_Auditore_da_Firenze) in the [Vatican Vault](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Vatican_Vault) and warned him of the coming disaster, and how to prevent it. Aware that Ezio's descendant Desmond Miles would watch this meeting via the [Animus](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Animus), she addressed the message directly to him.[\[3\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC2-2) However, instead of finding the temples, Ezio and [Niccolò Machiavelli](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Niccol%C3%B2_Machiavelli) concentrated on unseating the [Borgia](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/House_of_Borgia)'s control over the [Papacy](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Papacy), and left his [Apple of Eden](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Ezio%27s_Apple_of_Eden) for Desmond in the [Colosseum Vault](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Colosseum_Vault).[\[4\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-Brotherhood-3) In 1512, Ezio viewed another message for Desmond from Jupiter left in [Altaïr Ibn-La'Ahad](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Alta%C3%AFr_Ibn-La%27Ahad)'s [Apple](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Apple_of_Eden_2).[\[2\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-Revelations-1)[\[5\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-4)

Even by late 2012, neither the Assassins or the [Templars](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Templars) truly knew what was about to occur, although the Templars had their suspicions,[\[6\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-5) having read Minerva's message in the [Prophet's Codex](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Prophet%27s_Codex),[\[7\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-6) and sought someone with Ezio's [genetic memory](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Genetic_memory) to locate his Apple.[\[3\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC2-2)

**Juno's machinations**
Before her consciousness was imprisoned within the Grand Temple, Juno disposed of the discs that were left behind by Jupiter and Minerva to guide Desmond, so that he had only her words to follow upon his eventual arrival.[\[8\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-7)

Following this, in 1769, Juno used one of the [Crystal Balls](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Crystal_Balls) to converse with the future Assassin [Ratonhnhaké:ton](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Ratonhnhak%C3%A9:ton); she urged him to join the Order in order to obtain the [Grand Temple Key](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Grand_Temple_Key) and hide it, so that his descendant, Desmond Miles, might one day find it.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

Then, in February 2011, the Assassin [Clay Kaczmarek](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Clay_Kaczmarek) was captured by the Templar front company – [Abstergo Industries](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Abstergo_Industries) – for use in their [Animus Project](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Animus_Project). Forced into the Animus, the [Bleeding Effect](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Bleeding_Effect) soon overcame Clay, and he found himself unwillingly exploring the memories of his ancestors without the use of the Animus, driving him insane. It was in this induced state that Clay happened upon Juno.[\[9\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-TLA-8)

Desmond killing Lucy after Juno revealed she was a Templar

Juno used Clay to pass on the word of the true nature of the coming apocalypse to another Assassin, Desmond Miles, in the hopes that he might be able to reach the Grand Temple and activate the Eye.[\[9\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-TLA-8)In order to facilitate this meeting, Juno later manipulated Desmond, who had reached the Colosseum Vault on 11 October 2012, into assassinating his close friend and Assassin colleague [Lucy Stillman](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Lucy_Stillman), and drew him into an induced coma.[\[4\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-Brotherhood-3)

Once freed from his coma, and furnished with the knowledge of what he needed to do, Desmond Miles and his companions entered the Grand Temple. After a month, wherein they found themselves forced to locate several batteries for the Temple, as well as the Key itself, the Assassins finally managed to enter the Temple's inner chamber, and on 21 December 2012, approached the Eye.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

**Release of Juno**
**Minerva**: "_Better the world burn than she be loosed upon it._"
**Desmond Miles**: "_Whatever Juno's planning – however terrible it might seem today – we'll find a way to stop it. But the alternative, what you want... There's no hope there._"
―Minerva and Desmond on the consequences of his actions.[\[src\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Assassin%27s_Creed_III)
Desmond sacrificing himself to save the world

It was here that the Assassins finally learned the truth about Juno, and what exactly saving the Earth would mean for humanity.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

If the Eye was used, the Earth would be saved, but the binds restraining Juno in her prison would be severed, and she would be free to take her perceived vengeance upon humanity. If Desmond let the world burn, however, the cycle would begin anew, where Desmond would act as a leader whose message would become misinterpreted after his death, leading to humanity repeating its history of war and conflict; but Juno would remain imprisoned.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

Ultimately, Desmond decided that the cost of keeping Juno imprisoned – the near complete extinction of the human race, followed by his legacy being turned to justify mass genocide – was too great, and that at least humanity stood a chance against Juno, following which he activated the Eye.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

The Eye then activated what was described as a "global _aurora borealis_", which shielded the Earth from the worst part of the coronal mass ejection, and ensured the continued survival of modern human life. In doing so however, Desmond sacrificed his own life and subsequently released Juno from her confinement.[\[1\]](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster#cite_note-AC3-0)

**Trivia**

-   The Second Disaster is based upon the real world [2012 phenomenon](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2012_phenomenon), in which many believed the world would end on 21 December 2012, at the end of the 5,126-year-long cycle in the [Mesoamerican Long Count calendar](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mesoamerican_Long_Count_calendar). Incidentally, a [solar storm](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_storm_of_2012) took place on 23 July 2012, missing the Earth as well.

[http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second\_Disaster](http://assassinscreed.wikia.com/wiki/Second_Disaster)

# Thread: Testing RF levels - help interpreting the data
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testing-rf-levels-help-interpreting-the-data.21033/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ObstreperousPolarBear — 2018-03-12T05:36:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testing-rf-levels-help-interpreting-the-data.21033/post-237025>

I'm a new member from Denver. I just bought an RF meter and was checking levels driving out of Denver, down route 83 (it runs southeast and ends up in Kansas). I wanted to share my results and ask people for help understanding them.

I am using a Model AM-1o acoustimeter from EMFields.
\- it does not give a breakdown of exactly what frequencies it is picking up
\- it is omni-directional
\- it can detect down to 0.02 V/m or 1 uW/m2
\- what units do most people use to express EMF levels? I will list my results in V/m.
\- basically, everywhere along that route is above 0.50 V/m, probably averaging around 1.0 V/m
\- this seems high, considering that the lowest detectable by my meter is still something like a million or a billion times the background EMF levels
\- I drove all the way to Elizabeth, CO, and saw little if any drop off in RF levels, which is shocking. It is a very rural area. There are horses and cattle - probably more of both than actual people.
\- That's more than 40 miles outside of Denver - yikes!
\- Are the persistently high levels likely due to military activity?
\- I did not go off the main road down side roads
\- I saw no difference with my car radio on or off
\- I saw no difference with my car on or off (it's a 2017 jeep wrangler, they are low tech, for those of you who are in the market for a new vehicle)
\- I did not see a difference with my phone on or off, unless it was actively transmitting
\- I did not see a difference with passing cars, pedestrians, etc. Readings where I stopped and parked the car were pretty steady.
\- in relocating, what level does Jack give an A? I am tired of moving - I only really want to do it one more time. I'll be testing the area before I go.
\- surprisingly, my home just southeast of Denver is actually fairly low in terms of EMF exposure. Tonight, my reader is not detecting practically any radiation. This afternoon, peaks were around 0.05 V/m and averages during the rest of the day were about 1-10 microwatts per meter squared. I'm curious what people think of that in terms of severity of exposure.
\- The area southeast of Denver is rolling hills/plains. Would people expect there to be pockets of lower exposure West of Denver, in the mountains? Aside from Lookout Mt, where all the radio stations are.

Thanks for any help interpreting this data. I appreciate it.

### Omelchenko — 2018-03-15T18:35:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testing-rf-levels-help-interpreting-the-data.21033/post-237251>

that's odd. I live outside of a major city in South Carolina. The area here isn't ideal due to a close cell tower and an airport a couple miles away. So, I'm doing the same as you and checking out property further out in the country to move to. The things that screw the reading for me are cell phones and the car. I have to turn the cells to airplane mode or off and also turn the car completely off to get an accurate reading of the area. If you have both those in check then I'd start looking for cell towers, airports, railways and broadcasting towers. [http://antennasearch.com/](http://antennasearch.com/) is an amazing resource for this.

At least a mile away from all but airports I read at about the lowest point on my Acoustameter. Either, the meter has issues or theirs something else going on in the area. My bet is something else. You mentioned military in the area and that could definitely be the culprit.

### Sun Disciple — 2018-03-15T18:57:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testing-rf-levels-help-interpreting-the-data.21033/post-237252>

Acoustimeters have the audio feature so you can pinpoint the source by sound. Im starting to get the hang of differentiation but if your in a super saturated area good luck.
if you scroll down to the bottom right of this page you will find example sound files of sources.

# Thread: Importance of EPA/DHA from seafood, NOT grass-fed beef
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-epa-dha-from-seafood-not-grass-fed-beef.21035/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-03-12T19:53:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-epa-dha-from-seafood-not-grass-fed-beef.21035/post-237061>

From Calories proper:

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/13840426](https://www.patreon.com/posts/13840426)

Quote:

"After adjusting for a plethora of confounding factors, patients with red blood cell phospholipid DHA > 8.1% had a significantly lower risk of all-cause mortality than those with DHA < 7.2%. _No effect of EPA._ "

For those who are wondering why there's no effect with EPA:

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23206328](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23206328)

# Thread: Cold after CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael F — 2018-03-13T14:03:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237103>

Hi.
I'm a new member here but I've followed the doc and the protocols for almost a year. With CT, I started with face dunks, then ice blocks and then cold baths 6 months ago. I'm doing CT 3-4 times a week, half an hour in 50-55F. I feel ok during the session, skin turns pink by the time I'm done. I don't shiver though and it takes hours to heat up. I'm cold all over, my hands and feet are like lumps of ice and my lips are blue.

Should I continue doing CT, will it get better with time - or is it possible that it doesn't work for me? Would drinking DDW water help?

### shah78 — 2018-03-13T15:47:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237110>

how much dha do you eat?

### Jason Coates — 2018-03-13T16:29:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237112>

Do you dress or shower immediately upon exit? I would suggest air dry or dry off and then get under a blanket or in a gown that allows for heat the build up around you.

### drezy — 2018-03-13T17:08:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237116>

FWIW I time my sessions as much as possible to get sun. Lately I'm getting UVi 7 - 9 sun right after a session, but I notice that even on cloudy days I still can't stand being indoors for a while after a CT session. Your "solar panels" are at peak efficiency cold so avail yourself of sunlight right after CT if at all possible.

### freesia — 2018-03-13T21:47:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237128>

Agree it helps immensely to get some sun during or afterwards!

What's your body temperature doing while your skin's exposed to 50 - 55 F?
...Mine rises - but it took a few months for this to be happening consistently and had to build up the duration slowly to avoid crashing, afterdrop, mild hypothermia and other various horrors.

### drezy — 2018-03-13T22:27:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237130>

> ...Mine rises

I haven't checked but I suspect it rises because I turn red.

> \- but it took a few months for this to be happening consistently and had to build up the duration slowly to avoid crashing, afterdrop, mild hypothermia and other various horrors.

Do you know or suspect why it took you a while? Was it thyroid related?

### John Saf — 2018-03-14T00:02:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237134>

I was also very cold after CT. No shiver, but very cold "inside". It lasted up to 90minuts and was not good.
But it never happens anymore, i think my mitochondria has become stronger.

### freesia — 2018-03-14T00:21:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237135>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=237130)
>
> I haven't checked but I suspect it rises because I turn red.
>
>
> Do you know or suspect why it took you a while? Was it thyroid related?

I started with a low basal temp and extreme cold sensitivity. I'd experienced this since early adolescence...pre developing ME/CFS. I strongly suspect thyroid, though I didn't have recent tests when I started CT.
My temp at first went down while in a 10 minute cold bath and it took literally hours to warm up afterwards. After about 5 weeks my temp was rising 1/2 a degree celsius immediately on exposure to cold. Then a bit further on I observed it was actually rising \*in anticipation\* of an icebath - ie when I was getting the ice out of the freezer and into the bath. Conditioning I guess.
From memory by the end of the first winter my temp (measured under the tongue) was rising .7 of a degree celsius while in the bath.

(My body temp has been normal since 2012. Woot!)

### freesia — 2018-03-14T00:25:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237137>

> [John Saf said:](/goto/post?id=237134)
>
> I was also very cold after CT. No shiver, but very cold "inside". It lasted up to 90minuts and was not good.
> But it never happens anymore, i think my mitochondria has become stronger.

Yep the brown fat becomes more immediately responsive in my experience. Magic.

I pushed it too far once - about 3 weeks in I think - got mild hypothermia and took 9 hours to come back to baseline. I caution everyone to take it slow.

### Brent Patrick — 2018-03-14T10:08:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237175>

**Inger certainly is an inspiration! But is it necessary to go to such extremes? I am not directly overweight and do not have any significant health issues that I know of. Or what exactly did you have in mind?
**
Extreme is really only defined by each individual and their perceptions ,as others have mentioned DHA ,,,has to be pre-loaded into the system.
Do you consume high fat /protein meal before CT ?
Bitter Melon ? helps stimulate BAT from WAT.

You may still have excess omega 6 the closer your 06/3 ratio is to 4:1 the better your response will be to the cold. You will need more Omega 3's for CT and a cold environment to provide proper cell signalling and increase fluidity in the cell membrane

Oxygen + DHA + Water =Paramagnetism = increases the electric and magnetic fields in mitochondria

Magnetic fields repel cold

I also think attitude plays a big part I actually get a dump of dopamine with just the preparation of CT then with execution, the thought of getting in the Cold gives me a high.

Hang in there Michael don't give up the benefits of CT are many ,you just have to find your n=1

And this ,,

**Individual difference in humans who cold adapt**
Special forces training has been at the fore front of this type of information. I have a few friends who are former Seals and Rangers. I spent a lot of time talking to them picking their brains about their training. Most of what they were taught and trained for is not published for obvious reasons. Here is what I found. Cold tolerance is increased by large body size (small surface area – body mass ratio), abundant subcutaneous fat, good physical fitness (good ability for heat production, good circulation), male gender (predominantly due to larger body size), young age (via muscle mass and circulation), cold adaptation and good health. **Recently it has been shown that also personality affects thermal responses, especially the levels of extrovertion and neuroticism. Increased level of neuroticism dampens or slows the autonomic thermoregulatory responses whereas increased extrovertion has an opposite effect.** [https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-5-biologic-magnetism/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-5-biologic-magnetism/)

**The cold PUFA connection to grounding effects**

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-cold-pufa-connection-to-grounding-effects.18433/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-cold-pufa-connection-to-grounding-effects.18433/)

You may want to consider scuba diving shoes and gloves ,this makes a big difference to CT.I personally always wear the shoes as my feet get really cold as well.

### John Saf — 2018-03-14T20:27:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237197>

It took me me aprox 6 mounts. Now it feels great during (not the first 30sec into ct), and after. In this time several things changed:
More seafood
More animal fat
Less nnemf
No bluelight after aprox 18.00
All the sunlight this place can give me in the winter (often with air CT) living at 62' N Norwegian mountains

### freesia — 2018-03-14T21:40:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237204>

Glad you've ruled out thyroid issues @[Michael F](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21174/)
Blue light after sundown will have your body set firmly to 'winter-resistant' but it sounds you likely have all that and a lot more under control.
The only further suggestion I can offer is that as an experiment you ease back on the duration and/or the water temperature and monitor how you respond to that. Training the body to re-warm (the thermogenesis part of the deal) doesn't go smoothly for everyone. In my case pushing too hard just resulted in 'post exertional malaise' which undermined forward progress.
I used a hot water bottle, and my dog, to warm my feet after for the first few months. And going into my 7th winter at this I still wear polypropylene socks in cold water. Believe me my feet still get cold enough!

### Michael F — 2018-03-15T08:28:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-after-ct.21040/post-237239>

> [John Saf said:](/goto/post?id=237197)
>
> It took me me aprox 6 mounts. Now it feels great during (not the first 30sec into ct), and after. In this time several things changed:
> More seafood
> More animal fat
> Less nnemf
> No bluelight after aprox 18.00
> All the sunlight this place can give me in the winter (often with air CT) living at 62' N Norwegian mountains

Thanks. So maybe there's hope. I have been doing this for 6 months. I don't appreciate the first few seconds either, but the rest of my session feels perfectly ok. I could stay in longer, I don't feel really cold, but I tend to feel bored. It's when I have got out that I see that I don't get warm.
I would suppose I could eat more seafood again - and less pork belly. Albeit I was hoping depleting deuterium through eating you animal fat would help.
I use artificial lighting as little as possible daytime and none between sunset and sunrise. In the evening I have my fire place as the only lighting at home.
nnEMF is impossible to avoid fully where I currently live. However, my bedroom is well shielded and I don't get any readings on my nnEMF meters there. When inside at home, I spend as much time as possible in there.

> [freesia said:](/goto/post?id=237204)
>
> Glad you've ruled out thyroid issues @[Michael F](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21174/)
> Blue light after sundown will have your body set firmly to 'winter-resistant' but it sounds you likely have all that and a lot more under control.
> The only further suggestion I can offer is that as an experiment you ease back on the duration and/or the water temperature and monitor how you respond to that. Training the body to re-warm (the thermogenesis part of the deal) doesn't go smoothly for everyone. In my case pushing too hard just resulted in 'post exertional malaise' which undermined forward progress.
> I used a hot water bottle, and my dog, to warm my feet after for the first few months. And going into my 7th winter at this I still wear polypropylene socks in cold water. Believe me my feet still get cold enough!

Thank you for sharing your experience! Perhaps I could try to make my sessions shorter. Instead of 30 minutes 3-4 times a week I could do 15-20 minutes, more or less every day? I like the idea of using a dog to heat up.
My feet don't really feel cold while in the tub, it's only that they and my hands are the slowest parts of my body to heat up afterwards.

### Jason Coates — 2018-03-15T15:24:43+0200

# Thread: Methylene Blue consolidated applications
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-consolidated-applications.21045/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AstralJam8 — 2018-03-14T09:34:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-consolidated-applications.21045/post-237174>

Hi all,

I've been looking for a thread that consolidates the applications of Methylene Blue / MitoBlue / MB and have only found a few mentions scattered here & there, so I'm thinking it might be a good time to ask everyone about their ideas and applications so far, and gather any questions or suggestions.

I'll get it started on something I've been thinking about trying with it. I want to use it in conjunction with PBM on a scar that I had received during my last relocation. It spans two fingers so I'll have a "control" for comparison. It has been several months since the incident so the tissue is mostly "repaired", but it's not too pretty.. My idea is to do a localized exfoliation using some Co-Q10 lotion mixed with Diatomaceous Earth to gently "re-activate" the wound site and gradually remove the excess fibroid tissue, then dab with MB and apply concentrated IR in the 650nm and 810nm ranges from LEDs, pulsed at ~10Hz to get the Mitochondria buzzing. Here's the article that gave me the idea about IR PBM "re-activating" scars:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3926176/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3926176/)

And more about the specific effects of PBM on skin fibrosis:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4848333/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4848333/)

When I get the LED rig setup I'll start posting more details and pictures and hopefully figure out doses for both the MB concentration and PBM duration (suggestions are welcome!) Hope to see your ideas, applications and results here as well!

### AstralJam8 — 2018-04-09T04:10:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-consolidated-applications.21045/post-239021>

Ok, sorry for the long delay, I ran into a problem where my 3D "PrintrBot" couldn't receive files bigger than 3mb so I had to reverse-engineer Brook's crappy cloud service before proceeding past the MK1 design

So here's what I've done so far:

Build the PBM prototype MK1
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pbm_mk1_800_600-jpg.7302/)

One LED per wavelength doesn't seem like enough, so I printed an emitter array for the MK2 design (all LEDs focus to a single point 1 inch from the parabolic dish)
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pbm_mk2_complete_800_600-jpg.7304/)
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pbm_mk2_emitter_800_600-jpg.7305/)

Add a TTL driver board, 3 channels (3x 650nm, 3x 530nm, 8x 810nm) controlled by Arduino
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pbm_mk2_driver_800_600-jpg.7307/)
And here's the pair of scars I'm working with (smaller one on the middle finger will be the "control")
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pbm_scar_test1_800_600-jpg.7308/)

Also, while reading Robert Becker's "Cross Currents", I happened across an interesting tidbit in one of the later chapters, in which it was found that **Silver ions dediferentiate Fibroblasts into Stem Cells**! So, I want to try this:

1\. Exfoliate with Diatomacious Earth in Co-Q10 lotion (should also re-activate the healing process via micro-abrasion)
2\. Apply ~800 millivolts with a Silver positive electrode for a few minutes
3\. Apply MB
4\. Apply PBM with 10Hz pulse rate for ~15 minutes a day

For the PBM, I'll have to experiment with the 3 wavelengths, either alternating the 530nm/650nm cluster in the middle with the 810nm outer ring, or all on/off. Looking forward to the weekly updates!

### AstralJam8 — 2018-04-15T04:22:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-consolidated-applications.21045/post-239419>

quick update on the MB observations. I've been trying to get it to absorb deeper into the skin, and I noticed that if it's applied just after getting out of the shower (while the skin is still soft & expanded, but not "pruned") it will stain much better and can't be washed off. After letting it sit while the skin dries & shrinks down again, I run the PBM for ~15min. and notice the fibrosis will redden, but also the blue will start to disappear. I've gone to sleep and woken up the next day with the stain still present, but then after a few ~15min. PBM sessions it's gone! My question is, does this occur because of the enlarged capillaries carrying it away or is the MB getting reduced in an elevated oxygen environment due to the PBM? Here's a quote from wikipedia: "Solutions of this substance are blue when in an oxidizing environment, but will turn colorless if exposed to a reducing agent."
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Methylene\_blue](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Methylene_blue)

If it is getting reduced, what is the reducing agent? What is happening here?

### drezy — 2018-04-22T00:13:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-consolidated-applications.21045/post-239664>

Have you ever use kicad or eagle and have boards fabed for you?

### shannoncorrin — 2018-03-14T16:24:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/airplane-mode.21046/post-237185>

apologies if this is a silly question. Does airplane mode really help decrease emf? Can I have my phone in my pocket to listen to podcasts if it’s on airplane?

### Sun Disciple — 2018-03-14T16:53:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/airplane-mode.21046/post-237187>

Yup thats a wise move. Ive measured on my Iphones and as long as you have location services, blutooth, wifi and cellular off your good to go.

### Omelchenko — 2018-03-14T22:09:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/airplane-mode.21046/post-237208>

if you still have to get calls, you can always turn off cellular data. You can still make and recieve calls but it stop your phone from pinging cell towers constantly. THen you only get the increased RF when a call is happening. I tested it with my RF meter. This is at least true for iphones

# Thread: True Seafood Allergy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-seafood-allergy.21050/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kai-Robin — 2018-03-15T22:01:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-seafood-allergy.21050/post-237261>

I have had TRUE IgE seafood allergy since I was a baby. I almost died from it as baby.

In my grown up years now it seems like at least some of the allergies are milder or maby gone. I can eat most shellfish without swelling up in the throut.
I can eat tuna but I'm haveing a small reactions to it, but small enough so that I dare eat it. All other fish I get stronger raction from, even handling a fish or to be in the cooking steam.

Should I keep on eating and try to train the allergy away, or could this be a dangerous game.

Any thougts suggestion. What should people with true IgE fish allergy do to become optimal ?

### Allin — 2018-03-16T18:30:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-seafood-allergy.21050/post-237314>

> [Kai-Robin said:](/goto/post?id=237261)
>
> I have had TRUE IgE seafood allergy since I was a baby. I almost died from it as baby.
>
> In my grown up years now it seems like at least some of the allergies are milder or maby gone. I can eat most shellfish without swelling up in the throut.
> I can eat tuna but I'm haveing a small reactions to it, but small enough so that I dare eat it. All other fish I get stronger raction from, even handling a fish or to be in the cooking steam.
>
> Should I keep on eating and try to train the allergy away, or could this be a dangerous game.
>
> Any thougts suggestion. What should people with true IgE fish allergy do to become optimal ?

_On the road to optimal this autoimmune reaction will turn off. You are not vibrating at the frequency you were designed to be at. Living in Norway you have to do A LOT of things right just to make headway. Can you get better - yes. Can you get to optimal - no. However you have made and can make more progress toward optimal: Keep eating as much shell fish as you can, eat as much pork and lamb as you can, STAY AWAY from fruits, drink deuterium depleted water everyday, get IR sauna everyday, do CT everyday, see the sunrise everyday in your bare feet, turn off the power to your house every night, use only candles, the fireplace and a kerosene lamp at night.....or move to 25 degrees latitude or less. Good luck brother._

### JanSz — 2018-03-16T18:59:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-seafood-allergy.21050/post-237320>

> [Kai-Robin said:](/goto/post?id=237261)
>
> I have had TRUE IgE seafood allergy since I was a baby. I almost died from it as baby.
>
> In my grown up years now it seems like at least some of the allergies are milder or maby gone. I can eat most shellfish without swelling up in the throut.
> I can eat tuna but I'm haveing a small reactions to it, but small enough so that I dare eat it. All other fish I get stronger raction from, even handling a fish or to be in the cooking steam.
>
> Should I keep on eating and try to train the allergy away, or could this be a dangerous game.
>
> Any thougts suggestion. What should people with true IgE fish allergy do to become optimal ?

Seafood is mostly about DHA and some micronutrients.
When I eat lots of seafood it does not cause me any problems,
but when I do Fatty Acids Analysis I find my EPA thru the roof.
So I limit my seafood part of food that I eat.

Why do not you get analysis and find out if you are actually missing on DHA.
DHA is available from many other foods, other that seafood.
DHA when in the body lasts long time (so it takes a while before one gets low on it).
Omega-3 fatty acids are strong when comparing to Omega-6.
Excess of O3 will deprive you of O6 that is also highly important.
O3 and O6 are not individual fatty acids, that are groups.
**You should monitor each individual fatty acid, as individual, not as a group.**
You can monitor your status as often as you wish.

May want to peruse my posts on this (short) thread:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/)

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

.................

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-seafood-allergy.21050/post-237588>

> [Allin said:](/goto/post?id=237314)
>
> _On the road to optimal this autoimmune reaction will turn off. You are not vibrating at the frequency you were designed to be at. Living in Norway you have to do A LOT of things right just to make headway. Can you get better - yes. Can you get to optimal - no. However you have made and can make more progress toward optimal: Keep eating as much shell fish as you can, eat as much pork and lamb as you can, STAY AWAY from fruits, drink deuterium depleted water everyday, get IR sauna everyday, do CT everyday, see the sunrise everyday in your bare feet, turn off the power to your house every night, use only candles, the fireplace and a kerosene lamp at night.....or move to 25 degrees latitude or less. Good luck brother._

### Kai-Robin — 2018-03-20T07:33:05+0200

Thanks for all the tips! I really enjoy the life here in norway, actually people are in pretty good shape over here consider the high latitudes. # Thread: Can melting ice deplete deuterium?

### kris90 — 2018-03-17T17:36:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melting-ice-deplete-deuterium.21057/post-237365>

In terms of freezing water, we can dump the frozen layer and the remaining liquid should be relatively lower in deuterium. My understanding of how this occurs is that cooling water increases the EZ, and larger EZ = excludes protons including deuterium.

Now, what if you put a bucket of ice cubes out in the sun and let it melt? Would the melted water be deuterium depleted in a similar manner because sunlight builds the EZ? Wondering if this might be a good hack using ice cubes that come from my natural groundwater.

### Jonathin — 2018-03-17T18:00:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melting-ice-deplete-deuterium.21057/post-237366>

Last thing left is low test and libido which I have been agressivley Cting to offset symptoms. 3 weeks ago one of my more peculiar symptoms popped back up. I used to get this stiffening feeling in my mid back area just below my ribs. It seemd to happen after either working out or coming in contact with a stressfull situation. Its never painfull it just gets stifff and hard in that area and seems to calm down when i sit or lie down. # Thread: Yummy Uyghur skewers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-uyghur-skewers.21076/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2018-03-22T20:46:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-uyghur-skewers.21076/post-237764>

Every spring I start remembering eating the interesting [Uyghur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uyghurs) ethnic food when I was in China's capitol and it was yummy. Since it was being served in the Capitol it was one ethnic group's take on another ethnic group's food.

I whipped up some of these on a trial run and will be making them for dinner with the below edits. You may want to tone down the red pepper flakes to whatever your spicy sensibility level:

[https://www.seriouseats.com/recipes/2015/05/spicy-lamb-skewers-chinese-street-food-recipe.html](https://www.seriouseats.com/recipes/2015/05/spicy-lamb-skewers-chinese-street-food-recipe.html)

Edits to the recipe:
Fatty Pork shoulder substituted for Lamb (it fits right in)

Added 1 t powdered Fenugreek to dry rub
Added 2 t turmeric to dry rub

Social implications:
I have this [cheap tabletop grill](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B06XSJ7C7P/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o02_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1) we all sit and talk and tend to our own skewers. I like this so much more than having one cook running around like an idiot(aka me usually). There is something about having a little fire near you and a swirl of cooking food smells while we all wait that seems to encourage more discussion and just a plain old happier family.

### caroline — 2018-03-25T05:25:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-uyghur-skewers.21076/post-237918>

Looks so good - thanks Dan.

You sit this table top grill on your table? seems like a stupid question I know ..........but I would worry about harming our table......

### drezy — 2018-03-25T16:02:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-uyghur-skewers.21076/post-237930>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=237918)
>
> Looks so good - thanks Dan.
>
> You sit this table top grill on your table? seems like a stupid question I know ..........but I would worry about harming our table......

FWIW I think the recipe mentioned by @[John Saf](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20790/) is more optimal in cooking technique:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/#post-237367](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/#post-237367)

It's just once in a while in spring I just like to reproduce one of the best street foods I ever had.

The grill I have sits 5"(12+cm) above the table and I placed a small oven/smoker rack wrapped in foil right under the grill. It's seems to reflect the heat away from the table well. If it ever proves insufficient I'll get some metal plating. There is something nice about cooking together in the evening with a little hot infrared bathing everyones face as they cook. I can feel any tension in my own face get totally relaxed after a few minutes.

Maybe when putting my best face forward to my family it's a infrared heated face that's best...

# Thread: 5G Shielded Clothing: Is Lead Stupid Or Smart?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-shielded-clothing-is-lead-stupid-or-smart.21082/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2018-03-23T05:40:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-shielded-clothing-is-lead-stupid-or-smart.21082/post-237803>

Something like this:

[https://www.universalmedicalinc.com/quickship-lightweight-lead-flex-guard-apron.html](https://www.universalmedicalinc.com/quickship-lightweight-lead-flex-guard-apron.html)

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-03-28T19:16:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-shielded-clothing-is-lead-stupid-or-smart.21082/post-238131>

Adam,

I don't know if we have to resort to lead garments for 5G shielding. That doesn't make sense to me, unless you know something I don't.

I know that 5G wave forms will be in the millimeter size range, and if someone here knows if existing shielding materials and garment (made of net-like mesh of silver impregnated fabric) will have small enough mesh holes to block these-- please let us all know.

There is some debate about the safety of reflective shielding fabrics in the first place. I can imagine that if any RF EMF gets past or through shielding this reflective material would keep EMF radiation bouncing around your body for a while until absorbed fully by body tissue.

### Skylife — 2018-11-07T23:54:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-shielded-clothing-is-lead-stupid-or-smart.21082/post-255190>

ElectricUniverse, i ve been wondering about the same thing - will any shielding fabric in the market like swissshield or aaronia be able to shield against the new mm waves of 5G? I think aaronia is tested for higher frequencies but maybe up to 20 or max 30 Ghz. I also just heard Dr Klinghardt in interview with Mercola talk about him recommending t-shirt and underwear with silver thread for EMF protection for his patients and that it really helps. At the same time testing by Geovital confirmed that eEMF protective clothing doesn't do a thing currently (in our 4G world) for shielding because they test the body absorption of microwave radiation considering it the best measurement of how much a body is affected by RF. I really would love to have some shielding option for the night as i live in an apartment building with many wifi from neighbours and smart meters and have been searching for something that has a chance of working in 5G world? the worst part is that we can't even measure if any of these fabrics are making a difference any more... So i wonder will all those people who spent thousand of dollars on shielding canopies, sleeping bags, tents etc will have to throw them away in 5G world?

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-11-08T01:23:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-shielded-clothing-is-lead-stupid-or-smart.21082/post-255191>

Skylife,

Shielding is currently a conundrum with 5G type RF radiation switching on all over.

I understand that 5G millimeter waves will be lit first up in population dense areas like downtowns initially (so urban hipsters who love their urban high rise loft apartments beware-- they will be fried for sure). 5G mm waves will probably be activated later in suburban and rural areas as soon as they have infrastructure in place.

So we don't know if current shielding products (such as Swiss shield fabrics) will provide any protection against the much higher frequencies and mm waves until testing is done with 5G-aware RF meters. My guess is they will be mostly ineffective and obsolete.

Yes, I watched the Dr Klinghardt video where he mentioned recommending shielding clothing for his patients. Interestingly, expectant mothers in China have been wearing such garments for decades, ostensibly to protect the unborn children from microwaves. I am not convinced this is a good idea because if you place your body in a RF reflective covering you risk amplifying any RF signals that slip through somewhere.

Dr Kruse hinted that the Farm is working on some breakthrough RF protective products for a 5G world, and I hope shielding fabrics is one of them.

### Skylife — 2018-11-08T04:26:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-shielded-clothing-is-lead-stupid-or-smart.21082/post-255210>

I know the worst thing is that for regular folks its kind of impossible to figure out what will work. its also impossible to even measure if they are affecting one currently as everywhere i inquire nobody seems to have a meter to test the 5G frequencies and exposure. Have you heard when such meter might be available to general public?
He did mention Chinese pregnant women do use protection and there are different wraps and covers on the market that are supposed to protect but i wonder if anyone else apart from Geovital tried measuring what impact those garments have on the body?
Geovital is interesting in their approach as they don't think testing the air (which most instruments do) is a true measure of body burden. They use a special meter - The HF Field Probe that goes up to 8Ghz only (will be obsolete now i guess with 5G) that measures the absorbtion of radiation by the body, which they consider a true measurement of RF burden. This testing actually showed that when one is wearing a shielding garment the body is still acting as an antenna absorbing radiation significantly, sometimes even a bit more than without the garment.
I would imagine that if one was to wear something like a sleeping bag that zips all over and has no leakage of RF, then one would be protected kind of like in a bed canopy (but of course that was in the past up to 4G).But if it's only a part of the body thats shielded which is always the case when one is out in the world then it seems like it may not be a good idea to wear such garments? But then again many report they feel so much better just wearing a cap with silver thread inside (for instance the study by Dr Trevor Marshall who had their patients with autoimmune wear and sleep in a shielding cap for some hours every day and 90% of them had significant improvement). Is that placebo effect? Why was it helping them?

To me all of this is very confusing and I also heard Dr Kruse mention the amplification and jump conduction that may be happening with shielding garments. I also wonder that now with mm waves coming the fabrics likely have to be very tightly woven and have to be all silver? so many questions and not many answers...

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-11-08T17:33:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-shielded-clothing-is-lead-stupid-or-smart.21082/post-255245>

Yes, the nnEMF environment is rapidly changing and for the worse, and it's a challenge for us Kruse Swans to adapt, adjust, and hopefully thrive and not just barely survive in this toxic man-made world.

Dr Mercola has mentioned that when he travels he takes along a special shielding cocoon sleeping bag for protection, and he reports it really helps with RF exposure in hotels.

Also, extremely sensitive EHS patients have been known to resort to full body shielding garments when they must leave the house (think a head to toe type full body covering like a burqa).

My hope is that the Farm or another research and development center will develop a shielding fabric that is absorptive not reflective in shielding against RF. Something perhaps along the lines of a nano carbon material.

### Dean6789 — 2018-03-29T06:29:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/california-proposition-65-warning.21106/post-238189>

I received a bag of whole organic rosemary leaf in the mail. Origin: Morocco

I called the supplier, Starwest Botanicals and they told me that it was because some soil has more lead than other soil and California required this warning sticker on the package. Naturally, Starwest said that the lead was at a "safe level".

Do you think the herb is safe to eat or would you throw it out?

### Karlos Amigos — 2018-03-29T20:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/california-proposition-65-warning.21106/post-238250>

Don't throw it out! I live in CA and buy a lot of herbs on a regular basis from various suppliers. I have never received a shipment that didn't have this warning on it. The California standards for lead are much higher than the federal standards and i believe this prop 65 statement is a CYA move to avoid potential lawsuits. The same product is sold in other states without the warning.

### Greenie — 2018-03-30T10:25:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-exposure-potential-vs-nnemf-in-cars.21118/post-238302>

The triumvirate of light, water, and magnetism are all interconnected; however, when it comes to selecting an automobile, does having a convertible that allows more exposure to the sun better than having a low nnEMF vehicle without the option of letting in the sun other than by rolling down the windows?

(I should add that I am not talking about electric cars that produce copious amounts of nnEMF.)

### Trev Douglas — 2018-04-04T15:12:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-exposure-potential-vs-nnemf-in-cars.21118/post-238668>

I have an early Porsche Boxster which is maybe best of both...being a '97 car it is very analogue, and being a convertible great for sun exposure.

All I know is it feels fantastic driving it early morning and late afternoon/early evening with the top down!
I'll often go for a drive at those times for no other reason than it feels good to...not very scientific, but that is my experience.

### Stian Van Zweden — 2018-04-04T21:21:52+0300

### ObstreperousPolarBear — 2018-04-06T08:23:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-exposure-potential-vs-nnemf-in-cars.21118/post-238824>

I've been thinking a lot about this one.

Older cars are definitely better, since they are less wired. I just bought a brand new jeep wrangler, however, and they are keeping them true to form and not adding all the garbage like seat warmers and hand holders. I haven't tested the magnetic fields in my car, but there is essentially no RF radiation difference in it with the car on or off. And yes, being able to get sun is a major benefit.

The caveat to all of this is simply that when you are in the car, you are neither grounded, nor active, plus most of your time on the road is going to be spent along a major thoroughfare what is loaded with RF radiation. So you can't really win in the car. You have to win where you live, where you play, and where you work.

tl;dr: I will always own a jeep and you should consider one too.

There are a lot of variables here-- including how much the car wi-fi and cell sourced RF radiation (and other nnEMFs that car is generating itself-- and gets ricochet-reflected around and around in the metal cocoon we call cars). Car windows are essentially transparent to RF waves, but metal as we know is a reflector.

Jack,

Do you have thoughts on the best general principles to follow when voluntarily waking up before sunrise for a person w/ no acute or chronic Neolithic diseases?

i.e. red light only (?), CT immediately upon waking (?), etc...

### kris90 — 2018-04-05T17:03:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-before-sunrise.21124/post-238751>

I'm always up before sunrise, except summer when I wake up with it.

I just do CT right away, red lights only, and blue blockers when exposed to headlights and streetlamps on the road.

### drezy — 2018-04-05T18:14:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-before-sunrise.21124/post-238756>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=238751)
>
> I'm always up before sunrise, except summer when I wake up with it.
>
> I just do CT right away, red lights only, and blue blockers when exposed to headlights and streetlamps on the road.

I continue tinkering with my routine about every 3 months. I'm doing morning CT and still throw in an evening CT here and there.

The only rule I seem to remember that I may be bending is the "fatty meal before CT" unless I'm not remembering that correctly. Doing the cold in the morning seems to supercharge my productivity.

Have you found any upsides to morning CT?

### kris90 — 2018-04-05T21:39:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-before-sunrise.21124/post-238781>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=238756)
>
> I continue tinkering with my routine about every 3 months. I'm doing morning CT and still throw in an evening CT here and there.
>
> The only rule I seem to remember that I may be bending is the "fatty meal before CT" unless I'm not remembering that correctly. Doing the cold in the morning seems to supercharge my productivity.
>
> Have you found any upsides to morning CT?

I think if you are quite lean, the fatty meal before CT will help keep you warmer due to the effects of mitochondrial uncoupling when you eat a high fat meal. I think if you are fasted you may have a lower tolerance to the cold due to down-regulation of the metabolism, especially if you are already lean. To me, being lean and doing fasted CT is just badass. It's learning to love the cold. It's never "fun" per se, but addictive in some weird way. Last night I was feeling quite cold before bed, and needed to shower. I thought maybe I would take my first hot shower in years, and then when I went to go in, I couldn't. Just could not abandon my CT. I am seriously a slave to it.

First thing in the morning is probably the best wakeup aside from sunlight. I think it probably does something similar: definitely a cortisol boost (like blue light from AM sun) and then red light being released from our fat. CT before bed = amazing sleep.

The way I see it is that if we are lean, and not basking in the sun, we should feel cold period. When we do not have an external energy source to tap into (i.e. sun) we have to tap into the energy stored within us. It's somewhat uncomfortable, but the animals don't seem to complain.

# Thread: Grounding mat in car
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mat-in-car.21125/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jenny S — 2018-04-01T05:57:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mat-in-car.21125/post-238440>

Anyone know if these are a good idea or not? Thanks

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-04-01T20:10:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mat-in-car.21125/post-238473>

Jenny,

I know there are claims for using grounding mats in vehicles to release static charges on occupants bodies and relieve driver fatigue-- but I am skeptical.

Since you can't ground to earth in a rubber-tired vehicle the actual claim is that the car mat grounds to the metal frame of car and so works that way. Technically, if you could wire a mat to a discharge wire dragging on pavement that might work for earthing-- but only for concrete roads not asphalt roads.

Really, the only way to prove or disprove a car static charge grounding notion is to conduct a personal field test with a body voltage meter and see if your body does indeed discharge voltage with a mat.

But even so, that would not deal with all other EMF pollution found in modern cars-- including WiFi, Bluetooth, traffic sensing radars on board, and ELF radiation from batteries and electronics, and so forth. Not to mention exposure to death towers (cell base stations) that are so conveniently located in dense patterns very close along major arteries and highways so vehicle users can always have complete access to cell signals.

The best thing you can do right now to protect yourself is to drive as little as possible.

But since we know so little about EMFs in our daily environment, and even less about safe mitigation techniques I say the jury is out on the whole question of grounding safely in our urban, built-up environments. For example, some people say that grounding in the house via the grounding plug of outlets or a wired
metal stake in our backyards will do the trick.

Because the electric grid in US was built economically by using the ground to send excess electric currents back to the grid (ever notice the guy wire-looking attachments to your local power pole-- that's its purpose) I personally believe you can't ground with complete safety anywhere there is potential dirty electricity lurking around in built-up areas.

But if you have some evidence-based science to prove otherwise please share.

### Jenny S — 2018-04-02T05:12:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mat-in-car.21125/post-238497>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=238473)
>
> Jenny,
>
> I know there are claims for using grounding mats in vehicles to release static charges on occupants bodies and relieve driver fatigue-- but I am skeptical.
>
> Since you can't ground to earth in a rubber-tired vehicle the actual claim is that the car mat grounds to the metal frame of car and so works that way. Technically, if you could wire a mat to a discharge wire dragging on pavement that might work for earthing-- but only for concrete roads not asphalt roads.
>
> Really, the only way to prove or disprove a car static charge grounding notion is to conduct a personal field test with a body voltage meter and see if your body does indeed discharge voltage with a mat.
>
> But if you have some evidence-based science to prove otherwise please share.

I asked how they could ground from place bought them from (I'm in Oz) & they said it's because they are steel belted they still ground.
Did have grounding sheets etc for house but after buying a stetzer meter & asking guy where bought it from have stopped using them all.

# Thread: Red eyes in the sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-eyes-in-the-sun.21127/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### John Saf — 2018-04-01T22:23:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-eyes-in-the-sun.21127/post-238480>

The sun is back here at 62'N. We are spending all day outside. My kids love the sun.
My oldest girl (4.5years and blue eyes) get red eyes after some hours outside in the sun/snow. No pains, complains or problems. It is ok the next morning. My son (2years brown eyes) dont get red eyes.
Shold i put on sunglasses mid day? She wears a cap without any less red eyes.

### JanSz — 2018-04-02T03:38:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-eyes-in-the-sun.21127/post-238491>

> [John Saf said:](/goto/post?id=238480)
>
> The sun is back here at 62'N. We are spending all day outside. My kids love the sun.
> My oldest girl (4.5years and blue eyes) get red eyes after some hours outside in the sun/snow. No pains, complains or problems. It is ok the next morning. My son (2years brown eyes) dont get red eyes.
> Shold i put on sunglasses mid day? She wears a cap without any less red eyes.

If someone is not sun hardened,
it take about a month to get used to.

....

### JanSz — 2018-04-02T03:38:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-eyes-in-the-sun.21127/post-238492>

Take it easy,
specially first 14 sunny days

...

### WalterNL — 2018-04-02T13:06:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-eyes-in-the-sun.21127/post-238503>

Yeah ease it in the sun, especially with younger ones who have been inside (?) for winter. We are not all like @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) who walk around with golden tanned skin and can adjust immediately to the equator sun.

### John Saf — 2018-04-02T21:04:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-eyes-in-the-sun.21127/post-238543>

Thank you for your comments. Today they had sunglasses 3 hours in the middle of the day, and her eyes was not red before bed. She did not like them at all, but we will ease in to it so we can put them away.
We are always outside when we can, but the wintersun is weak. Mid winter we only have 1 hours sun over the mountain tops. (Now we have 12 with a different strength). So the difference is huge.

### John Saf — 2018-04-02T21:12:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-eyes-in-the-sun.21127/post-238544>

Sunset mid winter from our house.

I have a good amount of info to work with and I Thank you from my bottom of my heart Dr. Kruse for giving us this info. I am determined to do better. **[Is energy flux the be all end all?](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?posts/238533/) I have to get my sleep right, period. That's all for now.**

### Lahelada — 2018-04-04T20:51:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turmeric-dose.21131/post-238705>

Take it with fat and pepper for better absorption.I use it liberally.

### Billybats — 2019-01-31T22:24:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turmeric-dose.21131/post-261960>

Is Curcumin a part of turmeric or turmeric itself. Who uses powdered and who uses fresh.

EDIT: never mind I found the answer.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-02-01T18:35:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turmeric-dose.21131/post-262052>

I use a lot of turmeric in cooking-- and stopped taking curcumin supplements entirely.

I tended to follow the adage "if a little bit is good then a lot is better" but I am a little wiser now. Herbs and spices have many substances (phytochemicals) that can have very powerful effects on body-- good and potentially bad...depending. We should respect that and practice careful moderation.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turmeric-dose.21131/post-262123>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-infrared-bulb.21147/post-238755>

A bit puzzled as to what type of bulb to use for infrared therapy. Would a 250 watt near infrared bulb give me some of the benefits of a near infrared sauna ? Don't want to mess myself up either.

TheraBulb Designed To Maximize Near Infrared Output, NIR-A, IR-A, Red Light Therapy 800 nm Peak - Emits Optimal Near Infrared Healing Wavelengths 700 nm to 1200 Nanometers.

The brand of bulb i was looking at is called Therabulb. Available on amazon

### primalmofo — 2018-07-09T03:28:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-infrared-bulb.21147/post-245382>

I have that bulb , it’s been recommended by members on the site

### Matt Justice — 2018-12-16T22:02:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-infrared-bulb.21147/post-258242>

Therabulb should work fine for what you're trying to do.

Is there a master NIR sauna/heat lamp thread here somewhere? I searched, but couldn't find a solid all encompassing thread. The sauna industry drives me nuts with this "near infrared sauna" crap...

They're HEAT LAMP saunas!! There really is no such thing as a "Near Infrared" Sauna. Anything under 900nm, does not heat the body up when isolated. Light therapy yes, sauna no.
