# Thread: Dermatologist blue/red light treaments
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dermatologist-blue-red-light-treaments.21172/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Polito — 2018-04-11T18:57:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dermatologist-blue-red-light-treaments.21172/post-239239>

My 15 year old daughter goes to the dermatologist for light blue/red light treatments for her acne. Would just normal sunlight be a better alternative? Is there harm in the treatments or am I wasting my money? Always wondered about this. So much mixed information. Thank you

### SlamSlask — 2018-04-11T19:38:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dermatologist-blue-red-light-treaments.21172/post-239241>

I cant give you any geeky research but here is my own experience with acne:
At the age of 13 i started getting heavy acne which has followed up untill now (im currently 22)
In this time gap i tried many of the commercial ways to get rid of acne such as the creams, face masks etc. keep in mind that my environment in all these years got worse and worse (See the first post in my journal for a more detailed explanation)
Nearly 4 years ago I started working as an IT worker under heavy amounts of radiation and blue light etc. this made my acne go all crazy (i got no idea why at the time) so I went to a dermatologist which used different creams and steam this helped in the days after but the effect did not last more that a couple of days - I never tried the light teraphy though
At this time i also started doing all sorts of diets etc. none of them fixed the problem
After stumpling onto Jacks work i finally decided to quit my job last new year this let me get much more sun, grounding and seafood (was allready on a paleo/low carb diet)
Now a little over 4 months later I am very close to having completely reversed my acne to the point of there only begin small red marks where it was the worst

So my best bet is that there is something in her enviroment that is keeping her from healing (think Becker/DC current)
In my case this was high amounts of nnEMF and bluelight from my work enviroment and living in the city

How i think about it at this point is that it is just another symptom of a DC current begin low in a particular tissue

Thank you for your reply. Yes. I feel blue light exposure and emf certainly has something to do with it. Like a 15 year, she and her friends are glued to thier phones and computer screens. Hardly ever get out and eat loads of wheat/flour carbs. Trying to explain the light to them only amuses them like most adults. It’s funny, I do just about everything Jack suggests and more on my own with exercise, fresh air and grounding. I hardly ever get a sniffle and everyone around me is totally sick all the time. > [SlamSlask said:](/goto/post?id=239244)
>
> The best thing I learned was that when it comes to the female mind you have to change the mood, not the thoughts

SlamSlask my ass. More like SlyDog. Watch out ladies we've got a smooth operator on the loose.

> [Michael Polito said:](/goto/post?id=239239)
>
> My 15 year old daughter goes to the dermatologist for light blue/red light treatments for her acne.

There is a high chance that for about the next decade she will know everything.

Here's a quote from Mark Twain I always like:

> When I was a boy of 14, my father was so ignorant I could hardly stand to have the old man around. But when I got to be 21, I was astonished at how much the old man had learned in seven years.

FWIW my old old 41 yo skin gets clearer with sun and lower deuterium eating too.

### ScottishEmma — 2018-04-12T19:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dermatologist-blue-red-light-treaments.21172/post-239304>

I had acne from age 14 until pretty much a few years ago (I'm 39).

For me, cutting out sugar was huge but also my face would clear whenever I went on a sunny holiday or under sunbeds. It also went away 2nd and 3rd trimesters of both pregnancies and during breastfeeding - then came back.

When I cut out using makeup it never came back. Now I live in the sun it's clear as a bell.

I also swear by essential oils of tea tree and rosemary to kill active acne dead - just won't stop it from coming that's all.

### jrobertson1984 — 2022-05-27T01:41:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dermatologist-blue-red-light-treaments.21172/post-313179>

Thanks for some good advice in this thread. This gives me hope that someday I will get rid of my acne.
Even though I think that I have it not due to environmental reasons, I live in the countryside and spend a lot of time outside. Therefore, I get quite a lot of sunlight in the summer, which does not really help. However, when I used to work right by the seaside all day long, my face got really tanned, and the acne was not seen so well.
Anyway, I made an appointment with [Bella Pelle cosmetic dermatologist](https://www.vitalskinderm.com/locations/bella-pelle/). I waited for them to examine my case and tell me the exact reasons for my problem.

# Thread: BPI TINTED glasses
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bpi-tinted-glasses.21177/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mover — 2018-04-15T16:12:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bpi-tinted-glasses.21177/post-239428>

Hello Everyone. The members on this forum including of course Dr.Kruse have been amazingly helpful and responsive and I have learned a great deal. Thank you.

I have a question regarding BPI tinting. I recently bought a pair of decent frames and had the eyeglass store tint them using the Uvex lens as a guide. They did not come out as well as seen by the attached photo. I am guessing the BPI on the Uvex are BPI 500? I think mine ended up being closer to BPI 540. They allow quite a bit of light to be transmitted so that I can be at work during the during the day and night under LED lighting. Dept Store type lighting. Wondering if these would work as well. Also, during the day, daylight is being transmitted through the large windows, but the windows are tinted to block UV light i suppose. Wonderng if I should be wearing my glasses while the outdoor light is coming in through the tinted glass. The Area where i work is still heavily iluminated by LED overhead lighting. BTW, my glasses are above the UVEX glasses in the image attached. Thank you in advance for your suggestions.

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-04-15T16:16:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bpi-tinted-glasses.21177/post-239429>

Uvex = 500nm indeed. Your BPI tinted glasses _look_ 500nm to me as well, but the material seems to be thinner.

BPI 540 or 550 look darker than the your BPI tinted glasses.

Windows block a lot of UVA, and _all_ UVB.

### Mover — 2018-04-15T16:26:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bpi-tinted-glasses.21177/post-239430>

> [KalosKaiAgathos said:](/goto/post?id=239429)
>
> Uvex = 500nm indeed. Your BPI tinted glasses _look_ 500nm to me as well, but the material seems to be thinner.
>
> BPI 540 or 550 look darker than the your BPI tinted glasses.
>
> Windows block a lot of UVA, and _all_ UVB.

Thank you so much for your reply. So you feel, my lens will block as much or close to the 500? Learning quite bit here.

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-04-15T16:47:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bpi-tinted-glasses.21177/post-239433>

I cannot be 100% certain through a picture, but yes, by the looks of it, your lens seems to block up to 500nm.

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-04-15T19:01:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bpi-tinted-glasses.21177/post-239437>

My female intuition is rarely incorrect...

### Billybats — 2018-04-19T17:27:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bpi-tinted-glasses.21177/post-239554>

In the link below is a chart that you wear your blue blockers to see if they work:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/)

# Thread: The human skin as a sub-THz receiver – Does 5G pose a danger to it or not?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-skin-as-a-sub-thz-receiver-%E2%80%93-does-5g-pose-a-danger-to-it-or-not.21201/>

**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2018-04-22T03:07:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-skin-as-a-sub-thz-receiver-%E2%80%93-does-5g-pose-a-danger-to-it-or-not.21201/post-239668>

"Starting from July 2016 the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) has adopted new rules for wireless broadband operations above 24 GHz (5 G). This trend of exploitation is predicted to expand to higher frequencies in the sub-THz region. One must consider the implications of human immersion in the electromagnetic noise, caused by devices working at the very same frequencies as those, to which the sweat duct (as a helical antenna) is most attuned. We are raising a warning flag against the unrestricted use of sub-THz technologies for communication, before the possible consequences for public health are explored."

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0013935118300331](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0013935118300331)

# Thread: Getting your salt in
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-your-salt-in.21203/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Technosmith — 2018-04-23T12:59:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-your-salt-in.21203/post-239703>

Hey folks,

Any ideas as to how to consume a decent amount of salt whilst avoiding excessively salty meals?

I think Jack has said to avoid adding it to drinking water, so just wondered if there is another approach?

What do we think to dissolving salt in a small amount of water and drinking just before/after the meal?

Thanks,

Phil

### Sue-UK — 2018-04-23T15:16:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-your-salt-in.21203/post-239705>

I'm using a very coarse sea salt, I like the crunch of it. I don't eat peanut butter now but when I did I didn't like smooth, it had to be salty and crunchy. When I'm ready to eat I make salt spots on the food on my plate, so that on some mouthfuls I get a salty crunch, but it doesn't overpower the whole meal.

### Technosmith — 2018-04-23T16:54:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-your-salt-in.21203/post-239713>

Thanks Sue. Sounds like a decent plan.

### Jan Christer — 2018-04-23T16:54:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-your-salt-in.21203/post-239714>

> [Technosmith said:](/goto/post?id=239703)
>
> Hey folks,
>
> Any ideas as to how to consume a decent amount of salt whilst avoiding excessively salty meals?
>
> I think Jack has said to avoid adding it to drinking water, so just wondered if there is another approach?
>
> What do we think to dissolving salt in a small amount of water and drinking just before/after the meal?
>
> Thanks,
>
> Phil

I use about 10 grams of Redmond Real salt per day on food, which is unsalted otherwise, and my food doesn't taste too salty. But I need extra salt because I have POTS and CFS.

I think it's good to salt to taste, so maybe you need less salt if you think it tastes too salty when you take more.

And I think Jack recommends salt with food and not adding it to drinking water.

### Michael Polito — 2018-04-23T19:23:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-your-salt-in.21203/post-239736>

I add a pinch of Celtic sea salt to my Zero filtered water to replenish the minerals. Zero Water filters out all minerals,fluoride and chorine.. It's basically a R.O. Filter

### Jan Christer — 2018-04-23T20:41:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-your-salt-in.21203/post-239747>

I think I have heard something to the effect that adding salt to water alters its electrical properties, which affect its function as EZ water in the cells.

Anyone who knows more about this?

### Billybats — 2018-04-23T19:33:13+0300

# Thread: UK Sunrise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uk-sunrise.21214/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Technosmith — 2018-04-24T19:18:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uk-sunrise.21214/post-239839>

Hey folks,

For those based in the UK, how long are you gazing at the sunrise in the morning?

I believe Jack encourages us to eat within 30 minutes of rising so I'm just curious as to how long you guys are watching the sunrise for before hitting the BAB?

Also, do any of you do this through a window indoors, which I appreciate is far from optimum?

Thanks,

Phil

### Sue-UK — 2018-04-26T12:28:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uk-sunrise.21214/post-239949>

As long as possible.  I try and get outside even before sunrise, the light frequencies are transitioning, and I think that helps the circadian cycle. I also pay attention to what is going on in the environment, whether its the first bird sounds which are before sunrise, or a flock of birds that goes south east at around 15 mins after sunrise, the changing foliage on the trees etc. I work my senses. For me its information, just like food.  If I'm eating within 30 mins of sunrise I'm going in and grabbing something to eat and going outside to eat it if I can, but I'm not doing the leptin reset so getting as much info from the light frequencies and the changing environment is critical. I eat if I'm hungry, or if the weather is crap for several days or I can't spend long outside. If the weather is too bad then I might stand in a doorway, or look out of an east facing window, its better than doing nothing, but I've found that when I'm scavenging light, grounding is even more important. I use a grounded yoga mat as a runner at the bottom of the bed, linked to an outside grounding rod, and using that overnight has made a difference, especially in the winter when I've been living in the cold lane. Grounding, living in the cold lane, working my senses and scavenging light as much as I can on cold surfaces makes a difference. So does a minimum of 10 hours complete darkness at night, after using the transitioning evening frequencies (either natural or from some evening blockers I've rigged up).

### Technosmith — 2018-04-26T14:24:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uk-sunrise.21214/post-239956>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=239949)
>
> As long as possible.  I try and get outside even before sunrise, the light frequencies are transitioning, and I think that helps the circadian cycle. I also pay attention to what is going on in the environment, whether its the first bird sounds which are before sunrise, or a flock of birds that goes south east at around 15 mins after sunrise, the changing foliage on the trees etc. I work my senses. For me its information, just like food.  If I'm eating within 30 mins of sunrise I'm going in and grabbing something to eat and going outside to eat it if I can, but I'm not doing the leptin reset so getting as much info from the light frequencies and the changing environment is critical. I eat if I'm hungry, or if the weather is crap for several days or I can't spend long outside. If the weather is too bad then I might stand in a doorway, or look out of an east facing window, its better than doing nothing, but I've found that when I'm scavenging light, grounding is even more important. I use a grounded yoga mat as a runner at the bottom of the bed, linked to an outside grounding rod, and using that overnight has made a difference, especially in the winter when I've been living in the cold lane. Grounding, living in the cold lane, working my senses and scavenging light as much as I can on cold surfaces makes a difference. So does a minimum of 10 hours complete darkness at night, after using the transitioning evening frequencies (either natural or from some evening blockers I've rigged up).

# Thread: Let's Talk Water Fasting
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-water-fasting.21223/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2018-04-26T03:58:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-water-fasting.21223/post-239943>

Jack,

Suppose a person has high redox, good circadian rhythm, and is doing CT as the primary form of physical training -- think Ben Greenfield but without all of the endurance and weight lifting stuff.

At a lean body composition (BF of 5% - 10%), is water fasting either every other day or for every two out of three days acceptable? (Assume eating is DHA-loaded and iodine-loaded when it happens).

I've done several 6+ day water fasts and I love the way that I feel on them, so I'm wondering how it works as I get leaner and have only ~2 weeks of fat reserve stored up at a time.

A) Yes, could be ok.

B) Not wise unless you're sure everything is dialed in, and even then it's a maybe.

C) No.

D) Dr. Kruse has an opinion on this, but it's not appropriate or feasible to voice that opinion on a forum.

### AdamF — 2018-04-26T04:00:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-water-fasting.21223/post-239944>

I am thinking that this can't be a terrible way of constant deuterium depletion, so long as the DC Current stays high to power all of the regeneration processes.

# Thread: Where I live
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-live.21227/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JohnnyAZ — 2018-04-27T02:24:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-live.21227/post-239991>

One thing I can not seem to figure out is if where I live is an issue. I live 30 miles outside of Phoenix and pretty much on the edge of civilization. We are right in the foothills of the Superstition Mountains and we get very poor cell reception. This seems like the making of a good start. However, as jack says, “you can’t get healthy in the environment you got sick” I find myself wondering if I should move. I live in a small town with no towers and no street lights anywhere and that all sounds like good things. But I also remember Jack saying to “watch your pets” and we just found tumors on my dogs mammary glands. The vet says it’s because she was never fixed and never had puppies so her hormones built up and created the issue.

Basically is AZ a good state? Should I be more focused on the lack of towers and cell reception in my town as a good sign or in the health of my dog as a bad sign?

I am at a stage where a move is possible...but as I am still way behind in all the material (halfway through 2015) I don’t want to make a bad move and make things worse.

### primalmofo — 2018-05-07T15:24:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-live.21227/post-240692>

I would think it is a good place to live, not perfect tho. You have access to great light in AZ and based on the fact u have poor reception/are out of civilization-your nnemf exposure is probably pretty good. Just make sure ur drinking good water and grounding (or getting cold)

### Bic — 2018-05-07T15:39:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-live.21227/post-240693>

Where did you mother's people come from 1000 years ago? 10,000 years ago?

# Thread: trying to avoid getting mirena iud/ and exogenous hormones
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-avoid-getting-mirena-iud-and-exogenous-hormones.21229/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2018-04-27T12:57:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-avoid-getting-mirena-iud-and-exogenous-hormones.21229/post-240007>

I am such a believer in not taking exogenous hormones that i have never been on birth control pills. i have always used condoms. after my 2 children were born i had a tubal ligation. i started having heavy periods around 40. still i avoided the uterine ablation, mirena iud, and bc pills that were recommended to me. not from a doctor, but from the internet (dr weil) i found that women could take Aleve and cut down on their menstrual flow. that has worked for me somewhat for years. now using NSAIDs like aleve to cut down on menstrual flow is mainstream and is mentioned as a possible solution by places like the mayo clinic.

after finding this site and employing some of the ideas, i found my blood sugar was so much better that i lifted the restrictions on myself that had been controlling my tendency to gain a lot of weight on my abdomen. i had been very restrictive since 2009. that meant just about no fruit or rice or any starchy veg. mostly meat and vegetables/nuts (some occasional berries) with occasional low carb crap food like sugar free pudding.

so i actually started eating fruit and rice again. what happened is that since december to now i have had 7 pounds of fat grow on my abdomen. starting in december when my habits changed, my menstrual cycle started to get irregular whereas it had been pretty much on a schedule, even though i was 52 years old. i figured the irregularities had more to do with inevitable menopause than my change in eating. then in april i starting to have such heavy bleeding- it was non stop. i had to take norethindrone pills- aleve no longer worked. i am still taking the pills and will get a mirena iud if i can't find my own way to cut down on the bleeding. no conventional doctor as ever been of any use here. i lied to my ob/gyn and told her i wanted the mirena which she had suggested was the best option since its hormonal effects were localized to the uterus instead of being systemic like the pills.
actually my plan is to go back to my low carb eating, get out in the sun wearing my tan through swim suit, etc. i have already started back on low carb, but i can't get the amount of sun exposure i want until the end of june due to my work schedule.

i had a transvaginal ultrasound that showed possible adenomyosis. "Though ultrasound cannot definitively diagnose adenomyosis, it can help to rule out other conditions with similar symptoms." i am also getting a uterine biopsy to rule out cancer as a cause of the heavy bleeding. anyway, i am still taking the bc pills as i prefer not to bleed out all over the place. i am hoping losing that estrogen producing 7 lbs of abdominal fat will allow me just to never schedule the mirena insertion. 2 years ago i had one similar incidence of bleeding so heavily that i needed norethindrone. i saw the trigger- it was flonase. i just took the norethindrone for one cycle- then stopped it on my own and was fine- all without any doctor's guidance. i hope i can be successful this time as well. i'll let you know.

### JanSz — 2018-04-27T14:25:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-avoid-getting-mirena-iud-and-exogenous-hormones.21229/post-240008>

Yesterday I was thinking about you on this post.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...nces-of-contraceptive-pill.21225/#post-239993](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/health-consequences-of-contraceptive-pill.21225/#post-239993)

### taiyang — 2018-04-27T23:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-avoid-getting-mirena-iud-and-exogenous-hormones.21229/post-240057>

Thank you for caring that's a rare thing and I appreciate it.

# Thread: Effect of pomegranate extract on vagina, skeleton, metabolic and endocrine profiles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effect-of-pomegranate-extract-on-vagina-skeleton-metabolic-and-endocrine-profiles.21289/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2018-05-06T06:16:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effect-of-pomegranate-extract-on-vagina-skeleton-metabolic-and-endocrine-profiles.21289/post-240606>

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/29603520](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/29603520)

**CONCLUSION:**
In rats fed with pomegranate extract, estradiol levels increased and tibial bone cortex thickness and vaginal epithelium thickness also increased. Pomegranate itself or its formulation extracts may be a support or an alternative to the main treatment modalities in the preservation of bone density and the treatment of vaginal epithelial atrophy.

### shiran — 2018-05-06T08:30:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effect-of-pomegranate-extract-on-vagina-skeleton-metabolic-and-endocrine-profiles.21289/post-240608>

Interesting - pomegranates are a polyphenol which have a benzene ring - aromatic - they donate hydrogen - I wonder if it is deuterium depleted since it's gone through photosynthesis or is the tree storing its deuterium in the fruit - so like ascorbic acid, beta carotene, alpha-tocopherol (vitamin e), since the benzene ring is a photon trap - it said something about a pi electron cloud in there - this is why this stuff is ok to eat in the sun but not in blue light - they absorb light - obviously there are vast gaps in my knowledge base here, but I think that's why the latest FB post on vitamin E said it works in the sunlight to help eyesight, but is a fail in artificial light...

[https://www.mdpi.com/1420-3049/14/6/2202/pdf](https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=16&ved=2ahUKEwiGlMfuk_XeAhVFYKwKHV9LAE4QFjAPegQIERAC&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mdpi.com%2F1420-3049%2F14%2F6%2F2202%2Fpdf&usg=AOvVaw2bE4nigojvTfXc7MKfAjYo)

So, the Stokes shift means that when any wave hits an atom that absorbs light then emits it (absorption/emmisivity), the light that is then emitted is less than the light captured... so say vitamin A blasts vitamin B12, does that mean B12 then emits light when it's supposed to keep it? What does this mean?

Of course, today I am still reeling from that chromaphore mitochondria respiratory proteins being destroyed by vitamin A thing... Christ, just when you don't think it can get any worse...

### drezy — 2018-11-30T02:28:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effect-of-pomegranate-extract-on-vagina-skeleton-metabolic-and-endocrine-profiles.21289/post-257012>

> So, the Stokes shift means that when any wave hits an atom that absorbs light then emits it (absorption/emmisivity), the light that is then emitted is less than the light captured... so say vitamin A blasts vitamin B12, does that mean B12 then emits light when it's supposed to keep it? What does this mean?
>
> Of course, today I am still reeling from that chromaphore mitochondria respiratory proteins being destroyed by vitamin A thing... Christ, just when you don't think it can get any worse...

I encourage everyone to get the basics of [stokes shifts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stokes_shift) as well as the [Planck–Einstein relation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Planck–Einstein_relation).

It's there where It's unavoidable that something funny is going on.

# Thread: Sleep getting better
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-getting-better.21303/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### halvibe — 2018-05-09T14:08:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-getting-better.21303/post-240889>

I have a bad F'up due to mag field in my house. (F'up stands for Folding Up of Proteins)
I had bad insomnia for about 3 yrs, then, for the last year, it's gotten a way better. (I'm 5'10", 140 lbs soaking wet, 73 \[going on 25, still shoot up stairs 2 at time\] don't exercise enuf (worried about losing stem cells)).
I've been taking a bunch of stuff from about 3-4 holistic folks. I've taken 0.3 Mg of natural Melatonin, lots of minerals and a few other things here and there. I also got down to Fl in Feb to get more sun. I saw sunrise almost every morning there on the beach. I bought the magnetico about 6 weeks back, I have the Quantlet, (but I mostly forget to use it), I do appreciation exercise with my wife at bedtime and usually meditation also.
Last night I got about 7 hrs and I skipped dinner last eve- AND I didn't take a single pill at bedtime, not even a mineral.
I'm going to experiment more with time restricted eating. (Saw some cool vids with Satchin Panda about it.)

I occasionally get a bad night (3-4 hrs) every week or two, but it's much better than a year ago.

I follow a bunch of ultra-cool dudes, like Asprree, Grnfld, Merkole, and others (names spelled wrongful to protect the innocent (I worry about the jckboots in gumint). However Jack K. is the coolest dude of all, with the largest amt of the fastest firing neurons on the blue pearl. Truckloads of L.A.R.A. to Jack.
(that's for - Love, Admiration, Respect and Appreciation)

### caroline — 2018-05-10T06:21:18+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-beginner-with-sudomoter-failure.21311/post-241044>

I am going on holiday 22nd May.
I have sudomoter failure, and thus can not tolerate heat or cold. YET I know I can change that.

I have Jacks book and I am a patron, but the CT being described is not possible for me. How could I safely start? It seems Jacks advise is not really aimed at disable people, but those already mobile who want to become optimal.

Feet in ice water? Ice pack on neck? When I tried having a cold shower I almost went into shock and my heart rate shot to 144. I had tremors after.

I want to cold and heat adapt. I eat seafood. I try to get sunlight, but when it was hot here in Manchester last week I had trouble breathing.

Any tips?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-6-3-ratio.21314/post-241093>

Trying to understand Omega 6:3 ratio test results(used Quest Diagnostics).

Arachidonic Acid = 5.9%
DHA = 4.4%
EPA = 4.1%
EPA/Arachidonic Acid = 0.7
Omega 3(EPA+DHA) Index = 8.5%

Omega 6 / Omega 3 Ratio = 2.5

How does this translate to Dr. Kruse’s Omega 6:3 Ratio <4:1 ratio in Epi-Paleo RX book lab test #14?

Also, how healthy are other test factors?

Thank you in advance for your responses!

### Martha Ray — 2020-07-10T00:46:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-6-3-ratio.21314/post-290846>

I hope you found your answer,

I have wanted a test called BrainSpan developed by LTC(Ret) Daniel T. Johnston, MD, MPH who I heard presenting on a Health Summit a few months ago. I have heard Dr. Kruse mention that it is important to a Omega 6 to Omega 3 blood assay but I not seen a lab recommended. [https://www.brainspan.com/consumer](https://www.brainspan.com/consumer) It looks like a great test too me but does need to be re-run ongoing to see effectiveness of therapy.

Test says it tests

-   **the Omega-3 Index EPA/DHA**

-   **the Omega-6/Omega-3 Ratio**

-   **the Palmitic Acid level**

-   **Full, 4 part cognitive function test to include scores on memory, attention/focus, processing speed, and cognitive flexibility.**

-   **the BrainSpan Index**

-   **12-page patient friendly report and interpretation and recommendation. A provider is also assigned and available to answer questions.

    Looks to run 'on special' $149. plus shipping**

In the past I pushed to have several _**Genova Diagnostics Laboratory**_ tests Cardio Ion and GI Effects Stool Test etc but I was not easy to get done and the doctor I was seeing really did not understand how to use the data. I want an affordable Functional Integrative Functional Practitioner to partner with on Remediation. I learn all I can online but testing, remediation and followup are not optimal for me yet. I will find someone who can answer questions like the one you asked and the ones I wonder about.

What Dr. Jack Kruse has shared is the best help I've come across. in the past 11 years of working on my health. I am very thankful for what he shares and while impatient at times I am moving forward.

He shared this on Facebook not long ago

" **SUNLIGHT, WATER AND MAGNETISM
Mitochondriac Wisdom
You might only need one or two really good ideas to improve 80% of your life.
Figure out what those are and apply them. It's not about knowing every single self improvement strategy.
It's about finding principles that have the biggest impact on your life**."
​

It encouraged me and I hope it is encouraging you and to others in progress...

# Thread: Hang on... Makeup & 5G?

**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 23

### ScottishEmma — 2018-05-13T13:21:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hang-on-makeup-5g.21315/post-241107>

So I've heard all the talk about tattoos and 5G but what about makeup? Makeup has metallic compounds and heavy metals and is slathered all over women's eyes, face and lips. Surely the number of women wearing makeup outnumbers the number of people with tattoos? Does it? Wouldn't that be more of a problem walking around with metal in your pores up near your face where you're holding your phone??

What about the absorption of these metals into the skin? We're told that we absorb up to 60% of what we put on our skin.

Is makeup more of an issue?

I don't personally wear it.

### ValerieBee — 2018-05-13T13:41:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hang-on-makeup-5g.21315/post-241109>

TIny diamonds and rubies enclosed in a small circle of fabric that allows light through, on the third eye chakra point, resting in bright sunshine this morning was awesome.  Better there than on the belly button.

electronic gem therapy uses high intensity light, apparently the light is "stressed" with the qualities of precious gems to "profoundly alter the body's mood and chemistry." (Although I think it has more to do with the gems being " stressed" by certain frequencies of light, and fluorescing others.) Electronic gem therapy also uses brainwaves, they induce theta, alpha and low beta etc depending on the situation. So I'm testing it whilst we've got some fab sunshine. I'd tried it before with bioptron light, but didn't really notice anything much, possibly too low an intensity light, and I was doing it through the wrists, and the palm of my hand. Rings were made with no metal behind the gems, so the light through it was directed at the skin, (mini laser?) but I'm also thinking finger bones, the breast bone for necklaces etc. .... maybe I sensed it on my forehead because it was close to bone.

### LieselK — 2018-05-15T19:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-carbs.21322/post-241256>

I'm realizing that I am not eating hardly any carbs which made sense when I was doing the Leptin Reset in January... however I'm rereading Jack's book and trying to get a better grasp on what my intake should be to be optimal. Since I'm following the Epi-Paleo diet with the addition of the AI components I'm just curious where y'all get carbs from at all? If my daily caloric intake is around 2,000 calories and at least 20% should be carbs... how in the world do you incorporate them? Sweet potatoes are out of season now, I've got greens (romaine, collards, kale, etc), broccoli, and spinach and then some fruits that are in season. 400 calories of greens seems like A LOT.

### Billybats — 2018-05-15T20:05:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-carbs.21322/post-241262>

I have read many times on here no need to count calories. I personally go by how I feel and what is in season. That's just me. I do however mess up on and off (with junk type food) but I don't worry about it ss much as I use to. I am still trying to change my eating habits and doing 70% better then I did a few months ago. What I want to get perfect is the sun, ct, grounding and minding my nnEMF and blue light. Hope that helped.

### JanSz — 2018-05-15T23:34:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-carbs.21322/post-241294>

> **thoughts on the FODmap for veggies???**
>
>
>
>
>
> .

FODMAP stands for Fermentable Oligosaccharides, Disaccharides, Monosaccharides, and Polyols,
which are short chain carbohydrates and sugar alcohols that are poorly absorbed by the body,
resulting in abdominal pain and bloating.
FODMAPs occur in some foods naturally or as additives.

There is a Polish story about donkey that died of hunger because he was given too many choices of food.
[@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)
we need English story

Osiołkowi w żłoby dano
W jeden owies, w drugi siano.
Uchem strzyże, głową kręci,
I to pachnie i to nęci;
Od któregoż teraz zacznie,
Aby sobie podjeść smacznie?
Trudny wybór, trudna zgoda:
Chwyci siano, owsa szkoda,
Chwyci owies, żal mu siana.
I tak stoi aż do rana,
A od rana do wieczora;
Aż nareszcie przyszła pora,

Że Oślina pośród jadła
Z głodu padła.

\================================================

A donkey in the manger was given
In one oat, in another hay.
He cuts his ear with his ear, he shakes his head,
And it smells and lures it;
From wherefore he will begin,
To make yourself delicious?
Difficult choice, difficult agreement:
Grabs hay, oats damage,
He grabs the oats, he pities his hay.
And so it stays until morning,
And from morning to evening;
Until finally the time has come,

That Oślina was in the midst of food
She fell from hunger.

///

Did a work exchange at a yoga ashram in Virginia where we were harvesting rainbow chard and other hardy greens (protected by low fabric hoops at night) in early March.

I wonder whether “season extended” produce is cheating or perfectly fine? No supplemental light—just the VA sun.

I'm also going to add now I've started shucking them myself. It's not as scary as we generally think it is... if done mindfully. So actually I've found it to be a really fantastic way to connect with my food and be present with the moment. I had the dude at the Whole Foods give me a tutorial and I just did it. EVERY person I have told about this so far has the initial reaction of "Woah! You have to be really careful!" because of either potential illness or stabbing yourself. They decline eating them (except my fiance will now that he's seen me do it a few times and not get sick). While I'm not going to downplay needing to pay attention, i'm going to say that using common sense and a little education will quickly eliminate the illness issue, and not getting stabbed is simply a matter of paying attention. I've really loved shucking my own.

# Thread: Power Grid Has Changed - Did You Notice it ???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/power-grid-has-changed-did-you-notice-it.21334/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### EStein — 2018-05-18T00:12:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/power-grid-has-changed-did-you-notice-it.21334/post-241460>

.
US Gov ended the regulations on the power grid being set at 60 hertz. Due to the extreme cost of maintaining the precise measurements needed it was canceled. The power grid is running much higher than 60 hertz. Rarely does it dip below 60 hertz but it can. Some people have noticed their plug in clocks are running much faster due to the change in the power grid.

I can only imagine the health effects this will have along with added 5G ?!?

[http://www.kptv.com/story/38215738/clocks-may-go-a-little-cuckoo-with-power-grid-change](http://www.kptv.com/story/38215738/clocks-may-go-a-little-cuckoo-with-power-grid-change)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: What do you guys think?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-guys-think.21335/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ccajigal — 2018-05-18T00:18:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-guys-think.21335/post-241461>

I have a new job and I'm extremely close to my place of work. Today during the day I ran from my office to my home and it was 8-9 min. I walked back immediately to my office and was 14-15 min. The drive in the car is 2 min each way.

What do you guys think? Regardless if I walk or drive, the road I take has power lines parallel to it. Walk grounded to and from work 14 min each way with sunlight in AM with power lines above above or drive for 2 min same road not grounded? I'm leaning towards walking. Thoughts? Thanks in advance.

# Thread: EMF playing electric guitar
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2018-05-21T11:45:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-241639>

Hello guys.
I just wanted to start again with playing guitar with my 28 years. I was on the run to buy an acustic guitar manly do ditch the EMFs I supposed to get from my electric guitar.
Now I tested this thing with my meter and there was absolutely no EMF coming out of the back where the coils are. Only big magnetic EMF is in Front of the AMP which I can midigate by staying far away.

Is that possible? Or did I measure wrong? Anyone did some readings on electric guitars?

### OptimalSkr — 2018-05-21T16:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-241653>

If the pickups are passive there should be no emfs for the guitar

### Butters — 2018-05-21T20:04:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-241688>

What do you mean by passive?

### OptimalSkr — 2018-05-21T22:43:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-241712>

Active pickups have barteries like emg style. Strat style are passive so no electricity is required. Good to see you measured no emf though,

### Butters — 2018-05-21T23:11:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-241715>

Ah ok. Thank you very much. I thought I would having a grill party for my sausage when playing.

### OptimalSkr — 2018-05-22T14:55:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-241761>

What type of amp do you have? Tube or solid state? I assume the tube would have more emf.also I am sure pedal boards have a bunch near the power supply.

### Butters — 2018-05-22T15:59:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-241767>

I have a fender frontman 15G but it seems it is not functioning well, so I just use the raw guitar now . No emf at all.

### Butters — 2018-06-08T11:43:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-242997>

Just wondered why Jack then said, playing electric guitar is so bad. Must be the other EMF environment in which you are usually when playing.

### OptimalSkr — 2018-06-08T15:12:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-243006>

Well at a show with a whole band it probably sucks for emfs. Also most concerts are at night in crazy lighting. And super loud, I always got tinnitus from them so had to stop going.

### Butters — 2018-06-12T22:12:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-243274>

Yeah. I will try my way with an acoustic now. Kinda like the vibrations, getting deep inside my body. Feels calm. Lets see what an old man can do.

Edit:
Its difficult to play in the evening, because of my kid.
Is there a possibility for playing electric guitar over headphones without much EMF? I know headphones are overkill, but maybe not with every amp or every headphone.

### drezy — 2018-06-20T02:54:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-243835>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=243274)
>
> Its difficult to play in the evening, because of my kid.

FWIW I found almost everything more difficult with a young child. If you're just finding guitar practice difficult you're way ahead of where I was.

Be careful Butters. The days are long but the years are short. You'll get tired but you'll blink and have a young adult instead of a child. it will be a good kind of ache but your heart will ache a bit. Buckle up mein Freund.

### Butters — 2018-06-20T12:07:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-243855>

Oh. Always a joy to read a comment under my posts

To this topic: It should be possible to play with battery powered effect pedals or headphones amps without much EMF.

### Butters — 2021-09-04T12:31:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-playing-electric-guitar.21347/post-303257>

I wish to reheat the topic again. Ive been playing acoustic guitar for a while now, but the wish to play some electric guitar again manifests somehow. But this is a topic rarely discussed on the internet. I couldn't find anything substantial on EMFs from electric guitars connected to amps.
I found some posts in the forum, in which people said they dont play electric guitar anymore but again I found no measurements. I would do measurements by myself but in the moment I own no amp. Buying a guitar and an amp to find out there is too much dirty electricity would be a little too expensive.
So I try to find a differentiated view on that topic.
Is anyone here to provide some measurements or talk about different situations (amp on powergrid, amp with battery) and the corresponding EMFs?

### Phosphene — 2018-08-10T00:32:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tan-through-swimwear-a-new-company.21378/post-248055>

“Forest of heaven“ — that’s a new one for me. I like it!

I have a Kiniki and am a bit disappointed with the tan lines. Hard to avoid with the straps and seams. It’s obvious that SOME tanning occurs through the fabric, but not a whole lot.

My yard is fairly private but not where the best morning sun comes through the trees. Still working on a solution that doesn’t involve a strange and inconvenient corral of tangled lawn furniture and sheets. I personally am getting to the point of not caring if anyone sees, but my family would be mortified. (I know, I know, I shouldn’t care at all...)

I also find the Kiniki fabric kind of scratchy—like a bra you just want to ditch halfway through the day. Not that I wouldn’t prefer that entirely, but still... Hopefully there will be better options out there. Might try Cool Tan.

### Jenelle — 2018-05-25T13:27:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/left-sided-radiculopathy-disc-bulge-and-stenosis.21386/post-242030>

My comment is completely anecdotal, but I can tell you from my husband's experience that when someone is at the point where your friend is ~ they are willing to try just about anything to avoid surgery.

I hope he is grounding. If not, he should start.

My husband is making a conscious effort to get barefoot in the grass EVERY day, and he does feel better. I can tell you that his life is not JK in pretty much any other way, but making this one change has shown enough improvement in his pain to keep him at it.

Get the uv light signal once it enters your atmosphere. 3 hours of uv sunlight (build up solar calas slowly if pale). Observe the sunrise and sunset everyday. Did I say AM sunlight. No fluoride toothpaste. A lot of good clean water! No grains. Eat in a 8 hour window only each day. Go to bed with the sun. Ground as much as possible especially when sleeping (don’t use the outlet with sleep pads) and in the sun. Eat less food drink more water. Never exercise in blue light.

### halvibe — 2018-05-29T02:58:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-thru-with-little-pain.21404/post-242239>

I had another win. I underwent a dbl hernia operation on May 14th and although they Rx’d Oxycontin-Sodium-Phuc-it-all, I never had to use one of the pills. I didn’t even take Tylenal. I just used ice. Maybe a little more than 20 min. at a time, but it was enough to get me through fairly easily and only needed it the first 3 days.
In “The Leptin Rx”, there’s a section where Dr. Kruse underwent an operation just to prove that ice is such an effective painkiller, that alone deserves way more than just a Nobel Prize. (Although, those folks don’t even have enough credit to kiss Dr. Kruse’s shoes)
I hereby give another LARA Award to Dr. Jack Kruse. (That's for Love, Admiration, Respect and Appreciation)

[https://www.npr.org/sections/thetwo...-why-sunflowers-turn-to-follow-the-sun-solved](https://www.npr.org/sections/thetwo-way/2016/08/05/488891151/the-mystery-of-why-sunflowers-turn-to-follow-the-sun-solved)

"Just like people, plants rely on the daily rhythms of day and night to function," Anne Sylvester, director of the National Science Foundation's Plant Genome Research Program, says in a press release. # Thread: Usefulness of St. John's Wort induced photosensitivity?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/usefulness-of-st-johns-wort-induced-photosensitivity.21410/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### YellowEmperorsNewElixir — 2018-05-29T23:49:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/usefulness-of-st-johns-wort-induced-photosensitivity.21410/post-242321>

_Hypericin phototoxicity induces different modes of cell death in melanoma and human skin cells | Request PDF_. Available from: [https://www.researchgate.net/public...f\_cell\_death\_in\_melanoma\_and\_human\_skin\_cells](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/5508885_Hypericin_phototoxicity_induces_different_modes_of_cell_death_in_melanoma_and_human_skin_cells) \[accessed May 29 2018\].

STUDY CITED:
Hypericin, the major component of St. John's Wort, absorbs light in the UV and visible ranges whereupon it becomes phototoxic through the production of reactive oxygen species. Although photodynamic mechanisms (i.e. through endogenous photosensitizers) play a role in UVA phototherapy for the treatment of skin disorders such as eczema and psoriasis, photodynamic therapy employing exogenous photosensitizers are currently being used only for the treatment of certain forms of non-melanoma skin cancers and actinic keratoses. There are few reports however on its use in treating melanomas. This in vitro study analyses the phototoxic effect of UVA (400-315 nm) - activated hypericin in human pigmented and unpigmented melanomas and immortalised keratinocytes and melanocytes. We show that neither hypericin exposure nor UV irradiation alone reduces cell viability. We show that an exposure to 1 microM UVA-activated hypericin does not bring about cell death, while 3 microM activated hypericin induces a necrotic mode of cell death in pigmented melanoma cells and melanocytes and an apoptotic mode of cell death in non-pigmented melanoma cells and keratinocytes. **_We hypothesis that the necrotic mode of cell death in the pigmented cells is possibly related to the presence of melanin-containing melanosomes in these cells and that the hypericin-induced increase in reactive oxygen species leads to an increase in permeability of melanosomes. This would result in toxic melanin precursors (of an indolic and phenolic nature) leaking into the cytoplasm which in turn leads to cell death. Hypericin localisation in the endoplasmic reticulum in these cells shown by fluorescent microscopy, further support a disruption in cellular processing and induction of cell death.\*_** In contrast, this study shows that cells that do not contain melanosomes (non-pigmented melanoma cells and keratinocytes) die by apoptosis. Further, using a mitochondrial-specific fluorescent dye, we show that intracellular accumulation of hypericin induces a mitochondrial-associated caspase-dependent apoptotic mode of cell death. This work suggests that **_UVA is effective in activating hypericin\*_** and that this phototoxicity may be considered as treatment option in some cases of lentigo maligna or lentigo maligna melanoma that are too large for surgical resection.
**
_\* Emphasis mine_**

Here is a study detailing the mechanism by which hypericin induces what they are calling "phototoxicity". However, this is surely to be a dose-dependent relationship. Could it be possible that lower doses of St. John's Wort could upregulate mitochondrial or melanin sensitivity in a way that is beneficial? What about apoptosis? Could this be effectively integrated for those engaging in hybrid tanning?

Calling all you quantum biologists. Thanks in advance.

### lakers — 2018-07-26T21:43:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/usefulness-of-st-johns-wort-induced-photosensitivity.21410/post-246768>

Bumping this, should one be weary of taking this supplement?

# Thread: Help re sun exposure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-re-sun-exposure.21419/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Steph Roch — 2018-06-01T23:27:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-re-sun-exposure.21419/post-242525>

How can I explain concisely to a geneticist who insists that sun exposure is detrimental to health that it is essential for well-being? I explained some of what Jack has been saying about the necessity of light on skin, posted Dr. Wunsch's YouTube video entitled "Why the Sun is Necessary for Optimal Health" and her response is:

"No. I am a geneticist, and UV radiation causes mutations in your DNA. The melanin your body produces provides a small measure of protection from this, and your body has repair mechanisms to fix the mutations. But sometimes it doesn't, or fixes them badly. Every time you get more sun exposure, you increase the number of times that one of those mistakes happens, and if that mistake is in a cell cycle gene it can cause uncontrolled cell growth, which is cancer. It's like driving without wearing a seatbelt - knowing someone who managed to do that for years and by chance not have an accident doesn't change that wearing a seatbelt is still a really good idea."

Any help is greatly appreciated. Her analogy that sun exposure is like driving without a seatbelt seems utterly ridiculous to me. I am still in the process of learning myself. Thank you.

Update: This is what I replied to her from my knowledge. I hope that I got it all right:

"I really appreciate your comments. I am not trying to be a pain here but let me explain myself. We need sun exposure, and much more than we currently get. The sun (full spectrum) regulates pretty much all of the hormones in our body. It allows us to produce Vitamin D, which is oncoprotective. We need the sun for energy production in our mitochondria. Light basically charges our water battery in our cells, which means it regulates metabolism. All living organisms are based on energy from light that is stored in water. Sunlight induces biochemical reactions in our body. Vitamin D is synthesized under the influence of UV light. The process of photolysis is necessary to properly break down hormones such as adrenaline, testosterone, estrogen, growth hormone etc. in our bloodstream. Each of these hormones have absorption bands in the range of the solar spectrum. UV radiation is therefore essential to help regulate our endocrine system. It is a major circadian and seasonal stimulus. We need light to stimulate the SNC (central body clock) to synchronize all other cellular body clocks in our system. Vitamin D regulates organ function as well as melatonin. Your analogy of the seat belt does not make sense here. The sun clearly provides numerous essential benefits to our body. We need to build up our tolerance, based on seasonal shifts, but staying out of the sun is not going to help us steer clear of cancer. It would be like saying that we shouldn't breathe because we would be breathing in oxygen. Oxygen is the precursor molecule for Reactive Oxygen Species (ROS) or free radical production. These are often the starting points for cancer activation in a cell. Should we then advise against oxygen? I think not. Sorry, but I am trying to understand exactly what you are saying...."

### drezy — 2018-06-02T01:05:34+0300

Just a discussion I was having. Not talking to a geneticist for any particular reason. She just thinks she knows better than I do since she is a geneticist. Haha!

### Lahelada — 2018-06-02T06:12:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-re-sun-exposure.21419/post-242553>

A word to the wise,when talking to doctors arguing "what" is a losing battle. You can show as many papers for as against. She is not incorrect according to what she has read. Ask "WHY " You could ask why all cause mortality is lower with sun exposure, why Dr Hollwich found what he found, why we advise sun avoidance if we know that melatonin is made in the eye, why is everyone talking about melanin but not urocanic acid, why noone is talking about the lack of sulfation of cholesterol to make vit D ,why a mitochondria out of a cancerous cell switches on the oncogenic genes in a previously healthy cell when transplanted Do not make this a case of one upmanship though. Your doc friend as misguided as she seems to be in our eyes is trying to help.Keep communication open.

### JanSz — 2018-06-02T15:06:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-re-sun-exposure.21419/post-242555>

Your perspective on people comes from the cage you were held captive in.
Wise folks understand **there no facts**, only interpretations.
This is why one person's "crazy" is another person's reality.
This makes intuition and insanity relative.
It depends on "who" has "who" locked in 'what' cage.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/#post-196437](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/#post-196437)

### karenr — 2018-06-02T16:06:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-re-sun-exposure.21419/post-242556>

It can be very tempting to engage in ego-driven discussions that aren’t useful. Even when topics of little consequence are on the table, I still often feel that drive to assert that I'm right. Now I'll step back and look at my motivation for that. And sometimes hold back from reacting to the other person right away rather than jumping in with my assertion. Because in that moment of choosing not to go along with the knee jerk reaction, I feel a sense of freedom. Ahh, not having to control everything, what a relief! Being right falls away, and being natural replaces it.

### Butters — 2018-06-06T12:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optometrist-exam-experience.21437/post-242826>

Hello,

I had an eye exam by an optometrist.
The exam was hold in almost pure blue light. Best was around any other room there was more natural light than in the refraction room.
His answer to my comment "this is very bright and garishly" - "everyone see that different" and on my question "do you have any other light for the test" - "we do that so since always".
Great, no wonder I had my problems to focus because blue lights scatters most. My eyes also were burning after a short time and I had to blink all the time.

Should I give a \*\*\*\* on the values and search for an other optometrist?
I think they know what they do there, so they can presribe stronger lenses to people.

What are your thoughts, experiences?

I decided to have some tests that were not usually requested. No GP does these in the UK.

Any input would be appreciated.

Reference range on the right

**INTRACELLULAR CONCENTRATION**
**Zinc 15.3 mg/l** 10.5 - 13.7
**Zinc/Copper ratio 22.2** 9.0 - 16.0

**Ammonia (plasma)\* 47 µmol/L** 8 - 40

**ELEMENTS IN WHOLE BLOOD**
Sodium 1875 mg/l 1820 - 2050
**Potassium 1998 mg/l** 1670 - 1970
Calcium 54.5 mg/l 48.0 - 61.0
Magnesium 39.4 mg/l 34.0 - 48.0
Zinc 7.5 mg/l 5.3 - 6.5
Copper 1.0 mg/l 0.8 - 1.3
Selenium 0.178 mg/l 0.120 - 0.410
Manganese 8.8 µg/l 14.0 - 37.0
Chromium <0.1 µg/l 0.2 - 1.6
Cobalt 0.1 µg/l 0.1 - 0.7
Molybdenum 0.6 µg/l 0.4 - 1.9
**Vanadium 0.60 µg/l** 0.04 - 0.08

TOXIC ELEMENTS
Aluminum  µg/l <= 17.0
**Arsenic 9.0 µg/l** <= 3.1
Cadmium 0.13 µg/l <= 2.30
Gold <0.05 µg/L <= 0.30
**Mercury (Organic / Inorganic) 11.9 ng/mL** 0.0 - 2.0

ESSENTIAL FATTY ACIDS Essential fatty acids,
Omega 3 series
18:3Ω3 Alfa-linolenic acid (ALA) 0.07 % 0.10 - 0.40
20:3Ω3 Eicosatrienoic acid <0.01 % 0.00 - 0.10
**20:5Ω3 Eicosapent. acid (EPA) 0.45 %** 0.60 - 1.70
22:6Ω3 Docosahexaenoic acid 5.99 % 4.00 - 9.40

Omega 6 series
18:2Ω6 Linoleic acid 13.79 % 10.60 - 16.00
**18:3Ω6 Gamma linolenic acid 0.01 %** 0.10 - 0.30
20:2Ω6 Eicosadienoic acid 0.26 % 0.00 - 0.30
20:3Ω6 Dihomogammalinolenic acid 1.06 % 1.20 - 2.20
20:4Ω6 Arachidonic acid (AA) 18.62 % 14.20 - 19.20
22:4Ω6 Docosatetranoic acid 3.72 % 1.60 - 4.40

VITAMINS (MOL/L)

# Thread: Educational videos for friends and family
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educational-videos-for-friends-and-family.21441/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Billybats — 2018-06-06T21:58:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educational-videos-for-friends-and-family.21441/post-242861>

Hello all,

Somebody here at work was asking about my trip to Vermont and always asking about Dr. Kruse information. I was having trouble explaining it to her so I looked for some videos for her to understand what blue light and nnEMF does to our bodies and found 2 that are short and to the point. # Thread: Shakshuka!

**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Phosphene — 2018-06-06T22:50:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shakshuka.21442/post-242863>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Or Shakshouka...however you want to spell it, this recipe for eggs poached in a chunky, spicy tomato sauce is just fab. I know tomatoes are a seasonal thing for many of us, but it’s almost the season for me so I stretched a little. Plus I had canned local tomatoes in my pantry just begging to be used, and we don’t have any (known) issues with nightshade veggies.

I made this almost as written, which is unusual since for me recipes are just suggestions. Only changes were nearly doubling the onions and garlic, a generous tablespoon of turmeric, a large can of salmon flaked in right before the eggs, plentiful cilantro sprinkled on top, and (sadly) no pita.

Ok so I guess it was pretty far from as written.  That’s the beauty of this recipe—it’s easily adapted for what’s available/allowed.

Enjoy!

[https://www.saveur.com/article/Recipes/Eggs-Poached-in-Tomato-Sauce](https://www.saveur.com/article/Recipes/Eggs-Poached-in-Tomato-Sauce)

### Lahelada — 2018-06-07T00:37:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shakshuka.21442/post-242868>

I LOVE Shakshuka. GReat recipe. Add curcumin and this dish rocks.

### Anneh — 2018-06-09T21:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lowbluelights-com-bulbs.21452/post-243087>

Hello everyone,

I am really hoping to get some help from you guys?

In terms of illumination in the evenings, it seems that people are discussing red lights and black lights (and candles and no lights). And from what I can tell Dr. Kruse also doesn't use them, so there must be a reason for that. But I am wondering why people are not using bulbs from lowbluelights.com, as they claim to prevent blue light spectrum, and they are linked to on the Jack Kruse website.

Thank you so much in advance for sharing your wisdom and experience.

Anne

### Anneh — 2018-06-10T07:38:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lowbluelights-com-bulbs.21452/post-243132>

Ok, after a LOT more reading, I am going to assume it's because whatever blue blocking capacity they have, the lux is just too high in them. (I just measured all the different ones I own with an app and the lux is quite high on the regular bulbs. Even on the nightlight and the booklight it is potentially high enough to disrupt melatonin, although I'm still not completely sure about that?.)

I noticed a big difference in my head (migraines and eye pain) from using these bulbs. I simply can't have white lights on in the evening -- they make me sick, so these bulbs have been a big improvement that way. However, the lowbluelight.com bulbs are still quite bright, and I have not noticed any improvement in sleep from using them.

So onward and upward as I continue to reduce and modify the light in my environment at night.

### shiran — 2018-06-10T09:02:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lowbluelights-com-bulbs.21452/post-243133>

hey
It is important at night to protect the eye with eyeglasses and skin with clothing
Artificial light is not optimal only sunlight is optimal and correct for us
I personally and since I do not live alone uses the
[https://lowbluelights.com/product/12-led-gooseneck-usb-light-copy/](https://lowbluelights.com/product/12-led-gooseneck-usb-light-copy/)
I adjust and reduce the light to a minimum possible by a long press behind..
When I put on my UVEX glasses on The light from the lamp does not change and it means that there is no blue light in it
Me and the children also go to sleep very early an hour after sunset so I do not have much dealing with the darkness just going to sleep is the best for us..

# Thread: Spotting Deuterium on MRI
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spotting-deuterium-on-mri.21457/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michalis — 2018-06-11T13:21:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spotting-deuterium-on-mri.21457/post-243176>

I know it is super technical question , but is there a way to interpret the results of an MRI to spot deuterium concentrations in tissues and bone?
For example MRI shows bone edema, how do i go about reading it properly as i got the files.

I understand that Protium has a different spin than Deuterium. How do i spot those deuterium ridden molecules on the MRI with that spin?

I read [https://jackkruse.com/redox-rx-2-biohacking-mri/](https://jackkruse.com/redox-rx-2-biohacking-mri/) but i cant piece the puzzle together.

Can i for example extract the results of the MRI into an excel spreadsheet for each slide and calculate the deuterium content based on the spin detected? Am i too optimistic?

### Michalis — 2018-06-13T12:10:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spotting-deuterium-on-mri.21457/post-243307>

Anyone nows? I know [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) knows

I had a look on the MRI and each slice contains a lot of data.

From what i read on how MRIs work the magnet lines up hydrogen atoms to the field and then an RF pulse is transmitted that excites them. Then the scanner detects the weak radio frequency as the nucleus are relaxing back to their normal spin.

I would expect the deuterium to return a different frequency than protium back to the scanner as it has different mass.

Is this visible on the MRI? if not can we infer anything about this from what we see or the data coded in each slide? Are the scanners normally designed to detect protium only and they sort out only those frequencies out when received by the scanner?

### Michalis — 2018-06-27T18:00:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spotting-deuterium-on-mri.21457/post-244440>

I had a look at cignaturehealth.com website and apparently they got an MRI product which is called D-MRITM scan. It provides a picture of protons and deuterium.

I dont know if it is some kind of contrast or they read an existing mri. Would be interested to find out. Anyone knows anything?

### JanSz — 2018-06-27T18:58:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spotting-deuterium-on-mri.21457/post-244447>

..

### Michalis — 2018-06-27T21:13:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spotting-deuterium-on-mri.21457/post-244453>

I asked, and they said :

_"We could potentially interpret an MRI that has already been done, but it would need to fall within the parameters we need for the scan: performed on a 1.5 Tesla machine with a “gate” or “weight” of 1, and no contrast. If none of your past scans meet those criteria, you would need to obtain a new scan for us to visualize deuterium in your body. The MRI would need to be ordered by your regular physician, and we could provide the specialized interpretation from there"
_
What i am interested in is to see if i have deuterium in my knee's osteoarthritic Bone Marrow Edema.

I read a paper about mitochondria and osteoarthritis and there is definitely a link there. So i was thinking if the mitochondria in the joint starts failing as the heteroplasmy of the tissue rise then maybe the water that is created is not DDW.

paper : Mitochondrial Dysfunction And Oxidative Stress In Osteoarthritis
[https://theses.ncl.ac.uk/dspace/bitstream/10443/1599/1/Gavriilidis 12.pdf](https://theses.ncl.ac.uk/dspace/bitstream/10443/1599/1/Gavriilidis%2012.pdf)

Also considering that i used to be quite fat before that means leptin resistance. Leptin resistance (among other things) is also linked with breaking down the cartilage so possibly that was causing the mitochondria to fail, so i guess heteroplasmy goes up slowly and chronically. Then things break down.. This is what i understand as a layman.

### Dennis Clark — 2020-04-03T19:20:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spotting-deuterium-on-mri.21457/post-288300>

Hi, Michalis and others...

I'm not sure whether anyone commented on MRI regarding protium vs. deuterium. Way back in 'pre-MRI' days the technique was referred to as NMR (nuclear magnetic resonance). We knew then that the proton signal (energy flip) relies on its spin quantum number of one-half. Deuterium has a spin number of 1, which makes it invisible in the NMR's magnetic/RF field scan. We took advantage of the difference for determining structures of organic molecules by comparing NMRs before and after adding D2O to the sample. Hydrogen signals collapsed where deuterium substituted for protium. Fun stuff, eh?

### JanSz — 2020-04-03T19:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spotting-deuterium-on-mri.21457/post-288302>

>
> Hi, Michalis and others...

> I'm not sure whether anyone commented on MRI regarding protium vs. deuterium. Way back in 'pre-MRI' days the technique was referred to as NMR (nuclear magnetic resonance). We knew then that the proton signal (energy flip) relies on its spin quantum number of one-half. Deuterium has a spin number of 1, which makes it invisible in the NMR's magnetic/RF field scan. We took advantage of the difference for determining structures of organic molecules by comparing NMRs before and after adding D2O to the sample. Hydrogen signals collapsed where deuterium substituted for protium. Fun stuff, eh?

[@Dennis Clark](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12267/)
Any comments on
**[extracellular (competent) mitochondria in human blood](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/extracellular-competent-mitochondria-in-human-blood.24856/)**

We knew then that the proton signal (energy flip) relies on its spin quantum number of one-half. Deuterium has a spin number of 1, which makes it invisible in the NMR's magnetic/RF field scan. We took advantage of the difference for determining structures of organic molecules by comparing NMRs before and after adding D2O to the sample. Hydrogen signals collapsed where deuterium substituted for protium. Fun stuff, eh?

Really interesting! Thanks Dennis , for replying to this old thread. Nobody commented really..

Ok, I can spot the edema in the bone and it is really subtle.. So maybe that edema water could be DDW as it is visible.. So cells breaking down for some reason could release their matrix water so this is easily spotted.. So if this is correct my initial thinking is wildly off..

### Dennis Clark — 2020-04-30T18:15:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spotting-deuterium-on-mri.21457/post-289181>

Hi, Teddyjones:

I hope others chime in here, too. I've dug into the deuterium issue for a while, so it may become clearer to me someday. One of the keys, as with many of our body processes, is how and where things work, not necessarily how much you have or consume. Regarding deuterium, what we have in blood plasma is of benefit for its action in UV light from sunshine on skin. That's where deuterium emits UVC light - an internal disinfectant. On the other hand, deuterium in mitochondria clogs up ATPase, thereby inhibiting energy metabolism. Without getting too technical, all hell breaks loose when that happens.

On the other hand, deuterium in mitochondria clogs up ATPase, thereby inhibiting energy metabolism. Without getting too technical, all hell breaks loose when that happens.

Thanks Dennis

# Thread: FINDING AN ANTENNA IN A HAYSTACK I.E. CAR
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-an-antenna-in-a-haystack-i-e-car.21479/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ObstreperousPolarBear — 2018-06-16T04:01:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-an-antenna-in-a-haystack-i-e-car.21479/post-243500>

I have a Jeep Wrangler, 2017. It is awesome and I love it because I can put the top back and enjoy the sun. But there is something in the jeep that is transmitting RFs in a pulsatile fashion.

I HAVE HAD THE DASH RIPPED OUT AND THE RADIO AND SATELLITE RADIO UNPLUGGED. No difference. I HAD THEM FOLLOW THE WIRING FOR THE ANTENNA ALL THE WAY BACK INTO THE ROLL BAR. No difference.

I am not sure what on earth to do next. I wish I had a directional RF meter, so that I could find the antenna that is functioning in the jeep.

I'm frustrated by how hard it is to find this antenna and get it out. The irony is that Jeeps are the most customizable cars on the planet - there has to be a way to deactivate the RF coming from my vehicle.

Where do I go next?

Thanks for the help in advance.

### Billybats — 2018-06-16T13:38:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-an-antenna-in-a-haystack-i-e-car.21479/post-243510>

Hi Leland,

This may help:

[http://www.jeepforum.com/forum/f96/where-sirius-antenna-located-819792/#/topics/819792?page=1](http://www.jeepforum.com/forum/f96/where-sirius-antenna-located-819792/#/topics/819792?page=1)

### OptimalSkr — 2018-06-16T14:41:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-an-antenna-in-a-haystack-i-e-car.21479/post-243515>

Just curious how did you have this type of work done? And what was the cost?

### ObstreperousPolarBear — 2018-06-18T02:49:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-an-antenna-in-a-haystack-i-e-car.21479/post-243643>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=243515)
>
> Just curious how did you have this type of work done? And what was the cost?

Jeep wranglers are unique among cars because they're extremely customizable. The base model has hardly any frills. The idea is that people customize their jeeps and I had no idea how much they put into it! The cool thing about that is that it's easy to take the car apart and put it back together. There are a TON of shops that specialize in just jeeps and customizing them. The dealers are no help at all - they're too busy to even return my messages saying I need an appointment. So I just looked up the local jeep shop and, since they didn't get the problem, they didn't even charge me for it. I'll be headed back there to finally get the job done.

UPDATE I used my meter (which isn't directional, unfortunately) to narrow in on the antenna, and I think I found it under the dash. When we pull it apart again and get the sucker, I will post an update.

Things I learned: You can't just unplug the antenna's connection to its device (GPS, radio, etc), you have to be sure to unplug the power supply. I didn't think of that initially, but now I know.
