# Thread: NutrEval + GI Effects --- go with antibiotics suggestion?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutreval-gi-effects-go-with-antibiotics-suggestion.21480/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gina R — 2018-06-16T04:03:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutreval-gi-effects-go-with-antibiotics-suggestion.21480/post-243502>

HI I posted this in my journal but I'm trying to decide if I should go the antibiotic route, so I'm going to post it here too...
Labs are dropbox links at the bottom. Thanks for reading!

**Basic Info:**

37 yo female, will be 38 in october, single, business owner, NTP
history of "Ulcerative Colitis" Diagnosis and conventional meds, including almost 5ys of Remicade, "symptomatic" from age 19 to age 30. Got of meds 2011, flared 2013 and did FMTs, and since then no "classic UC symptoms".

**Top current health concerns:**

-   fatigue + neuro/cognitive episodes that started late April 2018 after a flight to Austin, TX and then after May 2 following a flight back home to Baltimore, MD.

The best way to describe it when it's happening, it feels like the onset of a mushroom trip --- visual disturbances (less peripheral vision, and extreme fixation/notice on certain details), brainfog, poor memory, finger touch/sensation is off, feel "clumsy" with walking, bumping into things, basically feeling like the onset of magic mushrooms... When it's really bad, I don't feel like I can drive and am so out of it, I don't leave the house. Some background about mushroom use, I had been micro-dosing for about 8 months with taking 1.5-1.75 grams once/month or once every couple months. HOWEVER, during my trip in Austin, I took 1.75 grams twice in a 4-day time span. But, the first ever episode happened the day I landed and BEFORE I took any mushrooms. Not sure if there's any cause/effect relationship there....These episodes last varying lengths --- first one in Austin lasted 3 days, next one in Maryland lasted 2.5 weeks, then 9 days of feeling 80% normal, then next one in Maryland lasted 2 weeks.

Did a niacin challenge test and no effect with 50mg of niacin on empty stomach. Tested that 2x in one day and then 2x another day.

-   "fungal" issues toes/toenails that has been going on for almost 10 years but increasing in severity last year or so, affecting all toenails except one. Previously, lots of oozing sores on the toes, between toes. Toes are sometimes red/purple if cold or really hot. Nails were turning all yellow, thick, discolored. However, as of June, there is evidence of re-growth of normal toenail. In May, I had recently cut out all fermented foods & kombucha and was applying iodine topically.
-   abdominal distention/bloat for about 2 years now. On and off but last 6 months or so, consistently distended no matter what I eat. Previously tried low-fodmap and I thought I had some relief but that didn't last more than 4 months or so. Prior practitioner suspected SIBO, said test was negative Spring 2017. There's more history here but trying to keep to most relevant. Currently very distended and stools are Bristol 6-7 with other daily GI distress.
-   missed menstrual cycles. I was regular for about 5 years until Spring 2017 after I traveled to the Middle East and didn't get my period there which I should have. Since April 2017 to June 2018, I've only had 6 periods.
-   facial puffiness/body composition puffiness.

**Daily priorities:**

-   Morning light with mobility movement, meditation almost daily, mid-day sun when it's out (most times in bathing suit) and evening walk after sunset. I need to be better at doing this in the winter here in Maryland but I do this now consistently. Red light at night, redjuvenator, wear blue blockers, acupressure mat 3-4x/week, epsom salt baths 2-3x/week. I wake on my own about 6am. Acupuncture 1/week. Currently have been doing electro-scalp acupuncture after recent episodes started.
-   I haven't done any form of intense exercise since mid April 2018 before my trip to Austin.
-   RO water + liquid minerals added.
-   Currently taking naps almost daily.
-   \*EDITED TO ADD\* "Paleo" diet, egg yolks, white rice, ghee. Was eating some forms of organic corn but stopped when recent LRA revealed strong reaction. (The first food template I did was GAPS, then Paleo, then AIP for about a year and recently swung back out into "paleo".) Working on keeping complex carbs higher than in years past but still keeping fat high.

**Current dilemma with next step bc of a couple conflicting opinions:**

-   Bc of GI effects results, bc of overgrowth of ecoli and a few others, functional practitioner/DOM suggests 2 weeks of oral tetracycline then 10 days fluconozale along. Taking a particular probiotic orally, via vagina, via suppository and up nose while taking the tetracyline and fluconozale. Continuing with the probiotic for 6 months afterwards.
-   Current acupuncturist who is also DOM suggest herbal formula to combat the overgrowth and not to do the rx antibiotics...but that would take 6+ months to balance. Also suggests to test for lyme and possibly add in herbal formula protocol for lyme.

**Questions:**

-   Main question - do antibiotics + antifungal course? And if not, what is a better option to tackle the GI issues and these neuro/cognitive episodes + fatigue?
-   Test for lyme? There's lots of controversy about testing, so which one to do and also does it matter if "lyme" or not?

There is SO much more information but this is what's present and current.

I don't have many supportive relationships here/community, so my temporary relocation to Austin is to explore some professional and personal possibilities. Winters here also don't work for me, so I need to be somewhere year-round with good sun along with lots of like-minded people/community. My optimal health seeking also radiates out to hoping to be able to have a child at some point. Lots of financial issues, current lease is ending and also a reason of going out to Austin to stay with a friend to regain some footing with finances.

I could go on and I'm glad to share any more information. I hope Jack may see this and be able to put in some input about my current dilemma with next step?

Thanks in advance and for everything you all do.

**GI Effects May 2018** ---
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/j4qg8tj4v3n43v9/GI Effects May 2018 wo name.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/j4qg8tj4v3n43v9/GI%20Effects%20May%202018%20wo%20name.pdf?dl=0)

**NutrEval Plasma April 2018** ---
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/vp15vwulytdnn1i/Nutreval Plasma April 2018 wo name.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/vp15vwulytdnn1i/Nutreval%20Plasma%20April%202018%20wo%20name.pdf?dl=0)

I have 23&me done but I just have the raw data so far. Not sure what system is best to run it through to extract the data? I'm open to running through whichever database is recommended to get the best information from it.

Thank you!

Whilst I'm protecting the effects of inbalanced blue light through my eyes, I'm concerned about the effects of blue light hitting the rest of the skin on my face. Are there any recommended blue blocking screen attachments that can span the size of my laptop screen so it blocks all blue light coming from the screen itself?

That way I'll be blocking blue light from hitting the rest of my face too. I know about internal blue blocking screen protection such as f.lux but I've heard they aren't up to scratch. If its misguided there, please correct me.

Oz

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mike David — 2018-06-27T16:28:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters.21526/post-244429>

steamed 10 oysters. 2 didn’t open and the grocer doesn’t want anyone getting sick so he says toss em. Should I just boil them longer till they open and eat them or open them myself and eat them or toss em? Does the dha and other positive benefits decrease dramatically when steaming? Is 10 a day a good amount? Are wild better than farmed? Should I buy Chesapeake because their the closest? Are there positive benefits from the salt water juice there in and if so what are they?

### JanSz — 2018-06-27T17:11:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters.21526/post-244432>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-82#post-241765](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-82#post-241765)

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-80#post-240559](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-80#post-240559)

### JanSz — 2018-06-27T17:19:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters.21526/post-244433>

There are two types of oysters.

Eastern oyster (Crassostrea virginica)
Invasive Oyster Crassostrea gigas

We usually see the Eastern type.

At my Asian supermarket NJ 07054 I can get both types.
The Gigas are from Pacific but in the previous post you can see analysis of Gigas from France, latter that analysis is referenced and used for evaluation of Eastern types.

Fresh meat from Gigas I can get in one pound containers, $10/pound

That amount of meat from Eastern oysters would cost $100/pound and up.

....
Per the analysis
I concluded that oysters are the only seafood that I know of that have less EPA and more DHA.

////////////

In stores or restaurants, further classifications are often made, by place of origins.
But eventually all of them are small (Eastern Oysters), that is small in physical size, when comparing to gigas.

////

### Mike David — 2018-06-27T17:39:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters.21526/post-244437>

Raw fresh oysters are the best IMO. Of course . You get all the viruses and bacteria too. Raw seafood has great benefits! But steamed are good too of course.. I only eat mine raw, I did not find them as delicious cooked.

> Is 10 a day a good amount? Are wild better than farmed? ?

I think 10 is a great amount daily, about what I eat on average too. I guess farmed are as great as wild ones, not any big difference there. If you pick yourself, be careful to not pick in polluted waters.

> Should I buy Chesapeake because their the closest? ?

IDK

> Are there positive benefits from the salt water juice there in and if so what are they?

Sure! The water is awesome! The salt in it.... and I bet there were other thing that I cant remember - I always drink the water too.

# Thread: What to do while lying down?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BrunoB — 2018-06-29T16:42:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/post-244561>

Hi good day. What do you recommend doing while I'm lying down waiting for sunrise? I clean my nails, I listen to the radio and I should start meditating. What do you suggest? Thank you.

### drezy — 2018-06-29T18:25:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/post-244570>

I prefer going back to sleep. I'm fortunate enough to be tuned to wake within a 20 minute window pre sunrise each day.

in lieu of that you may want to experiment and note daily changes with things like:

Chanting "Ommmmm" (It's a phonon thing)
Concentrating on out-breath emphasis (aka deeper outbreaths)
Start on some ice water prior to the light for better light absorption \*
Work on fuller recall of your dreams
Focus on one thing in your life that you are extremely grateful for

\* - My rough notes mention previous posts of early morning CT shunting sex steroid hormones. I don't think ice water would have as pronounced an effect, but it's worth monitoring.

### caroline — 2018-06-30T03:23:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/post-244597>

I recommend - just being still ........

why do you need to do something at this important time of day???????

Just being still is the very best meditation ....in my opinion.

It kind of empties my mind of clutter .......especially at the ocean

### Mike David — 2018-07-04T00:12:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/post-244904>

Appreciate nature. The complexity of just a tree is amazing. Notice every last detail then look harder there’s even more details. Don’t just say yea that’s a tree whoohoo look at how it’s constructed think about all the little critters it supports in their daily activities ext... I notice something unique in my habitat every day it never lets me down.

### Meli — 2018-07-16T00:26:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/post-245939>

I get up and lay down on the floor with a blanket in front of a sliding door that I open up. The sun shines on me as it rises. Once it is shining I get up and go outside on the deck and get a shot of sun sans blanket. Then I work my way outside and stay out for about 2 hours - I meditate, stretch, pray, rub my feet, do a breathing exercise... sit and look at the sun and the birds.

# Thread: Mitochondrial Heteroplasmy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-heteroplasmy.21555/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason Coates — 2018-07-03T09:41:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-heteroplasmy.21555/post-244832>

[https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-018-04797-2](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-018-04797-2)

Vital mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) populations exist in cells and may consist of heteroplasmic mixtures of mtDNA types. The evolution of these heteroplasmic populations through development, ageing, and generations is central to genetic diseases, but is poorly understood in mammals. Here we dissect these population dynamics using a dataset of unprecedented size and temporal span, comprising 1947 single-cell oocyte and 899 somatic measurements of heteroplasmy change throughout lifetimes and generations in two genetically distinct mouse models. We provide a novel and detailed quantitative characterisation of the linear increase in heteroplasmy variance throughout mammalian life courses in oocytes and pups. We find that differences in mean heteroplasmy are induced between generations, and the heteroplasmy of germline and somatic precursors diverge early in development, with a haplotype-specific direction of segregation. We develop stochastic theory predicting the implications of these dynamics for ageing and disease manifestation and discuss its application to human mtDNA dynamics.

### Jason Coates — 2018-07-03T09:42:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-heteroplasmy.21555/post-244833>

A mathematical model that can predict the nature of the mitochondrial DNA a child will inherit from its mother was part of a study that also found that maternal age counts in mitochondrial health, too.

This part of the study, in mice, showed that the older the mother, the higher is the risk that mutated, disease-causing mitochondrial DNA will be passed along to offspring.

The study, “[Large-scale genetic analysis reveals mammalian mtDNA heteroplasmy dynamics and variance increase through lifetimes and generations](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-018-04797-2),” was published in the journal _[Nature Communications](https://www.nature.com/ncomms/)_.

Most cells have multiple mitochondria, which house the mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) — an integral component of the cell’s energy-producing machine.

mtDNA can acquire mutations over time, which can lead to different mtDNA sequences within different mitochondria of the same cell. This phenomenon, called heteroplasmy, occurs when a cell has some mitochondria with a mutation in its mtDNA and some that does not.

Heteroplasmy can influence the inheritance and onset of serious mitochondrial diseases, which develop due to mutations in key mitochondrial genes.

People inherit their mitochondria from their mothers. If the mother has heteroplasmy, it can be difficult to determine how this will affect an unborn child.

In particular, scientists don’t know how heteroplasmy changes over time in women, and whether the risk of transmitting a disease-carrying mitochondrial mutation increases or decreases.

Researchers in Europe analyzed mtDNA segregation in two mouse lines with heteroplasmy, using mothers with a range of ages. Measurements were then taken from 1,947 oocytes \[immature egg cells\] and 899 pups.

Using this extensive data, they were able to create a mathematical model that was descriptive of mtDNA inheritance — they transfer of mitochondrial DNA from mother to offspring, taking maternal age into account.
[https://mitochondrialdiseasenews.co...ail&utm\_term=0\_fdcf314ce5-8ecfca31ec-71855073](https://mitochondrialdiseasenews.com/2018/06/29/older-moms-more-likely-to-pass-mutated-mitochrondrial-dna-to-offspring-study-finds/?utm_source=Mito+Disease&utm_campaign=8ecfca31ec-RSS_EMAIL_CAMPAIGN&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_fdcf314ce5-8ecfca31ec-71855073)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### Jenelle — 2018-07-05T19:16:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measure-ketones.21563/post-245049>

Blood ketone measurement is by far the most accurate method of testing.

The meters themselves are not that expensive ~ it's the cost of the strips that can be prohibitive for many.

[@shiran](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20240/) ... Best wishes for your friend or family member.

### Marko Pollo — 2018-07-07T16:35:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measure-ketones.21563/post-245226>

Check out Ketonix. They sell a ketone breath meter that works as advertised. Non invasive way to get feedback when going Keto and a way to see how different changes affect ketone levels. Site has good info. I don’t use mine often now, but when I started Keto I liked seeing how my ketone charged during the day and climbed over time.

### Jenelle — 2018-07-07T17:31:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measure-ketones.21563/post-245231>

> [Marko Pollo said:](/goto/post?id=245226)
>
> Check out Ketonix. They sell a ketone breath meter that works as advertised. Non invasive way to get feedback when going Keto and a way to see how different changes affect ketone levels. Site has good info. I don’t use mine often now, but when I started Keto I liked seeing how my ketone charged during the day and climbed over time.

Had one when they first came out years ago ~ and yes, it was interesting. But for anyone who wants/needs true accuracy, blood ketone measurement is the way to go. Not knowing what sort of situation we are talking about here ... I'm assuming health condition ~ and I would not recommend the Ketonix under those circumstances.

### shiran — 2018-07-08T07:12:23+0300

# Thread: 5G and nnEMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-and-nnemf.21606/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NADme — 2018-07-10T11:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-and-nnemf.21606/post-245507>

Well Jack, you are not as dumb as Janz looks. I am about 3/4 of the way through your vid on 5G. I have many comments and a few intuitive theories that may serve in future exploration since we all cant obviously move to the equator. As John Lennon said, there are no problems, only solutions. This is were I get semi frustrated reading your forums. It seems much of the time you point to the big green monster without a solid means to mitigate.

In a past life I was a classical guitarist who studied with the worlds best. Harmonics and the effects on the human psyche was at the forefront of my attention for years. The reason being is that I had amazing luminaries shape me to understand that performance was not about entertainment.

IT WAS ABOUT BEING A DOCTOR OF ETHOS!...emphasis added

Sidebar: The NAD story is essential to me not for the reasons most assert. The Ca homeostasis is key and the three ribose expressions from that coenzyme creates intracellular homeostasis of Ca2+. It was asserted in commentary on my reads that lack of intracellular Ca homeostasis was at the root of most cancers. I would appreciate your comments on that notion.

Where does this self inflicted idea of being a Doctor of Ethos come from? Pre Renaissance, Medieval, two texts existed. I have not tried to see if there were any available in modern translation, assuredly would have been Greek etymology. They were known as the doctrine of ethos and doctrine of pathos. **Rx for the moving of emotions.**

In our society, with the exception of Jazz, we listen to music in major (Ionian) and minor (Aolian) keys. Keys are also known as modes. There were 5 other modes in addition to the two cited. Think of modes as moving the start point of the octave and centering the start at various root or tonic notes within the octave. By example C to C, all white keys, in the key of C major (Ionian). In the key of G you should have a black key F# (f sharp). However within the modes there were no black keys also known as accidentals, realizing G to G all white keys as the mixolydian mode, based on the 5th, C1 D2 E3 F4 G5 A6 B7.

That interval from the tonic of C to the 5th at G is known as the DOMINANT.

This is where it gets good: They would shape character by listening to certain modes. A warrior would listen to the dominant/mixolydian because of the aggression it would instill. Other modes such as locrian or phyrgian were somber and dark and applied to poets and those to induce the melancholy you hear all through Renaissance music.

**Fast forward:** Here we are confronted with all of the criminal atrocities rendered by a 5G assault and make no mistake Jack they are picking up where Maurice Strong left off regarding eugenics and Agenda 21 published by the UN, restated by the Georgia Stones and since the genome has been mapped seek to reduce population to 500 million globally. They the proverbial do not need us anymore. The world of have and have not as two on different sides of the coin is over now with AI and blockchain instigation.

As I listen to you pointing out the problems as you do so well it makes me reflect on past hypothesis and how it may possibly give us a tool or tools to push back.

One of my projects was to use tactile transducers to be able to sync your resonant signature and then to introduce an inverted wave less 1 less full form (causing near wave form flat line), similar to a helix, to see the effects on the subjects density. I theorized that it would impact density and enable what I was developing as intelligence infusion, where information on any given subject would be infused since water has memory. I also use to float tank extensively.

**Sidebar:** Did you know that when MgSO4 (Epsom salts) has sound projected on it that it will increase in temperature? Sound converted to heat.

With everything going on with all of mans brilliance as we race to assimilation or self destruction, it strikes me that we all can't wear faraday cages.

**POTENTIAL #1
HOWEVER:** As extrapolated from above: What if within the octave as you cite we were able to develop a means to use the modes and tetra modes combined with sound induction to cancel the signal or modify the nnEMF wave form assaults with a self-imposed inverted or cancelling signal in such away that we preserve ourselves?

**POTENTIAL #2**
I had a friend who made a Tesla cloud buster. I tried it Jack, I was literally erasing clouds from the end of his driveway. When Fukushima happened and we witnessed the radiation move in clumps, not waves, via the jet stream that comes in over BC/Vancouver, turns South down through the great plains and the continues East when hitting Texas almost following Interstate 10 as considered the primary path of that jet stream.

**My friend theorized that we could set up a number of these cloud busters and use it as a "Harmonic Fence."**

The G5 map and the metros as you cited may be the explanation for the phenomena I have not been able to explain.

I live in close proximity to the Kennedy Space Center and use to work Shuttle ops and other Space transport systems. This place is turning into a real shit hole. Anyhow, there has been on at least two occasions that I went to TN and DC and started dumping water like crazy, 20 lbs in a few days. I was pissing to the extent that the boy in the Brussels fountain had nothing on me. I return to Florida and it all stacks back on. I tested for mold exposure, negative.

When I backdrop with your theory about cellular dehydration, sodium and potassium wasting come to minds but also the effects of the 5G as you pointed out and how maybe some harmonic shift enabled the water shedding.

As a young man I was able to sense tube TV's, that 60 cycle 3 phase running through tubes, being on in peoples homes from the sidewalks definitely their front doors. It felt like a buzzing or vibration that was distinct to me. I am highly sensitive to sound and harmonics, besides my physical but even more because we were taught as a musician to instill the ethos of the piece. To make people cry over a lament or get up and want to march when playing a march, etc. I am hypersensitive to resonance to the extent I can reproduce 440 cps from memory and can feel overall resonant signatures from people instinctively. It can be a big nuisance at times.

This is a free form rant, I understand it is all over the place, I sense relevance.

Looking for some leads for low-emf vehicles...

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/), you suggested an old jeep but what could be 2nd best to that? Looking to trade my 2010 prius in at a carmax ASAP. My trade-in value will probably be about 7k or so.

Lots of conflicting info online and so many rabbit holes...

Has anyone else been through this process/research? What could be next in line after an old jeep as I need a reliable vehicle to get me halfway across the country several times!

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-23T23:26:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-emf-vehicle-needed-asap.21613/post-256373>

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-24T15:45:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-emf-vehicle-needed-asap.21613/post-256406>

I imagine on the old carbureted cars, the only major emf that is of much significance would be the alternator. I wonder if one could get a DC motor put in place of an alternator that would do it. Alternatively I imagine you can stick a faraday cage around an alternator. THe whole wifi run car has sounded insane to me even before worrying about emf's just from the perspective of viral hackability. I had an carby car until about 4 years ago and always thought I would be one of the few driving around if even a EMP weapon was released, though I'm sure I would have been relieved of that privilege quite fast.

One other point. In Japan I used to see trucks dragging a rubber cord off the back of their trucks. I wonder if you attach that to your car with some sort of titanium wire if that would created a frequent ground. Ofcourse if there is a lightning strike you may get hummed pretty bad, but if you are that unlucky maybe this life was not meant for you. LOL

### Inger — 2018-11-24T16:47:28+0200

### tsoang — 2018-07-13T18:12:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendation-for-treating-health-issues.21632/post-245822>

Hi everyone,

I'm new to this forum. I just discovered Jack through YouTube and Podcasts. I'm not sure where to post this so I'll start with the beginners area. I had a sudden health issue a few months ago in January and ever since the issues continued. My symptoms are brain fog and dizziness, shortness of breath, chest pain, body feels weak. Went to the doctors office. They found nothing wrong with me in the blood test, urine test, and heart ultra sound.

I just want to know where I should start reading and learning in this forum. I see there are lab sections, what kind of testing I should do to find out my current health issue.

Thanks so much for your help and pointers.

### caroline — 2018-07-14T08:29:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendation-for-treating-health-issues.21632/post-245869>

Hi and welcome.

Please start a journal in the Optimal journal section and give Dr. K. your health history, also your Mom's and Gran's.

Where do you live? work?

Have you watched Jack's Nourish Vermont Podcasts? there are three now and they are hugely important.

Please read the beginners guides etc. and then look thru the list of blogs and see what might be relevant to you.

Do you have Jack's book? The Epi Paleo RX ....it is the first few years of the blog.

If you can manage it - become a Gold member and you will have access to all Jack's monthly blogs and the follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything.

We have a thread on the June Q&A - have a look.

What can we call you? A better pic would be nice - Jack Likes to know who he is talking to.

### tsoang — 2018-07-15T04:52:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendation-for-treating-health-issues.21632/post-245897>

Hi Caroline, thanks so much for the info. I'll definitely start a journal in the optimal journal section. I have watched Jack's Vermont video and many others on YouTube. Still learning and listening. So much information. I'll definitely buy the book as well.

\-Steve

### caroline — 2018-07-15T09:49:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendation-for-treating-health-issues.21632/post-245901>

Hi Steve ......nice pic!

Check out the Patreon blogs - they are very affordable.

Where you live and work is so important.

How is your sleep? libido?

nnEMF?

what happened in January? new job? new home? smart meter? new cell towers in your area? 5G? ....what changed????

# Thread: First Vitamin D Results from UK - 64 mg/ml
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-vitamin-d-results-from-uk-64-mg-ml.21654/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Neil Taylor — 2018-07-17T18:20:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-vitamin-d-results-from-uk-64-mg-ml.21654/post-246074>

Just had my first test around the summer solstice in the UK. It has been a good summer and I've spent a lot of time outside as naked as possible.

**My results are 160 nmol/L or 64 ng/ml** which I believe [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) considers the low end of optimal.

These are completely un-suplemented. I was hoping for closer to 80 ng/ml but I'm pleased with this. My conclusion is that Jack is right - we need to spend as much time in the sun as naked as possible. It is difficult to make vitamin d and it is only going to get more difficult with 5G.

# Thread: Hilarious Dr. Kraus review
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hilarious-dr-kraus-review.21682/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Brett Jacques ND — 2018-07-22T22:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hilarious-dr-kraus-review.21682/post-246459>

I received this interesting review of dr. krause/kruse information
Hilarious!
**[Cold Shock Therapy: Too Shocking](http://chadco.trk.klaviyomail.com/wf/click?upn=LT1QtOY1WAhKxzb1On5Gc2lrPyBSHZq4zZyay7kfrjTcmY3ddMaqqqVDPmC2SyKtw5pCaemObFEUE2N19mn5xgSJtacTVPUQ-2FQTdUwDr1IPPlE38Ki2XF5Re6lEfpEzGcEq1tznXpqlApN9BlDEA8mIIqj9oaNmwYit7JuxkNg-2B1RP3TZukq11ASE7WQdx2lwJgmmljrsR1jh6EvRQEYc56Cc9guJ2aKfxKTc9TzPwlXuPr1Nl8wujbv7AAD9EXHQpQuTSeWWs-2BTz1xKUW4ZBZInv7cGkfa558C9Ht-2BGoMQ2FFq2Ucuvr1vbWQPgObLQx5xhj1dqy1vaBWBv5yydaYSgt-2Fe3MjVNONF-2BXmwuGlpMtGjcJatpY9DfDsVMQj1szcJJwGi-2BweJbCAxZa3X1uw-3D-3D_5kDdq-2FckLHUiutNw0-2Fk-2BQ08h7LEO8DZ-2FirTOs9LMPGj1qqIk9eL3ZrWyCgHweY8Wv-2BVZ2hjJbm7ChVeCfrJO9GpkSuHqC3OP6qAV84fZayFr137-2BhJDD2RqsN4so5xDmvIGxlelFWSX4xjpoKa9enSJyCmSOOGdB27JTfbvC7pZeD9cFKnYSuX5zXfiHjZ7l-2BYObZK-2BkNRCkuUfR09ll0CqAhkhQgP16sKiR88IfM7OSG67kI5TCIwP-2B1-2BBh-2Bo0dwId819fNFBaZzZl2CSk-2F-2FqA77eNGuIEiCOwNOVShrrVFT62-2FRqDxW-2F9eX5r45AhgJTbeHrnWUHfY-2F-2F-2Bgq37xSOw8QcHUOAiR5D6ekh3BlxscIYvVaL6bdtJvbP3aRqhZ)**
This blog is a wordhack on Dr. Jack Kruse the neurosurgeon, biohacker, Paleo blogger, who is a proponent of cold shock therapy, sunlight, and high dose vitamin D.

Kruse runs his findings through the lens of endocrine hormones, which he says control everything in the body. But what makes and controls hormones – what are their building blocks? Protein, fats, vitamins, and minerals with the most important mineral being magnesium.

What kind of attention does Kruse pay to magnesium? Not so much! Of course he says people should take magnesium but he adds that since it causes diarrhea you can’t take too much. He does not mention that it’s required for making energy, for vitamin D metabolism, hormones production, and supporting the thyroid and adrenals, which are among magnesium’s 800 different enzyme processes in the body.

According to his book outline on Amazon, Kruse is “A champion of ‘biohacking’ the art of tinkering with one’s own biology.” Of course when you use faulty data to perform your hack you are just going to get faulty results. Most biohackers put down the ineptness of medicine when it comes to chronic disease but they somehow think that ineptness doesn’t translate into the laboratory. They recommend all manner of lab testing so you can manipulate your diet, detox, supplements, and drugs – as if you actually know what you are doing!

First of all – labs are not foolproof and never offer the optimum values for a healthy body. And in the case of serum magnesium testing, they are just plain inaccurate. So why would you take Krause’s advice and spend a few thousand dollars every 3 months to run tests that aren’t accurate?

Step back a moment and look where biohacking to control “nature” has gotten us. Environmental pollution, rainforest devastation, fracking, nuclear meltdown to name a few. We’ve already biohacked our bodies to near death with antibiotics and magnesium depleting drugs. What makes a person think they can know enough to biohack their health by tinkering with their biology?

Krause makes the statement that “sunlight on our skin has our mitochondria create water in our cells” but he leaves out all the biochemical steps – such as performed by magnesium and other important vitamin and mineral cofactors. Biochemically it is actually well-absorbed minerals getting into cells that ultimately pulls in water? He’s big on mitochondria creating energy but that only happens if the Krebs cycle has enough magnesium for 6 of its 8 steps. It seems that Krause is missing the biochemical steps and just focusing on what he imagines to be the driving force of cellular quantum physics.

Energy medicine, including energy from the sun, to properly work in the body, needs minerals - especially magnesium. Minerals function as electron donors and acceptors in all biochemical reactions.

I’m probably being a bit too rough on Krause but I guess I’m just frustrated with all the “breakthrough” theories that the population has suffered over the past 50 years! Look at a short list of what we have endured with alternative medicine constantly turning up new theories and writing the definitive health book.

· Wheat intolerance, Gluten intolerance, Grain intolerance – Dozens of books

· Histamine intolerance – Over a dozen books

· Oxalate intolerance – A half dozen books

· Lectin intolerance: _The Plant Paradox: The Hidden Dangers in Healthy Food_

· _Leptin resistance_: Kruse’s book _Epi-Paleo Rx_

· MTHFR – Over a dozen books

In his book, _Epi-Paleo Rx_, Kraus enthuses about his personal cure for obesity and chronic disease and says it can be yours. But it comes with the price of wading through a few hundred pages of complicated hormone biochemistry and alternative medicine knowledge that Kruse has acquired in the last 7 years. It ends with him telling you that the cure is to pack your body with 40 pounds of ice to drop your temperature to 55 degrees and all will be well.

I am concerned that Kruse is offering an unattainable “cure” and his cold shock therapy will attract people who are too sick and toxic to take on such extreme therapies. After all, the means by which cold shock, heat shock, and extreme exercise work is to “damage” the body enough that it creates body-wide emergency survival reactions that stimulate various hormones and anti-inflammatory chemicals. Researchers get all excited when they measure these chemical reactions, but at what cost to the individual? What other chemicals are being depleted. In order to produce these hormones and chemicals how much have they dipped into their reserves of nutrients? These are unanswered questions.

After all, even simple sauna therapy depletes magnesium that most people don’t even realize and certainly don’t replace. The day after I did a number of ice water plunges going in and out of a sauna and Jacuzzi I developed a cold sore on my lip, which I hadn’t experienced in years. I presume the cold shock woke up my dormant herpes simplex virus and brought it to the surface. Such things happen under stress. Such stress under the guise of a health therapy will not be good for everyone.

Learn about magnesium, and much more, by subscribing to my health tips by email. You can subscribe here: [https://drcarolyndean.com/subscribe](http://chadco.trk.klaviyomail.com/wf/click?upn=LT1QtOY1WAhKxzb1On5Gc2lrPyBSHZq4zZyay7kfrjQfeMbapv4z0oqR2D2OJDTT8T1hmI74Zz6Hz3-2FBmswLXq8blzCuBujfCLEp1L0GW-2FxoL8obphNbXNFPCQYBgN1eO5zTyRMfgbZtCJhh5c-2F2YHlyUv1V7dPjxPF6f1dEyMJdYMvom-2FiuRABDVb-2BnvgF2iJAqCYaMpVxBF4jj41Qr1lKTHpSdO-2BEdZHDRNUfmMf5ftwixqsh4qO6WlDOs8CFrWYq3FcEplRSyGuNX5-2F2KC62p1QUP5GhSFwRRHLwkGNcb7IBaCZRhUq3X65jiCU-2Bkic2pwD0J2JhqRwxY2e-2Bz2fNaixl6-2FPbz-2F1bie1DWXT8-3D_5kDdq-2FckLHUiutNw0-2Fk-2BQ08h7LEO8DZ-2FirTOs9LMPGj1qqIk9eL3ZrWyCgHweY8Wv-2BVZ2hjJbm7ChVeCfrJO9GpkSuHqC3OP6qAV84fZayFr137-2BhJDD2RqsN4so5xDmvIGxlelFWSX4xjpoKa9enSJyCmSOOGdB27JTfbvC7pZeD9cFKnYSuX5zXfiHjZ7l2IZRSGzwD3JWJEmANq-2BtdX2bdc-2BYe6zk3VSLzVm-2Bar1MnnLfimAMtK7uWmFzBgPFJ3Mw0-2BHucpEBLq51g0LzwHKjqR-2FO5TVVxxc-2F8Ux-2FUNVOhxU61X-2FZtXIFUua9bPCRsuYjDrYeRRwW2CAEm2mjHyGB1mViCN5UAJVzt-2BXyDU-2B8OeOi5fGjmOADXCQEvFnQ)

# Thread: When staying inside is so addictive...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-staying-inside-is-so-addictive.21691/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tarun — 2018-07-23T22:51:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-staying-inside-is-so-addictive.21691/post-246536>

Hi all,

As you're probably well aware, Dr. Jack recommends getting outside as much as possible when the sun is out.

I'm having difficulties implementing this and I was wondering what your experiences have been and if you have any advice to ease the transition.

To keep it simple, let's keep the discussion to leisure time. I find this time is getting completely filled up with
catching up on my mail and RSS reader
browsing
catching up on my twitter timeline
catching up on my podcasts
reading e-books
watching educational YouTube videos.

I work in IT and I feel I constantly have to read and watch videos to keep my skills and knowledge up to date and stay competitive. I fear if I spend this time outside I pay the opportunity cost of losing out on growth in knowledge and skills.

I rarely go outside during work days, on the weekend I do force myself to go outside by taking about 7x 45-minute walks spread out over the weekend. I find being outside very boring without at least a podcast on, and am glad to return back indoors to my cozy workstation.

Have any of you made the transition from indoor junkie to outdoor junkie, without sacrificing your hirability?

### drezy — 2018-07-23T23:10:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-staying-inside-is-so-addictive.21691/post-246537>

I'm not sure anyone can help you.

Still, do you know that there is now a portable compute platform called a laptop?

Are you actually a penguin?

>
> Have any of you made the transition from indoor junkie to outdoor junkie, without sacrificing your hirability?

Your hire-ability will drop when you will start getting sick. And you will will at some point if you keep doing that, the question is when. I suggest taking it easy with the indoor living and go out and enjoy the sunshine...If you are young and healthy you can afford to be broke...

### Michalis — 2018-07-23T23:39:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-staying-inside-is-so-addictive.21691/post-246541>

Dude if you are feeling bored outside without a podcast on you are getting it wrong big time... I don't want to be a dick but i will be.. Go and meet some people, go and chase girls/boys/penguins, climb a mountain, do some workouts, go camping, go to a festival. All the blue light is killing your dopamine.. You need to do radical changes.

You are a human being, not a commodity to market yourself.. Unfortunately this is how people are getting programmed these days..

Go and buy this book and read it under strong sun. It is amazing. And i think it will help you see the matrix your thought is stemming from

[https://www.amazon.co.uk/What-about-Me-struggle-market-based/dp/1922247375](https://www.amazon.co.uk/What-about-Me-struggle-market-based/dp/1922247375)

Enjoy

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-staying-inside-is-so-addictive.21691/post-246542>

Buy one of those laptop covers so you can use it outside. Everything you can do inside, you can do outside.

### Tarun — 2018-07-24T07:19:08+0300

Thanks for the advice.

I'm not actually a penguin, but I know a real picture is required. Before submitting this post I spent over 30 minutes browsing my external hd for a picture of me but couldn't find one. Still I could've made one and uploaded it.

Will try the book.

Do have almost no social life.

I didn't know laptop covers stopped the EMF from laptops and thought having an uncovered laptop on your lap outside would be worse for your body and sitting in front of a desktop inside.

### drezy — 2018-07-24T15:58:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-staying-inside-is-so-addictive.21691/post-246586>

Use a table not your lap
Use ethernet

If you have no ethernet port on your rig, these are everywhere:
[https://www.amazon.com/Anker-Portab...8&qid=1532434046&sr=8-9&keywords=usb+ethernet](https://www.amazon.com/Anker-Portable-Aluminum-Ethernet-Supporting/dp/B00PC0H9IE/ref=sr_1_9?ie=UTF8&qid=1532434046&sr=8-9&keywords=usb+ethernet)

Use this if you need a longer cable:
[https://www.amazon.com/UbiGear-4445...34206&sr=1-3&keywords=300+feet+ethernet+cable](https://www.amazon.com/UbiGear-4445608-Ethernet-Network-Patch/dp/B00BNL001Y/ref=sr_1_3?s=electronics&ie=UTF8&qid=1532434206&sr=1-3&keywords=300+feet+ethernet+cable)

You can even find an external usb keyboard if you want to go a bit further. It's what my wife uses.

Based on one of your previous posts you mention your sunset time in July it looks like you may be near 57ºN. If that's the case you really better soak in summer sun while you can.

Hi Jenny,

I am a total non-techs and very ES. I too, downloaded IRIS. I fiddled around with it and got to the place where you see an option for :"Type"--there, I click on "Sleep" for most of my reading online.
I feel a quite palpable sense of relief when I click on "Sleep" and a reddish shade covers the screen.
When I can't see something because the red cancels it out, I switch briefly to "Health" to get the missing words, then back to "Sleep: type. Hope that helps you.

I was wondering if anyone knows much about grounding foot wear. I have been looking at getting a pair of fibram 5 finger shoes but not sure if there as good for the grounding aspect as earth runners?

### johnsonmd — 2018-08-10T18:16:20+0300

I looked at grounding straps and it got me thinking... If I am wearing my normal thick cotton socks, I don't see how I could be well grounded. The strap is not touching my skin in the leather soles either.

My hubby told me that a blanket on the ground would not provide good grounding. That's where I started using a wet sheet outside and then went to a metal tank with water on a wet ground for CT and grounding.

### Dani — 2018-10-29T19:57:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.21700/post-254502>

> [Julian said:](/goto/post?id=246667)
>
> I was wondering if anyone knows much about grounding foot wear. I have been looking at getting a pair of fibram 5 finger shoes but not sure if there as good for the grounding aspect as earth runners?

The one thing about the 5 fingers is that they do allow your foot to move through its full range of motion which is great for keeping the rest of your body in alignment. You may be able to place a copper tack in the bottom for grounding.

[Minnetonka](http://www.minnetonkamocassin.com) makes a nice selection of soft-soled moccasins.

For the fashion oriented females, there is a company called [pluggz](http://www.pluggz.com) that makes a specific grounding shoe in a ballet slipper and a loafer. I have not tried them out, but am thinking about doing so here in the near future.

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-23T01:10:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.21700/post-256294>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=248119)
>
> It's supposed to go inside the sock:

Why the 1MG resistor? For potential electrocution if stepping on an ungrounded circuit?

### drezy — 2018-11-23T03:03:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.21700/post-256299>

> [ALEXIS TUDOR said:](/goto/post?id=256294)
>
> Why the 1MG resistor? For potential electrocution if stepping on an ungrounded circuit?

Human Saftey:
"Where higher voltages are present, extra resistance (0.75 [megohm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ohm_\(unit\)) per 250 V) is added in the path to ground to protect the wearer from excessive currents; this typically takes the form of a 4 megohm resistor in the coiled cable (or, more commonly, a 2 megohm resistor at each end)."

With a 1 meg resistor your body will not be able to develop a relative static charge. Rubbing a balloon on your hair and then touching a friend's ear would be way less fun by not producing a zap.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canadian-dairy.21705/post-246705>

So ive been on the leptin rx now for about 2 weeks , slowly seems to be working. Just wondering about Canadian dairy. Ive been using butter and ghee, should i be doing this?

I know Jack has said no US dairy and im assuming Canadian is the same. But in the leptin rx it advocates for lots of butter. Can i get away with using without screwing up reset?

Butter and especially ghee have low amounts of protein so im guessing the casein is less of a problem as compared to milk.

# Thread: New baby
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-baby.21707/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2018-07-26T08:25:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-baby.21707/post-246741>

My wife Tania and I are expecting our first child on Aug 7. Unfortunately, a c-section is scheduled for that date. I thought I'd do a little write-up on how it's gone sofar.

We did a couple of rounds of IVF last year. My very low sperm count made it impossible to become pregnant naturally, hence the need for IVF.
Each round of IVF produced around 7 viable eggs that were fertilized via ICSI, and in the end we were left with 4 viable embryos in cold storage. One was implanted Dec 7, 2017 and pregnancy was achieved.

We spent 4-weeks in Mexico in Dec/Jan to hopefully help get a good start to the pregnancy with lots of sun exposure. Otherwise, we've been stuck up at the 55th latitude for the remainder of the time. Pretty early on Tania found she lost her appetite for butter and salmon and switched from a low carb diet to a lot more carbs, dairy, honey, fruit, etc. Oysters have been tolerated and we've eaten them regularly. She's used her phone, wifi, and computer more than I have liked, but it was difficult to compromise on that. Wifi and power to the bedroom get turned off every night, although wifi from neighbors houses has the same signal strength as our own router. Also, there are at least 2 wireless smart meters at our house, one in the basement and one on the outside of the house.

This summer we've been getting tons of sun and going to a nearby river valley where there are no cell signals. A day in the sun at the river is noticeably more refreshing than a day in the sun in town (a small city of about 60,000 people, ton's of cell towers, airport).

A day of sun at the river, water is still pretty cold:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The final ultrasound was completed last weekend. It showed baby is in the 80th percentile for fattiness, and Tania has a lot of water. The baby is sitting laterally instead of head down, with the cord wrapped around the neck. The obgyn does not feel it's safe to attempt to move the baby into proper position, a breach birth is too risky, and has gone ahead and scheduled the c-section. Aug 7 will be 38 weeks & 4 days of the pregnancy.

### Jenelle — 2018-07-26T15:09:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-baby.21707/post-246749>

[@Rubicon](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18225/) ~ congratulations & best wishes!

My clinical experience last night was in labor & delivery, and I woke up this morning still thinking about it. How timely to see your post today and this wonderful picture of your wife. Your baby is so fortunate already.

Please update when you can! I'll be thinking of you.

### Phosphene — 2018-07-26T18:13:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-baby.21707/post-246758>

Wow that’s a great pic! I imagine it couldn’t have been all THAT comfortable for her. Most mothers will do almost anything to give their kids the best.

Speaking of which, I’m curious what Dr. K thinks of this. Unnecessary? Dangerous? I’d be tempted in a healthy mother.

[https://www.newscientist.com/article/2075768-boost-c-section-babies-by-giving-them-vaginal-bacteria/](https://www.newscientist.com/article/2075768-boost-c-section-babies-by-giving-them-vaginal-bacteria/)

FWIW, my first son was born with the cord wrapped around his neck. Dad still cut it and now son is doing this:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...he-wants-to-do-right.21506/page-2#post-245472](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/page-2#post-245472)

You still have time for the positioning to shift naturally. Best wishes!

### caroline — 2018-07-27T03:56:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-baby.21707/post-246783>

thinking of you and your wife and baby ........Can't wait to see pics of your bouncy bundle of joy!

### Rubicon — 2018-10-14T18:38:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-baby.21707/post-253130>

Update!

A baby girl, Sara, was born via c-section in early August and is doing great! 6lb 14oz and 19" long at birth.

Mom had rising blood pressure leading up to the delivery date so the Obgyn opted for delivery at 37 weeks and 4 days. Following the c-section, mom had some complications that led to internal bleeding. It became such an issue that she ended up requiring a transfusion of 2 units of blood. A little scary there, but everything stabilized quickly afterward. I used a red UFO light on mom while she was still recovering in the hospital.

Mom had difficulty getting enough milk the first few days after delivery, so we purchased a 4-day supply of donated human breast milk from the local milk bank. Since then mom's production has been steadily rising and there have been no issues breastfeeding 100%.

Sara's had some ongoing reflux, but has been generally in great health. She's gaining weight fast and has nearly doubled in weight to 12 lbs.

Here she is at 0 days old in the recovery room. We changed out the bulbs in there to red lights of course

### Rubicon — 2018-11-26T21:22:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-baby.21707/post-256646>

Temperatures cooled off unusually early this year and in Sept/Oct it was typically too cold to take Sara outside without bundling her up with many layers of clothing and blankets. It was only warm enough to take her out naked for full body sun exposure probably about 3 times in her first two months since being born, but we were able to at least get her some exposure on her face for a few minutes at a time throughout each day.

The south wall of our house has no windows, so there's no option to let sunlight in during the main portion of the day at any time of the year. However, the east and west walls do have small windows, and for a few weeks every year in the Fall/Spring the sun rises/sets at just the right angle and position to receive morning/evening sun through a window that can be opened. The sun moves fast during those times and it only lasts for about 30 minutes before the sun is blocked by the wall. We took full advantage

Leading up to delivery, mom was getting a lot of sun exposure daily in the summer months and when Sara was born her skin was quite tanned brown...surprisingly dark. But by the end of September and into October both mom and baby's skin started to lighten quite a bit. I was not expecting that.

Into Nov/Dec things start to get really dark at 55 degrees latitude. The sun rises so far south on the horizon and does not get much higher than 10 degrees altitude by Dec 20. For a baby like Sara born late in the summer, her first scheduled vaccines are due to begin being administered in late fall at 2-months and continue regularly throughout the winter. In light of all of that, we've decided to put our house up for short-term rental on AirBNB, and re-locate to 5 degrees latitude in Colombia for maternity/parental leave the next 5-months. Sara's 3rd to 7th months of life will be spent deep in the tropics.

I've rented a property out and have also rented houses.

The process is not difficult.

Someone needs to be able to transfer the key.

Cleaning needs be done.

The users of the service will usually not require any furniture to be moved at all. Yes, they will sleep in your bed.

### Rubicon — 2018-11-27T09:09:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-baby.21707/post-256684>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=256648)
>
> How this AirBNB works?

AirBNB lets anyone list their residence on the site for short-term rentals. People book for a short time, and then you can approve/deny them (I do the booking). Everyone has a profile so you can see their rental history and reviews. I've stayed at AirBNB's that are basically residences converted to hotels, and also in people's places that leave most of their personal stuff in the residence. Some people list whole places, or just rooms within a shared home with people living there. While away from home, we're often staying in an AirBNB apartments/houses. It's a nice alternative to hotels.

We have a 'manager' available to stop by if there's an issue that requires urgent attention. We also have a cleaner that will clean the house everytime someone checks out.

A number code lock lets me just give the entry code to each new guest so there's no need to meet to distribute keys. Mine needs to be programmed manually by the cleaner, but it's possible to get automatic ones that can be accessed via wifi and changed remotely.

We moved our personal belongings and mattresses into the basement and locked everything up down there. Beds on the main floor have new mattresses with good mattress protectors for use by guests. We'll switch everything back when we return in the spring.

AirBNB provides insurance coverage in case of any damage to the premises by guests. I've been meaning to purchase supplemental insurance on top of that as an extra layer of security as well.

All in all it was a bit of an initial expense/effort to get setup, but it runs with not too much ongoing work now.

### Phosphene — 2018-11-27T17:09:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-baby.21707/post-256710>

It can be very lucrative. My friends in Maine are renting out their large family home for the winter for $1700/month (plus some utilities).

I’m sure it’s taxable income but I imagine you can deduct some expenses. My friends have another rental property as well, so use a tax accountant. However, so many people are getting into this now it can’t be that hard.

There are also services like this that handle all the details for a percentage of the income. Might be a good place to ask some questions.

[https://evolvevacationrental.com/homeowner-faq?utm\_source=programmatic\_vt&utm\_medium=vt](https://evolvevacationrental.com/homeowner-faq?utm_source=programmatic_vt&utm_medium=vt)

### Marko Pollo — 2018-12-03T17:33:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-baby.21707/post-257154>

> [Rubicon said:](/goto/post?id=256646)
>
> Temperatures cooled off unusually early this year and in Sept/Oct it was typically too cold to take Sara outside without bundling her up with many layers of clothing and blankets. It was only warm enough to take her out naked for full body sun exposure probably about 3 times in her first two months since being born, but we were able to at least get her some exposure on her face for a few minutes at a time throughout each day.
>
> The south wall of our house has no windows, so there's no option to let sunlight in during the main portion of the day at any time of the year. However, the east and west walls do have small windows, and for a few weeks every year in the Fall/Spring the sun rises/sets at just the right angle and position to receive morning/evening sun through a window that can be opened. The sun moves fast during those times and it only lasts for about 30 minutes before the sun is blocked by the wall. We took full advantage
>
>
>
>
> Leading up to delivery, mom was getting a lot of sun exposure daily in the summer months and when Sara was born her skin was quite tanned brown...surprisingly dark. But by the end of September and into October both mom and baby's skin started to lighten quite a bit. I was not expecting that.
>
> Into Nov/Dec things start to get really dark at 55 degrees latitude. The sun rises so far south on the horizon and does not get much higher than 10 degrees altitude by Dec 20. For a baby like Sara born late in the summer, her first scheduled vaccines are due to begin being administered in late fall at 2-months and continue regularly throughout the winter. In light of all of that, we've decided to put our house up for short-term rental on AirBNB, and re-locate to 5 degrees latitude in Colombia for maternity/parental leave the next 5-months. Sara's 3rd to 7th months of life will be spent deep in the tropics.

Where in Colombia are you going? We just returned from the Santander/Boyaca area with intent to settle there in the next year. I felt more energy almost instantly after resetting circadian rhythm. Hope you have a similar experience. We stayed in an air b&b outside of Barichara that was mito-noteworthy; dark cool adobe bedrooms attached to a completely open kitchen, living and dining area. Private enough that i didnt need a tankini ever.
Took my emf meter. Found less emf while driving in Bogota than Seattle and just like every other place Ive taken a reading, my own cell phone is a bigger liability to me than the most heinous looking cell tower i can find and stand next to.
Hope you have a great time and share some thoughts on living there.

### Billybats — 2018-07-30T03:53:33+0300

Thank you

[@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) or anyone who can give me there thoughts on this. Please give the science version too. My question above in a nutshell is . Are there really oils that increase photosynthesis. If so would that be messing with nature.

Thank you

### drezy — 2018-08-02T18:00:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/photosynthesis-from-citrus.21731/post-247302>

My take.

Conventionally, it looks like the eukaryote (our domain of life) may have appeared 2.7 billion years ago sporting a brand spanking new membrane "technology" and prokaryote-like organelles (or just plain captured prokaryotes)\*. Fast forward over 2.7 billion years here we are with exponentiated membranes,numbers of cells, density of prokaryote-like/captured organelles in crucial organ cells, and some consciousness to boot(currently used by most for netflix binge watching).

Hanging out around here you get the drift that all these varied cell types and bound up prokaryotes, which otherwise would love to go their own direction leaving us as a mass of useless non-conscious gel, are orchestrated and choreographed in an amazing dance by the energies we allow on our surfaces.

Given the fact that this surface was "developed" over billions of years and coordinates all of our functions I feel like rubbing some substance on that surface is like saying "Alright last couple billion years, nice try, but I know way better!" Any exogenous substance will have a different absorption, reflection, and potentially stokes shift (re-emission) than than well developed surface we now posses. This applies even if the producer claims "native" roots(always great for marketing), has an extra "naturey" looking facebook and website(usually pictures bought off of [https://www.shutterstock.com](https://www.shutterstock.com)), or has a howling wolf on the label (because isn't everyone a sucker for howling wolf art?).

\* - "The Vital Question" by Nick Lane is a good book that covers much of the science and lingering theories
Any blog here and in the member blogs containing the words prokaryotes and eukaryotes is a perfect follow up

disclaimer - I do sometimes put a few drops of mint oil(vs chlorine) in my cold tub to extend the clarity of the water.

Any exogenous substance will have a different absorption, reflection, and potentially stokes shift (re-emission) than than well developed surface we now posses. This applies even if the producer claims "native" roots(always great for marketing), has an extra "naturey" looking facebook and website(usually pictures bought off of [https://www.shutterstock.com](https://www.shutterstock.com)), or has a howling wolf on the label (because isn't everyone a sucker for howling wolf art?).
>
> \* - "The Vital Question" by Nick Lane is a good book that covers much of the science and lingering theories
> Any blog here and in the member blogs containing the words prokaryotes and eukaryotes is a perfect follow up
>
> disclaimer - I do sometimes put a few drops of mint oil(vs chlorine) in my cold tub to extend the clarity of the water.

# Thread: Glyphosate in area

**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bionaut — 2018-08-02T03:11:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glyphosate-in-area.21754/post-247255>

I live near some orange groves and I can get an epic sunrise running through the roads that connect them. It's a low emf area. I came to the realization after seeing vats of glyphosate at the hardware store that the field is probably being sprayed.

Does anyone know if glyphosate evaporates into the air? Or is it absorbed via skin contact. I try to minimize contact with the plants and stick to the road.
My thinking is I could make sure I am getting proper amounts of glycine from broth to offset any I am absorbing. Thoughts on this?
If anyone knows any hacks to mitigate this compound it would be helpful.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-08T18:00:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glyphosate-in-area.21754/post-247911>

My information indicates that glyphosate is a water soluble toxin/poison, which makes it incredibly more dangerous to the environment (as opposed to the usual man-made fat-soluble toxins commonly in our environment-- which tend to get sequestered in body's fat cells) .

Since glyphosate is water soluble it has a free pass to go anywhere in biosphere-- evaporated to clouds and falling as precipitation everywhere. It gets in our waterways, our soils, our air, our foods. You don't have to live next to intense farming areas to get the 'benefits' of glyphosate contamination.

Yes, i've heard that glyphosate disrupts pathways that utilize glycine amino acid. So supplementation may help. As you know already, glyphosate also attacks our gut linings integrity, and among other things prevents our bodies from fully utilizing plant alkaloids that are so helpful to us for detoxing and as adaptogens.

### Katie Durham — 2019-02-07T23:25:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glyphosate-in-area.21754/post-262531>

> [bionaut said:](/goto/post?id=247255)
>
> Does anyone know if glyphosate evaporates into the air? Or is it absorbed via skin contact.

It is absorbed through the skin. Do not walk barefoot, or walk your dog, in any area where glyphosate has been applied. (Do not expose your cat to a GMO corn litter.)

# Thread: My patients handout
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-patients-handout.21757/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inna — 2018-08-02T03:43:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-patients-handout.21757/post-247259>

For some reason I was unable to upload the file, so just doing copy/ paste:

This is a handout I use for my patients:

Strategies for Optimal Health

Mitochondria have a crucial role in the cell in processing information and generating energy for the body to function properly. Mitochondria determine how close or far you are from optimal health. With aging and chronic diseases mitochondrial dna mutations accumulate (the percentage of mutant mitochondrial DNA in a given cell is called heteroplasmy rate).

Higher heteroplasmy rates adversely affects cell signaling - which is a basis for chronic diseases like cancer, diabetes, mental illness, dementia and many other so called Neolithic (or chronic) diseases.

If you want to be healthy stay connected to nature as much as possible

Strategies to improve health, to influence your mitochondria - power stations of your cells:

1\. When indoors block artificial (blue frequency spectrum) light. Make sure you wear blue blocker glasses - block artificial blue light, to restore your internal clock, which governs orderly functioning and signaling of your cells.

2\. [Expose your naked skin and eyes to sunlight](https://www.naturebuildshealth.com/blog/vitamin_D_ultraviolet) during the day, especially early at the morning - when the sun is rising. Do not wear sunglasses, wear least amount of clothes you can get away with. In the evening – try to be out for sunset as well.

3\. There are only so many sunrises and sunsets in a one’s lifetime, make a goal to be outside and see as many of them as possible!

4\. Expose yourself to cold (might have to do it gradually to adapt).

5\. Eat a seasonal diet. That diet means eating fewer carbs during wintertime, and eating local produce that grows in a particular area during a particular season; fasting is healthy, eat breakfast with a lot of protein in the morning. Eat plenty of sea food (DHA – omega 3 fatty acids) are essential part of cell mebranes. Last meal of the day should be at least 3-4 hours before sunset to preserve circadian rhythms

6\. Exercise outdoors. Walking outside is generally the best exercise

7\. Ground yourself, walking barefoot or swimming in a lake or ocean. Especially beneficial to combine grounding with sun exposure in the morning and in the evening (see 2 and 3)

8\. Sleep remains the foundation for your health. Sleep quality is very important. Your body restores itself during sleep; bad or poorly functioning mitochondria are repaired or replaced during sleep. (Process of autophagy and apoptosis)

Want to learn more about mitochondrial health: See Dr. Jack Kruse – videos and interviews on You Tube

Also [www.jackkruse.com](http://www.jackkruse.com/) - blog (beginners protocols) and forum.

### Jenelle — 2018-08-02T16:16:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-patients-handout.21757/post-247287>

I wore contact lenses for most of the day, from ages 11-39. These proudly boast on the box that they block UV light.

...Do you know how many people wear contact lenses?

Definitely worth a mention, the next time you revise your handout. Glasses aren't good either ~ I know ~ but you can look over the top of your glasses. You can take them off ... off and on ... off and on ... all day long. Not so with contacts. People won't do it.

And while I was doing some googling about my particular brand (which says it offers the _highest level of UV protection available in a contact lens_) ... I came across these new **Transitions lenses** which will apparently be available in 2019:

[https://www.slashgear.com/acuvue-oa...darken-automatically-to-block-light-12527161/](https://www.slashgear.com/acuvue-oasys-transitions-contact-lenses-darken-automatically-to-block-light-12527161/)

[https://www.acuvue.com/acuvue-oasys-transition-contact-lenses](https://www.acuvue.com/acuvue-oasys-transition-contact-lenses)

And they say they also filter blue light. Oh boy. I've asked Jack if there was EVER a good time to wear contacts ... and do they filter blue light at all ... and the answer was a resounding NO. Now here is a new product to confuse people who want to have their cake and eat it, too.

### Inna — 2018-08-23T21:21:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-patients-handout.21757/post-249214>

Good question – actually Neolithic – definition is relating to stone age,

What is referred here is more “diseases of civilization” – contemporary chronic illnesses, like cardiac disease, neurodegenerative diseases, cancer, diabetes, metabolic syndrome, arthritis and other inflammatory conditions, autoimmune disease to name a few, those illnesses that used to be rare in the past are now very common and growing in number and afflicting younger population than in the past

Perhaps “Stone Age” could be referred to a current state of medical science and medical practice, sadly it is utterly inadequate to address those diseases of civilization…

# Thread: Thanks To Jack....

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2018-08-03T07:39:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-to-jack.21764/post-247347>

I just had a building biologist who was personally trained and mentored by Stetzer come out to where I'm staying.and here's what he found:

#1:
The entire house was bathed in a MASSIVE magnetic field of up to 10 mG that was being carried everywhere from the water line; power from the grid was coming in through under the street and latch8ng on to the water line to produce both huge currents and magnetic fields which were then coming up through the floors.

The minute he cut the line I could immediately feel a relaxation response to the point where I starting yawning continuously for the next hour, lol.

#2: Even though I was using incandescent bulbs, faulty wiring was producing a large jump in mag fields for some rooms whenever the lights were turned on.

#3

I thought hardwiring in to the modem was enough, but it's not unless it's done through a SHIELDED cable.

#4

BIG ONE:

The metal lining on my work desk was conducting a giant electric field across the entire desk from my laptop, even when the laptop was grounded.

Anyways, just wanted to say thanks to Jack for opening my eyes to these things.

I knew you were right, but I had no idea how much of a difference fixing these things made and how little the other stuff truly matters compared to this.

I believe it all now, every fucking thing you've said about the priority of the environment.

# Thread: Tomatis method - beneficial?

### Lahelada — 2018-08-04T16:41:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatis-method-beneficial.21768/post-247462>

I have been asked to maybe co operate in a project to bring the Tomatis method to language learning.
It is based on three things it seems, music in 432 HZ, spoken word and bone conduction via special
headphones.

"During the listening sessions, music and voice are transmitted through headphones equipped for both air and bone conduction. The sound therefore reaches the auditory canal through the headphones placed on each ear and through a vibration from the top of the skull.
**With a specific setting applied, the sound transmits first through the bones of the skull. As a result, the auditory system naturally prepares itself to receive the sound through the air channel."**

It is the bone conduction part that sparked my unease.
Reading the client evaluation crib sheet the needed observations all go to evaluating dopamine levels,even though it is not explicitly stated.
The equipment itself operates on a battery pack. I have not seen it yet though.

Any one want to share their impression or experience even ? Thanks.

### johnsonmd — 2018-08-10T01:52:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatis-method-beneficial.21768/post-248059>

I have not heard of Tomatis before but I have heard about music helping with children's learning difficulties. Classical music seems to broaden the variety of sound that one hears. It can sooth stress and help with memory and understanding concepts. I imagine the tones would effect the cells and mitochondria. I wonder about the RF and EMF. I worry about the headphones effecting the hearing, long term.

### Lahelada — 2018-08-10T02:52:41+0300

My 2 and a half year old eats fish every morning with her daddy. Her favorite part and I will quote is "skin and bone". Every morning I start her off right with a little am sun (Sure she won't look long but it's better than TV!) Her best meal of the day is always breakfast (bacon/eggs/fish) are the mainstays. Sure she is a kid and will want some carbs but you tell me what 2 year old will eat sockeye salmon out of a can with the skin/bones and drink the juice after lol. After breakfast we will go outside since it is summer and play/walk/swim etc for as much of the morning as she can tolerate. Jack she seems to get up close to when sun rises. I assume since she is so young that her circadian rhythm is much more intact vs an adult living a non-optimal life? Now only if I could get my wife onboard :/

# Thread: Is there any such thing as a good computer ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-any-such-thing-as-a-good-computer.21776/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Morthern — 2018-08-05T10:11:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-any-such-thing-as-a-good-computer.21776/post-247518>

hello
I am a writer And need a new computer to continue my craft. I wear glasses with orange filters and make sure I turn it off by 8 pm.

Can I be doing better ?

Is there a computer on the market that releases less emf’s?

What else can I do to decrease the hazards or writing on a computer ?

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-05T20:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-any-such-thing-as-a-good-computer.21776/post-247564>

I use a laptop but computers like it are apparently not the best from safety standpoint because of additional EMFs (ELF radiation) from their batteries plus the fact they can't be grounded. Don't ever put a laptop on your lap or anywhere close to your body.

A better PC choice might be a traditional desktop computer form factor with a metal chassis that can be grounded.

I don't know if computers are being marketed 'green' based on comparatively low RF and ELF emissions.

As an aware consumer I'm sure you are using a wired (ethernet cable) connection instead of wireless mode (but check your computer's network settings to make sure the wireless network signal is not active--- by default network antenna on board computer might continuously send out RF signals to ping a wifi connection unless you shut it down).

Ditch wireless mouse and other peripheral devices so enabled.

You are probably already using an app like f.lux to switch color temperature of your computer display from blue to warmer, more biologically compatible hues.

### Morthern — 2018-08-06T03:19:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-any-such-thing-as-a-good-computer.21776/post-247626>

Thank you jack and electric Universe for your feedback
Actually , my laptop died a few months ago and I’ve been using my phone as a computer
( I phone 7 .. big screen )
It’s this BETTER than a laptop perhaps ?
I’m concidering not replacing my laptop and just managing here and with my iPad

Ironically .. my main useage online is for forums and stuff on Health ... doh !
