# Thread: Genious way to Eliminate ALL Blue Light from your TV!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/genious-way-to-eliminate-all-blue-light-from-your-tv.21829/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PayitForwardinLife — 2018-08-12T23:54:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/genious-way-to-eliminate-all-blue-light-from-your-tv.21829/post-248329>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This is genius! This company, Saffron, came up with this device that’s called DriftTV and it plugs into your tv and elimates 100% of blue light! Check it out here- [http://seesaffron.com/drift-tv-box](http://seesaffron.com/drift-tv-box)

As we all learned in the Vermont 2018 talk that our skin is HUGELY sensitive to blue light and receives far more then then the eye does simple by the amount of surface area exposed to ANY and ALL artificial light sources. So wearing your blue blocking glasses is NOT enough! Period! Not even close! Lets face it people ANYTIME we are using these electronic devices, tv’s or under man made artificial light we are “dancing with the devil”. If you dance with the devil long enough...your gonna get burned! Just always keep that in mind. Don’t ever lie to yourself or accept it as a half-truth. The Vermont 2018 talk really should be EVERYONE’s wake-up call!

So how does this device work? You unplug the HDMI cable that goes into your tv and plug it into this device and then take the HDMI cable that comes from the DriftTV device and plug it into your tv. It basically acts like anin-line blue light filter/eliminator. You can actually adjust the percentage of blue light the tv receives from 0 to 100%. Pretty cool.An on screen menu allows you to adjust the percentage of bue light that’s displayed on the tv from 0 to 100% and even what time it does this from and to. All for 120 bucks! Pretty cool in my opinion. No tv is the best option in my opinion though. Nature and great friends is the best entertainment. Period.

If you have a smart tv and what you watch is NOT coming thru a HDMI source then the DriftTV device will NOT filter squat! So if you are using any of the smart apps such as NetFlix, Amazon Prime, etc that come with your tv your screwed.

However, I have a hack! Simple too. Get a Apple TV, Roku, etc. or DVD Player that has built-in smart apps (NetFlix, etc.) and then plug the HDMI cable that comes from any of those devices into the DriftTV and then plug the HDMI cable coming from the DriftTV into your smart tv and BOOM! Hack = zero blue light! \*Note- just always make sure to use the Apple Tv, Roku, DVD Player to watch your shows thru!

Hope this helps! Reviews online ALL sound great! The company is small and so they are currently just making small batches. So pre-order is the option for now..

After watching the Vermont 2018 talk we just ripped two of our three flat screen tv’s that we have in our house off the wall and going to give them away! We are DRAMATICALLY reducing this toxic shit in our lives, and our kids. Period. Next we are cutting a big hole in the wall where the tv’s where and putting in a big ass window to see nature. That will be our “entertainment”! The only tv in the house left is gonna be used mostly for watching Jack and used as a tool for learning!

We bought a high powered dark field microscope with a high powered camera which connects to our big screen tv so we can look at our blood and do better hacks and learn the effects on real-rime on what it’s doing to our bodies. Super great way to “awaken your consciousness” to how you and your environment are treating your body. Super cool! Our kids are stoked!

with gratitude and light, Jason & Jamie

### drezy — 2018-08-13T00:37:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/genious-way-to-eliminate-all-blue-light-from-your-tv.21829/post-248330>

# Thread: Day Moisturizers for skin

### Sajid Mahmood — 2018-08-16T00:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-moisturizers-for-skin.21845/post-248559>

Hi,

Does anyone know any good natural daytime moisturizers for face, that doesn’t block any UVA/UVB?

Tend to get a lot of dry flaky skin on face.

Does anyone know if Aloe Vera is okay?
Does Aloe Vera block UVA/UVB?

Would it be a bad idea to apply a natural moisturizer during the day?

Thank You

### Sajid Mahmood — 2018-08-16T02:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-moisturizers-for-skin.21845/post-248572>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=248562)
>
> coconut oil.

Coconut oil I think blocks UVA/UVB onto skin. Have read somewhere it’s a natural sunscreen and has a SPF of 6. Looking for a good natural moisturizer that doesn’t block any UVA/UVB.

Aloe Vera I think would be good. Not sure but I think it allows all the UV rays to get through. Don't like the idea of anything that blocks UVA/UVB onto skin

### Antonis — 2018-08-24T16:42:27+0300

> Be careful, as we know melanopsin is in the skin now.

Don’t think Aloe Vera blocks UVA/UVB, but perhaps it still maybe a bad idea to put anything on skin because of melanopsin in the skin.

# Thread: Are there contraindications for CT therapy?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-contraindications-for-ct-therapy.21852/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kris Kolo — 2018-08-16T17:26:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-contraindications-for-ct-therapy.21852/post-248605>

I'm a very new newbie - forgive this question. I'm reading as fast as I can but want to simultaneously start helping myself; doing sphinx, sunlight, walking barefoot, planning to go to to FL from MA for winter etc. I'm also starting with cold face dunks and cold-ish showers as baby steps of CT therapy. My issue is widespread pain for 9 yr, always worsening. Always cold, super low hormones due to condition & age (56) (but BETTER after 6 wk of midday sunning - googling for answers WHY, I bumped into JK's work for first time!!!!). If I have fibro (will do VCS test again later this week - in past it has been good at times, bad at times), is CT ok to start? I've felt worsening pain after first week of cold face dunking and showers alternating hot/ cold and also bath with 76 degrees water (coldest possible until I start adding ice later). Thanks!

### Nigel Clack — 2019-04-25T20:13:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-contraindications-for-ct-therapy.21852/post-268910>

Kris did you find out if CT was good or bad for your fibro? Cheers

### kris90 — 2019-04-25T20:46:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-contraindications-for-ct-therapy.21852/post-268913>

Based on everything I've learnt over the years, CT is always beneficial for any condition if we have a loosely coupled haplotype, however, there's a lot we must get right in order to EASE the adaptation process. I'm actually working on a Circadian Reset Program that is a step-by-step guide to doing everything you must do in the proper order to improve your health (what we learned from Jack). I'll share the order with you:

1\. Mitigate nnEMF and Blue light
2\. Restore the circadian rhythm (i.e. AM sunlight, Leptin Rx, PM darkness)
3\. Begin water loading (i.e. source good water, increase consumption and consider DDW if applicable)
4\. Learn optimal breathing to increase oxygen assimilation
5\. Begin CT Protocol (i.e. facedunks first, shower, then tub. Start temps high and gradually steepen gradient)
6\. Begin Intermittent Fasting Protocol (i.e. 16/8, 18/6, 20/4 or 1-2x 24 h fasts per week)
7\. Begin Exercise (i.e. basic movements)
8\. Seasonal Eating (i.e. add carbs back into diet in summer months)

Hope that helps!

### Kris Kolo — 2019-04-26T00:21:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-contraindications-for-ct-therapy.21852/post-268938>

Nigel, when I met Jack in Cancun for consult in Nov he said "go gentle." I told him that applying cold - if too much or too long - can make my pain (already awful) much much worse. I was in mexico for much of winter and I found I could hang out in 80 degree ocean water for even an hour with no problem...Jack said that THAT was a good level of CT for me for now. I do face dunking a couple times a day without issue (usually) but now am waiting for summer. Hoping in my new Vermont home to lie in cold water in a kiddie pool in the hot July sun...will see if sun makes cold like that more bearable LOL!

### kris90 — 2019-04-26T02:23:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-contraindications-for-ct-therapy.21852/post-268947>

That's the beauty of cold, anything less than 98.6 F is considered CT. Just being in water alone has many benefits. Natural faraday cage. 80 F is a perfect starting point, and just slowly steepen the gradient.

### Nigel Clack — 2019-04-26T10:54:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-contraindications-for-ct-therapy.21852/post-268970>

That's great, thanks both

# Thread: Seeing a Bio-Dentist, what are the best materials to replace amalgam fillings with??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-a-bio-dentist-what-are-the-best-materials-to-replace-amalgam-fillings-with.21874/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thrive1992 — 2018-08-19T14:23:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-a-bio-dentist-what-are-the-best-materials-to-replace-amalgam-fillings-with.21874/post-248792>

Hi team,

I'm Seeing a Bio-Dentist, what are the best materials to replace amalgam fillings with??

[http://www.dentistryonmerivale.co.nz/team/](http://www.dentistryonmerivale.co.nz/team/)

I have been told the main 2 types are "composite" and "crowns" ($$$$)

There are a few different types of "composite" options. The Bio Dentist can also probably get access to other options if i ask for that.

I have heard quartz mentioned in other biohacking groups. I think the composite does include some quartz but not completely quartz.

What is the best replacement material considering we are going into nnEMF world?

I want to do this right first time.

What sort of research can I do for this?

Would love to hear any opinions or thoughts on this.

Also if this is the wrong sub forum to post this in please let me know

Update 20th August 2018::

Today I saw the bio-dentist.
Turns out i only have 2 amalgam fillings (both on the right side of my mouth - side note: many of my health problems are on the right side of my body but not sure if this is coincidence)

I also have 1 composite filling on the left side - although this is not toxic like amalgam it may be from a less good brand of composite fillings and the bio-dentist is also able to remove this one.

I told the biodentist about Dr Jack Kruse and Dr Dean Bonlie (magnochelation). He had not heard of either but has said i can email him links as he is interested in other natural health doctors.
I explained how Dr Kruse has mentioned that blue light getting deep into gum/dentin during the amalgam removal operation is not good. The operating room has a huge window so maybe i can time it for a time of day that has the sun shining through? Not sure if that would make up for the blue light though...

My options for replacement are:

$1200 per ceramic filling (porcelain)

$300 per composite filling
[https://www.kulzer.com/en/int/dentist/products\_from\_a\_to\_z/venus\_2/venus\_diamond.aspx#wr-tab-3](https://www.kulzer.com/en/int/dentist/products_from_a_to_z/venus_2/venus_diamond.aspx#wr-tab-3)

Prices in New Zealand Dollars
Both prices include the dentists' cost for actually doing the procedure.

Ceramic advantage is:
I know exactly what is in the product. It is stronger than composite (= less risk of something happening later in life and it breaking). It is more "natural" than composite. With the composite product, i can't actually find out all the ingredients as apparently it is proprietary information.

Composite advantage: Price is lower. The Venus Diamond composite is better than most other composites as it is BPA free. The company also has a history of producing dental products that are more on the "natural" end of the spectrum according to the biodentist.

The Bio Dentists' wife is a holistic doctor/naturopath type and she can help me do a heavy metal lab test using my a hair sample (is this the best way to be doing it?).

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-amazing-news-article.21885/post-248884>

Dr. Michael Holick’s enthusiasm for vitamin D can be fairly described as extreme. The Boston University endocrinologist, who perhaps more than anyone else is responsible for creating a billion-dollar vitamin D sales and testing juggernaut, elevates his own levels of the stuff with supplements and fortified milk. When he bikes outdoors, he won’t put sunscreen on his limbs. He has written book-length odes to vitamin D, and has warned in multiple scholarly articles about a “vitamin D deficiency pandemic” that explains disease and suboptimal health across the world.

full article:
[https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/mar...wy-money-behind-it/ar-BBM7mTt?ocid=spartanntp](https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/markets/vitamin-d-the-sunshine-supplement-has-shadowy-money-behind-it/ar-BBM7mTt?ocid=spartanntp)

# Thread: Kids CT / Leptin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-ct-leptin.21889/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Erin Gaiser — 2018-08-21T15:20:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-ct-leptin.21889/post-248983>

How are people adapting the protocols for kids?
In terms of CT: just being outside and going in cold rivers/lakes/ pool?
What about in terms of Leptin? I can't imagine that my kids will eat for example 2 meals a day. Is three ok without snacks?

Any help appreciated!

### countingstarsx — 2018-08-21T19:28:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-ct-leptin.21889/post-248998>

For my daughter I cut all grains out of her diet, and am not letting her go crazy with carbs anymore. I am also structuring her meals, breakfast earlier, only one snack, and supper earlier. No more eating after dark. Once fall/winter is here I will probably cut her carbs back a little more.

As for CT, I have been giving her cooler baths lately, and we occasionally go to the pool. The real deal will start when winter comes and I let her play outside somewhat minimally clothed. She is 3 so I am going to let her guide the process.

# Thread: Does natural sunlight outweight hf-radiation exposure?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-natural-sunlight-outweight-hf-radiation-exposure.21913/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2018-08-24T12:45:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-natural-sunlight-outweight-hf-radiation-exposure.21913/post-249263>

So since Im measuring more often, I made the experience that you often have some kind of hf-radiation where you have a place to get sunlight.

The question is:

Is the exposure to natural sunlight outweighing the effects of nnEMF (HF) at this place?
Or is it nonsense because you get dehydrated?
Is is dependent on the peak intensity?

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-24T18:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-natural-sunlight-outweight-hf-radiation-exposure.21913/post-249297>

I'm sure this is a definitive _Dr Kruse question_, but I will weigh in.

Sure, sunlight will help you immensely in adverse toxic environments, but it is not a magic bullet that will on balance give you superhero invulnerability to nnEMF exposure effects . You would only be deluding yourself to believe that.

5G reality is destined to destroy a lot of peoples' illusions that they can still coexist safely with a pulsed microwave RF-saturated environment if only they do everything right with sunlight, diet, exercise, breathing exercises, and what have you.

There are still some places where you can be at peace with nature, safely grounded and linked to the sun, without too much nnEMF beating you up at the same time.

But time may be running out for those safe havens. Who knows?

To paraphrase a quote attributed to Einstein--

_"Two things are infinite: the universe and human stupidity."_

Why? The omission of the biophysics and the over-concentration on everything biochemical (foods, supplements and pharmaceuticals) to portray the narrative that "things of substance" are "ultra-primary" for life. Therefore, do not question or entertain ideas relating to light and energy. This is the brutal truth.

By playing down or dismissing the toxic biophysics from the formula under our noses (nnEMF) and demonizing the natural benefits of biophysics medicines in their correct dose sizes (sunlight and earthing), in combination, has been the formula for Modern Medicine since the 1930s. It didn't use to be this way. Once energy and light are added back to the formula for life, we are back to a normal playing field.

# Thread: Magnetico Sleep pad next best thing?

### PJP71 — 2018-08-26T22:00:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-next-best-thing.21935/post-249464>

I would love to get one of these but its just not in the budget right now.

Right now my bedtime routine is shutting of power to the bedroom. Then I go outside barefoot and ground myself in the grass on all fours. My bed has a wood frame and not box spring or any springs in the mattress.

Does anyone have any suggestions for anything short of getting a Magnetico sleep pad?

### Mike David — 2018-08-26T22:52:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-next-best-thing.21935/post-249467>

R

> [PJP71 said:](/goto/post?id=249464)
>
> I would love to get one of these but its just not in the budget right now.
>
> Right now my bedtime routine is shutting of power to the bedroom. Then I go outside barefoot and ground myself in the grass on all fours. My bed has a wood frame and not box spring or any springs in the mattress.
>
> Does anyone have any suggestions for anything short of getting a Magnetico sleep pad?

Run a bare copper wire from a ground rod into your sleeping quarters and have it touching your bare skin while you sleep. I ran it through a small gap I made in the frame for the screen.

# Thread: Chiropractic as preventative? Do you still believe in it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chiropractic-as-preventative-do-you-still-believe-in-it.21953/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LieselK — 2018-08-28T23:44:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chiropractic-as-preventative-do-you-still-believe-in-it.21953/post-249629>

I'm so curious to hear from everyone- chiropractors included! Do you still believe in the idea that chiropractic care is preventative medicine? If so, what does that look like to you? Is that weekly, biweekly, monthly or ??? If you are a chiropractor, how do you handle this with your patients? Do you promote regular care or develop plans based on the individual and/or encourage them to reach out when needed? Are most people so disconnected from their bodies that they need the regularity or they wouldn't ever realize that care is needed?

Here's why I'm asking... rabbit hole... a friend illuminated something for me today. Something I knew, but couldn't verbalize like she did for me. When we experience disease symptoms or receive a diagnosis I think it's natural for us to feel betrayed by our body or feel like it's broken. The truth is that it's not, the body is doing what it is made to do- survive. It's really working the way it's supposed to. It's freeing when you realize that you are not powerless and the changes you make to your environment will have a positive effect because your body is going to respond just as it did to negative impacts. So... I really dislike the meme that to be healthy I have to do regular chiropractic care. (It's essentially telling me that I'm constantly broken and need to be fixed.) If I'm optimal, why would my body be out of alignment so often and need to be adjusted? While I can definitely see changes based on how I feel and from scans in receiving regular chiropractic care over the last few years, I also feel like I shouldn't need it now because the stimulus for misalignment is gone (stress, anxiety, etc.) Being pregnant is a different story, but I also feel like it should be as "as needed" thing and not just scheduled regularly.

### countingstarsx — 2018-08-29T00:23:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chiropractic-as-preventative-do-you-still-believe-in-it.21953/post-249630>

I work as an office manager for a chiropractor, and I also have a personal problem with the idea that I need regular maintenance care, or that a newborn needs care to be optimal as well. I see many people daily benefit greatly from acute care, some are hardly able to walk in and once treated they walk out with no problem at all.

Most chiropractors in my area also really subscribe to the idea that alignment needs to be corrected by outside means (like orthotics for feet), and after reading Katy Bowman's work on alignment I think that is now a really flawed idea.

### LieselK — 2018-08-29T15:23:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chiropractic-as-preventative-do-you-still-believe-in-it.21953/post-249659>

> [countingstarsx said:](/goto/post?id=249630)
>
> I work as an office manager for a chiropractor, and I also have a personal problem with the idea that I need regular maintenance care, or that a newborn needs care to be optimal as well. I see many people daily benefit greatly from acute care, some are hardly able to walk in and once treated they walk out with no problem at all.
>
> Most chiropractors in my area also really subscribe to the idea that alignment needs to be corrected by outside means (like orthotics for feet), and after reading Katy Bowman's work on alignment I think that is now a really flawed idea.

I'm also really struggling with paying someone who isn't on the same page as I am. If that's going to be the case, that I'm really only paying for the actual adjustment and not any other support then I'll just go to a cheap walk-in/walk-out chiro clinic.

Interesting you say about newborns as I've seen the need for facial work in many babes, but I've also had a couple chiropractors over the years who have told me that children shouldn't need adjusted all that often- just when big growth spurts or changes occur such as learning to walk, etc. I wonder now at their reasoning and what really makes anyone \*need\* chiropractic care. As Jack would say... the hippos and lions do just fine without it. I suppose it's individual in finding the right amount of sunshine, meditation, exercise, grounding, etc, etc that keeps a person's body balanced.

We are working with a tensegrity engineering model, tension and compression forces working in coherence. The skeletal system provides compression while fascia provides tension, the skeletal compression members should float freely in fascial tension. Fascia is the one part of us that is in contact with every other part. Fascia is a water loving topological insulator, to a large extent collagen based. The fascia connective tissue system not only organizes cellular location, but it also extends into the intracellular space where it is the cytoskeleton. Think about how the cell changes the amount of space between the nucleus and mitochondria. The interaction between our light environment and fascia alters fascial tension and thusly skeletal compression within the tensegrity system. Scoliosis can manifest with blue light toxicity, the skeletal subluxation pattern is stored in the water loving topological insulator fascial system. BUT, the individual that lives in a garbage environment and has poorly functioning mitochondria will never be able to hold their alignment. # Thread: CT in long light cycles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mike David — 2018-08-29T21:34:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-249700>

So I hope some of the smarter people than me can clear this up for me. Our SCN is entrained to natural light cycles to regulate our body clocks. The cold disconnects the SCN from being yoked to light cycles. I wake up to see the sunrise partly to yoke the body clock to the natural light cycles. When I CT in the summer is the SCN able to reyoke to the light after I CT in long light cycles? If I CT in long light cycles does the SCN just ignore the cold trying to shut it off from being entrained to light? How long after CT does this shunting effect last? Would CT in the summer have its greatest effect right before bed or does it not matter?

### kris90 — 2018-08-30T01:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-249726>

I wouldn't overthink it. Basically, when temps fall in the environment, that becomes the dominant stimulus to entrain the circadian rhythm. In summer, light is the dominant stimulus, irregardless of how much CT you do I would imagine. Think of winter like a prolonged night, and think of summer like a prolonged day. In winter, we express eNOS in brown adipose tissue (BAT) to direct bloodflow to fat and skeletal muscle, because we need to expend energy to produce heat. In summer, we use vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) to direct bloodflow to the gut because we eat more (i.e. carbs) in long light cycles. Night time would mimic what winter does (i.e. doing CT).

### Antonis — 2018-08-30T16:32:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-249755>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=249726)
>
> I wouldn't overthink it. Basically, when temps fall in the environment, that becomes the dominant stimulus to entrain the circadian rhythm. In summer, light is the dominant stimulus, irregardless of how much CT you do I would imagine. Think of winter like a prolonged night, and think of summer like a prolonged day. In winter, we express eNOS in brown adipose tissue (BAT) to direct bloodflow to fat and skeletal muscle, because we need to expend energy to produce heat. In summer, we use vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) to direct bloodflow to the gut because we eat more (i.e. carbs) in long light cycles. Night time would mimic what winter does (i.e. doing CT).

that's why in the equator we can do well with the sun only?

### kris90 — 2018-08-30T18:03:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-249761>

> [Antonis said:](/goto/post?id=249755)
>
> that's why in the equator we can do well with the sun only?

Precisely, because the sun keeps us tightly coupled. The IR light from the sun heats the water that surrounds our tissues helping to bring deuterium up to the surface, and the UV light puts the pinch on deuterium to complete the human photosynthesis process of making UVC light in blood plasma.

In the cold, we need our mitochondria to release the IR light to do what the sun was supposed to do to bring the deuterium up. In order for this to occur, we needed to express the uncoupling proteins to allow protons to leak.

### Mike David — 2018-08-30T18:12:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-249764>

Well doesn’t that make a lot of sense. Thanks for clarifying. So based on where I currently reside CT is completely necessary in the UV winter. In the summer it’s the best of both worlds. I underestimated my fear of the cold. Showers are one thing the tub is a whole other beast to tame. Sometimes my Neolithic mind gets in the way and presents convenient excuses cloaked in comfort and peace of mind. All leading to a broken mind blinded from the ways of nature.

### Evan Leck — 2018-09-04T02:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-in-long-light-cycles.21959/post-250094>

With modern life, even our people aren't getting anywhere near the optimal amount IR/UV light exposure. So I would think that CT during summer makes sense as an adjunct to maximize the cellular battery charge, or to overcome some disease state.

I think the bigger issue, in terms of seasonal modalities, are keto folks who miss out on the benefit of carb consumption during summer.

# Thread: Deep fried drones
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-fried-drones.21963/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2018-08-31T07:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-fried-drones.21963/post-249788>

I live in a popular tropical surf destination and whilst the go pro is now firmly attached to most learner boards.....the use of the 'flying drone' to capture 'the shot' to try and satiate the insatiable appetite of the narcissism factories of facebook and instagram.....i often ponder what the operators of such are doing to themselves.

They dock their phone into a controller which i am guessing powers up the signal of the phone to allow it to be received by the drone. I was wondering if anyone has measured these devices, when in action with emf meters? I am guessing they are giving themselves the deep fry with lashing of calcium efflux and mito heteroplasmy as a double helping for dessert.

Outside of the idiots droning themselves to death........i wonder what ever happened to actually just sitting silently and contemplating the natural beauty that surrounds them.

### drezy — 2018-08-31T17:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-fried-drones.21963/post-249813>

These systems use 2.4GHz or 5.8GHz radios. It won't be that different that holding a modern router in your hands as well as staring into a blue-lit screen for however long.

There are multiple ways to remove them from your sky.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-31T17:53:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-fried-drones.21963/post-249817>

As a would-be drone pilot I can understand the thrill of ersatz flying experience with them using onboard cameras. But using them to take selfies to show off on social media is a bit beyond the pale.

Drones can be very useful tools for observation and surveillance, especially if you have a large property.

Drone controller units are probably like most other remote control gadgets used in toys, R/C hobbies, etc. There are good reasons not to go there with these RF devices.

For die-hard drone fans, no doubt there are ways to mitigate RF exposure using a shield similar to that used in cell phones (e.g. _DefenderShield_ product). Ideally, this way RF beam is deflected mostly away from operator and aimed at drone. But only some diligent RF meter observations could prove how safe it really is.

Let's see if Kruse members can report in with their experiences on this topic.

### drezy — 2018-08-31T19:48:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-fried-drones.21963/post-249828>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=249817)
>
> For die-hard drone fans, no doubt there are ways to mitigate RF exposure using a shield similar to that used in cell phones (e.g. _DefenderShield_ product). Ideally, this way RF beam is deflected mostly away from operator and aimed at drone. But only some diligent RF meter observations could prove how safe it really is.

I'm sending a heartfelt best of luck with that.

# Thread: solved finally gut issues!

### Erin Gaiser — 2018-08-31T11:39:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solved-finally-gut-issues.21967/post-249801>

So basically just by blocking blue light (we did stopped wireless and RF about 1.5 years ago) we were finally able to stop chronic diahrea in my second son. It only took about 2 weeks! Additionally, they were getting as much of the sunrise as possible (hard when a mountain is blocking it) and as much sun as possible.

My first son has had amazing results. He is no longer sensitive to oxalates, he no longer needs the vitamins for HPU, and he has amazing moods constantly now! He is a new person. Additionally, he had problems with undigested food and needing to go to the bathroom 5-7 times a day. That is now down, could be better. He still needs to avoid apples, tomatoes, gluten and dairy. Hope this might change as we keep up with the protocol.

We are just so happy because these solutions are relatively easy and super cheap. I thought I would have to buy them probiotics and all those other supplements forever!

We have so much to be thankful for! Thank you Dr. Kruse!

### Alan Jewells — 2018-08-31T12:09:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-vs-cooked.21968/post-249803>

Forgive me, as this has probably been covered elsewhere, but I haven't seen detail and I don't trust the on-line charts. For oysters, the delicate qualities of their nutrition is coming from the DHA, iodine, as well as a massive amount of key minerals and other nutrients like zinc, etc. In looking at most charts, they indicate very little variance when cooked vs raw. I've read where Dr. Kruse mentioned frozen seafood has about a 40% reduction in DHA. So fresh is clearly important. I'm wondering if anyone knows the reduction of DHA, Iodine, or other nutrients when cooked. I had two dozen raw oysters last night, and I'm still learning to "enjoy" them as they are not my favorite food. I would enjoy them much more if I could pan fry them in tons of butter and garlic, but I also want the key benefits, some of which could be totally lost if cooked. I just don't know. This is probably a common question, but appreciate any wisdom around this. Thank you! Alan

### Brent Patrick — 2018-09-01T09:24:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-vs-cooked.21968/post-249873>

Alan you have to overcome your negative bias ,I hated seafood and would never have entertained the thought of eating raw oysters but two years later I eat on average 4 dozen per week plus wild salmon 5 times a week.
I now eat for Optimal function rather than pleasure and the results I have seen since incorporating seafood into my diet has been all positive.

Stay away from fish supplements and Algae oil they are rubbish

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/seafood-for-beginners.21060/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/seafood-for-beginners.21060/)

**When DHA is in the SN-2 position it becomes planar. You need seafood to convert algae's DHA into the SN-2 position. Taking algal DHA is a waste of time. This conversion of DHA to the SN-2 position allows it to be both an N-type and P-type semicinductor........because of the 22 double bonds become flat like a wire. Members will learn much more about why this is big because of two non-linear effects of light on harmonic generations. Reject fake fish oil sources no matter how bad the marketer tries to tell you otherwise**. [https://www.researchgate.net/…/The-planar-structure-of-the-…](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.researchgate.net%2Ffigure%2FThe-planar-structure-of-the-preferred-DHA-conformation-is-a-fundamental-characteristic-of_fig1_263925583&h=AT2nqZszN2D7ikLxIcZ37P4Kj81TdnvYbc38qdMFCnSocgJv9KPec7CLS6AmFGOMAHvi163Bc4hxPo9EdoGgxEWcwKzcX1hn0erl4m3yJYnhkkhOLzXgMD2oedcgF0MCCVoEVnCv_g_AOTZcz9ntYDX3E0QkjQwdhLW37D1vL-YNbIuVFWYeL_iMgw9Wkji8o3_0gJaq_uhRapzfbQczdy8KG5A4RN2dlI9QcpEO8A-XRCD-cz5mo3Ujho_aIXv6IKNlUIhFgNeUuYok-LQMDzGKqisR407QdCdrW8Fy2p_FkrZAJqNobo_4wRyH5dTlv5N3EaVAU8N2YlNd-AxR48Op7mGgdCUa4fseeTa6lF1cuyOKFFBXi2lbEwu5cdYbm5nam2n4bfLH3i3G69pKbiMEBDMyXhxARs4jKVjjhFtauA) JK FB [March 13](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/2073438466053830?__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARBgnO7wH7v63zquRk73QDd3rPqLq7TzEzj8ixt2qr1OETykpiHDnvznlQt9fuauWpGXacCtYCH0dIfG7ILlPrTtlUivWLPzU1Bwn-ARzJ2QWn5-aoC-lRWf8OylKgOYeXF9_BLaPH2qsFTUjBnaEs2dGFQM7-MOc1XZR5S5zB_J4yB2BvgSJQ&__tn__=-R)

# Thread: Occasional CT time off + caffeine question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/occasional-ct-time-off-caffeine-question.21978/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Corey Nelson — 2018-09-01T16:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/occasional-ct-time-off-caffeine-question.21978/post-249884>

Background: all year I've done 2-5 CT sessions each week, most being in 50-55f water with 30lb bagged ice for 45-60+ minutes. I don't always love it but I know I'm quite cold adapted and sometimes I don't shiver at all and feel hot during the session. It's awesome!

Occasionally I feel like taking some time off (going a week with 0-1 sessions), mostly when it's been a stressful week and I'd end up missing some needed rest time or altering my schedule. I think I've done this twice and other times I've gritted through.

The question: for long time CT users have you found any rules of thumb for this? I know I don't lose my adaptation in that period gauging by physical response but I'm curious if anyone can share their own attitude towards how they view time off. And if you take time off at any particular time of year, for what reasons, and whether you found a limit for retaining adaptation. Anything relevant to this line of thought would be cool. I gather that less time off is better and some mini sessions can be helpful during travel. Is there any situation where time off is considered beneficial?

As a bonus unrelated off topic question that probably doesn't deserve its own thread: how often do you other strict adherents to Kruse protocols consume caffeine? I am definitely no longer psychologically or physically addicted to it but have found it's fun for recreational and cultural reasons about one to two times per month (1-3 cups of strong coffee or tea) , and never out of any feeling of necessity (not to be more productive or combat fatigue).

Thanks in advance.

### kris90 — 2018-09-05T21:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/occasional-ct-time-off-caffeine-question.21978/post-250277>

In my experience, it's like muscle memory. The adaptation sticks. As long as you don't use fake light at night and have a good circadian rhythm, you are designed to use BAT to burn off excess calories as heat every night when the leptin receptor is activated. Cold adapted or not, it seems BAT is an important type of metabolic tissue to regulate our weight year round. We probably just express it more when we live in the cold.

Trying figure out what is best? I also want to make sure that the containers are BPA free. Has anyone found a supplier that has all of this???

### PJP71 — 2018-09-06T00:44:21+0300

Such as:
Zephyrhills
Primo
Earth Fare's RO
Crystal Springs
Etc.

### Jenelle — 2018-09-06T16:28:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-water-suppliers-in-us.21989/post-250361>

I'm sorry, I don't know what are the names of the ones where we fill up our jugs at the store... but Primo sounds familiar to me. All we've worried about all these years is that it is RO ~ which means no fluoride.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-06T17:44:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-water-suppliers-in-us.21989/post-250369>

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=250361)
>
> I'm sorry, I don't know what are the names of the ones where we fill up our jugs at the store... but Primo sounds familiar to me. All we've worried about all these years is that it is RO ~ which means no fluoride.

I have an RO unit, and the manufacturer admits that osmosis membrane does not remove that pesky fluoride. I had to add a special filter to unit to remove fluoride, and even then it is not rated for 100% fluoride removal: more like ~ 99%. If you are financially capable you would be advised to buy top shelf expensive equipment that will remove 100% fluoride and other very hard to remove toxins.

If you have well water at your property it can be a blessing or dicey, depending. If your well draws deep from pure ground water you should be good to go unless there are _excessive_ natural contaminants like minerals and sulfates, etc.

But unfortunately not all well water is pure and safe. There can be pesticides and other pollutants in it, and there is a real concern in fracking areas of fracking chemicals in water. You need to have your well water tested regularly.

But even if you have the purest of water if it is in your household plumbing before it comes out of the faucet it will be devitalized by confinement in twisting pipes.

For those of us (and that regrettably is almost all of us) without direct access to pure spring water finding healthy drinking water is indeed a dilemma.

### Jenelle — 2018-09-06T19:15:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-water-suppliers-in-us.21989/post-250379>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=250369)
>
> If you are financially capable

Nope ~ not at all. But thank you.

Jack has said that RO is good ~ and if it's good enough for him, it's good enough for me.

One of the top things on my list when the funds are available someday is to take my family and _go to Mexico._ I guess we'll all be taking that 1% of fluoride along with us.

### Michael CULLEN — 2018-09-06T19:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-water-suppliers-in-us.21989/post-250386>

Dr Cheney's testing with 3D echo showed Iceland Spring increased cardiac output better than any other water. This means it supported redox best. Fiji was next.

### PJP71 — 2018-09-06T21:14:53+0300

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-07T21:09:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-water-suppliers-in-us.21989/post-250474>

High quality bottled water like Mountain Valley is an excellent choice.

However, as a frugal belt and suspenders character I won't rely on bottled sources because of potential supply disruptions in our fragile economy, or prices escalating because of energy/transportation costs.

As a note of interest, there is a website map that lists springs sites across USA: [findaspring.com](http://www.findaspring.com)

Detailed information on this site is sparse about spring locations and accessibility to public. Nonetheless, it is a starting point.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weighted-blankets.21993/post-250041>

[https://gravid.ca/](https://gravid.ca/)

"The heart of your Gravid. This is the inner layer that is weighted in proportion to your body weight.
The Gravid Inner Weighted Core has a specialized grid design to keep its weight evenly distributed. Non-toxic glass beads are sewn into small pockets throughout the blanket for a calming experience.
Studies have shown weighted blankets to be effective in elevating mood, reducing anxiety and improving sleep."​

They claim to improve sleep by increasing melatonin to promote deep REM sleep.

### 5G Canary — 2019-12-31T02:22:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weighted-blankets.21993/post-284085>

I always hated going to the dentist. Obviously it makes sense to me now... X-rays, bright lights, nnEMF’s. When I was little I used to ask if I could keep the lead vest on after they took the X-rays. I liked the way the weight of it felt on me. I actually would even fall asleep while they would work on me.

I have used a very heavy blanket my whole life. I have to admit these new weighted blankets that came out a few years ago are awesome but I still like to cut out a notch in the top... just like the dental lead apron. For me it’s all about covering and weighing down the shoulders and chest... it’s like when a massage therapist holds the tension in your shoulders down.

### 5G Canary — 2019-12-31T02:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weighted-blankets.21993/post-284087>

> Anyway, while in college, struggling mightily with social stress, she built a squeeze chute to calm herself like the ones used in cattle handling. This squeeze effect may be similar to the weighted blanket or that provided by submersion in water. OG biohacker extraordinaire! Changed and is still chsnging. her environment and the environment of other stakeholders (animals).
> I see many parallels for those with developed natural field sensitivities or nnemf . 5GCanary ! We can improve our own environment and advocate for other less represented members of our ecosystem.

I hadn’t heard of her... looking forward to watching her movie. It is funny how it mirrors both ways with animals. Animals, like us, are part of nature.... we aren’t separate from nature we are nature. We can learn a lot from them and the environment if we take the time to observe. They follow their instincts when to migrate... also when to isolate themselves so they don’t infect the pack.

Unfortunately we have been conditioned to ignore our natural instincts. When we do follow these natural instincts it’s usually now because of our subconscious. We don’t realize why we are doing certain natural behaviors and can’t articulate it. Sadly I think people can end up divorced or separated a lot of times when following these natural urges without understanding them or going deeper. Especially when we get sick....we unconsciously isolate ourselves as to not infect the pack or we migrate to save ourselves.

I remember when we where living in the 5G test area. Myself and the animals were all sick....skin issues, gut issues, joint issues, bone issues and major inflammation. The dog was on all kinds of creams, allergy injections, antibiotics and anxiety meds. The dog wouldn’t eat her food and she was always leading me to the door out of the house. The dog was actually trying to migrate- she was following nature’s rules. I remember the vet saying she will eat when she is hungry enough and not to worry about it. This kind of reminds me of what we do with intermittent fasting... as I did that for many years too. There was a reason I was not hungry and always felt better fasting. But it was also only another bandaid and it didn’t follow nature’s rules. I wasn’t using my sixth sense and I should have migrated.

I remember the little girl that died of leukemia a few doors down from us in the old neighborhood. She had a kitten and it died a week later also of leukemia. The kitten in nature probably would have migrated. But being forced to stay with her it developed the same illness. It mirrored.

The video below is by a veterinarian who had a horse named “Lily” that had EHS. What I found really interesting is that the horse with EHS in the stable affected the other horses red blood cells. Once she got her horse Lily with EHS red blood cells good... all the other horses in the stable self corrected. We are always mirroring our environment.
[https://youtu.be/QdXco1O6gdY](https://youtu.be/QdXco1O6gdY)

Specially veterinary Jan Pol.

Large parts of his busy life and his employees' other vets are around delivering very large calves and stapling turned cow's stomachs to prevent their turning.

Part of my life I spend on my parents farm.
I have newer, not even once, came across this type of situation.
No cow or calf died in delivery. Cows were able to go through delivery with practically no help.
Ok, my parents had was a very small farm.
The village we lived in was on a dead road in complete sticks.
But in total there were about 30 small farmers, likely even less — the same story.

I think cows there were not altered monsters as I see now.
Monsters that need IV calcium to produce this horrendous amounts of milk.

\==================
We are quickly becoming species that are not able to procreate naturally and deliver healthy ofspring
and are altering our animals to be in our image.
\====================

We used to be much smaller.
Now each new generation is taller by number of inches.
I am 5'8", taller than my father, and he was taller than his father (who spend about 20 of his working years in NYC area. Supporting his family in Poland (even before there was a Poland).
My son is at least 4" taller than me. Little over 6 feet.

I suspect that other than electronic technology there must be other factors at play.
I do not think that bigger people give humans a long term advantage.

What do you think?

..........

### caroline — 2020-01-01T04:41:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weighted-blankets.21993/post-284123>

since we are talking about animals .......there is something that is really bugging me and my DH and lots of others here as well.

We go every day to the ocean. This particular piece of paradise is NOT dog friendly. It is a pretty small bay and lots of people bring children here and have picnics etc.. There are multiple signs that say NO DOGS - at every single access to this beach ...and yet, every single day, people disregard all the signs and let their dogs run loose. This morning I was walking in the surf and a dog ran at me and jumped on me. The dog proceeded to run over a Lady and her two toddlers on their blanket. I said to the owner " no dogs on this beach" He totally ignored me and has been here very morning for a week.
We have inadvertently stepped in dog poo more times than I care to remember.

We have a gorgeous ocean pool here as well that our council maintains ......and people take their dogs in it.

I must say that we have many, many kilometres of beach here that IS dog friendly.

I don't understand people's complete disregard for the rules and, I am thinking, that is their attitude to everything. They will do what suits them, when it suits them.

This morning a lot of random people were picking up beer bottles and cans and wine bottles and chip bags and food debris etc. Every day our beautiful beaches and parklands are littered with garbage.

When we tell someone politely that our beach is not dog friendly - we often get very abused. One guy used more filthy language in one sentence than I have heard in a long time and became very agitated and hostile.

Yeah - I know - low dopamine. Just thought I would get that off my chest.

and no .....we don't hate dogs - not at all. We, in fact, love dogs and have had various and assorted kinds through out our lives.

> since we are talking about animals .......there is something that is really bugging me and my DH and lots of others here as well.
>
> We go every day to the ocean. This particular piece of paradise is NOT dog friendly. It is a pretty small bay and lots of people bring children here and have picnics etc.. There are multiple signs that say NO DOGS - at every single access to this beach ...and yet, every single day, people disregard all the signs and let their dogs run loose. This morning I was walking in the surf and a dog ran at me and jumped on me. The dog proceeded to run over a Lady and her two toddlers on their blanket. I said to the owner " no dogs on this beach" He totally ignored me and has been here very morning for a week.
> We have inadvertently stepped in dog poo more times than I care to remember.
>
> We have a gorgeous ocean pool here as well that our council maintains ......and people take their dogs in it.
>
> I must say that we have many, many kilometres of beach here that IS dog friendly.
>
> I don't understand people's complete disregard for the rules and, I am thinking, that is their attitude to everything. They will do what suits them, when it suits them.
>
> This morning a lot of random people were picking up beer bottles and cans and wine bottles and chip bags and food debris etc. Every day our beautiful beaches and parklands are littered with garbage.
>
> When we tell someone politely that our beach is not dog friendly - we often get very abused. One guy used more filthy language in one sentence than I have heard in a long time and became very agitated and hostile.
>
> Yeah - I know - low dopamine. Just thought I would get that off my chest.
> it is not nice to listen to low dopamine people but you cannot give consent to things that are wrong or wicked and not saying anything
> in my view (maybe wrong) is to give consent .
> i ll get you some earplugs xoxo
> and no .....we don't hate dogs - not at all. We, in fact, love dogs and have had various and assorted kinds through out our lives.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weighted-blankets.21993/post-284133>

i thought i put an answer, and cant see it! gone in the ether
not saying anything to low dopamine people is in my view giving consent to things
that are wrong or wicked.
i ll get you some earplugs for next time  you wont have to hear the answer xoxo

### LillianVaughanws — 2021-02-13T11:28:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weighted-blankets.21993/post-295467>

I had a deep insomnia for three months and I've tried several methods of the treatment. Even the psychologist wasn't able to find the hide reason of my disease, so I was upset at all. I was trying all known internet variants of treatment and to my big surprise i've read about the benefits of weighted blankets. I bought [a simple weighted blanket from a well known british store](https://sommio.co.uk/KnittedWeightedBlanket) and honestly my confidence in the success was very low. But thanks to this blanket I sleep like a baby now. It gives me the feeling of relaxation and calm. You should also try an ordinary blanket, it's cheaper than specialized ones.

### Nikkig-2813 — 2018-09-04T22:43:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbon-60-and-fulvic-acid.22003/post-250160>

I was listening to YouTube videos about 5g and this guy that had a degree in chemistry and biology talks about 5g and how it affects the water in our cells and the oxygen and he mentioned taking carbon 60 to help fight against the 5g because it'll absorb the radiation.
Anyone else know anything about this carbon 60 . He also mentioned carbonyl iron.

Now fulvic acid is suppose to be in our soils and helps the plants draw in their nutrients but our soils are depleted of this. It also donates up to 14,000,000,000,000,000,000,000.
That's 14 tetra trillion electrons. It turns free radicals into useable nutrients . I started using this from Dr. Nuzum and I feel a lot better, more feeling in my damaged nerves in my feet and legs and takes away my nerve pain.
I think these 2 products although they are supplements, we should probably be using.
Any thoughts ?

### Jason Coates — 2018-09-05T10:12:45+0300

I know that food is only 1/3 of the equation but if our soil is depleted of this , I would think we're getting even lower amounts of electrons than we should be getting .

Certainly, for Kruse Swans who have not been able to evacuate our urban "**Internet of Things/Smart City**" microwave kill zones something like C60 (as touted) could be a _silver bullet_ (but for the record I don't believe there will be such a bullet-- just the _**Precautionary Principle**_ which implies prudent avoidance).

Swans need every tool that can be mustered to help keep body and mind together until escape to safer, quieter nnEMF areas.

### Dani — 2018-09-05T21:13:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplotype-and-snps.22019/post-250278>

I am new to the site, but have diligently been reading and listening to Dr. Kruse this past month. In the questions segment following his Nourish Vermont 2017 talk he states whether or not where you live is right for you can be determined by your haplotype, 677, 1298, VDR receptor and CBS. I recently received my 23 and Me results and I'm looking for this information. I found my haplotype (J1c3), but can't seem to find the rest. Do I need to have the data extracted by another lab or am I just not looking in the right place? If another lab is needed, any suggestions on who to use? I want to have this information before I begin my Optimal Journal.

### drezy — 2018-09-05T21:44:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplotype-and-snps.22019/post-250280>

It should be there in your raw data file

### Dani — 2018-09-05T22:28:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplotype-and-snps.22019/post-250296>

Thank you for your reply. In looking at the Raw Data File under the header "Genes" I do see several results for CBS, but if I search for VDR receptor or the numbers (677 or 1298) I get the response, "No genes or markers matching..." Should I be looking for this information under the Genes header or under the header "Marker or SNP?"

### drezy — 2018-09-05T22:38:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplotype-and-snps.22019/post-250297>

I think you may want to search along these lines:
There are 3 common SNPs giving rise to [rs1801133](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/Rs1801133), also known as C677T or A222V

-   [rs1801131](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/Rs1801131), also known as A1298C or E429A

-   [rs2274976](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/Rs2274976), also known as G1793A or R594Q

from:
[https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/MTHFR](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/MTHFR)

### drezy — 2018-09-06T03:55:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplotype-and-snps.22019/post-250316>

So look through your raw data file for:
rs1801133
rs1801131
rs2274976

### drezy — 2018-09-06T03:56:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplotype-and-snps.22019/post-250317>

All your data is there. Any lab "analyzing" it is just looking like you can do on your own and they will try to sell you sh\*t you don't need after that.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplotype-and-snps.22019/post-250318>

Thanks a lot. That is very helpful.

So if we were ever cornered and got eaten by bears they wouldn't be be tasting the SNPS as much as the complexity of the SAPS and resultant luscious proteins we posses.

In this scenario the bear eating my (hopefully) lifeless corpse would look up and ask "Hey, do we have any horseradish and cabbage in the cave? I feel like it would pair really well."

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplotype-and-snps.22019/post-263719>

Have you run your raw data through Genetic Genie? That seems to be the easiest. It will tell you your VDR and COMT SNPs which affect each other.

[http://resqua.com/702188759/what-is-the-significance-of-a-vdr-taq-gene](http://resqua.com/702188759/what-is-the-significance-of-a-vdr-taq-gene)

For example I have a VDR Taq +/+ and all of my COMT SNPs are -/- (not good) so I am naturally the lowest in dopamine and may be why I have an insatiable need for chocolate every day. I spent last summer at the beach (and will again this summer) and got my Vitamin D up into the 60s I'm guessing (I tested at 55 in August after a month of not getting a ton of sun in Atlanta) and I felt great.

### Ricky Lynn — 2018-09-09T17:04:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ro-water-vs-distilled.22043/post-250568>

Hey you guy's, should I be doing RO water instead of Distilled? I've been doing distilled for a long time but Jack isn't mentioning it in the EMF Webinar. He just keeps talking about Spring Water and Reverse Osmosis Water. Can someone tell me the why behind this? I have always just tried to get my mineral content from other places because of all the crap in our ground water and environment. I thought it best to stear away from anything that was not pure H2O. I suppose Reverse Osmosis is almost as close to that as distilled though.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-12T20:41:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ro-water-vs-distilled.22043/post-250840>

Distilled water is about as 'pure' as water can be obtained. RO can be relatively pure after filtration process.

However, both distilled and RO water are dead or devitalized in the sense that they are in an 'unnatural' state because of their processing. So if you drink them straight from the distiller or RO unit then the sooner you are 'dead' metaphorically speaking. I believe they are unfit for human consumption until rectified.

If you study the work of Viktor Schauberger, Dr Pollack, and other water researchers you will start to understand why water in nature is an elixir of life and so important to our well-being.

### Ricky Lynn — 2018-09-13T00:44:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ro-water-vs-distilled.22043/post-250866>

I have actually read the 4th Phase of Water and also another one of Pollacks books. Maybe I need to go back though and read it again. It has been awhile since I have read it. I will check out the other Author as well, though, thank you! So, out of curiosity Electric Universe, what do you do for your drinking water?

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-13T01:28:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ro-water-vs-distilled.22043/post-250872>

RO, extensively post-processed with water vortex implosion, red lights, magnets, Himalayan sea salt, sea trace minerals, Crystal Energy product, plus shape power devices.

Still not equivalent to living water from a pure stream or spring. Never will be.

### Ricky Lynn — 2018-09-13T17:19:13+0300

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-13T18:39:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ro-water-vs-distilled.22043/post-250914>

Ricky,

If pristine spring water is not available (some members go with the bottled spring water option though) then your are stuck with filtering/distilling your own water.

Check out the popular _Berkey_ water filtration system. They have a fluoride filter for it. I might invest in it.

All post-processing gadgets I mentioned are minimal in cost, basically DIY. We have some creative Kruse members that are doing these things and more.

One thing I haven't tried is exposing drinking water to meditative music sound therapy. Water is incredibly intelligent and has **memory**. Check out the researchers investigating water memory. Note: in mainstream 'science' it is controversial.

Hi Kruse Tribe,

I have been listening to Jack's information for about a week and I get it!!

I have had health issues that have severely affected the quality of my life for many years and I am ready to get well. I have worked with some of the best functional medicine docs out there and tried just about every cutting edge health fad and diet and spent thousands of dollars with no real results and now I know why.

All my failures got me to Jack. When we know better we do better...that is so true. I can see that I need to get healthy mitochondria to get my health back and that its going to require a change of environment.

**After listening to the material, its a no-brainer that Playa del Carmen and that area is the best place to get healthy!**

**Can anybody recommend a contact person on that end who could help me find a place to rent in Playa del Carmen or in one of the nearby towns?**

**Any tips, connections or suggestions from folks that have moved there or folks that have been there would be greatly appreciated.**

I speak fluent Spanish which is a plus!

Super GRATEFUL to be here.

**THANK YOU JACK FOR ALL THE TIME, MONEY AND ENERGY YOU SPENT GETTING THIS INFORMATION FOR US!**

I want to get well and then help educate others as a service to humanity.

# Thread: Need advice – nnEMF causing health issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-%E2%80%93-nnemf-causing-health-issues.22050/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sea Girl — 2018-09-10T07:18:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-%E2%80%93-nnemf-causing-health-issues.22050/post-250615>

I have been having constant headaches, fatigue and muscle pain for 3 years which I can only attribute to a high level of nnEMF on the island where I live (my full story is in [my Optimal Journal](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sea-girl-journal-zip-code-makes-a-difference.21901/#post-249260)).

When I travel to other places my symptoms go away. Sun / grounding / swimming in the ocean also make me feel great really quickly, but as soon as I get inside my house (or most other dwellings on the island) I start feeling the jaw and muscle tension. I get the same sensations when I am in the car, passing any cell towers, especially with dishes.

There is a direct view to one high mast with dish from our house (which is only 500 meters away). I suspect it as main source of nnEMFs affecting my health. If I point Cornet ED88T in its direction outside it gets the reading about 0.2 mW/sq m with the frequency around 2367Hz. Inside the house in the front room - around 0.07, in the bedroom – 0.005. So it seems like the house screens this frequency quite well. LF magnetic and electric fields are low too. However, I feel worse inside than outside.

JK recommended hiring a building biologist with 5G meter. I talked to a few people, but most use GigaHertz Solutions which only measures up to 10Ghz. **Could anyone recommend a good building biologist or electrical engineer in Australia (Victoria) or NSW (as we might move north eventually). Or any other country who could help me understand what is going on.**

The other option is to invest in a better meter that would also come in handy if we decide to move. If I understand correctly Cornet does not measure the frequencies lower than 50Hz, between 10kHz and 100MHz or higher than 8GHz. **What meter would you recommend in my situation?**

**If you have any ideas or advice to help me understand what is going on, I’d be very grateful.** I am so tired of being tired while doing all the things right.

Sea Girl

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-10T18:18:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-%E2%80%93-nnemf-causing-health-issues.22050/post-250643>

Sea Girl,

If you are getting reliable meter readings from your _Cornet_ device then it appears your exposure levels to microwave RF radiation are not decidedly elevated.

Precautionary exposure guidelines rate exposure at **0.1- 10 μW/m²** as being of _slight concern_, and you seem to be well under those levels inside your house (of course less is always better). And frankly, I believe a majority of world's urbanized populations are getting far more power density than you, if that is any consolation.

Regarding **5G coming to Australia**, a report released by _LifeWire_ stated that: 'Telstra [announced in August, 2018](https://www.telstra.com.au/aboutus/media/media-releases/Telstra-turns-on-5G-on-the-Gold-Coast), that they had "switched on 5G technology across selected areas of the Gold Coast," giving Queensland the country's first 5G-ready network. According to the CEO, Telstra will have 200+ 5G-capable sites live before 2019.'

Other Australian telecom providers will be following close on heels of Telstra.

It is my understanding that 5G initial roll outs will use frequencies _< 6GHz_ (as do present 4G networks). The 'fun' will begin later with a broad spectrum of much higher frequencies and evil millimeter waveforms. So unless there is some other rogue RF source in your area using frequencies higher than your Cornet's 10 GHz limit then you are probably detecting everything near you in RF band.

Are you able to ascertain that your N=1 personal health problems are unique to you, or can you find other people (house mates, neighbors, etc) that have similar complaints?

Could mold, geopathic stress, or other factors be involved?

In any case, a good building biologist could be your best ally right now.

### Sea Girl — 2018-09-14T13:25:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-%E2%80%93-nnemf-causing-health-issues.22050/post-250963>

ElectricUniverse,

Thank you for responding and sharing your thoughts. I agree that the EMF level measured by Cornet inside my house is really very low, I would even say surprisingly low considering that the cell tower is only 500m away from our house. However, judging by my symptoms and my inner feeling there must be some weird frequency involved that is not measured by Cornet.

I am pretty sure it’s not mold or geopathic stress - as I mentioned in my Optimal Journal when we moved to another house in the same estate that is double storey and sits high on the hill (with great view to 4 mobile towers) I felt horrific with all the classic symptoms of electro hypersensitivity like more tension headaches, dizziness, heart palpitations, muscle spasms, depression, insomnia, brain fog etc. The headache pattern (locations / sensations) was exactly the same as in the old house, just 4-5 times stronger (multiplied by the number of cell towers?). I felt so bad - I couldn’t stay in that house for more than a week.

I also feel the same areas in my head (jawline, back of the head) start tensing up when I am in the car driving near cell towers, especially those with dishes on them. This happens anywhere, but is more pronounced on the island. The car cabin must be creating the resonating effect. I wonder why the house works the same way (could be aluminium insulation wrap behind the plaster?).

My headaches usually get worse inside the house when the temperature rises (when using heating in winter or when the weather is getting hot in summer). I was thinking maybe this source of nnEMF is responsible for creating positively charged ions in the air that aggravate my symptoms?

**Still looking for a good electrical engineer or a building biologist and searching for a decent RF meter that measures the frequencies that are blind spots for Cornet. Any advice?**

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-14T18:02:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-%E2%80%93-nnemf-causing-health-issues.22050/post-250970>

Sea Girl,

If your gut instinct is telling you that nnEMF is a causative factor in your health problems then I would advise you to follow it implicitly until you know otherwise.

Your symptoms definitely include some of the classic ones for **EHS** (_Electromagnetic Hypersensitivity_ Syndrome), so you may be one of our unfortunate 'canaries in the coal mine' that are 'ahead' of us in this problem.

With **5G and its demonic brethren** coming it is only a matter of time for those of us who are now 'pre-EHS' to develop into full-blown EHS. My heart goes out to all our unsuspecting neighbors and friends who have no inkling about what's insidiously coming to town on light beams.

Since you are Down Under, please contact the Australian Institute of Building Biology to help you locate a practitioner. Good luck there.

-   One more thing you can check with your Cornet is **electromagnetic ELF and electric fields**. Sleuth around your dwelling for any 'hot spots' that could arise from dirty electricity. You might be surprised.

### Sea Girl — 2018-09-18T01:46:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-%E2%80%93-nnemf-causing-health-issues.22050/post-251237>

ElectricUniverse,

Looks like I do have EHS – which means I have a bunch of symptoms in the bad environment, but they go away surprisingly quickly when I move to a better one. Luckily I don't have any chronic illnesses and never take any medications (even if I have a bad headache I prefer to know what is happening to my body). I feel fantastic after an hour on the beach or in the forest.

In a way, it’s a blessing, because my body tells me something that most other people don’t notice (until it’s too late and even when it is). When I am in the car I feel like I have a meter built-in in my jaw – it’s that sensitive. We’ll go north for the next 2 weeks to explore places, so I will keep a record of my readings.

I talked to a few Building Biologists from this Institute, but they don’t have 5G meters yet. Will try to contact electrical engineers.

Electric fields are low in my current house. Can you estimate the amount of dirty electricity by the electric fields readings or only with stezer meter?

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-18T18:12:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-%E2%80%93-nnemf-causing-health-issues.22050/post-251270>

My _Stetzer Microsurge Meter Model GS-M300A_ measures dirty electricity EMF using a somewhat arbitrary numerical scale, but they do classify the readings in an understandible way:

**Reading/Condition**
< 25 Good
25-50 Average
\> 50 Undesirable (need to install filters)

I just measured dining area receptacle and it read 55. Good thing I am vacating this place soon.

Stetzer and other companies sell special filters (they plug into wall outlets) to remediate high readings (and I have a few). They are not without controversy-- there have been questions raised about effectiveness and safety of these devices.

**As usual, it's complicated, always complicated. Technology used to solve technology problems is never neutral and always, always causes unintended consequences.**

You probably know that **RF armour** is popular among EHS sufferers (you know-- the RF reflective mesh or foil clothing that is supposed to shield body from effects of mw EF).

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

In fact, it has been popular for decades with pregnant women in China (by contrast, many pregnant women in USA are ignorant and put their smart phones, lap top computers, etc. on their laps and bellies).

I've considered buying protective briefs and T-shirts but am hesitant to do so. This clothing, as far as I know, has not been thoroughly tested for safety.

Big question is where does RF radiation go that slips into body that is not covered by shielding? Does it ricochet inside body tissues because it can't get out through shielding (thus amplifying exposure?

There are some severely compromised EHS patients that have to wear full body armour head-to-toe, including a veil over head. That might work, but who wants to go around looking like wispy ghost?

# Thread: Weird salt question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-salt-question.22058/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aerose91 — 2018-09-11T03:43:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-salt-question.22058/post-250712>

For point of reference I am a lyme/CIRS/adrenal fatigue guy. Amongst many things, i have a sensitive relationship with salt. I have to do regular detoxing (IR saunas, epsom salt baths, coffee enemas) to keep me upright but if i don't take in enough electrolytes beforehand, particularly salt, it unleashes hell on me. My thinking will slow down, mood plummets, i become irritable, lights get brighter, i become an emotional trainwreck.
The more dehydrated i get, the more sensitive i am to electrolyte levels and balances. If i detoxed too much and try to take in a lot of salt to rehydrate i will get absurd levels of anxiety, diarrhea, excessive urination. The crazy thing is that once that's triggered, it can last for months.
Nothing I've done over the past 3 years has improved this but i honestly don't even know what the cause is. I know it's a long shot but just putting it out there to see if anyone has any suggestions.

Ed note: when i brought this up to my doctor he tested my ADH and osmolality. My ADH has always been low or low-normal and my osmolality has always been normal

### Quantum1 — 2018-09-11T05:11:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-salt-question.22058/post-250720>

.
you mention salt.... are you using regular table salt? Using real sea salt you can take up to 25 grams per day or more. Roman soldiers consumed well over 25 grams per day.

### Aerose91 — 2018-09-11T05:38:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-salt-question.22058/post-250723>

Celtic sea salt or if im in some random health food store i may pick up a different unrefined sea salt. Never table salt
