# Thread: Why is red light after sunset still bad?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-red-light-after-sunset-still-bad.22071/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ossa — 2018-09-13T19:17:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-red-light-after-sunset-still-bad.22071/post-250919>

Say there was a bulb , lamp or computer screen that only emmited red light. What would be harmful about this? Since red light does not damage melatonin release how can it still harm your sleep? Does any visible light still affect other hormone cycles such as cortisol, adrenaline that could hurt sleep quality , onset?

### Lahelada — 2018-09-13T21:44:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-red-light-after-sunset-still-bad.22071/post-250936>

> [Ossa said:](/goto/post?id=250919)
>
> Say there was a bulb , lamp or computer screen that only emmited red light. What would be harmful about this? Since red light does not damage melatonin release how can it still harm your sleep? Does any visible light still affect other hormone cycles such as cortisol, adrenaline that could hurt sleep quality , onset?

Red light would be better but the light level is important. We are meant to "see" dark. Red light WILL still reduce your melatonin substantially. Plus the activity on the computer game will transport you into day mode activating daytime type responses like arousal to fight. The easiest way to say it day is light and night is dark. Anything else is a compromise you pay for.

### Penny — 2018-09-13T22:29:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-red-light-after-sunset-still-bad.22071/post-250938>

> [Ossa said:](/goto/post?id=250919)
>
> Say there was a bulb , lamp or computer screen that only emmited red light. What would be harmful about this? Since red light does not damage melatonin release how can it still harm your sleep? Does any visible light still affect other hormone cycles such as cortisol, adrenaline that could hurt sleep quality , onset?

Any fake light is bad because it flickers - this causes aging/cell degradation/ubiquitination/loss of ATP (due to the increase in amino acids bonding together - each peptide bond costs 5 ATP ) - you can bypass this effect by using a battery - like if you have an RV that uses lights plugged into the battery, it will be DC current and it won't flicker - there were some great Patreon posts on this BTW

[https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/flicker-effect-light-jack-kruse/?published=t](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/flicker-effect-light-jack-kruse/?published=t)

Also, there is melanopsin in every cell - if you hit it with blue light, it kills melatonin in that cell - this destroys autophagy (cellular repair) in that cell - so, even with blue blockers on, your face is still getting hit with a bunch of light - you can mitigate this by wearing a burka...

You should also run iris - which mitigates the flicker:
[https://iristech.co/](https://iristech.co/)

Always cover your neck because of thyroid damage also -

### Lahelada — 2018-09-14T15:41:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-red-light-after-sunset-still-bad.22071/post-250966>

[@Ossa](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21633/)
[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/030439409290595X](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/030439409290595X)
"The green light pulse (520 nm) given at 24.00 h suppressed the pineal and plasma melatonin to the day-time level for at least 2 h, while the red light (660 nm) pulse given at the same time of the day suppressed pineal melatonin only transiently and did not suppress the plasma melatonin at all. Both green and red lights given at 4.00 h suppressed the pineal and plasma melatonin to a similar extent. The results demonstrated that the suppression of melatonin by light depends on the wavelength of light and the circadian phase."
[https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/07420520500521988?journalCode=icbi20](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/07420520500521988?journalCode=icbi20)
"Results in hamsters show that 640 nm monochromatic light at 1.1×1017 photons/cm2 can acutely suppress pineal melatonin levels. In normal healthy humans, equal photon density exposures of 1.9×1018photons/cm2 at 460, 630, and 700 nm monochromatic light elicited a significant melatonin suppression at 460 nm and small reductions of plasma melatonin levels at 630 and 700 nm. These findings are discussed relative to the possible roles of classical visual photoreceptors and the recently discovered intrinsically photosensitive retinal ganglion cells for circadian phototransduction. "
[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/000689938491045X](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/000689938491045X) hamster ...

[https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0096532](https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0096532) Humans
"Red light also elicited prolonged pupillary constriction, but did not suppress melatonin levels. These findings suggest that, for red light stimuli outside the range of sensitivity for melanopsin, cone photoreceptors can mediate circadian phase resetting of physiologic rhythms in some individuals. Our results also show that sensitivity thresholds differ across non-visual light responses, suggesting that cones may contribute differentially to circadian resetting, melatonin suppression, and the pupillary light reflex during exposure to continuous light."

Not all red bulbs on sale are equal (nm ranges), light not directed at you but uplighted or downlighting at floor level is not the same as light from directly above your head and last but not least none of us are hamsters although we might be running on a hamster wheel daily.

And then there is flicker.

### Lahelada — 2018-09-15T21:36:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-red-light-after-sunset-still-bad.22071/post-251075>

> [Eric Waldron said:](/goto/post?id=251045)
>
> So then should we not use any red light after sunset inside even though there is red light up to hours after sunset?

If there is light outside,open the window and let it in.  If you cannot see anymore then you are not meant to see anymore...
And then there is modern life! Your red light is a compromise. Compared to sunset light it's is a tripod compared to a millipede. You get only a partial spectrum from a bulb.It is the correct colour but still an alien spectrum.

Your red light also comes with doubtful manufacturing practices. Red tinted through glass is expensive. Your bulb is therefore sprayed from the inside or the outside. This coating will deteriorate with time and let more blue light through.
Apart from that, light under 50 lux below eye level and not shining on you is best. All bulbs have flicker most of all LEDs.

# Thread: Using Antenna Search to Find Places in Suburbs to Ground and Sunbath
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-antenna-search-to-find-places-in-suburbs-to-ground-and-sunbath.22073/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evan Leck — 2018-09-14T01:37:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-antenna-search-to-find-places-in-suburbs-to-ground-and-sunbath.22073/post-250946>

Unfortunately for the time being, I have to live in a location that is .2 miles away from a 4g tower. My situation seems to be stable from using a magnetico and doing lots of CT, but I'm wondering if it is a bad idea to ground and sunbath in my yard so close to this tower.
My general location is at the edge of the Chicago suburbs with lots of forests and trails near by (Plainfield, IL)
So I was thinking it might be more optimal for me to ground and sunbath in locations near me that are at least a mile away from a cell tower and out in nature. That way I'm avoiding stray ground currents and minimizing the impact of cellular radiation.
What do you guys think of this? Should I stop sunbathing/grounding around my house. Would this strategy make much of a difference?

Edit: 1.5 miles is as far away from a tower as I can go without driving very far.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-14T18:07:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-antenna-search-to-find-places-in-suburbs-to-ground-and-sunbath.22073/post-250972>

I heard a Dr Kruse podcast where he discourages grounding in most places except for beaches that have no underground power lines near them. It is that serious these days, and getting worse.

Regarding the 4G cell tower-- you have no good idea what you are actually being exposed to unless you measure with a meter. A decent EMF meter is relatively modest in cost but is worth its weight in gold for what it can do for you.

# Thread: Timing - Solar time vs watch time
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-solar-time-vs-watch-time.22080/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kai-Robin — 2018-09-15T07:35:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-solar-time-vs-watch-time.22080/post-251019>

Do you ever take to account what the time REALLY is for the particular area you are in. let's say when you exercise or when you go to bed. Let us say that you are far west in your time zone, and we use summer time. The Solar noon could be when your watch is showing 13.30. When your watch is showing 24.00 its actually only 22.30. So you might think you're to late in bed when in reality you're maybe not.

If you look at the timezone for western part of Russia, the western part stretches over UTC +2,+3 and +4. But for practical reasons they use UTC+3 for the whole area. To take an example So you are in the most east part of the zone the peak protein turnover will maybe be at 15.00(3 pm) but in the western part at 18.00 (6 pm).

I assume the different times jack recomend for doing exercise/activities is in solar time.

### Wesley Fujishin — 2018-09-19T05:22:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-field-maps.22109/post-251318>

Seen other people post magnetic anomaly maps on here but the links seemed to be nonoperational or the maps seemed to be difficult to interpret. Here is a more useful set of links for magnetic field strengths, one map of the USA and one of North America. Backs up what Dr. Kruse says about Playa del Carmen and gives some good leads domestically and otherwise if you can't get there, at least magnetism-wise. Anyone found any strong magnetic fields anywhere else in North America as evidenced by anecdotal results or readings with a magnetometer or whatever other instrument you're supposed to use to find out the field strength that either back up or diverge from the following readings?
[https://mrdata.usgs.gov/magnetic/map-us.html](https://mrdata.usgs.gov/magnetic/map-us.html) (great manipulable map you can superimpose google map data over)
[https://pubs.usgs.gov/of/2002/ofr-02-361/mag\_home.htm](https://pubs.usgs.gov/of/2002/ofr-02-361/mag_home.htm) (static map of the USA)
[https://pubs.usgs.gov/sm/mag\_map/mag\_s.pdf](https://pubs.usgs.gov/sm/mag_map/mag_s.pdf) (static map of North America)
[https://mrdata.usgs.gov/magnetic/](https://mrdata.usgs.gov/magnetic/) (links for Google Earth overlay (KML) and other programs (ArcGIS, Geosoft, WMS, WMTS, other programs/formats, general information, and other items)
[http://geomag.org/models/wdmam.html](http://geomag.org/models/wdmam.html) (relatively low-res static map of the world and other overlays and program plugins)

# Thread: Why redox before detox?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-redox-before-detox.22118/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Elizabeth G — 2018-09-20T16:30:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-redox-before-detox.22118/post-251386>

This is the question that has been on my mind since I heard Dr. Jack say it in a podcast. If I think of my body like a house that needs renovating, I would go in and get rid of the junk that was in the house, removing left behind furniture dirt, a old drywall, old electric and phone lines, etc. Once that stuff was removed, I could go in and rewire the electricity.

Redox before detox feels like rewiring the house first. Anyone who's done a renovation knows that it can be done, but why would you do it that way? It seems much harder.

Before I heard that quote, I had already done a fair amount of detox, and redox is certainly the missing link. Working on that now.

### Mike David — 2018-09-20T18:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-redox-before-detox.22118/post-251392>

A higher redox is like the concrete foundation. Detox is like all the wood and eye dressing that goes on top. If your foundation has cracks in it your detox efforts will just turn into a pile of firewood. Then inflammation provides the match and your modern world gives a nice spark.

# Thread: Hardly a need for glasses anymore!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hardly-a-need-for-glasses-anymore.22123/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Erin Gaiser — 2018-09-22T17:51:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hardly-a-need-for-glasses-anymore.22123/post-251533>

I ditched my glasses and contacts altogether in May 2018 (except for driving). We were out in the sun as much as possible this summer. We stopped using artificial light during the day and evening somewhere during the summer when I started reading about all that stuff. At some point maybe August we started using blue blockers at night. Right now it is the end of September and I did another test at the optometrist: before -2.75 and now just -.75 and -1.25! I am back to the same eye sight of when I got my first glasses!

### caroline — 2018-09-24T09:31:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hardly-a-need-for-glasses-anymore.22123/post-251609>

nice success Erin. My partner has sustantiably lower pressure in both eyes when tested this morning - so all the morning sun etc is paying off.

### Dani — 2018-09-24T20:21:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hardly-a-need-for-glasses-anymore.22123/post-251629>

That's fantastic, Erin! And the optometrist always says, "Oh, you're just getting older. There's nothing that can be done." I bet the doc was pretty impressed by your improvement. Were you asked about what you did and if so, how did the doc take your response?

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-25T18:13:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hardly-a-need-for-glasses-anymore.22123/post-251671>

I've been myopic since childhood. I dislike corrective lenses (they are only a crutch and some in the field believe they do indeed weaken the eyes causing progressive myopia).

One thing I notice as I advance in years is improvement in my far vision. I can now go without glasses and still have far vision acute enough for tasks like driving. However, my once formidable near vision has diminished somewhat-- presumably presbyopia, attributable to aging of eye parts).

It is my understanding that it is actually common for many myopic people to have an improvement in far vision as they age, but also diminishing of near vision.

Myopia seems to be an acquired functional dysfunction in most cases, due to our modern misuse of eyes (used to be caused by book reading in childhood education, now also blue screen detail reading).

There are many alternative schools of thought that buck the conventional wisdom that only corrective lenses will help the problem--- probably the foremost practitioner of a natural approach to getting rid of glasses was _Dr Bates_ (who by the way was far ahead of his time for recommending what he called **sunning** the eyes to improve vision).

Dr Kruse would be proud.

# Thread: Nightshades and Teeth?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nightshades-and-teeth.22165/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AstralJam8 — 2018-09-30T08:18:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nightshades-and-teeth.22165/post-251999>

Something I've noticed about Nightshades (especially the skins) is that they do something rather unusual to my teeth. It's hard to describe, but it feels like anytime I eat cooked/softened Nightshades (bell peppers, tomatoes, sometimes potato skins, etc.) my teeth feel like they've lost their substance and smooth texture, like they're made of rigid micro-sponge that could easily scratch or break. It's rather unsettling when it happens, feels like if I were to bite into anything hard in this condition it would demolish a few teeth. The effect seems most pronounced if the food was sauteed or fried with oil (I don't do this when cooking for myself but I often encounter this when eating out,) and it goes away after about an hour and has always puzzled me. Anyone have thoughts on this?

### JanSz — 2018-09-30T14:50:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nightshades-and-teeth.22165/post-252000>

Get Spectracell Micronutrient analysis, possibly something shows up.

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=252000)
>
> Get Spectracell Micronutrient analysis, possibly something shows up.

I may be the nightshade astringents/tannins.

If you ever want to experience this to an extreme find a grocery store that sells persimmons. Find the least ripe one and bite in. All the saliva in your mouth will seem to disappear and all smoothness you are used to will be rough.

[https://theindianspot.com/natural-astringent-tomato/](https://theindianspot.com/natural-astringent-tomato/)

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19193699](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19193699)

# Thread: Quick fix housing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-fix-housing.22206/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Forest — 2018-10-02T08:50:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-fix-housing.22206/post-252148>

as a transitions place
For one living on a student budget

Make it so easy that a 5 y.o. Can implement

Plz

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-02T17:40:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-fix-housing.22206/post-252163>

Forest,

The only 'quick fix' for securing an EMF-safe domicile is the _absolute cardinal rule_\-- **Prudent Avoidance**. That means finding a place to live that is reasonably distant from sources of microwave RF radiation (cell towers, antennas, airport navigation radar, 5G DAS mimo antennas, wi-fry sources, etc.).

You can only determine how safe you are by reliable metrics-- by measuring ambient exposure with meter equipment (if you are on a student budget be creative and rent or borrow such equipment, or try to barter with a building biologist for services).

You may also have to contend with dirty electricity and stray voltage in the house, not to mention ELF radiation from wiring and appliances.

This may all sound overwhelming, so start with baby steps and work to ensure a clean, healthy environment. We assume of course that you have already ditched a personal cell phone/smartphone and wi-fi-- those are steps most in your immediate control.

Good luck

# Thread: Organite thoughts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organite-thoughts.22235/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JasonR — 2018-10-05T03:20:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organite-thoughts.22235/post-252385>

Anyone have a say about Organite. I find it interesting and suspect for the fact that (William Reich) was jailed and all of his research was burned and buried.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-29T17:18:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organite-thoughts.22235/post-254495>

Organite seems to have picked up a lot of public interest lately.

Anyone persecuted by TPTB for their innovations or theories that go against the grain is someone I would pay a lot of attention to. Reich was ahead of his time with his views on something he called Orgone energy and he deserves more than to be dismissed out of hand.

There are some extreme claims for Orgone practical benefits (such as curing diseases with Reich's orgone energy accumulator device, cancelling health destroying effects of cell towers with organite, weather modification, RF in general etc).

But extreme claims require extreme proofs. Therein lies the problem. There are no reliable/repeatable methods to test organite's ability to mitigate RF and other environmental toxins (at least that I am aware of).

I have made some Orgonite, but more for entertainment than in interests of science. I am always open to new ideas, especially if they can be validated by either N=1 or N=1000000.

Organite is unfortunately still in gray area of unproven beliefs. That may not always be the case. But _never ever say never_ in this amazing and surprising world.

# Thread: Grounding in the winter question?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JasonR — 2018-10-05T18:49:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-252412>

Seem like a dumb question but, I'm gonna ask anyway. Obviously being grounded is best with bare feet on the ground away from electrical currents is it the same in the winter. I live in Wisconsin and when the snow hits am I getting the same grounded effect by sticking my bare feet in the snow? water conducts and is still attached to the ground. or is the snow creating some sort of insulator and or is it diluting the effects of being completely grounded. switching gears, I always thought I was grounding every day for the better being that I install powerlines underground everyday day and I'm in the sun and my hands are in the dirt almost every day, but I'm surrounded by electric current. I have read into organite and I carry it with me everyday, as well as south poled a handful of magnets together to try and create some sort of repulse against what's coming at me. still learning the science of it all.

### Jan Christer — 2018-10-05T23:55:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-252441>

If you stand on the ground in snow I think you would be pretty well grounded, and the same for having your feet in moist snow, since water conducts the earth's ground. But if you just stick down your feet in loose, fluffy snow, without firm pressure on the ground, then I don't think you would be well grounded. Best check would be of course if you can test this with body voltage measurements.

### Phosphene — 2018-10-06T00:38:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-252448>

What I’ve done is put a black bucket over the spot where I want to ground. Then if it snows that spot is protected. Sometimes I’ll take out a kettle of hot water and pour it on the spot to create some nice warm mud. Depends how crazy I’m feeling that day...

### JasonR — 2018-10-06T02:52:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-252459>

yea that would work. I remember seeing a video of a guy put a coiled copper line buried into a compost pile and ran water through it, and the heat from the decaying matter produced hot water, wonder how big of a compost pile you would need to keep enough heat to maintain a hot spot im shooting off the hip here kinda thinking it up as I type hahah. it sounds good in my mind

### Simon Suh — 2018-10-21T22:40:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-253871>

I recently heard a Doctor named Sam Milham talking about how much dirty electricity is running on ground we stand on. I am curious to know if it is even safe to do grounding anymore.

### JasonR — 2018-10-22T02:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-253888>

I guess it would depend on what size wire and how deep its buried. most wire through residential area is pushing between 15kv and 25kv and the standard depth is 30 inches deep min most of the time its 36 to 48 inches deep. you get into cities that run more juice 750 kv and if its 3 phase wire times that by 3 same with 3 phase 15 and 17kv. every thing is just getting stepped down you have transmission lines that carry a lot of power long distances those are the lines you see 100 feet in the air lots of emfs. then they may go into switch gears and transformers and lower it to your service wires running to homes and business. if you see telephone poles with power lines on it where you live and your powerline comes off a pole to your home then theres no power under your feet. if you see green boxes about 30 by 30 square around you theres underground power.

### Sun Disciple — 2018-10-22T03:31:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-253894>

I ga

> [JasonR said:](/goto/post?id=253888)
>
> I guess it would depend on what size wire and how deep its buried. most wire through residential area is pushing between 15kv and 25kv and the standard depth is 30 inches deep min most of the time its 36 to 48 inches deep. you get into cities that run more juice 750 kv and if its 3 phase wire times that by 3 same with 3 phase 15 and 17kv. every thing is just getting stepped down you have transmission lines that carry a lot of power long distances those are the lines you see 100 feet in the air lots of emfs. then they may go into switch gears and transformers and lower it to your service wires running to homes and business. if you see telephone poles with power lines on it where you live and your powerline comes off a pole to your home then theres no power under your feet. if you see green boxes about 30 by 30 square around you theres underground power.

In north america the nuetral lines are completing the circut through the ground to reach the substation in most places. Anywhere there is dirty electricity those frequencies will be present in the ground current as well. I have not measured any house wiring yet that does not show these DE frequencies on the line. The only way to check the ground is with an ocilliscope with 2 stakes in the ground. Im working on getting my hands on one at the moment. In the mean time I do my grounding and morning sun routine outside of major cities.

### Bastian — 2018-10-22T06:43:08+0300

### Jan Christer — 2018-10-22T16:56:36+0300

Dirty electricity wouldn't eliminate the electrons themselves you get from the earth, but the DE fields would add harm to the body depending on their strength.

### Scompy — 2018-10-05T21:45:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/industry-burning-people-and-narrative-control.22242/post-252428>

Narrative is everything for controlling perceptions. Are we being "burned" by Industry? WATCH THE VIDEO. Adam is showing us, in part, how that old phone game of telling details about a story from one person the next is very much alive and kicking. Omission of information is often the larger problem: when details are removed from a story (AKA narrative), it typically leaves another person to make assumptions and fill in what is missing, When Person A whispers a story in Person B's ear, and the Person B whispers the same story to Person C, and the process repeats itself down to Person Z, it is rare if we DON'T GET a new story created. The wise will expect the original story may have nothing to do with the original story at all.

Do we see the narrative pattern on the EMF Issue? It is could be the largest in all history. Those that break down story-telling understand how the mental perception of the masses are controlled. Can you also see the 'hypocrazy' with how someone can be injured by hot water due to a short-term damage source, but when a person is damaged by a longer-term damage source, society can be convinced to look away from 'something implied as minor.' Society and even our court systems rarely consider, "Is it okay to be damaged at all by any source at any intensity or concentration?" Because some types of poisons and damage sources can slow-moving, many unlearned people around us tend to chalk up the injuries are 'lesser' or 'nothing to be concerned about' even though the damage could result in a glioblastoma a decade or two into the future. There are many slower-poison snake bite potentials in this world.

When human flesh is burned with 10 molar Sulfuric Acid, wounds show up very quickly. I have seen this first-hand on my arm. But if a person is burned with 0.01 molar Sulfuric Acid, it may take a lot longer for the damage to appear and manifest to know something is going on at all...sometimes after the lab technician has gone home for the day away from the lab and notices the red splotch appear; and yes I have seen this also first hand literally on my hand. This is one analogy I often use for nnEMF. The Intensity of nnEMF exposures (concentration for biochemicals) is a factor, but not the only one. There are a great number of synergies at work, due to the biophysics and biochemistry involved to promote damage and disease to every single one of our 40+ trillion cells, and then attempt to heal an insult to our biology.

Now listen to what Adam says about the "Disinformation Campaign" and how the "Media bought it."

# Thread: Feedback on hacking sunlight

**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Green Eyes — 2018-10-07T21:40:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-hacking-sunlight.22251/post-252578>

Would appreciate feedback on my newbie understanding. As I see it, Sunlight is good. There is no substitute for it. That said, my job as a nurse requires me to be at work before the sunrises during Winter. I barely get a chance to pee during the day, let alone sneak outside for Sunlight. While I am working on changing my life, would getting up earlier and sitting in front of a Solor glo lamp for 30 min be of any help or just a waste of time. I do avoid lights after sunset and wear blue blockers at work. Thank all of you, in advance, for pointing me in a helpful direction.

### drezy — 2018-10-07T22:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-hacking-sunlight.22251/post-252580>

Are you able to catch sunsets?

### Green Eyes — 2018-10-08T00:16:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-hacking-sunlight.22251/post-252584>

I work 10 hrs shifts, so generally I'm not able to catch sunsets during Winter. I do get Sunrise and Sunsets the three days of the week that I am off. I plan to pull off the road and catch sunrises during my drive time to work, once Summer returns. I was thinking using the Solar glo lamp on Winter mornings would be of some benefit. Trying to put this into practice in my personal life.

### Ted — 2018-10-08T01:29:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-hacking-sunlight.22251/post-252587>

Solar glo has UVA and UVB I believe. Those frequencies are not out at sunrise so you don't want to be looking at that light. If you need artificial lights, then an incandescent is your best bet.

### Green Eyes — 2018-10-08T01:52:54+0300

# Thread: Hacking occasional sleeping problems
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-occasional-sleeping-problems.22259/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2018-10-08T10:44:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-occasional-sleeping-problems.22259/post-252609>

Here is my problem. I generally have a very good sleep. My sleep recovered to being totally refreshing and Im up in the morning without coffee.
But in the last time I have these single nights, when I dont change anything in my environment but I cant simply fall asleep.
Im tired and want to close the eyes but then there come many thoughts in my mind and my mind is stirred up somehow. So I cant really fall asleep.
The next problem is when I indeed not fall asleep for a time. Then a cycle in my mind is activated thats going such like:

"I have so and so time to sleep until I have to wake up -> thinking I need to fall asleep soon -> I dont fall asleep -> time shortens and I get nervous - cycle begins again"

So I think this is a mind driven cortisol issue with me.

How can I hack that I dont even come to that issue? Experiences?

What can I do when I realize the cycle is starting again?

So far I avoided lighting a candle again and read because of the light, but would that be a good idea?

Can you help me out here?

One additional fact. I had the last bad nights on full moon and one night before new moon. I never had issues with the moon, I think.

### Inger — 2018-10-08T11:22:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-occasional-sleeping-problems.22259/post-252611>

Have you tried to exercise "being in the now"?

If I have this issue, and anxiety issues laying in bed trying to sleep, it has helped so much to just let go of the past and the future, and just be.. in the now... because then all is good, and it helps so much to relax!
The thoughts makes you not be able to relax.

Try if you can just silence your head/mind. It is like meditation.
Consciously relax your belly, breath deep, relax. If you get really bad feelings, just go deeper into them... let yourself fall deeper and feel it even more. Do not try to push the feelings away. When I have done this, the pain has magically disappeared.

And if you cant sleep right away, just relax with it! Just lie there in the dark, relax, and enjoy the silent, cozy darkness.. it is awesome too, and helps regenerate you, even if you do not sleep.
And when you do that, you will eventually fall asleep.

Evenings in bed are really not the right time to solve any issues  You can do that in the morning if you must, it is way better, your mind is clear and fresh and better solutions will come. Remaind yourself of that. Because it is true . And try to do not try too much to solve issues with your mind, but with the heart and belly instead.

Meditation.

### Butters — 2018-10-08T12:43:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-occasional-sleeping-problems.22259/post-252613>

Thank you for your words. I tried so far deep breathing in the belly, but somehow I cant get this feeling in the stomach go away. The feeling is not because of problems I think, it is because I know that bad sleep equals bad function the next day.
Jack Kruse wrote in the paleo fx something about 3min of bodyweight exercises prior to bed, I sometimes try that but only when I cant fall asleep. So far it didnt work out. I get more calm indeed but not so far I fall asleep.

### Sue-UK — 2018-10-08T14:13:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-occasional-sleeping-problems.22259/post-252616>

Alexander Wunsch in one of his videos mentions that he thinks we need more than 8 hours of darkness. Now that the days are getting shorter, I'm allowing 10 hours of darkness as "sleep opportunity", and I don't have a clock anywhere in view. I'm going to push that to 12 hours by winter solstice. It works for me because it takes the pressure off getting to sleep within a rigid time frame- there's plenty of time to fall asleep and still get the right number of sleep cycles in. Even if I go to sleep quickly but wake up for a little while in the middle of the night, the signal is "Its still dark, go back to sleep" not "OMG I'm going to be tired tomorrow/not regenerating/whatever…" If my "inner critic" doesn't know what the time is, or how long I've been awake, but that I've allowed plenty of sleep opportunity, it tends to shut up …. which in turn helps sleep.

Another thing I often do is when I'm lying there, I physically smile which relaxes my nervous system, increases my saliva, and I visualise swallowing the smile in the saliva, sending it down to my stomach. My face muscles tell my body I'm feeling happy, safe … and that its OK to go to sleep.

### Butters — 2018-10-10T10:41:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-occasional-sleeping-problems.22259/post-252787>

Hm. Thanks for the answers. I think the best thing is to wind down a time before bed for me.
The clock trick is very wise I think.
But have you ideas when you are in the cycle of not falling asleep. I mean I can try my best to wind down before, but in case it doesnt happen, what would be best to do?

### Simon Wright — 2018-10-10T11:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-occasional-sleeping-problems.22259/post-252788>

Questions

Is your room totally blacked out? ie you cant even see your hand
What lights are in your living room before you go to sleep?
what clothes do you wear in your house before you sleep?
How long before you sleep do you eat and what does this usually comprise of?
What is your living arrangements? Apartment/ house?
What is in the general area? city/ rural?...what is surrounding you
What do you do in the morning around the IR/UVA tansition?
Where are you at sunrise?

### Inger — 2018-10-10T12:08:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-occasional-sleeping-problems.22259/post-252789>

After addressing all above... and winding down a couple hours before bed in as dark as it gets, and you still have occasional nights with less sleep. I would just not stress about it.

Sometimes seldom, my sleep is really weird too, and I really do not know why. Or I know why, but could not change the circumstances.
I have learned to just not stress about it. I just lie there in the dark and cold, watch the stars and feel cozy.
One night of less sleep, spent in silent darkness, is not a big problem. If it happens more often, and sleep is regularly bad, then it is a real problem.
Just do not look at a watch or stress about it. I do not have a watch in my bedroom at all, so I never know how late or early it is..lol On purpose! Bedroom is for me clock free zone! When it starts to get a little light, I wake up. I do not use curtains, so I can go with the natural dawn, which I really really love

That is what I do. Of course, if you know you have done things that disturbs sleep, then you know the reason and can change it. It is different.

### Phosphene — 2018-10-10T15:22:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-occasional-sleeping-problems.22259/post-252797>

There is a fabulous practice done at the end of some traditional yoga classes called Yoga Nidra (sleep) where you lie down on the floor on your back, cover with a blanket (and sometimes eye mask), do several minutes of guided tensing and releasing of muscles from tips of toes to top of head, then a brief full body guided relaxation and a few minutes of silent stillness. The whole thing lasts about 12-15 minutes and is intended to create a blissful state of ‘almost asleep’ where the body and mind can assimilate the benefits of the preceding practice.

Folks range from antsy anxious “why are we just lying here, I have stuff to do” to flat out sleeping and snoring. The snoring ones must be gently awakened to not disturb the others. They are generally dumbfounded that they fell asleep in a few minutes, on the floor, in a room full of people, in the middle of the day. The anxious ones usually catch on but sometimes they can’t handle being in silent stillness with their minds, and don’t come back.

In the style of yoga I practice (Integral) there is another period of deep breathing and silent meditation at the end. It is transformative, but a big time commitment at 90 minutes per class. I highly recommend it, but if you can’t spare the time you can still do the Yoga Nidra before sleep to quiet the body/mind. There are many good free recordings out there, and some not so good ones as well. I’ll try to find one but maybe you can do a search and the perfect one will come. You can even make this into a fun thing to do with your wife and daughter if you all go to bed at the same time.

If you can find a teacher near you even better, even for just an introduction. I forget where you are in Germany, but here is a worldwide directory. Namaste.

[http://integralyoga.org/directory/](http://integralyoga.org/directory/)

### Butters — 2018-10-10T22:03:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-occasional-sleeping-problems.22259/post-252831>

> [Simon Wright said:](/goto/post?id=252788)
>
> Questions
>
> Is your room totally blacked out? ie you cant even see your hand
> What lights are in your living room before you go to sleep?
> what clothes do you wear in your house before you sleep?
> How long before you sleep do you eat and what does this usually comprise of?
> What is your living arrangements? Apartment/ house?
> What is in the general area? city/ rural?...what is surrounding you
> What do you do in the morning around the IR/UVA tansition?
> Where are you at sunrise?

I almost fixed the things. Therefore I say my sleep is generally very good.
One thing can be that the days I dont fall asleep my sleeping room (I sleep in the living) seems more bright. Light from outside gets in. I can hack that with letting blueblockers on despite lights are out and when putting them down close the eyes / letting them on till I realize I will fall asleep soon.
But the thing I experience and that disturbes most is this akward gut feeling like Im excited. When I get that fixed I can calm down.
But I need not to know what to do before, but more of a hack when I have that feeling coming and I cant fall asleep.

If you have ambient light your sleep will be 1. crap 2. broken or 3. you will awake tired. The ambient lightening is only as strong as I can barely see my hands. But maybe thats too strong too. But as I say, this wasnt the problem months ago and when I fall asleep I generally have no problems at all.
I wear long clothes from sunset on, before bed only candles + blueblockers the whole evening. Eating is min. 3,5hrs before sleeping time, mostly 4-5hrs. I fixed this and it fixed a bunch of problems. I see the earliest sun I can get from my window in the morning every morning - sun is still orange. The AM UV window is a better question, that needs to be fixed. Right now is autumn here in germany so I dont pay as much attention as in the summer time. I try to avoid every fake light during the day and try to get every hr sunlight into my eyes.

### Martha Ray — 2022-10-28T05:15:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foot-deformations.22264/post-317000>

[https://www.zappos.com/p/ugg-classic-short-chestnut/product/107213/color/278](https://www.zappos.com/p/ugg-classic-short-chestnut/product/107213/color/278)

> [Mike David said:](/goto/post?id=252653)
>
> [View attachment 9123](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/9123/) been this way since i can remember. the ball is more sensative and causes slight pain once in a while when walking. anything to be concerned about?

I think your feet look fine... similar to like mine. I have bunions on both feet. When I was a teen my feet were painful and I went to a podiatrist who wanted to do surgery on my rather large bonions but I thought no way. Somehow I learned to wear lower heeled shoes and look into quality all leather shoes for foot health and I wore Salvatore Ferragamo shoes. Those Ferragamo's were expensive but my feet loved them and both of my feet felt better in all leather. Over years of gaining weight and having knee pain I stopped wearing dress shoes and went for what they were selling for 'comfort' shoes big clunky New Balance Tennis / Walking "Comfort" shoes. Along with the medical industry and the food industry the fashion industry has cared very little about health and well being. If your feet are uncomfortable you are miserable.
Much later in 2009 when I lost 100 lbs my foot and knee pain improved but not totally pain free . At that time I was into all things health and while my clunky shoes were very comfortable I got tired to wearing them all the time (even to weddings), I got tired not being able to dance because of them and I did not think they were improving my knees or my overall health. When I studied some I discovered the zero drop shoes with large toe box styles -- options for a more natural, stronger and healthier foot wear. The podiatrists and the shoe industry pretty much like doctors and big pharma have been content with the direction their business were working for them. They do not want some new ideas of what they may have been doing to cause poor foot and body health. One angry 'comfort' shoe shop manager argued that I was making a grave mistake considering the new I ideas. He said I would be crippled within a year. That was about 7 years ago and while it was a hard painful transition to change, my knees and feet improved. Only issue now is the main shoe brand, **OESH,** [https://oeshshoes.com/](https://oeshshoes.com/) I had transitioned to has been disconinued and adding new options has not been as comfortable.

I also wear Ugg boots (zero drop)[https://www.zappos.com/p/ugg-classic-short-ii-chestnut/product/8727079/color/278](https://www.zappos.com/p/ugg-classic-short-ii-chestnut/product/8727079/color/278) Even though they say not for 'snow and ice' I use this boot in winter weather
Haflinger AS Classic Slipper (wool) [https://www.zappos.com/p/haflinger-as-classic-slipper-beige/product/7802004/color/43](https://www.zappos.com/p/haflinger-as-classic-slipper-beige/product/7802004/color/43)
and _Softstar_ _**Fireside slippers**_ (all leatherwith sheepskin insoles) [https://www.softstarshoes.com/adult-fireside-slipper.html](https://www.softstarshoes.com/adult-fireside-slipper.html)

Here is the video that began my zero drop interest in footwear
**Rebuild Natural Foot Health. A Correct Toes in-service presented to Egoscue Portland**
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2o-gnh1DZjE&t=304s](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2o-gnh1DZjE&t=304s)

# Thread: What to do before sunrise . . .
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-before-sunrise.22277/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scott Hardin — 2018-10-10T22:53:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-before-sunrise.22277/post-252834>

Do any of y'all wake before sunrise and what do you do with that time?

I've been watching the sunrise 95% of the time for the last two months and my circadian rhythm has adapted nicely. I'm much more productive in the morning and the first time I experience any brain fog is typically around 2PM. This is a huge improvement. I've also added blackout curtains to my room which has deepened my sleep significantly.

However, now I wake about an hour before sunrise naturally and this has created a new challenge. What do I do? I'm trying to avoid artificial light before sunrise. Sometimes I'll put my blue blockers on and make breakfast. Sometimes I'll listen to a podcast. Sometimes I'll just lie there and think about life. But I always try to avoid getting work done on any of my electronic devices.

### drezy — 2018-10-10T23:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-before-sunrise.22277/post-252836>

Harvest from my garden, watch deer and rabbits, read (paper/books), and think through a list of things and people that I'm grateful for.

### Phosphene — 2018-10-11T00:41:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-before-sunrise.22277/post-252847>

Some good info here:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-people-of-the-am-sun.20005/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-people-of-the-am-sun.20005/)

There was another good thread but I can’t find it.

There is this old warning about not waking up TOO early.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/dark-mornings.3171/#post-46254](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/dark-mornings.3171/#post-46254)

I like to meditate. Pre-dawn is a great time for it.

### drezy — 2018-10-11T00:45:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-before-sunrise.22277/post-252848>

Also don't forget that if you're waking up with wood and have a willing partner you could get a good lov'in session in.

Guess who has two thumbs and has done that many a times...

### Gina R — 2018-10-20T18:49:44+0300

> I've been watching the sunrise 95% of the time for the last two months and my circadian rhythm has adapted nicely. I'm much more productive in the morning and the first time I experience any brain fog is typically around 2PM. This is a huge improvement. I've also added blackout curtains to my room which has deepened my sleep significantly.

Sometimes I'll put my blue blockers on and make breakfast. Sometimes I'll listen to a podcast. Sometimes I'll just lie there and think about life. If that happens for me, I turn on a red incandescent light and read. However, I usually wake up at sunrise.

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx

### Anders Sagevik — 2018-10-12T00:44:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-el-car-radar-24-79-ghz-5g-range.22287/post-252931>

What would work most effective shielding Radar Frequency
from 24-79 Ghz.

The Yshield Carbon Based Paint (Tested between 1-18 Ghz)
VS Solid Lead plates ?

I am a EMF sensitive person.
My neighbours have just bought a two EL cars.
When it is parked close to my home I get very dizzy and EMF symptoms. I have checked all sources of EMF with my meter. It is not Wifi/Blueetotth/4G etc.
It is not any Low LF or magnetic fields either.
It is also not the charger that makes dirty EMF on the power grid, I hve checked all those.

A engineer friend told me that EL cars have Radar on both front and back.He told me that the frequencies of radars like this is in the high Ghz. Here is the specific info about radar in EL cars:

Reverse Driving sensor and parking assistance uses frequencies in the 24 GHz area and has a outgoing start effect at 100 milliWatt'

Cruisekontrol and distance sensor uses frequencies from 76-79 GHz and have max outgoing effect on 300 Watt average effect on 0,2 Watt..

I am trying to understand this logically,, the car is off but still makes me dizzy. The symptoms only arrives when the car is parked within about 10 meters from me, when it is over 10 meters and "out of sight" behind a wall or something, there is no issue.

I know that if the EMF wave is in the high Ghz it is a very intense and short wave, that should be rather easy to shield, and should get weaker on distance.

So if this sources is some sort of radar in different settings, the freqs would be 24Ghz and 76-79 Ghz.

I know there is some shielding paint from Yshield that gives 99.9% at 1Ghz. I asked the owner there, and he said it is only measured up to 18Ghz sources, but the painting should also reduce all freq above 18 as well. Would you think that both 24Ghz and 76-79 would be “short enough” waves to be stopped with a 30 cm thick concrete wall + a layer of the shielding paint?

The EL engineer I talked with told me that he thought that when 5G arrives with freq from 20-80 Ghz, concrete would not be enough to shield, he said it needed to be metall enforced in the concrete.I know they use solid Lead plates in hospitals to shield rooms from X-rays etc.

So I wonder if you think Lead plates would be more effective shielding in combo with the concrete rather than the paint, or if the paint also acts like metall and would do the same job?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-el-car-radar-24-79-ghz-5g-range.22287/post-252963>

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-12T18:38:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-el-car-radar-24-79-ghz-5g-range.22287/post-252977>

Unless you are positive that those electric cars are the culprit for your symptoms I would still be looking out for other EMF sources.

Are you sure you can rule out Wifi/Bluetooth/4G just by looking at your meter? I couldn't do that accurately with my meter, although it has an audio feature that help distinguish between different types of signals.

You didn't mention gauss readings for electromagnetic ELF exposure near the cars, so I don't know how you managed to rule that out too.

If this problem continues and you can not get to bottom of it you could always hire a building biologist with the proper equipment to find the cause.

Good luck.

### Linz — 2018-10-22T17:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-content-in-canadian-maple-syrup.22306/post-253920>

Patrick,
Your maple syrup question has disrupted my plans for the day! I intended a quick forum catch up, and rather than an answer for you I have many more thoughts (and lots of missing connections).

I'm not sure if there is any relevance to D levels in maple trees but I learned last week in a chance conversation, that maple trees do not take up dangerous radioactive isotopes but many other tree species do in the same circumstances. So maple syrup is less affected by a nuclear leak than food from other trees!

Sycamore trees have become very toxic to horses in recent years (high levels of hypoglycin-A in some of the seeds is thought to be the cause of a fatal atypical myopathy) but the trees have been present on grazing land in the UK for many years. One of our insurers has called the rise in poisonings as 'meteoric'. Before today I had been wondering about circadian effects on the equine immune system making the animal less tolerant of the toxin and also the implications of changing deuterium levels in the grazing, and how wild animals respond. This morning I found a paper (attached below) about Ackee fruit which is used in West Africa and Jamaica. The unripe fruit has toxic levels of hypoglycin-A but this is transferred to the seeds as it ripens (and changed to hypoglycin B) so the fruit can only be eaten ripe with all the seeds removed. The paper then goes on to say the fruit is canned in brine for export but a lot of ackee arilli waste is generated. This substance has a high lipid content and the toxic part is water soluble so it can be safely processed into ackee aril oil for consumption. The changes to Hypoglycin in reproductive/growth parts and the way it can be reduced in lipid processing makes it look like a deuterium problem.

In Sycamores, hypoglycin-a levels in the seeds vary a lot between trees and also between individual seeds on the same tree. Yet another question!

![Free Radical Scavenging Capacity, Carotenoid Content, and NMR Characterization of Blighia sapida Aril Oil](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/jl/2018/1762342/)

> [Patrick K said:](/goto/post?id=253906)
>
> 135 ppm is pretty good.
> I will send in a sample of eska (will contact lab and ask them how it works to ship)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/screens-iris-sunlight-blueblockers-clothing.22314/post-253364>

I guess I know the answer to my questions, but I might not like the answer.
So here goes...

I am always running IRIS on my computer to change color temperature and blue light exposure. Do I still need to wear blueblockers and clothing when I am using my computer
a) inside / daytime
b) inside / nighttime
c) outside

I guess for a+b the answer is yes to blueblockers and clothing since I don't get the IR/UV light to balance the blue (even so it is mitigated through IRIS)?!
For c, my hunch is that I am fine being naked and not wearing blueblockers when I am outside on the computer, particularly when the sun is out since that would give me full spectrum exposure?!

How am I doing?

Good question, and hopefully Dr Kruse has an answer based on ongoing development at the Farm.

I too looked into some of the tiny (and not so tiny) houses made from shipping containers that are increasingly popular.

I knew the relatively thick steel cocoon would provide more protection against RF radiation (even to an extent 5G mmW) as long as no radio wave source slipped in through windows, doors, etc. Also-- that you did not use microwave devices (routers, cell phones, etc) inside.

My gut feeling is that these metal-clad homes are not a good idea because of many unknowns. I know Dr Kruse has recommended steel roofs on structures (protection from space-beaming microwaves) and I think that is okay.

So, jury is out for me on this important topic.

Now, I hardly notice EMF when I am exposed (no debilitating symptoms). I still try to mitigate as much as possible in any case.(wi-fi and such has been off for a while at home)
I'm happy! Thank you for your information, Dr. Kruse!

# Thread: Aldosterone for Hearing Loss II
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aldosterone-for-hearing-loss-ii.22336/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stcrim — 2018-10-20T03:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aldosterone-for-hearing-loss-ii.22336/post-253772>

The original thread does not seem to be getting unlocked after the avatar was corrected so posting again:

New here and noticed there were several threads on Aldosterone. I have been through the only internet program offered and I can tell you (for me) it does not work. First aldosterone has a really short half-life so human available prescriptions are in and out of the system in and out of the system in minutes. I spoke with the only pharmacist that compounds aldosterone for humans (at the time) and she said she has never heard of it working for anyone with hearing loss but does have other values. Her honesty was refreshing.

The same one story about aldosterone success has been circulating the internet for years and years. If aldosterone was a success for anyone the internet would be teaming with stories - it's not.

Aldosterone is inflammatory and hearing loss is or appears to be caused by overactive inflammation.

Does anyone know anything that might slow or stop progressive hearing loss????? Or the hearing loss from Meniere's that doesn't have the associated dizzies, etc. - Steve


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: CT if you are lean? Stem cell loss on CT?

### Bastian — 2018-10-20T10:35:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-if-you-are-lean-stem-cell-loss-on-ct.22337/post-253781>

Just wondering about effects of CT as I am quite lean, not much body fat at all.
Anybody done a serious CT protocol being lean? Have been doing my own CT version (cold showers daily, sitting in a cold tub for 10-15mins 3-4x a week) for the last few months and about to start with CT prescription.
I think I just go ahead and see how I feel. My concerns is loosing/killing too many fat cells, especially if stem cells are hiding in my subcutaneous fat cells as I have learned today. I take it my body would be wise enough to not kill fat cells that provide a safe haven for my stem cells or would the need to survive the cold overwrite that. Hmm? And if survival is paramount would CT then deplete my stem cells?

### Bastian — 2018-10-23T10:51:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-if-you-are-lean-stem-cell-loss-on-ct.22337/post-254000>

Any thoughts on CT potentially destroying stem cells?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-if-you-are-lean-stem-cell-loss-on-ct.22337/post-254030>

I think when you become super lean, CT just enhances autophagy. It activates the winter programs that favor autophagy versus apoptosis, so I don't think you need to worry about depleting stem cells. Ain't gonna happen, especially when you are employing a high seafood ketogenic diet. This pathway is designed for ultimate longevity. I would re-read CT-6 blog from start to finish and you'll understand the magic.

I'm quite lean also and do daily CT. Cold baths for 10-15 mins as yourself and cold showers too in addition to getting as much cold exposure per day as possible (i.e. sleeping with windows open, driving car with windows open, going out in minimal clothing, etc).

### Bastian — 2018-10-25T00:15:04+0300

>
> I'm quite lean also and do daily CT. Cold baths for 10-15 mins as yourself and cold showers too in addition to getting as much cold exposure per day as possible (i.e. sleeping with windows open, driving car with windows open, going out in minimal clothing, etc).

I am with you Kris. Read CT6 a few times. The question arose in me when I heard Jack talk in the podcast with Aaron (

at 8:40) about the fact that our stem cells are located in our subcutaneous fat. If that is the case and CT burns our fat cells in order to create heat to survive it would follow that CT depletes our stem cells more quickly to stay alive. Just trying to figure out if I am missing something.

### kris90 — 2018-10-25T15:20:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-if-you-are-lean-stem-cell-loss-on-ct.22337/post-254112>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-if-you-are-lean-stem-cell-loss-on-ct.22337/post-254115>

human fat is not seal blubber. period ,end of story.

### Bastian — 2018-10-26T10:02:48+0300

>
> CT enhances autophagy. You won't deplete stem cells if you maximize autophagy. Winter and summer have different programs that keep us well.

"Leptin and IGF-1 are the mafia hit men of your fat cells in cold. They just melt your fat cells from your body, by killing them permanently!" this is from CT6 blog. Yes to increased autophagy in winter/via CT, but if WAT cells are destroyed thats irrelevant for those cells and if WAT houses stem cells they are gone!?

### kris90 — 2018-10-26T15:38:40+0300

Dude, are you telling me CT is going to put you at 0% bodyfat?? Ya, not gonna happen.

I'm maintaining about 8-10% bodyfat doing all the CT I'm doing. That's like 12-16 lbs of fat mass where I bury my stem cell supply. Autophagy spares stem cells. You don't need to replace a bad engine when you can properly repair it.

### axel brandt — 2018-10-26T16:25:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-if-you-are-lean-stem-cell-loss-on-ct.22337/post-254229>

CT for weight loss is extremely effective, the problem is that it take you out of your "comfort zone"and people doesn´t like that. what about doing CT in Summer? Does it interfere with our Summer Program?

### kris90 — 2018-10-26T17:14:00+0300

No, but you don't need as much CT in summer when you have the power of the sun. A moderate temperature swimming pool or access to the ocean or lakes would be helpful for assimilating more UV light (i.e. soak, air-dry in sun, repeat).

### kris90 — 2018-10-26T17:26:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-if-you-are-lean-stem-cell-loss-on-ct.22337/post-254236>

CT makes your mitochondria release heat. That heat shrinks the MINOS to bring respiratory proteins closer together to improve quantum tunneling of electrons and protons. This creates a favorable thermodynamic environment for the cell to make energy. When a cell is making sufficient energy, why would it ever need to get taken out via apoptosis? You have to consider this.

Now, when gym rats exercise 24/7 in blue lit gyms sans UV and IR light, they create excessive singlet oxygen that destroys their cells, and that's when apoptosis would be used. When apoptosis is used, stem cells would be required to replace the bad cells, BUT, stem cells get programmed by the UV (laser) light we make endogenously from the UV+IR pinch effect on deuterium. Because modern gym rats cannot do this, they are pissing away their stem cells. Hence stem cell depletion despite looking good and healthy naked.

That is why CT is NOT hormetic. It does something special and it can sustain life for a LONG time. Cold is the primordial environment.

### axel brandt — 2018-10-26T17:39:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-if-you-are-lean-stem-cell-loss-on-ct.22337/post-254239>

stem cells get programmed by the UV (laser) light we make endogenously from the UV+IR pinch effect on deuterium............congratulations, that´s well ahead of my current knowledge, and I´m a MD that reads a lot !

### Gina R — 2018-10-20T18:51:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taking-off-to-the-yucatan-anyone.22342/post-253808>

I am on the path of figuring out my next place to be/live and I'm in major transition...including getting my health on track & then finances on track. I'm currently not tied to any particular location except I want to be in the warmth and sun...Currently I'm staying with a friend in Austin (I know...I need to get out of this 5G asap!) I have 3 totes/bins of materialist possessions and a suv, to my name. That's the most condensed summary of my recent transition...

And so, I had a thought last night of --- What would it look like to just take off and go to the Yucatan, "now-ish"?

I was already trying to get there for the member's event, but I thought, what would it look like to go there "now-ish"? I have flight points to get there but finding a place for some time where I can feel safe, with other like-minded people around for community (it doesn't work well for me to be alone), is key. Financial struggles are a huge piece of this...the momentum in my virtual nutrition/ancestral health/biohacking business has majorly stalled in this transition and due to health stuff...

Has anyone done something similar to my thought here? Hopefully this is making sense! Looking for any insight/suggestions etc...

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-metabolic-trap-shines-during-the-symposium-on-the-molecular-basis-of-me-cfs-at-stanford.22349/post-253849>

[https://www.healthrising.org/blog/2...r-basis-of-me-cfs-at-stanford/#comment-832257](https://www.healthrising.org/blog/2018/10/18/the-metabolic-trap-shines-during-the-symposium-on-the-molecular-basis-of-me-cfs-at-stanford/#comment-832257)

Any thoughts on this would be appreciated. I would love to know what Jack thinks.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-21T18:04:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-metabolic-trap-shines-during-the-symposium-on-the-molecular-basis-of-me-cfs-at-stanford.22349/post-253859>

Judy Mikovits and other brilliant researchers were able to fathom that human endogenous retroviruses were a root cause of many apparently intractable diseases like CFS (lamestream science of course howls that this is controversial).

Endogenous means these viruses were present at birth, attached to DNA. But others can be acquired later in life.

Dr Dietrich Klinghardt was among first medical doctors to understand this and is developing natural treatments to help silence these retroviruses and keep patients healthy.

I believe nnEMF is causing even more havoc with reactivation of the human enogenous retroviruses. Stay tuned.

### DLO — 2018-10-25T05:52:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/underground-container-living-thread.22367/post-254105>

Unless there is already one out there what are people doing with these?
Are there some ideal prefabs meant for burying?
I would take it we want insulation?
How do we waterproof these?
Back fill depths?

### Bic — 2018-10-25T11:56:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/underground-container-living-thread.22367/post-254107>

Maybe this link will shed some light:
[https://risingsbunkers.com/](https://risingsbunkers.com/)

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-25T18:11:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/underground-container-living-thread.22367/post-254128>

> [Bic said:](/goto/post?id=254107)
>
> Maybe this link will shed some light:
> [https://risingsbunkers.com/](https://risingsbunkers.com/)

Wow, bomb-proof bunkers-- shades of the Cold War's fallout shelters. Only today we are sheltering from another form of radiation that is not hypothetical like thermonuclear war-- but is electropollution.

I suspect these custom-built bunkers are financially out of reach of the average person.

DIY options like burying a shipping container (2-3 $K) in the ground might be cheaper. However, you would still have to create a living space inside it and add electrical and plumbing. Those costs add up quickly.

Great things about it--- no outside noise or sounds-- dead silence. Below the worst of nnEMF. Storm, hurricane, and tornado proof. Steady temperature at around 54 degrees F (variable due to factors like depth, type of soil, etc)

Not so great-- pitch black eternally inside without artificial lighting or solar tubes. Ventilation can get complicated, as well dealing with possible ground water/rainwater at that depth. If your access hatch gets covered or tampered with then you are screwed. And so on.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-25T20:08:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/underground-container-living-thread.22367/post-254141>

If you are planning on a subterranean escape from growing nnEMF onslaught you might want to consider all your options.

Besides the full Monte true underground shelter there are other variations with certain compromises to suit individual tastes.

For example, you could build a Hobbit house:

Or purchase or build an earth contact/earth bermed house or full scale earthship (with or without an earthy green roof).

# Thread: Light Wavelengths
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelengths.22373/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jackson Brown DC — 2018-10-25T20:30:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelengths.22373/post-254144>

Ok so forgive my ignorance... but I am wondering if colored light emitted from a TV screen is actually in the light wavelength for therapeutic benefit, ie: If my TV screen is all red, am I receiving red light wavelength information just like a red lightbulb, etc.?

I have a DVD from Wholetones called Chroma, which is supposed to be color therapy set to music at associated frequencies with the light wavelengths of color shown on the screen.

Is it that simple, that "color is color" - or would a TV screen corrupt color wavelengths with a source of evil blue light?

Thanks!
Jackson

### JanSz — 2018-10-25T21:13:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelengths.22373/post-254147>

What kind TV would you use?

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Sun Disciple — 2018-10-25T21:28:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelengths.22373/post-254150>

> [Jackson Brown DC said:](/goto/post?id=254144)
>
> Ok so forgive my ignorance... but I am wondering if colored light emitted from a TV screen is actually in the light wavelength for therapeutic benefit, ie: If my TV screen is all red, am I receiving red light wavelength information just like a red lightbulb, etc.?
>
> I have a DVD from Wholetones called Chroma, which is supposed to be color therapy set to music at associated frequencies with the light wavelengths of color shown on the screen.
>
> Is it that simple, that "color is color" - or would a TV screen corrupt color wavelengths with a source of evil blue light?
>
> Thanks!
> Jackson

Pretty much every monitor/TV available comercially is intrinsically blue backlit. you really have to physically filter the colours with an external physical filter. But then there is flicker and colour temp to contend with.....

### Lahelada — 2018-10-26T02:48:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelengths.22373/post-254196>

I voted no because
1.you do not filter all blue
2\. Green is a colour to be used in doses.
3\. You have no control over the type of red you get,no circadian match
4\. The number of frequencies is limited
5\. You have flicker, nnEMF to contend with.

That from the top of my head. Very likely more

Hey Everybody, I ordered from Kiniki and got my order today. But I am such a newbie I ordered almost all blue colored clothes (Azure style/ and Amalfi which is a bit more variegated), and blue even for my husband's swim shorts, and I'm learning how greatly blue light toxic I am (but I will wear in the sun so does the blue even out?) - I actually don't even like the way the Azure looks (it's too dull) so I will return them but I still don't know what to buy. What suggestions do you all have for good colors and good patterns? I know Jack says he wears red and purple. Appreciate it...

### Lively Bright — 2018-11-14T01:25:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kiniki-66-off-this-weekend.22377/post-255596>

Hi Amanda,
I got my order today too. It cheered me up as I've been cooped up inside with the windows and doors closed since Thursday due to the horrible air quality with the fires to the north and south of me in California. I'm very impressed with the color accuracy on the Kiniki website. The items I ordered were as expected. I got Etna and Luanda and am happy with those colors. Etna is turquoise green and the design is like semi-precious stone cross-sections. The Luanda is a neutral tan and dark brown animal print. I was tempted to order their red design but am somewhat shy to wear red tan through fabric. Maybe I'll be braver with the next order. I didn't see any purple when I shopped the sale. I think you would look great in the Etna turquoise pattern. But I think those blues would look good on you too. I wonder if the color really matters with the smaller items like the women's bikini top and bottom and men's bottoms? There's so little fabric to those.

### drezy — 2018-11-15T20:45:55+0200

> Mine was off only 1 hour, as I had to work and then rushed home and just had that much time before the sun went behin a tree...
> But tomorrow I will get at least 4-5 hours kiniki-off-time, sun will be out and I am off work

I do strongly encourage people to try to find a place where even the kinky can come off.

Some member somewhere should be naked at any given time 24/7. That would be pretty cool. Hawaiians would have to be naked for extra long, but it's an island paradise anyway. Somebody would really need to convince the Chinese. Good luck with that...

I think kiniki is a great option, but here are some numbers:

Measured UVA at my location right now = 3.9
Through a conventional t-shirt (slightly stretched) = 0.8
Though any of our kinikis = 1.8

That's great in comparison to the t-shirt, but wow if you can find a way to get all the light even a few times it's hard to give it back up. It's like social media mental hooks that use fomo\*. It's a very primate trait if ever there was one.

I think we could all use a little case of fomo but for natural light.

\*- FO·MO - Fear Of Missing Out
/ˈfōmō/
_noun_
INFORMAL

1.  anxiety that an exciting or interesting event may currently be happening elsewhere, often aroused by posts seen on a social media website.
    "I realized I was a lifelong sufferer of FOMO"

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=255733)
>
> So - How do you deal with FOMO??????????????

Long quiet walks in the sun.

If you have 30 minutes to kill, like I did waiting on a vehicle inspection, this is a good antidote to much of what ills our societies.

[https://soundcloud.com/artofmanliness/458-in-praise-of-wasting-time](https://soundcloud.com/artofmanliness/458-in-praise-of-wasting-time)

I liked the emphasis on how the subconscious mind needs slack to solve our problems and create solutions.

I was saddened by the fact that measured American creativity has been on a decline since 1990 [https://www.wm.edu/research/ideation/professions/smart-yes.-creative-not-so-much.5890.php](https://www.wm.edu/research/ideation/professions/smart-yes.-creative-not-so-much.5890.php)

Also the Greek concept of chromosomes and kiaros time was interesting. I'm always riveted when Doc writes or says "we create time." I'm convinced that phrase is heavily loaded with meaning on many levels.

# Thread: Quick Question re: Fading Tan
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-question-re-fading-tan.22384/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hunger4More — 2018-10-27T19:54:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-question-re-fading-tan.22384/post-254366>

Been reading and following for some time. Haven't read anything about this, so I figured I'd ask:

Spent 2 weeks in Playa Del Carmen this summer. Plenty of sun and grounding, and no so sun screen for me.

What I found interesting: following my trip, my tan faded much faster than normal. 2-3 days by my estimation, whereas previously my tan would last 7-10 days.

Could this possibly indicate anything? Perhaps my body REALLY needed that UV light?

Just pondering out loud...

### Mike David — 2018-10-27T21:49:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-question-re-fading-tan.22384/post-254370>

This was interesting
[https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2018/10/181025142055.htm](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2018/10/181025142055.htm)
Got it from docs Facebook
My tan faded fast too after solar UV went way down the last month. From what I understand the tanning process starts in early spring. The only way I can keep a tan in the northeast would be to go tanning but that’s not happening.

My wife (47 year old) has nodules and a biopsy came back undetermined. She also has symptoms of menopause, lack of cycle, hot flashes, etc. I came across a thread that emphasized importance of cortisol and iodine being tested for in addition to the typical tests for a better overall view of thyroid health. I had to push the endochrinologist to order these additional tests. Now I am unable to find the thread where I originally found the info.

Can anyone help me or enlighten me on what to look for, etc.?
