### JanSz — 2018-10-28T13:38:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/significance-of-cortisol-and-iodine-when-testing-for-thyroid-function-health.22385/post-254407>

We are creature build by sunlight.
Without sunlight there is no chance for survival.
Cortisol testing, level and profile over time, gives indication of compliance to sunlight exposure.

Iodine came to the headlines because of thyroid hormone.
That is rather unjust because satisfying thyroid needs for iodine became primary objective.
Thyroid needs for iodine are 50
Needs for the whole body are 1550.

So when one does not have enough iodine to make thyroid hormones,
he/she first must have got into 1500 deep hole.

Testing for iodine obscures problem.
It is a busy work.

# Thread: Environments for Patients
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environments-for-patients.22387/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2018-10-28T00:06:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environments-for-patients.22387/post-254380>

Dear Public,

This statement from a Scientific Journal puts all doctors, nurses and health-practitioners ON THE SPOT.

_**"Through epigenetic mechanisms cells integrate environmental stimuli to fine-tune gene expression levels."**_

Here is some simple logic. If our environments are a proxy for epigenetics and our mitochondria, and we have quadrillions of mitochondria in our bodies at the very basis for our health, why isn't it **standard protocol** for doctors to ask their patients about their environmental exposures? I'm not talking about only the biochemistry of things, but especially the biophysics and the energy and light we absorb into our bodies each day, like artificial lighting over our heads, stray voltage in spaces and on computer desks, electric fields from the powergrid and the ever-growing loads of nnEMF sources from wireless.
So you'd think a doctor would be wondering about the more sacred space of all: where the patient sleeps during our critical regeneration cycles. Yes, food matters, but water matters even more: what could be lurking in the water and the water's deuterium content for starters.

Because we have THOUSANDS mitochondria in our brain cells and heart cells to generate the energy we need to live, and our society suffers from now HUNDREDS of modern diseases related to our mitochondria, this it obviously telling us about the QUALITY of our environments around us. Here is my strategy: I have spectroscopes and EMF meters to measure, so I know what I'm getting. It's pretty easy when we "see all of the light", and not simply a tiny slice of the EM Spectrum.

Sincerely,
\--The "Wise" Among Us

PS: The "Smart" can go suck on their pacifier-devices for all I care.

[https://www.cell.com/trends/cell-biology/fulltext/S0962-8924(17)30028-4?\_returnURL=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0962892417300284?showall=true&fbclid=IwAR3sgdPp1JhPSEFFbdSFdagDkHzKAmilUiaM7Ns8Lju2r1lvSFGk7NgAKsY](https://www.cell.com/trends/cell-biology/fulltext/S0962-8924\(17\)30028-4?_returnURL=https%3A%2F%2Flinkinghub.elsevier.com%2Fretrieve%2Fpii%2FS0962892417300284%3Fshowall%3Dtrue&fbclid=IwAR3sgdPp1JhPSEFFbdSFdagDkHzKAmilUiaM7Ns8Lju2r1lvSFGk7NgAKsY)

### Scompy — 2018-10-31T23:59:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environments-for-patients.22387/post-254667>

> [Dylan Petkus said:](/goto/post?id=254657)
>
> Scompy,
>
> The medical curriculum is stuck in antiquity. Biophysics to your average doctor starts and ends with action potentials. The mitochondria is this "bean" inside of a cell that makes ATP. And that's it.
>
> What we in medicine learn is simply an unbearably large checkers games. Life is 3D chess with superimposed pieces and ever changing rules.
>
> For every student, resident, doctor etc. there needs to be a seed of quantum biology planted in their mind that will grow through the cracks of their cemented "wealth" of knowledge. It's a difficult process. To remove your grip from a myth and reach for the truth.
>
> But, that's the only way we can move up.
>
> In terms of patient environments, do you think the average person would really know all these things off the bat? There needs to be a baseline level of EMF knowledge. I've been learning everyday about nnEMFs for the past 3 years and I still find new energy vampires in my environment along with ways to counter act them. Even with patient education, a formal assessment of their exposure would need to be in order. Home, travel, work, where they play, etc.
>
> What to do? Lend out meters to patients? Can we reasonably do that when an average primary care doc has a patient panel of 3000?
> Can we reasonably have discussions on this truly important stuff when a doctor needs to devote some time to check some boxes off on a list to get medicare reimbursement and avoid fraud?
>
> It needs to come from both ends, physician and patient. Docs need to learn about this as much as the general public does.
>
> I think we're approaching a tipping point. I get a lot of curious students and doctors when I do case reports that have EMF implications woven throughout. For my OBGYN rotation I covered that EMF-Miscarriage study by Kaiser. For my pediatric rotation I discussed screen time and the implications of RF and blue light on the eyes, the development of myopia, and psychiatric conditions. For my psych rotation I discussed the role of gaining solar exposure improved symptoms just as much as popping a Prozac. I already have my case report on age related macular degeneration and LEDs in progress for my Family Medicine rotation. And I wear my ultra-obnoxious RED Ra optics just to start the conversation on it.
>
> We have to start somewhere with doctors. Jack didn't start with all the quantum biology/ physics as heavily from day 1. He started with more approachable topics. Others went down the rabbit hole as they pleased and many have been "filtered" out in the process. Then there are those that have taken the "red pill" and continue to do so everyday.
>
> It's the people who take the red pill that are the important ones.
>
> All you need is a small group of individuals spread homogenously across an area to enforce their values on an entire population.
> America is less than 3% Jewish. Yet, nearly 100% of all beverages are Kosher.
>
> The truth that we have been pointed towards is not for everybody. Only a few. But that is all we need.

Yep, perfect for Halloween also. Thanks for your insights. I do find fault with how our indoctrination institutions have been _**designed**_ to control and omit most of the biophysics to the world. It's up to those of us that know to seed the knowledge in others.

# Thread: Northeastern Winter Sunlight Protocols?

### Mark Osinski — 2018-10-28T15:03:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/northeastern-winter-sunlight-protocols.22392/post-254412>

Would anybody be so kind as to point me in the right direction...

We are in a weather pattern in Boston this fall where it rains more often that it is sunny. We are also approaching the very short days of daylight standard time where daylight of any sort will be limited. I am not asking for advice to move locations or take a vacation to Mexico here, but rather asking if we should still go outside for the sunrise, barefoot, for morning light or if that is futile for the next few months? Any advice as to what is recommended is appreciated.

MarkO

### Phosphene — 2018-10-28T15:39:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/northeastern-winter-sunlight-protocols.22392/post-254415>

Yes it’s still advised since a certain amount of UV still comes through the clouds. I’m far less motivated when the sun is hiding but I do go out. On the nastiest days it may not be long, but it’s still important for the body to register the changing color temperature throughout the day. I’m still a bit confused about the benefits of just being near a window, but that’s what I try to do when not outside.

### kris90 — 2018-10-28T15:59:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/northeastern-winter-sunlight-protocols.22392/post-254416>

You have to remember your eye has a dual function: eye camera and eye clock. The eye camera works on visible light while the eye clock works mostly on the non-visible spectrum. Therefore the frequencies you CANNOT see are the frequencies your eye clock uses to function.

That means that while your brain is not perceiving a lot of visible light, every mitochondria in your body is still sensing the light environment that you cannot visually perceive. So you must get out every morning to absorb the AM frequencies, sunny, rainy, cloudy or snowy.

Therefore, what I think you are saying is we should still try to get that morning sunlight (red/blue) as much as possible regardless of the clouds or very short days?

Thanks. Mark

### Katie Durham — 2018-11-21T16:21:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/northeastern-winter-sunlight-protocols.22392/post-256184>

Also a Boston native here, hoping someday it will stop raining/snowing.

My question is whether as I drive into Boston in the morning, heading due east, am I getting any of the red/blue light I need through the windshield, those times I can't roll down the side window?

### drezy — 2018-11-21T16:55:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/northeastern-winter-sunlight-protocols.22392/post-256189>

> [Katie Durham said:](/goto/post?id=256184)
>
> Also a Boston native here, hoping someday it will stop raining/snowing.
>
> My question is whether as I drive into Boston in the morning, heading due east, am I getting any of the red/blue light I need through the windshield, those times I can't roll down the side window?

Blue and red come though fine.

Windshields block UV light. So that is the important factor that you will be missing through a windshield.

Another member asked me to measure morning sun back in August '17 around 8AM at my 30ºN Latitude. Here are the results so you can see the ratio of reduction. I'll explain any details if you'd like:

FJ side windows closed with direct light on window
UVA = 26 mw/cm^2
UVB = 108
Calculated UV index = 0.2

FJ window open (direct sun through)
UVA = 1338
UVB = 870
Calculated UV index = 1.8

FJ windshield
UVA = 188
UVB = 350
Calculated UV index = 0.6

\[vehicle 2004 Jeep TJ ~ 8:10\]
Side windows closed
UVA = 348
UVB = 235
Calculated UV index = 0.5

Side windows open (direct sun through)
UVA = 1418
UVB = 885
Calculated UV index = 1.9

Windshield
UVA = 0
UVB = 35.3
Calculated UV index = 0

### recoen — 2018-11-21T21:37:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/northeastern-winter-sunlight-protocols.22392/post-256232>

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) those two equations mean different things.

With the SWE you normally know (or guess) V, the potential and solve for psi, the wave function. Then you can find expectation values (physical things) by having operators act on psi. Basically the math of measuring a quantum system. Where that measurement is the physical system that we see.

Z in the “theory of everything” is the partition function which describes statistical properties when things are in thermo equilibrium (think heat baths). From Z you can find state variables, physical statistical mechanics properties like temperature and pressure.

> Blue and red come though fine.
>
> Windshields block UV light. So that is the important factor that you will be missing through a windshield.
>
> Another member asked me to measure morning sun back in August '17 around 8AM at my 30ºN Latitude. Here are the results so you can see the ratio of reduction. I'll explain any details if you'd like:
>
> FJ side windows closed with direct light on window
> UVA = 26 mw/cm^2
> UVB = 108
> Calculated UV index = 0.2
>
> FJ window open (direct sun through)
> UVA = 1338
> UVB = 870
> Calculated UV index = 1.8
>
> FJ windshield
> UVA = 188
> UVB = 350
> Calculated UV index = 0.6
>
> \[vehicle 2004 Jeep TJ ~ 8:10\]
> Side windows closed
> UVA = 348
> UVB = 235
> Calculated UV index = 0.5
>
> Side windows open (direct sun through)
> UVA = 1418
> UVB = 885
> Calculated UV index = 1.9
>
> Windshield
> UVA = 0
> UVB = 35.3
> Calculated UV index = 0

interesting!
What spectrometer do you have?
Jack have several times talked about public health decline started around 1880 with the powergrid and man made lighs. But he and A.Wunch says sun through a window is man made. window glass i houses have been around for a long time before light the bulb, so even if it is not good, i can see in your tests it has a little purple, i guess light through a window it is better than the best bulbs(if you dont have IR blocking windows):
It has no flicker, it has the correct color temp all day, no powergrid nnemf (if you have kill switches), a little purple and red!
But it has to little purple and red, and it has only 95% color rendering? So during the day, if you have to stay indoors/in your car at day with the windows closed, could heatlamps and blacklighs with lights through a window be better then "shit"? 12v UVA bulb is easy to find.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/proposed-new-word-for-dictionary.22399/post-254464>

Voguan : (vo gan)

1\. One who gets dietary advice from Facebook only and does no further research to validate such.

2\. One whom regularly follows 'new diet/supplement' fads because the person selling it has such white teeth and neat hair and speaks in an invigorated healthy manner (also usually wearing lycra). Such inspiration is once again found on Facebook and is accepted without further research or validation.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-29T17:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/russian-5g-study.22395/post-254489>

According to Wiki, Russia has had nation-wide **3G networks** since 2010, and now have LTE.

That's very interesting since Russian scientists were advanced in studying biological effects of microwave RF (at approx. same frequency as modern Wi-Fi). They were early to weaponize it and deploy it to study effects on unwilling volunteers (US embassy personnel in Moscow during Cold War).

# Thread: The V5 chip for 23 and me
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-v5-chip-for-23-and-me.22404/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### James Gerard — 2018-10-29T18:35:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-v5-chip-for-23-and-me.22404/post-254500>

On 23 and me's website they have a page about their Raw data number. Here is the link.
[https://customercare.23andme.com/hc/en-us/articles/115004459928-Raw-Data-Technical-Details](https://customercare.23andme.com/hc/en-us/articles/115004459928-Raw-Data-Technical-Details)

...The top box of the page has a disclaimer of sorts.

"_The raw data provided by 23andMe has undergone a general quality review however only a subset of markers have been individually validated for accuracy. The data from 23andMe’s Browse Raw Data feature is suitable only for informational use and not for medical, diagnostic or other use. Consult with a healthcare professional before making any major lifestyle changes."
_
So I contacted mthfrsupport.com about it to ask if this has any implications for results. I just got an email from them that linked me to this page.
[https://mthfrsupport.com/2017/08/the-new-v5-chip-from-23andme/](https://mthfrsupport.com/2017/08/the-new-v5-chip-from-23andme/)

If you scroll down to the MTHFR SNPs there are 5 that are gone in the V5 chip. From what I understand there are 9 common variants and 34 rare ones that are not categorized as SNP's. I'm unsure what Sterling's app looks for. I am looking at this. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK6561/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK6561/)

mthfrsupport has this posted at the bottom of the list.
_"As you can see, a good alternative to 23andMe’s service is Ancestry DNA. Normal pricing for Ancestry DNA is only $99 and has been on sale for as low as $69. Their current v2 chip will at this time give you a more complete Variant Report than 23andMe’s V5 chip. Additionally there are a wide range of genetic apps and services online that are also compatible with Ancestry DNA’s format, including ours."_

I have asked whether this is still the V2 chip and whether it's still a more complete variant report. The price of Ancestry is still 99 dollars, so we shall see. I'm not sure for when or where it has been on sale.

I have only glanced looking and I haven't seen any threads mentioning this, so here it is perhaps again.

# Thread: A Ha moment over the dumbest thing

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jaime T. — 2018-11-01T15:10:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-ha-moment-over-the-dumbest-thing.22424/post-254709>

My printer has a blue light showing when it's on. I only have it on when I need to use it. It was on and I printed a few things and continued my computer work with my blue blockers on. Looked over at the printer and thought "crap, printer must have died since its not on now (no blue light on  then, wait did I shut it off and not remember?
DUH.
I'm wearing blue blockers! And they really work.

I'm erring on the side of caution and avoiding literally half the house when darkness falls because of all the tech that goes on here... but, at least my youngest is starting to wear his blue blockers and is getting up in the morning now instead of staying up \*all\* night - I have been hammering home BLINDNESS and have been getting some traction...

Where less than 5 photons hit you in my book.

I have one charger left with a blue light I use to charge a device at night on the furthest side of the house. Still, it has black electrical tape over the light so zero light is emitted.

### James Gerard — 2018-11-01T21:20:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-ha-moment-over-the-dumbest-thing.22424/post-254761>

I cover mine up with clay. Two on my vacuum charger ( that remains unplugged). Two that have a glowy thing on my laptop. ( I have a blue block screen on my laptop). I walk through my house in the morning getting pissed because of all the little lights that I am planning to cover 1 at a time. My mother starts turning lights on and I turn them off. I have a "newer" thermostat. It's BLUE I am going to fashion a plat that needs to be lifted to view it. My dad bought a faucet that had an led light on it... I'm just gonna replace that when he's not looking. Neither of us knew the implications at the time he bought it.

### nonchalant — 2018-11-06T18:21:07+0200

# Thread: using a laptop inside an aluminum camper - antenna ideas?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-a-laptop-inside-an-aluminum-camper-antenna-ideas.22428/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2018-11-01T19:48:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-a-laptop-inside-an-aluminum-camper-antenna-ideas.22428/post-254737>

Has anyone had experience hooking up some kind of remote external antenna to their laptop to reduce exposure to the computer signal? I don't want to get microwaved while using my laptop in my camper!
I know that you can purchase external antennas for laptops but do they turn off the transmission from the internal antenna when they are hooked up? If they don't then they would be useless for reducing exposure.
I'm sure others have run into this problem.

### Michalis — 2018-11-02T12:07:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-a-laptop-inside-an-aluminum-camper-antenna-ideas.22428/post-254826>

Disable the onboard Wifi first.
Buy a 10 meter or longer usb extension and plug in an external Wifi USB adapter on it so you can utilise the inverse square law.

Pin the adapter on a tree outside and away from the camper.

For better reception you could use one with a detachable antenna and buy some RF cable to move the antenna further.

[https://www.amazon.com/external-wireless-adapter-Electronics/s?page=1&rh=n:172282,k:external wireless adapter](https://www.amazon.com/external-wireless-adapter-Electronics/s?page=1&rh=n%3A172282%2Ck%3Aexternal%20wireless%20adapter)

### drezy — 2018-11-02T17:11:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-a-laptop-inside-an-aluminum-camper-antenna-ideas.22428/post-254848>

> Buy a 10 meter or longer usb extension and plug in an external Wifi USB adapter on it so you can utilise the inverse square law.
>
> Pin the adapter on a tree outside and away from the camper.
>
> For better reception you could use one with a detachable antenna and buy some RF cable to move the antenna further.
>
> [https://www.amazon.com/external-wireless-adapter-Electronics/s?page=1&rh=n:172282,k:external wireless adapter](https://www.amazon.com/external-wireless-adapter-Electronics/s?page=1&rh=n%3A172282%2Ck%3Aexternal%20wireless%20adapter)

Put in an inline usb optoisolator for extra credit.

### Cpt.Tired — 2018-11-02T18:19:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-a-laptop-inside-an-aluminum-camper-antenna-ideas.22428/post-254861>

### Cpt.Tired — 2018-11-02T18:29:59+0200

would this work with a regular PC tower as well?

### drezy — 2018-11-02T18:56:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-a-laptop-inside-an-aluminum-camper-antenna-ideas.22428/post-254865>

# Thread: EMF Takes EZ Voltage to Zero
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-takes-ez-voltage-to-zero.22446/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimal Health is EZ — 2018-11-03T02:48:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-takes-ez-voltage-to-zero.22446/post-254928>

**Microwave exposure drops EZ voltage to zero**

**Intro**:

I wanted to share with the forum the results of a recent scientific experiment done testing the effects of microwave radiation on the voltage of water. Eventually a full scientific write-up will be done detailing the experiment.

I played a small role in this experiment where we set out to test the idea that EMF in the microwave range is lowering the voltage of water. This could be determined by measuring the exclusion zone or “EZ” voltage of water. As charge separates in an exclusion zone voltage can be measured in millivolts using electrodes.

**Background**:

Many on this forum are highly facile with the science of water and the implications for biology. We assert that water is the primary source of bioelectricity for all biological life. Nature takes advantage of the unique properties of the hydrogen/oxygen bonds in many ways. The exclusion zone is one such way. Gerald Pollack speculates that most all the water in a cell is in the form of EZ. The nature of the exclusion zone isn’t one of a static or binary process however. Instead, the formation of EZ can be thought of as the crystallization of H3O2 through a range of varying densities. Looking at the interaction of water and sunlight, the EZ density peaks with infrared light. This creates the greatest H3O2 density. Moving away from infrared, with either increasing or decreasing EM vibrations, these frequencies are less effective at EZ formation. Peak consonance is with IR light. With peak EZ formation the exclusion zone is most dense and crystallized. This translates to a higher voltage. As the light frequencies either increase beyond IR, or decrease from IR, the density and voltage of the EZ will decrease. Thinking in terms of a dynamic zone of interaction between electromagnetic radiation and water better describes the nature of EZ. In this model there will come two points where EZ formation falls apart completely. This experiment was designed to test a frequency in the lower microwave range, beyond where EZ has fallen apart, and that should be completely discordant with the formation of EZ.

**Experiment**:

Lab-grade deionized water was tested for initial voltage. A Nafion tube with small copper wiring was submersed for the negative electrode reading, and the positive electrode placed in the bulk or amorphous zone. Once an exclusion zone stabilized, DI water before microwave exposure measured about -140 mV.

Then, the DI water was placed in a plastic container inside of a Faraday cage. The microwave source was an audio-only baby monitor emitting 1900 MHz for roughly 21 hours a day. After 11 days the DI water was removed and voltage retested.

**Results**:

The new voltage readings dropped to zero. At points the reading went into the positive mV range. Effectively no EZ voltage existed in this water after 11 days exposure to a baby monitor.

**Discussion**:

What are the implications? The frequency of 1900 MHz used in this experiment is a common frequency used for wireless communication in the modern world. This signal and the entire range of microwave radiation is present in our environment to an incomprehensible degree. Because the voltage of the EZ was nonexistent we can assert that there was no exclusion zone. The effect of a cheap baby monitor was enough to eradicate the exclusion zone entirely. Returning to our initial statement that water is the main source of all bioelectricity, we can draw a clear connection between wireless communication and degradation of bioelectricity.

By overemphasizing a range of the electromagnetic spectrum that is discordant with water we are witnessing the effects on human health. Wherever the mitochondria in the body are least able to revitalize and restore the voltage of water lost due to microwave radiation is where disease will manifest.

With some funding and additional research much more could be tested in relation to microwave radiation and the exclusion zone of water. For example, 11 days exposure was an arbitrary time. The loss of EZ voltage could be measured continuously over time to graph the rate of degradation. Other frequencies should be tested, and frequencies should be tested in combination.

The exclusion zone and subsequent voltage can be thought of as a zone of interaction with electromagnetic radiation and water. Because it peaks with exposure to IR light, there should be a corresponding upside down "peak" representing maximum degradation of EZ. This frequency is maximally discordant with the creation of voltage in water. We believe this frequency lies within lower band microwave radiation and should be determined.

# Thread: Reducing ELF from appliances
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reducing-elf-from-appliances.22447/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Andrew H — 2018-11-03T05:26:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reducing-elf-from-appliances.22447/post-254939>

How can I reduce ELF from appliances that already have a grounded power cord? Certainly I see ELF reductions when using a ground cord in my laptop.

I have in mind electric guitar and bass amps. I had a building biologist measure one when he came by. The ELFs by the amp were 50 V/m, while by the guitar (passive humbucker pickups) were 10-20 V/m. The guitar was around 4 feet away from the amp. Magnetic fields are low (~1 mG). So it seems the ELF from the guitar is a portion that makes it way from the amp through the instrument cable.

The amps generall have inputs for speaker cables (usually for connection to other speaker cabinets or footswitches). Perhaps I could take a speaker cable, and splice on a ground cord to that?

Replace the existing grounded power cord with a better shielded one? Or perhaps there are some materials I could wrap around the instrument cable to help absorb ELF?

I know the temptation will be to say just give it all up and play only acoustic guitar. Even before my environmental illness days I did prefer acoustic over electric (and still do). But I sometimes like to play electric guitar for the unique sounds. It brings me great joy and peace, which is in short supply these days. So even if I don't play it super often, I would like to not give it up. I do not care if this means having multiple weird-looking spliced ground cords sticking out the back of the amp.

# Thread: Gas or electric power
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gas-or-electric-power.22459/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Solkysset — 2018-11-04T00:15:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gas-or-electric-power.22459/post-255001>

Having the option we can use gas to power dryers, fireplaces, hot water heaters, furnaces and space heaters, ovens, stoves and refrigerators but we risk inhaling: methane, radon and other radioactive materials, BTEX (benzene, toluene, ethylbenzene and xylene), organometallic compounds such as methylmercury organoarsenic and organolead, mercaptan odorants, nitrogen dioxide, carbon monoxide, organic compounds (including formaldehyde), fine particulates, and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons, ect.

But there's more.

[https://eatbeautiful.net/2015/12/13...-risks-best-cook-food-dry-clothes-heat-house/](https://eatbeautiful.net/2015/12/13/natural-gas-propane-major-health-risks-best-cook-food-dry-clothes-heat-house/)

So should we create more magnetic fields and pay more for electricity instead?

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

One of these would only reduce some risks associated with gas.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-11-04T17:07:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gas-or-electric-power.22459/post-255025>

For heating requirements **I would vote for gas (utility natural gas or propane) hands down.**

The only exception is unvented cooking (gas ovens and stovetops). Cooking should be done with electric appliances or wood (wood stove).

I fail to see an indoor pollution hazard using **modern** gas appliances and heating systems. Modern venting systems apply positive venting/drafting mechanisms to ensure combustion exhaust is fully vented to outside.

From personal experience, I know electric heating systems are the most expensive man-made heat on planet. Unless you have a super duper insulated dwelling installing an electric furnace is going to rob you blind in energy costs for years to come. Utility electric rates can only go much higher.

Don't count on solar to save the day-- it may work for heat in mild winters in sunny desert areas but in temperate climes with extremely cold and cloudy days it comes up short.

Avoiding more household dirty electricity is another advantage of ditching electric heating appliances.

Of course, heating with wood in super-efficient wood stoves is a _gold standard_, but there is only so much fire wood available, unless you have a thickly wooded property and you are willing to do all the heavy labor involved in harvesting and preparing it.

### JanSz — 2018-11-04T19:01:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gas-or-electric-power.22459/post-255038>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=255025)
>
> Of course, heating with wood in super-efficient wood stoves is a _gold standard_, but there is only so much fire wood available, unless you have a thickly wooded property and you are willing to do all the heavy labor involved in harvesting and preparing it.

One can also heat water in outside furnaces like the one on the right in picture.
Hot water can then be used as a heat source to heat:
hot water for kitchen, showers etc
house heat
swimming pool heat

In this house there is also backup oil system to heat heating water (when old lumber jack quit or was fired and new is not yet hired).

[https://www.zerohedge.com/news/2018...ns-how-global-economy-more-fragile-today-2007](https://www.zerohedge.com/news/2018-11-04/nassim-taleb-explains-how-global-economy-more-fragile-today-2007)

Nassim Taleb helped popularize the term black swan. Jack obviously loves the term.

If you're like me, you read financial news with the eye of someone who understands that the underlying value of an economy is the health of the populace - nothing more, nothing less.

This article could word for word be used to refer only to the health of the global populace, rather than the health of the global economy, and would still persist.

### Butters — 2018-11-06T12:48:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-frequency.22469/post-255102>

Im doing CT adaptation bath again and I wonder how often I should do this.
Every day? Every second...?
Im on 10min Cold baths now and need quite some time to warm up afterwards, with feet and hands coldest and needing most time.

### Inger — 2018-11-07T14:18:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-frequency.22469/post-255160>

I think it depends very much on the person, the N=1

What I do is, I do quick-CT every day, but only a minute or two
and the temperature is always the same than it was in the night/outside.
I take a warm shower in the mornings after watching the sunrise, then I dip in my rain barrel.. and after that I stay nude in the morning light for at least another minute or mostly longer.

I also sleep with wide open window, no curtains, year round, also in freezing temps in winter, and never use heating in my bedroom. So I guess I get kind of air CT in the night too

I do this, always, religiously.
I love my morning routine.
I feel, how it is so good for me.. I just feel it!

If I take a walk in the woods, I often take short dip/swim in the tiny lake I walk by, but also only a minute or two (in the cold season). I never plan that dip tho, I just take off my clothes and dip in the lake if the water calls me.. and it almost always do....lol I cannot resist that silent voice/invitation

I guess you just need to find out what is the best way for you...through testing

### Marko Pollo — 2020-01-04T01:58:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriacs-team-up-to-save-money.22483/post-284211>

Hey mito friends,

I think we all like to spend less, many like to spend consciously, and everyone appreciates value; however I don't see much hope in negotiating discounts. What we can do is post what we think are good products or services, at a fair price, that don't leave a skidmark on the earth or rely on unfair labor practices.

I'll go first.
From Costco i like fresh dungeness crab meat. A good value at $25/lb. Caught sustainably off Oregon coast by independent small boat fisherman. I think it is better than king crab or lobster and I can prepare a couple of huge gourmet meals like omlettes, melts, bisque or crab cakes. Crab is also pricey and spoils super fast. Small fishmongers and many grocery stores will wash older crab with a dilute bleach solution rather than throw it away. Im 99.9% sure costco moves it quickly and doesn't rinse it.

Costco pork belly. Great price. No nitrates or nitrites. Probably feedlot\\ factory raised so this is far from perfect but until i can raise my own...
I cut the belly into four equal pieces, rub with redmonds kosher salt and wrap pieces in parchment paper. After a week or so in fridge i freeze three. Sometimes an hour in a cold smoker for variety. Really enjoy slicing it by hand every morning.

Buen provecho.

I can spend 3 night without sleep to do one small homework. We should close it. # Thread: Chronobiology Works - Dad's take a note for your wife and daughters
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronobiology-works-dads-take-a-note-for-your-wife-and-daughters.22486/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### James Lech — 2018-11-09T17:10:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronobiology-works-dads-take-a-note-for-your-wife-and-daughters.22486/post-255321>

In an interview I did with Jack \[ [http://jameslech.co.za/2018/07/23/e...rgeon-understanding-how-nnemf-is-child-abuse/](http://jameslech.co.za/2018/07/23/ep-7-superdads-dr-jack-kruse-neurosurgeon-understanding-how-nnemf-is-child-abuse/) \]

One of the key messages was calling out BS on what I had been told by low dopamine environments. That you are good for nobody but yourself, and if you cannot be good enough for yourself, then you cannot be good enough for others.

Jack also shared his great steps of how he F\*cked himself, wife and children by his own actions and choices. An important message because by learning from our own failures, we are able to first fix ourselves before we can be an example to others.

The interview was also impactful in that God was kind enough to give me my stroke at the start of the year, and the strength to use the knowledge learned from Jack's teaching (in the interview he says how the translation of the word Surgeon, is to teach). My predicament was how do I lead by example to wife, a very hard-headed person at times, to change in the shortest time to not only help me get even better but also get herself and our daughter better, because, daughters always want to follow their mothers.

I have but one image to share:
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

\[ P.S. there was no photoshop in this image whatsoever \]

My wife did a shoot of herself with our, in a few days, 3-year-old daughter. She is nearly 7 months and our upcoming third-child, also a daughter is in her tummy. What is great that after some serious chats with my wife, and her getting a greater understanding from my wife, her motivation to embrace chronobiology was to avoid the risk fo a c-section, and avoid a shitting pregnancy filled with months of pregnancy.

I explained the pregnancy biohacks that quickly removed nausea, along by doing them with her and she did them on her own. She also understood the responsibility as a mother, that our daughter Lily will embrace and follow in her footsteps. My wife loves the grounding, sunlight, CT, changes in the of the lights in the house, computer, and blue blocking glasses. Even Lily, insists on wearing blue-blocking glasses, and when I forgot her pair at home when we went to the shops, she gave me a very big talking to and asked me to say sorry. Her vocabulary is amazing and her understanding of the science is sound.

Everyone is about the proof is in the pudding. Well, I love how seeing the sunrise every day creates a cognitive bias toward nature. It has an infectious effect on my family. Alexa (my wife, nearly 7 months pregnant) has put together a special campaign to help get women enticed into Chronobiology.

By taking all women, and giving them a special photo shoot to experience how nature changes their beauty. Having employed Chronobiology, this third pregnancy she has been her slimmest (no dieting), actually eating more, no exercise, and the specialist 4D scan showed the healthiest out of all our children, and in the doctor's practice, the specialist OBGYN asked again permission to use my wife's results at a Congress.

If you want to learn and experience Chronobiology in a different way, I recommend booking a Real Women = Every Women campaign shoot.

P.S. this shot was taken at sunset....get the idea?

Seriously ladies, and husband, if your wife feels she is too overweight for the shoot, you would be surprised what this photographer can do, with NO Photoshopping at all (my wife).

People call me odd, and often reference me to Sheldon from Big Bang Theory. But I am happy that female quantum biology presents alternative means to help women change their biology, create their cognitive bias toward nature, and thereby raise their dopamine levels.

The sum of the point of this post:
Literally but your skin in the game and into the sun because Chronobiology works.

# Thread: We cannot rely on a Device's Signal Bars for EMF Exposures
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-cannot-rely-on-a-devices-signal-bars-for-emf-exposures.22488/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2018-11-10T05:23:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-cannot-rely-on-a-devices-signal-bars-for-emf-exposures.22488/post-255375>

And here I go again breaking news over at EMF Warriors that the media dare not report. Just as I expected, people are being microwaved more than they realize, if we have a reliance on the phone signal bar-strength. This is very much like PhoneGate Scandal to me: the public has been getting misinformation about their radiation exposures in a plethora of ways. Because there are no standards, laws or FCC Regulations about the formula of connection bars actually mean and/or how they actively update, Industry continues to get away with misinformation into infinity ∞ because the public isn't aware there is a deeper health-problem at play. Public Health and Safety is literally being played.

Now, listen to what the reporter says about each carrier using a different formula. Marketers must have known about this for a decade or more: if a telecom creates a formula that shows less bars, you have more customers flee from your service. That is their bottom line, but what they don't want to let the public know is that if a person is relying on "more bars" to minimize their RF/Microwave exposure, it can be a bad move. Instead, the media shows an app about the number of nearby phone towers that are around to make a good connection (implying that when more phone towers go up and are outside someone's window, everything is magical fairies and flying, rainbow-colored unicorns for service), omitting any health implications living and sleeping next to a phone tower 24/7.

What the media will never report is that anyone can get an EMF meter and measure for oneself. This fact is always omitted on news reports because Telecoms know very well that if even 1 in 10 people carried an EMF meter on them and saw what was going on with their own eyes, it would be game over for Industry's current marketing plan. My experience has been similar in seeing higher bars than I would expect when measuring with my EMF meters; for those canaries, we already know via feel when the intensity is spiking. One final point: remember, the harder the phone is straining to keep a connection to a tower, the higher the RF/microwave emissions will be around the phone.

[https://www.abc15.com/news/let-joe-know/is-your-cellphone-lying-about-signal-strength](https://www.abc15.com/news/let-joe-know/is-your-cellphone-lying-about-signal-strength)

# Thread: Hacking your Clearlight Infrared Sauna
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-your-clearlight-infrared-sauna.22489/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### James Lech — 2018-11-10T12:22:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-your-clearlight-infrared-sauna.22489/post-255379>

This is a post reply to Valuation Man \[ [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/valuation-man.19094/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/valuation-man.19094/) \]

Hi Valuation Man, I purchased one and needed to make a bunch of hacks to it.
Disabling the WiFi is easy, when you open the top, and then the silver powerbox you follow the anntennae cable and unclip it from the circuit board. The bluetooth, that is a different story but it remains dormant unless you have other bluetooth devices in the area that ping to try connect it it.

Second, it is a good idea to place a metal mesh on the seat and by your feet, then run a cable outside to give you a grounding while in the Sauna. This helped.

Third, this was an amazing diff, install UVA blacklights in the unit. You can install tubes on either side, and behind your head. If you have an electrician, he can remove the AC ballast and connect it directly to the DC circuit board of the unit, that way the flicker and MF are altered. For testing you can run a cable from the outside into the unit (thick cable)

Blue-light filter: the display screen has a super duper bright light, it is a good idea install some red film on top of it (couple of layers) else put a lowbluelights.com sticker film on it.

If you want an ozone effect, on the wooden wall panels you can purchase and install Himalayan salt rock lamps (they sell them as facade panels for saunas), once the temp hits 60C, they begin to produce ozone.

Install a wine cork to plug up the tempreature sensor. Ben Green Field also did a post on how to rebuild the top layer with insulating board in order to raise the heat further for his training.

Audio, use the stereo jack.

Vitally important, make sure you do CT immediately after to counter the excess protons generated. You can either have the ice in your bath, else build a CT DIY tank, Ben Greenfield also has some posts on it. I find the stepping stone, of bath, chest freezer, and then outside animal trough with cooling heatpump and ozonator. If you can afford a clearlight, then affording to built a good CT tank is really easy. (you can also DIY installing infrared lights in the unit but not fully needed.

Jeremy Thomley has some great pictures for the Fire Box for it.

What you will find that being in nature is easier and better. However, if you do have some rough calls and need a good boost during the winter, the above combo is helpful.

One can also do an inverse with a Hot Tub (warm water) and wearing a CoolFatBurner.com Chest and Tummy effect. Jack explains the Fornier effect to this.

### recoen — 2018-11-10T14:51:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-your-clearlight-infrared-sauna.22489/post-255383>

[@James Lech](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21577/) what does your hot tub/ cfb protocol look like? Do you wait some time between the two?

### James Lech — 2018-11-10T17:45:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-your-clearlight-infrared-sauna.22489/post-255396>

For the Sauna, directly after you go into the CT ice tub. I enjoy doing breathing hypoxia exercises and holding, hypoxia brething exercises underwater. Longest I managed was between 6-7 mins, however, don't be stupid and ensure someone is near by. Or was your question about the hot tub (hot water) and ice vests?

### recoen — 2018-11-10T17:58:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-your-clearlight-infrared-sauna.22489/post-255398>

[@James Lech](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21577/) the hot tub and ice vests. Thanks!

### John Saf — 2018-11-11T12:01:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-your-clearlight-infrared-sauna.22489/post-255433>

Great podcast. Jack talk about IR sauna hacking. He says if you cover the areas between the panels with real gold foil, it is a good IR reflector to get more red on your body
Sauna timing is also important: Not the last two hours before sleep he says.
you will find the sauna information in the second half.

[https://physics.stackexchange.com/q...gold-foil-on-equipment-and-gold-coated-visors](https://physics.stackexchange.com/questions/74412/why-does-nasa-use-gold-foil-on-equipment-and-gold-coated-visors)

### James Lech — 2018-11-15T12:25:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-your-clearlight-infrared-sauna.22489/post-255653>

> [recoen said:](/goto/post?id=255398)
>
> [@James Lech](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21577/) the hot tub and ice vests. Thanks!

the hot tub and CT fornier effect is described by Jack in the following podcast
[https://bengreenfieldfitness.com/podcast/lifestyle-podcasts/building-a-healthy-home/](https://bengreenfieldfitness.com/podcast/lifestyle-podcasts/building-a-healthy-home/)

### Matt Justice — 2018-12-16T21:38:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-your-clearlight-infrared-sauna.22489/post-258239>

Love what you're doing sharing this here James... This might make me come off like a dick, so I'll just approach this as if I know nothing about it:

Are you sure it's a good idea to intentionally ground yourself in a carbon panel infrared sauna?

Most carbon FIR saunas usually usually do not have sufficient electric field mitigation in them, and by adding yourself to a circuit in close proximity to that, wouldn't that start leeching and allowing your body to complete the new path?

I could be wrong, but might be something very important to look into.

### James Lech — 2018-12-17T06:58:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-your-clearlight-infrared-sauna.22489/post-258294>

Good morning Matt

Thank you for the question as perhaps I didn't elaborate well enough in my original thread. Grounding into wall socket, yes, is a particularly bad idea. Especially in the place we currently stay where one get's a strong electric field reading from the grounding wire by itself out of the wall socket. Don't worry, I did make sure the live and neutral cables were disconnected completely at the source connect (physically no connection) when I made this test and scary measurement. Also the EMI, in the electrical circuit reads high voltage transients, over 800mV.

Regarding the earthing grounding within the sauna, I placed a mesh sheet on the seat and by the feet. Then I have a wire that leads out of the sauna and into a stake in the ground that is moist.

The grounding cable I installed wasn't for the sauna's fields, as the Clearlight guys built a very good shielding and circuitry system, where usual carbon FIR saunas are terrible. But I get what you mean about completing a new path. The reason I installed the cable was when I was using the sauna I would get an excess proton reaction, inflammation, but I did CT after it was fine. However, when I hacked the grounding solution I was able to be in the sauna and high temperatures for much much longer, and I didn't get the excess proton reaction.

Then when I installed the UVA blacklights as well, wow, that made an immense difference to the energy levels. I haven't installed the gold leaf yet for better IR reflection. Instead, I installed aluminum foil to better reflect the UVA light, then as a secondary, it provides better thermal insulation and IR reflection.

P.S. it is a good idea to install the UVA lights as a DC setup.

# Thread: The Modern Mythological Significance of Light, Water, and Electromagnetism
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-modern-mythological-significance-of-light-water-and-electromagnetism.22490/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Steven1 — 2018-11-10T15:58:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-modern-mythological-significance-of-light-water-and-electromagnetism.22490/post-255385>

I rarely post anything on here, but I have something I want to share with you. First, I’m long overdue in thanking Dr. Kruse for the positive impact he has had on my health and my life. Thank you for your tireless efforts, Dr. Kruse.

Now, what I want to share with you---I just finished releasing a fictional podcast narrative in which I integrate many of the concepts Dr. Kruse has taught me. Why do so in the form of a fictional narrative? Read on.

Humans connect through stories. Stories and myths have a long history of being used to communicate complex ideas to the masses. In the Netflix era of today, people are consuming hours upon hours of stories through an endless supply of TV shows and movies. Watching such content is generally not a productive use of time, but it is possible for stories to serve a greater purpose if they can convey meaningful information to the audience. While there are stories that do this in some ways, there aren't many examples of fictional narratives that convey health information to the audience. This is at least partly because conventional health information is too dull to include in a story that is meant to be entertaining (can you imagine a narrative structure where the grams of carbohydrate the main character eats serves as a major plot element?).

The situation is different regarding quantum biology and the message that Dr. Kruse shares with us. The essence of this message is to connect with nature to improve your health. Nature has been a plot element in stories throughout history and still is today, usually in an adversarial role (man versus nature). I think it’s time to reframe that narrative and depict nature in a positive way, so I ventured to create a story in which the relationship between nature and health serves as a major thematic element.

As I mentioned, stories often distill complex ideas into a format that is easily consumed by the masses. This is the case with many religious stories, where meaning can be derived at the surface level or on a much deeper level, depending on the individual level of analysis. In this way, stories can have a positive impact on diverse audiences (black swans and white swans alike). Certainly, quantum biology can be categorized as a difficult concept to grasp, but at the same time, the take-home message about connecting with nature is very simple. In this way, it is a good fit for a fictional narrative that allows the majority (white swans) to consume it in a basic, surface-level manner and the minority (black swans) to take a deeper dive into the science behind it.

One could argue that too much might be lost by simplifying complex ideas to fit within a fictional narrative, but I think, if done well, there is far more to be gained. Not only could it allow these ideas to reach a wider audience, there is also the counterintuitive notion that fiction is sometimes the best way to communicate the truth.

Of course, we need people like Dr. Kruse to communicate the deep science of quantum biology, but we also need artists and storytellers who can communicate that information in different ways. The more people we have coming at this from different perspectives, the more people will benefit from the wisdom of that information.

My hope is that my podcast introduces these ideas to a new audience. I also hope it serves as a proof of concept for other artists and writers whose creativity can continue to bring these ideas to the world in new ways.

If you’re interested in listening to my podcast, just search for Awake the Future in any podcast app. You can also read the book version by searching for Awake the Future on Amazon. You can access both via my website at awakethefuture.com.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-find-a-cell-tower-map.22496/post-262968>

Antennasearch.com btw is most definitely not comprehensive. I have personally seen a number of cellphone antennas with my own eyes (and RF meter) that are not in the database. Be careful and always measure. Radio-locator.com (U.S. only) and I forget name of T.V. antennae site have also been useful.

# Thread: Breast health questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-health-questions.22498/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jen N — 2018-11-13T01:48:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-health-questions.22498/post-255526>

I'm mostly read and learn from this forum and I'm grateful for all the expertise, so I figured you all would be able to help me with questions about breast health.

Here's a bit of my health history
Age 43
No children yet, actively trying to conceive, 3 chemical pregnancy's this year so getting closer!
History of birth control bill (age 16-25) and IVF in my early 30's before I knew better!
Diagnosed with late stage Lyme disease and co-infections in 2012
I've been healthy and free from the infections listed above since 2014
Paternal aunt had breast and lung cancer, she was also a smoker, worked night shift & had a terrible diet
Been following Dr Kruse's advice regarding LWM, diet, etc since 2015

In Nov. 2016 I had what I believe was an inflamed milk duct that caused moderate swelling in my left breast that came on suddenly. I could feel a "hard spot" and it subsided within 72 hours after I used massage plus comfrey and castor oil topically. I had my first thermogram a couple weeks later to establish a baseline and have been using that to track any changes.

My OB/Gyn also suggested I have an ultrasound to check it what may have caused the swelling but I couldn't find any doctor willing to do only an ultrasound, so I was forced into a mammogram. Of course they "saw something" on both breasts during mammogram but it was not in the same area as the swelling occurred and then that led to a recommendation of a biopsy on my left breast. The biopsy came back negative.

Since 2016 my OB wanted me to have a mammogram every 6 months, I told her I wasn't doing that and asked for ultrasound monitoring instead since I have dense breasts. During the ultrasounds they have been tracking an area of micro calcifications in my right breast. They have not been concerned about my left breast, which was originally what flared up.

Today, I had a follow up ultrasound and the radiologist said the area has increased in size slightly (they never tell me the actual size!) and there is now vascularity so they want to do a needle biopsy on the area. I should note that I am in the luteal phase of my cycle and could be pregnant. I honestly do not want to do the biopsy!

Two weeks ago, my follow up thermogram said this "there is increasing activity in both breasts since the previous study, especially in both upper breasts. This is non-specific, and may be due to fibrocystic/hormonal variation, though more serious developing pathology is not excluded. Further close interval follow-up is warranted." During the thermogram, I was on cycle day 1.

I would like to hear from other more educated folks on breast health, on what to really be concerned about, and if I should have the biopsy. What I've read about calcifications is that they are often due to magnesium deficiencies, I've been working on increasing magnesium. I'm also open to reading any books/blogs on the topic breast health from a holistic perspective.

Thank you!

Jen

# Thread: Sun with Carbs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-with-carbs.22499/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cap — 2018-11-13T02:11:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-with-carbs.22499/post-255527>

could anyone here link good research showing

1\. the improvement of Carb metabolism/partitioning through sun exposure?
2\. The sun burning calories

Thank you

### drezy — 2018-11-13T16:44:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-with-carbs.22499/post-255554>

> [cap said:](/goto/post?id=255527)
>
> could anyone here link good research showing
>
> 1\. the improvement of Carb metabolism/partitioning through sun exposure?
> 2\. The sun burning calories
>
>
> Thank you

How is this for a start?

"A study in the September 2006 issue of _Progress in Biophysics and Molecular Biology_ demonstrated that in young and elderly adults, serum 25(OH)D was inversely correlated with blood glucose concentrations and insulin resistance. Some studies have demonstrated high prevalence of low vitamin D levels in people with type 2 diabetes, although it is not clear whether this is a cause of the disease or an effect of another causative factor—for example, lower levels of physical activity (in this case, outdoor activity in particular)."

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2290997/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2290997/)

### cap — 2018-11-13T17:30:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-with-carbs.22499/post-255555>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=255554)
>
> How is this for a start?
>
> "A study in the September 2006 issue of _Progress in Biophysics and Molecular Biology_ demonstrated that in young and elderly adults, serum 25(OH)D was inversely correlated with blood glucose concentrations and insulin resistance. Some studies have demonstrated high prevalence of low vitamin D levels in people with type 2 diabetes, although it is not clear whether this is a cause of the disease or an effect of another causative factor—for example, lower levels of physical activity (in this case, outdoor activity in particular)."
>
> [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2290997/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2290997/)

Thank you for the response drezy. It's interesting but it doesn't show whether low levels of sun exposure or (low levels of vitamin D in this case) are the "cause or an effect of another causative factor"...basically this could be just from not exercising enough. I also am particularly looking for sun exposure not just vitamin D levels as that could be supplemented and by default low vitamin D levels will create an unhealthy environment, I don't think anyone is debating that

### drezy — 2018-11-13T18:05:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-with-carbs.22499/post-255561>

I think your closest link will end up in the insulin & leptin corner:

"We observed that chronic exposure to UVR suppressed weight gain and the development of glucose intolerance, insulin resistance and signs of NAFLD in C57BL/6J male mice fed a high fat diet"

"Ongoing artificial sunlight (UVR) “ameliorated hepatocyte apoptosis, inflammation, fibrosis, and insulin/leptin resistance” when administered for 12 h a day for 6–12 weeks to rats compared to untreated controls"

from:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5086738/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5086738/)

Enough keyword searches and you'll probably be able to scrape something more substantial together along with come of the references of the article.

Anecdotally, sun exposure helps me reduce the amount I eat moreso than burning calories as far as I can tell.

>
> Anecdotally, sun exposure helps me reduce the amount I eat moreso than burning calories as far as I can tell.

### victor_dan — 2018-11-28T14:37:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-with-carbs.22499/post-256835>

Hmmm....... **Sun does not burn calories in any way!**
Heat makes the body cool down so there is no carb-consuming process..
Sun exposure is great to produce Vitamin D if you eat milk products. I think it's enough to expose your hands for 30 minutes a day. So Calcium metabolizes properly.
The heat from the Sun will make your body stock more fatty layers because: the temporary glucose deposit (liver) will not consume on its own.

Here are some tips.

If it is biotoxin the unfouridated water and cholesteramyne (CSM) will be important as well as lots of sunlight. CT will help in the long run but may make you feel worst short term.

CT depletes the body of fat but then the csm will be needed to bind the toxins stored in the fats.

MSH is imporoved with the cold. Start with face dunking.

Best bet is to listen to Jack’s earliest podcasts available here: [https://jackkruse.com/podcasts-featuring-dr-jack-kruse/](https://jackkruse.com/podcasts-featuring-dr-jack-kruse/)
Start with the oldest interviews.

Get spring water.

# Thread: Trolls on the street - When blue-light tech cause a misinformation effect
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trolls-on-the-street-when-blue-light-tech-cause-a-misinformation-effect.22509/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### James Lech — 2018-11-15T12:22:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trolls-on-the-street-when-blue-light-tech-cause-a-misinformation-effect.22509/post-255652>

Today I had an experience that made me reflect on my own experience when I was blue-light and nnEMF toxic. I was parked by the Petrol station (Gas Station), with my kids asleep in the back and dog behind them. When I was out the car my dog jumped over and onto my kids to get to the driver's seat. My daughter is awoken and begins to cry, I'm angry because our dog is not meant to climb over, and my daughter is crying. When I open the driver's door, my dog understands this and cowers in panic only to get her collar caught in the handbrake and begins to panic and squeal. I get her loose and take her to the back of the boot (we have a van type car), and put her in, and hit the sports bag below her. This I did for two reasons, one I was angry but too my dog was in a panic and wanted to jump over again, so hitting down she cowers to lie down and squeals again. Now you have to keep in mind that our dog is a squeeler at times, even when she somestimes would meet another dog and they jump by her she would give off a squeal and cower like she was bitten and the owners would go into a state wanting to correct their dog, and telling them no, yet I have to explain to them that their dog was not in the wrong.

I get it, the whole explanation above is not an ideal situation, and it, unfortunately, attracted the unwanted attention of a public low dopamine troll. A lady (very overweight, huge sunglasses, fully covered) on a very overcast day (the picture of a blue light toxic, low dopamine person), is filling her car up, approximately 15 meters (50 feet) away. So not only is she inhaling the petrol fumes, wearing sunglasses in a low-light environment, but also my body and back is in her line of view between her and my dog in the back of the car. Naturally, the described scene doesn't help. She then starts raising her voice and accusing me of abusing my dog. Despite my informing her that my daughter is crying and I need to check if she is hurt and console her, the lady only carried on, wanting me to not attend to her daughter and get attention instead. Then when not happening, getting her girlfriend to try to join in too.

Now the breakdown. The advantage of attending to your chronobiology, is you are able to raise your dopamine levels. This enables you to think laterally and not have your brain go into stupid mode. As JK explains in the Time Series, when performing properly, our quantum computer operates at immense speeds and enables us to make quick decisions. However, when this is not working like it is meant to, the Neurochemistry and cause a number of side-effects. For example, low-dopamine and acting on the backup ancient pathway of a low oxygen tension leads to glycolysis and PPP. When this happens, our vision of things narrow, and we are not able to see the whole picture. Our IQ and EQ drop. Then we also experience a thing called the misinformation effect, where I brain sees and processes things differently to what the camera lens in our eye saw.

I experienced this personal years ago when I was a blue-light, nnEMF, vegan toxic affected person. My eyes and recollection of the incident was different to what the CCTV footage showed. Being low dopamine as well, it was even harder for me to accept and I would try run to every person to get acknowledgement as a means to try and raise my dopamine levels. Why did this misinformaiton effect occurr, because when you are blue-light toxic, and your dopamine is low, you develop a **Fractal View of Nature**.

We understand from JK, that blue light destroys melatonin and dopamine in the eye and in the brain. When we develop a cognitive bias toward nature, out eye produces Beta-endorphin via POMC (Pro-opiomelanocortin) and solar exposure of the eye. This POMC is vitally important in combating any opiad addiction because it aids in developing an opiad within our eye and brain byproduct that is more powerful.

The inherent curse with this blue-light bidirectional microwave environment, is that the environment not only drops dopamine but destroys DHA as well. Thereby, people will tend to go off the deep-end. The lady went onto FB social media, posting pictures of my car, writing a whole bunch of statements and getting a opiad kick from the social media kickback. One of the co-administraters / community inspectors of the closed FB community group, was made aware of the situation and was kind enough to give me a call and get the other side of the story. Clearly, the posts and comments must of been really going out of control. I did not get to see them. I explained to her the first paragraph above and she was satisfied and ordered the FB post by the lady to be taken down by the administrator. I appreciated her experience in such things, as clearly it is not an uncommon situation in our blue-lit environment. At first she asked me to write what I explained on the FB post as a comment but then after further applying her mind, told me rather to not make any statement and rather ordered the post removed and for her to act as the proxy. My assumption that if I had made a comment in the explanation, like with the situation of me stating my daughter's needs in the car is more important to the lady's dopamine fix, it would have only spurred more of the other low dopamine trolls to carrying on even further in their opiad fix. Especially, since low dopamine inhibits our brain from seeing the full connection and how all the pieces fit together. Hence, a Fractal View of Nature.

The above is well explained in a podcast interview with Jack with the link below.

In [Luke's story, the most epic Jack Kruse interview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?time_continue=4843&v=5W6x7EsE8C4&ab_channel=LukeStorey)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Couple simple questions on CT Kruse’s way. I learned the Wim Hof way which is a bit different.

1- How do you decide when to get out at the beginning?

2- Do you stay in if shivering?

3- Over time, does the shivering go away?

### drezy — 2018-11-15T16:26:06+0200

>
>
> 1- How do you decide when to get out at the beginning?

My family's N=3

10-15 minute timer. Now a 20-30 minute timer. I sometimes get a little burp near 20 minutes in that I assume is a signal of some sorts like my gut vitamin d receptor calling up from the engine room.

> 2- Do you stay in if shivering?

None of us remember shivering much.

> 3- Over time, does the shivering go away?

For all in my family we do not shiver anymore. I did 2 x 5min dunks at 42ºF yesterday and layed on a lounge chair afterwards. No shivers.

Answers above from my wife and son.

### Butters — 2018-11-22T13:17:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-simple-questions.22510/post-256270>

Other question:

Jack wrote in the CT protocol, that adapting to the cold would take shorter time with eating lot of carbs and vice versa longer time with eating ketogenic?
Is that a mistake, because he is advocating a ketogenic diet and in the autumn and winter there are less or no carbs?

### Sean Waters — 2019-06-11T15:34:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-simple-questions.22510/post-272446>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=255684)
>
> I’ve been doing longer (50 min) but less frequent sessions because I (mistakenly?) thought longer was better. Plus getting the tub filled enough to cover mid-torso uses so much water it feels wasteful to not stay in as long as possible. Plus damn the warmup is difficult now that the sun can’t joyfully help it along...
>
> I don’t shiver much beyond the initial shock (~50F well water) till about half an hour in, then I slog it out the last 15-20 minutes because I feel I should be pushing myself. Maybe I need to just get out before it becomes torturous.
>
> Still hate cold showers (and so does my tangly hair). Currently start with warm to tepid, then end with a reluctant minute or so of cold on head and backside.

I get the same experience.... initial shiver, 20-30 mins I'm chilling (relaxed), and then from there I shiver progressively more violently until about 50-60 mins when I get out.

I wonder if we have similar Haplotypes and what our SNPs are?

I have a huge amount of 03 daily. I tested 1:1 in blood serum 06:03.

But, if that last comment is right - then more 06 and carbs = better adaption? I read that O3 is needed in Cold, but maybe I'm wrong.

### Phosphene — 2019-06-14T19:28:08+0300

### Saichi — 2019-06-14T22:47:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-simple-questions.22510/post-272666>

It's not the noise, it's the seawater in ears I dislike. Surfer friend of mine lost good amount of hearing in both ears after surfing for decades without a head cover. I'd much enjoy swimming out past the waves but then a great white might find me a good meal considering how few seals are left in their breeding waters.

### Phosphene — 2019-06-19T23:21:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-simple-questions.22510/post-272975>

Interesting. Did he get lots of ear infections? I wonder how many decibels crashing waves produce? My father is almost deaf from decades of lawn, farm, and other loud equipment use without hearing protection. Never thought about the possible effect of constant heavy sea waves (not one of his problems, unfortunately). I love diving under them and body surfing.

Something like this plus silicone earplugs might help? Blocks a bit of sun but not as much as a cap. If you’d be caught dead in it...might offend the sharks and keep them away.

# Thread: Need some advice on CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-advice-on-ct.22518/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-17T17:18:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-advice-on-ct.22518/post-255834>

I've been starting CT slowly over the last three weeks with 10 days of face dunks, then I tried to jump into a cold shower but my mid back started to spasm too much so I stopped that. I think weak adrenals are an issue. So added 10 days of dunking just up to the knees in 45 deg water with the rest of the torso and head kept warm.

Yesterday, I was able to do CT up to my navel in 45 deg water for 15 min. It was very doable while I was doing it so next time I will go up to my upper chest I think. My dilemma is that I feel fine after for about an hour but then beyond that it takes hours to fully warm up. I am also getting a bit of mid back pain again as I am guessing is CT overstimulating my adrenals(which alt therapist say are a bit weak). Its possible my liver is weak and not producing enough colesterol(I've been eating plenty of colesterol). The problem is I remain cold for about 3 hours and for a during which time the cold makes me slightly incontinent. It seems like my body has not enough energy to warm myself back up for a long time. I am wondering about what is the protocol to rewarm the body for skinny people like me with prolly only 5% fat and being underwieght (5' 7",128lbs sadly I have lost 6 pounds since doing Jack's diet and CT, but hoping it is temporary direction). I decided to go for a brisk bike ride after about an hour which helped generate some internal heat.

From my understanding the effects of CT will only last while the surface of the skin stays under 60 deg. Should I just monitor the skin, and as soon as the skin temp rises above 60 I should just jump into a hot shower and get the body temp back up? Ideally seeking advice from a lizardtype instead of beartype on this issue LOL.

Any ideas anyone.? Thanxs for looking.

Listen to your body, i think it is telling you to slow the #\*@! down! How is your energy level when you're done?
i'm not an expert on CT, but as someone who also has issues w/ chronic fatigue, i can tell you from personal experience that you can really screw yourself up if you aren't careful. Maybe you could increase the water temperature a bit-i started out at 65 degrees with the water just past my knees after doing the face dunks.
the effects of CT last well beyond the point of your skin returning to normal temperature.

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-18T05:02:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-advice-on-ct.22518/post-255874>

Maybe you could increase the water temperature a bit-i started out at 65 degrees with the water just past my knees after doing the face dunks.
> the effects of CT last well beyond the point of your skin returning to normal temperature.

Yes could be. I had CFS years ago. Mine was primarily due to weak liver chi.

I usually feel pretty good afterwards but after about an hour I start to feel cold to the core and I think it takes too many resources out of me. I think you are right in me trying too much at once between the change in the lights, diet and CT. I was just so impressed that the first day I did it it hurt like hell in 55 deg water and then suddenly it didn't hurt at all in 40 deg. I am ditching the strict diet part for now and eating when I am hungry and whatever my body craves. It seems like a lot of the focus it is for fat people and I am the opposite. I will prolly try and stick a few pounds back on before resuming CT, but at least once more I was thinking I will try it once more and just do a hot shower after thirty min. If that don't work out well then I will not do much more CT till the wieght come back a bit.

### Inna — 2018-11-18T15:16:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-advice-on-ct.22518/post-255890>

I ct in the ocean t low 40s F, or 3-7 C,
I do well in the water and even enjoy the feeling, I freeze during 5 min walk back to my condo (weaning my towel, mittens and boots), I leave the door open, my hands are so frozen, it is hard to turn the key, then I ride my stationary bike to warm up, I have 2 reptilian glo lamps full spectrum (the larger lamp) and UV spectrum (smaller), I ride 25 min (5 miles) on the bike with the lams in wearing my kiniki swimming suit which usually dries up on me, and I feel great afterwards. I usually ct in the morning at sunrise
