# Thread: CT: The Next Level
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-the-next-level.23102/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2019-02-10T07:54:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-the-next-level.23102/post-262759>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
34/M/Ancestry From Cold-Adapted (Poland/Russia/Scotland)

Current CT Level -
Full Body (Up to chest) @ 38 deg.F - 44 deg. F outside with ambient temps in the mid 30's for up to 20 minutes
Cold Shower @ 45 deg.
Ambient Air CT down to 20 deg. F (outside in underwear and shoes/socks) for up to 15 minutes

Goal: Full body immerson in 32 deg. F water for 60 minutes.

Any thoughts on my next productive step to ramp things up to Jedi status?

Biggest weak point right now is my f\*ckng feet get cold on the ground when barefoot in ~35 deg. weather and below.

Am looking at doing regimented extremity water immersion training indoors until I'm hitting 32 deg. F w/ regularity for at least 15 minutes in both hands and feet.

### Bob Stirling — 2019-02-10T20:53:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-the-next-level.23102/post-262797>

Suggestions for cold feet. Be sheltered from the wind, wrap a sheepskin around them, movement, wear wool socks. Stand on a block of Styrofoam SM insulation. The SM gives an R10 for every 2" of thickness.

If you have in-floor radiant or hot water heat. Add a circuit for an outdoor concrete slab and maintain the slab at a comfortable temperature.

### Sun Disciple — 2019-02-10T23:06:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-the-next-level.23102/post-262803>

> [AdamF said:](/goto/post?id=262759)
>
> [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
> 34/M/Ancestry From Cold-Adapted (Poland/Russia/Scotland)
>
> Current CT Level -
> Full Body (Up to chest) @ 38 deg.F - 44 deg. F outside with ambient temps in the mid 30's for up to 20 minutes
> Cold Shower @ 45 deg.
> Ambient Air CT down to 20 deg. F (outside in underwear and shoes/socks) for up to 15 minutes
>
> Goal: Full body immerson in 32 deg. F water for 60 minutes.
>
> Any thoughts on my next productive step to ramp things up to Jedi status?
>
> Biggest weak point right now is my f\*ckng feet get cold on the ground when barefoot in ~35 deg. weather and below.
>
> Am looking at doing regimented extremity water immersion training indoors until I'm hitting 32 deg. F w/ regularity for at least 15 minutes in both hands and feet.

I can do 25 mins at 32F in the frozen lake but I dont find any benefit to doing more than 15 mins at a time. Get some neoprene boots and gloves the fingers and toes are what is going to stop you from staying in for super long.

### AdamF — 2019-02-11T00:05:48+0200

# Thread: Here's A Rather Controversial Opinion

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1

### AdamF — 2019-02-11T23:13:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heres-a-rather-controversial-opinion.23113/post-262923>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

In light of your most recent Podcast:

The smartest Mitochondriacs are not the ones who are hanging their hat on the Inverse Square Law and expecting that to save them.

The Smartest Mitochondriacs are the ones who understand the inverse square law will fail & are already working on the remedies for when this happens.

The real Mitochondriac Arms Race is not the race to prevent disease, it is the race to use the Inverse Square Law & Mitochondriac protocols for buying time to develop solutions to fit in a world where cancer will be as common as the Flu --

We **don't** need to be faster than the _start_ of a disease -- we need to be faster than the point at which the disease crosses the Rubicon.

Rapid and continuous rejuvenation techniques (currently not available) will end up overtaking Prevention-based strategies, for many.

The high-level trauma, acute incidents (i.e. electrocution through jump conduction) are the biggest priorities to protect against , and that's the real value of the Inverse Square Law.

# Thread: Coupled or uncoupled? Haplotype

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 22

### Stephen W — 2019-02-11T23:44:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coupled-or-uncoupled-haplotype.23114/post-262926>

My maternal haplotype is H1b.

How can I find out if I’m an uncoupler or am coupled?
Thanks

### Michalis — 2019-02-12T14:38:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coupled-or-uncoupled-haplotype.23114/post-262979>

I have exactly the same question. I don't think you will be solely a coupler on uncoupler, probably something between the spectrum.

I think we need to check the haplogroup distribution around the globe during the time the group forked out from the parental group ? I think you have to take into account the climate conditions at the time that the haplogroup appeared so you get an indication.

I think you also have to take into account your DNA SNPs as well.. This is the hard part in my opinion and it is different for everyone as it is where the rubber meets the road. It is your context and your DNA . I am afraid i haven't got any rule of thumb as no rule of thumbs exist for that. (I guess only the 3 legged stool applies everywhere)

Also be practical , how do you feel with cold? Have you tried CT?

Check this thread too : [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/is-ct-beneficial-to-asian-and-african.21836/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/is-ct-beneficial-to-asian-and-african.21836/)

### Sergio Valadez — 2019-02-12T22:04:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coupled-or-uncoupled-haplotype.23114/post-263009>

Nice, your 23andme results are in.  I second what Michalis said about this stuff being complicated and there’s still a lot more that I need to learn. Your specific case (haplotype), however, does seem less complicated than other cases (and I am open to being corrected if I am mistaken). My original suspicion when you said your 23andme results hadn’t arrived was that your maternal haplogroup (and your ancestry in general) would be Northern European. As far as I can tell, you appear to be a standard white Australian who can trace your ancestors to within Australia before WWII. Before WWII, the vast majority of immigration to Australia came from British Isles and to a lesser extent from continental—mostly Northern— Europe as well. Thus, if your maternal grandmother was from Australia, then your haplogroup is likely Northern European. Your 23andme results only confirm my original suspicion because your haplogroup seems to mostly be found in Northern Europeans and Northwest Siberians. While it’s not super common in the British Isles, it does exist there (more common in Eastern Europe and Northwest Siberia). I found conflicting reports as to where H1b originated (one said Iberia or Southern France and another said Central Asia or Anatolia). it is believed to have originated around the end of the last ice age. While those may seem like southern locations (relative to Northern Europe/British Isles), due to the Ice Age, Northern Europe was frozen/uninhabitable and those southern locations were much colder than they are today. Anyway, Dr. Kruse has repeatedly said that Northern European haplotypes are loosely coupled and do better with CT. As far as I can tell, you appear to have a Northern (European) haplotype. If CT isn’t working for you (as you’ve expressed in another post), my guess is that your haplogrounp is _not_ the problem but probably one or more of the other factors Dr. Kruse mentioned. Hope this helps and if anyone notices anything wrong with my logic please correct me.

### Stephen W — 2019-02-12T22:26:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coupled-or-uncoupled-haplotype.23114/post-263019>

Thanks so much guys for the reply.
[@Michalis](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18092/) yes I’ve been struggling with CT for about 6 months.
[@Sergio Valadez](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/13827/) cheers for the detailed reply too. I think you are most probably right. Here is the exact snippet from my 23&me...

**H1b**
6,000
Years Ago
**Origin and Migrations of Haplogroup H1b**
Your maternal line stems from a branch of H1 called H1b. The common ancestor of haplogroup H1b lived approximately 5,000 to 7,000 years ago in southwestern Europe. Her maternal-line ancestors had been among the former inhabitants of continental Europe, but massive glacial ice sheets during the last great peak of the cold at the end of the Ice Age pushed them out of the continental interior. They sheltered for thousands of years in warmer refuges along the Mediterranean. Then, as the Ice Age faded away, they re-emerged and migrated north.

While the woman who gave rise to H1b probably lived in southern France or the Iberian Peninsula, her descendants are most frequently found among eastern Europeans. Women carrying H1b journeyed eastward from southwestern Europe, passed north of the Italian Alps and entered present-day Slovakia. From there, H1b spread north throughout the region surrounding the Baltic Sea and the Volga-Ural area of Russia. Members of H1b also moved into southeastern Europe to Ukraine, the Balkans, and the Caucasus Mountains.

### Sergio Valadez — 2019-02-14T22:45:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coupled-or-uncoupled-haplotype.23114/post-263198>

> [Stephen W said:](/goto/post?id=263021)
>
> And it’s showing
> **58.5% British & Irish**
>
> **18.3% French & German**
>
> **6.4% Scandinavian**

Yup, all Northern European and mostly British Isles. Exactly what one would expect from a non-recent immigrant white Australian. Like Dr. Kruse said over in your other post: there are a number of different things that could be the problem. I suspect it’s safe to rule out your mitochondrial haplogroup. Also, you asked the following question on another post of yours:
“So, can certain haplotypes run lower body temps? Or is that a different issue all together? Cheers”
I’ve heard/read Dr. Kruse answer that question. If by lower body temps you meant _core_ body temps then the answer would be no. CT is not supposed to cool down your _core_ body temperature but only your _surface_ temperature. This makes your surface (incl. skin) more receptive to and more effective at absorbing light (one of the main benefits of CT). Dr. Doug Wallace (mitochondrial expert) explains that Europeans, as opposed to equatorial Africans for example, have accumulated mutations in their mitochondrial DNA that have helped them adapt to colder climes and to being exposed to cold on the surface of their bodies.

So sorry to hear about your dog. I know from personal experience that it can be really rough. I’m pretty sure I remember hearing Wim Hof say that people from colder climates have an easier time adapting to the cold which makes sense intuitively so maybe that’s playing a role too. Or, it could also be some of the factors Dr. Kruse said on your other post: “SNP profile. Could be a low redox.” Like Dr. Kruse said: “Many paths to Rome.”

# Thread: Homeopathic Progesterone & Testosterone?

### Nardia — 2019-02-12T09:34:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homeopathic-progesterone-testosterone.23120/post-262973>

Is homeopathy an option to correct these hormone deficiencies? Does anyone have experience/success with using them?
I have read that homeopathy stimulates the body to produce the hormones rather than replace them - is this true?
Thanks

### Katie Durham — 2019-02-12T15:16:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homeopathic-progesterone-testosterone.23120/post-262982>

Potentially yes, but the questions would be which remedies and at what potency and dosing. It is very difficult to effectively use homeopathy for chronic conditions. You would need a very skilled practitioner. Remedies are chosen based on the symptoms and modalities manifested by the individual at that time, not the name of the condition. Hundreds to choose among. Homeopathy isn't benign, you can mess up your energy using it wrong. A remedy is homeopathic depending strictly on how it was prepared (serial dilutions energized at each step). Because of our western medicine mindset, the inclination is invariably to use the remedies according to allopathic principles, usually with very poor results. If you want to explore this, find a good classical practitioner.

### Nardia — 2019-02-12T23:09:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homeopathic-progesterone-testosterone.23120/post-263031>

Thanks so much, that makes sense. I have used homeopathy years ago when I was very ill. I recall the practitioner asking many questions and it took nearly two hours of getting details before he made the remedy. That would have been 28 years ago! It sure worked when nothing else could at the time.

YES! I have had great success in my twenty year practice utilizing the homeopathic hormone stimulating remedies as well as a protocol monthly for remedies like estrogen and testosterone ( case taken first to prescribe based on individualized symptoms/diagnosis/basis)...using daily potencies of remedies that are inner changeable and as needed. Compliments the Black Swan Mitochondriac very WELL in optimal level.

I live outside Raleigh, NC and The Triangle is a 5G hub so that sucks for me. My husband works near the airport so sucks for him big time. I stay at home with kiddos so I have the freedom to be outside a lot and set my schedule. We turn off internet at night and sleep in the dark. We live in a less populated area with some acreage around. I will say that beginning in September my consistency with most things wasn't as great as it had been (1st miscarriage was beginning of Sept) and I'm reevaluating and buckling down. I was doing CT daily and then really didn't do any in Dec/Jan. I drank Aquafina Jan-June then our well water until Oct when I got Evian and Aquafina. Now on Aquafina only. I eat fish/seafood daily- 1-3x/day depending. Been eating lots of dried seaweed. I started lifting heavy weights in October- it was in morning and that DID NOT agree with my body. Switched to afternoons and only 1x/week instead of 2. I feel like I got ahead of myself in the summer because I was doing SO great and so I let some things in because I assumed my redox was high enough to handle it.

# Thread: Shopping malls ......and me
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shopping-malls-and-me.23139/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2019-02-15T08:23:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shopping-malls-and-me.23139/post-263252>

I had a revelation this morning of sorts......

We now live in a relatively small village so when we need to get the truck serviced or a major shop we need to go to a nearby city - about 60K away. This is not a huge city by any stretch but it has a shopping mall.

I have never, ever noticed before what I noticed 3 weeks ago and then again this morning.

First time ......Upon entering this mall at 8:00am I became ravenously hungry - like out of control hunger and I NEEDED to eat NOW. I became hyper focused on getting something to eat.

I hadn't eaten very much for a number of days prior because we had been at the ocean so much and I simple wasn't very hungry ......I just thought ...okay - my body needs food.. Most things in the mall weren't open yet so I finally settled on some roast beef from the grocery store. I just devoured about a half a pound of meat but I definitely didn't feel satisfied.

Fast forward to this morning .......We enter the same mall at approx the same time ...8:00am ....and the exact same thing happened. I was out of control hungry. My appetite has been very stable lately. I am not all that hungry mostly...when I am - I eat until full. I only have 2 meals a day ....1 larger one and one quite small one. Sometimes I only eat once a day.

I understand all the nnEMF and stuff affecting us in malls. I usually feel the buzz and how over the top everything is and I gotta get out ......but I have never noticed this uncontrollable hunger before.

This is something totally different I feel .......Are we being targeted to eat all this junk?

I know we are being targeted in a million different ways to spend money ......but to eat?????

Or ....Am I just losing it?????

### Bob Stirling — 2019-02-16T01:18:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shopping-malls-and-me.23139/post-263295>

I would say it has been subtle programming over the years.

### caroline — 2019-02-16T09:05:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shopping-malls-and-me.23139/post-263309>

I do understand the subtle programming - I know they have been doing that for years in movie theatres.

But the extreme hunger is really off the charts - or is this how nnEMF is affecting me now????

synchronicity of my triggers? so not everyone would be affected the same way I guess? I have noticed that my partner acts quite aggressive in a Grocery store \[Aldi} that we go to for a big shop. This is in the same town. I have asked him to now let me do the shopping alone in this store ....he has to wait in the car! His behaviour in this store is very untypical for him. He doesn't act like that anywhere else. It is as if he has ADD all of a sudden.

In the mall that I have the hunger issues - he doesn't seem to be affected at all....

I feel like we are sitting ducks .......targeted by who ever wishes to do so. I am definitely opting out of FB. I will miss all of Jack's nuggets but I hate being a target.

### caroline — 2019-02-16T09:10:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shopping-malls-and-me.23139/post-263310>

I was just re reading Jack's thread about Dry eyes .......definitely a correlation. Next time we have to go to this mall I will wear my blue blockers and see what happens. I will also ask Tony to wear his in Aldi.

sounds a great idea and bring ur own food with you if it happens again shall i send you a cow or 2 ?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shopping-malls-and-me.23139/post-263349>

if i go to a supermarket , i crave bread!!!
i never usually want bread and i am hungry even if not b4 getting in

# Thread: Flickr and Blue Light?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flickr-and-blue-light.23140/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shiran — 2019-02-15T09:53:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flickr-and-blue-light.23140/post-263257>

Is a LCD screen less bad than LED in connection with Flickr and Blue Light?

### Sajid Mahmood — 2019-02-15T19:43:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flickr-and-blue-light.23140/post-263279>

IGot a few other questions that are related to this.

Does blocking blue light reduce flicker?

Does blue light have more flicker than any other wavelength?

### John Saf — 2019-02-15T20:40:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flickr-and-blue-light.23140/post-263282>

LED screens use LED backlights, LCD uses fluorescent-type backlights.
"The most common cause of **flicker** is fluctuation in the power source of a light. In incandescent lamps, this was the natural fluctuations of the AC grid that was “filtered” only by the heat storage of a filament, resulting in 6-11% **flicker**. Early magnetic ballasted **fluorescent** lights had extremely high **flicker**, 80-100%."
So you would have to test your screen, there is no "one size fits all" answer.
This is the app i use to test flicker.
[http://www.oulight.com/](http://www.oulight.com/)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 4

### Rob Alexander — 2019-02-17T18:06:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-any-members-in-playa-del-carman.23147/post-263390>

Hello
All in member with shit mitochondrial function- story on my optimal journal
I have nothing at all holding me back but low funds so working on sorting that out.
Looking for help from anyone that has already made it out to assist with suggestions on the move, required amount of funds to make the trip. Jobs there ect, tips any information at all really. I have never been.

Working on flying in to scope it out and try to make some things happen asap
would be great to connect with anyone that is there or if have a floor to sleep on that would make things far easier for me to make trip happen

N=1

Thanks
Rob Alexander

### Dave Key — 2019-04-07T19:29:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-any-members-in-playa-del-carman.23147/post-267554>

"Moving to the Yucatan" would be a good sub forum.
I'm in the same boat about to go down and figure out how to start over.

### Novah — 2019-04-08T00:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-any-members-in-playa-del-carman.23147/post-267581>

> [Rob Alexander said:](/goto/post?id=263390)
>
> Hello
> All in member with shit mitochondrial function- story on my optimal journal
> I have nothing at all holding me back but low funds so working on sorting that out.
> Looking for help from anyone that has already made it out to assist with suggestions on the move, required amount of funds to make the trip. Jobs there ect, tips any information at all really. I have never been.
>
> Working on flying in to scope it out and try to make some things happen asap
> would be great to connect with anyone that is there or if have a floor to sleep on that would make things far easier for me to make trip happen
>
> N=1
>
> Thanks
> Rob Alexander

Hi Rob,

Take a (preferably extended) visit first - you may love it or hate it there and you won't know till you go. I wouldn't make any plans for moving there until you've done a long visit first and see how you feel. Interjet has cheap flights.

If you're looking for affordable accom, check out the Selina line of hostels - I didn't stay there, but I know people that have and liked them. Apparently the one in Cancun is quite nice - you can check out their reviews online.
[https://www.selina.com/mexico/cancun-downtown/](https://www.selina.com/mexico/cancun-downtown/)

They also have a location in PDC:
[https://www.selina.com/mexico/playa-del-carmen/](https://www.selina.com/mexico/playa-del-carmen/)

There are a bunch of facebook groups for expats in Playa Del Carmen as well as housing for rent - you can join and ask any questions you have.

You can move around cheaply via collectivos and the ADO bus.

### Dave Key — 2019-04-08T04:58:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-any-members-in-playa-del-carman.23147/post-267600>

Thanks a lot Novah! Thats just the info I needed to hear. I will definitely check it out.

I havent had Ross River but have had a couple of other mosquito borne viruses.

i have had chikungunya...which is similar apparently...with regards to the initial fever i bought it on. I didnt suppress like alot of others do with aspirin and alike. To do this i drank 500ml of strong ginger and garlic tea and put on a hoodie and trackpants and went to bed. Sweated lik a maniac but it broke the fever.

I then did ozone therapy (EBOT) once and drank papaya juice and cooloidal silver every alternate hour for 2 weeks.

For the arthritic pains (which were intense and lasted a few months) I went heavy on the tumeric, garlic and ginger teas for 3 months. Also got alot of sun.

I couldnt touch a drop of alchohol within this time and for about 6 months after.

I also did rhe same with Dengue and recovered fully as well (Dengue doesnt have the bone aching pain as much as chikungunya does) .Both are hard on your liver so be aware u dont want to stress that much in anyway.

rgds

# Thread: A New Form of Neural Communication or Quantum Tunneling
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-form-of-neural-communication-or-quantum-tunneling.23160/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DrEttinger — 2019-02-19T16:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-form-of-neural-communication-or-quantum-tunneling.23160/post-263548>

A New Form of Neural Communication or an example of Quantum Tunneling? We are more than just cells. We are quantized beings. Standard biology or chemistry can't explain the totality of who we are, what we are and how we operate. We possess electromagnetic fields, morphic fields, and quantum fields. Anything and everything, from within and from the outside could potentially affect us, or maybe not. I believe that our thoughts, emotions, and perceptions have the biggest influence on our level of resiliency - positively as well as negatively. This is followed but light, then water.

"One of the most beautiful concepts in quantum biology, quantum tunneling (QT), states that if the ratio of transmission coefficient (T) is greater than the ratio of reflection coefficient (R), then a specified mass with defined kinetic energy can translocate through an impregnatable barrier."

**(2-18-19) Neuroscientists Say They've Found an Entirely New Form of Neural Communication:** Scientists think they've identified a previously unknown form of neural communication that self-propagates across brain tissue, and can leap wirelessly from neurons in one section of brain tissue to another – even if they've been surgically severed. The discovery offers some radical new insights about the way neurons might be talking to one another, via a mysterious process unrelated to conventionally understood mechanisms...

[https://www.sciencealert.com/neuros...-an-entirely-new-form-of-neural-communication](https://www.sciencealert.com/neuroscientists-say-they-ve-found-an-entirely-new-form-of-neural-communication)

# Thread: Improved redox = better performance in an elite athlete
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-redox-better-performance-in-an-elite-athlete.23161/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Corey Nelson — 2019-02-19T18:00:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-redox-better-performance-in-an-elite-athlete.23161/post-263554>

The athlete in question went on to win the European championship in rowing. The study authors concluded that phytonutrient intake could help athletes with low redox or disrupted redox homeostasis, but I think their other conclusions are relevant to the quantum athlete or quantum health practitioner.

[Alterations in Redox Homeostasis During Recovery From Unexplained Underperformance Syndrome in an Elite International Rower.](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28422588)

"Alterations in redox homeostasis should be considered as part of the medical management in UUPS \[unexplained underpormance syndrome\]. This is the first reported case study of an elite athlete with alterations in redox homeostasis in conjunction with a diagnosis of UUPS."

# Thread: Nov 2017 Proton recycling

**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1

### l.i.am — 2019-02-19T23:59:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nov-2017-proton-recycling.23166/post-263579>

I am listening / reading the the webinar and blog post and want to clarify a couple of things. In the primitive oceans the deuterium levels were expected to be higher, meaning in the higher PPM levels (200 - 500 PPM) correct? I ask because another sentence reads " has reversed where deuterium is no longer the dominant isotope". This implied to me that if it was the dominant isotope we would be talking straight total percentage of hydrogen to deuterium. So are we talking higher PPM as I am supposing or straight %?

Another idea to clarify in my head is the way mitochondria deplete deuterium. Do the dehydrogenase enzymes selectively react only with protium and not deuterium isotopes to separate the proton and not a proton/ neutron nucleus? But if that were the case then the matrix wouldn't have deuterium nuclei to slow down or inhibit the ATPase to begin with. What is so far not clear to me is the mechanisms used by the body to deplete the deuterium level. I would say mitophagy and exosome excretion to the blood stream followed by renal excretion and / or sweat would be the answer but I want to solidify the reasoning. Also portal blood flow and excretion through the bile. Am I getting it thoroughly correct or is there still some processes to clarify?

Thanks

# Thread: Non-UV Blocking Sunglasses (occasionally)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-uv-blocking-sunglasses-occasionally.23180/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Balfi — 2019-02-22T00:30:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-uv-blocking-sunglasses-occasionally.23180/post-263752>

Hi all!

Sometimes I like to read a book outside in the sun - don't we all? It can be a little technically difficult in terms of lighting. The direct sunlight on the page itself is blindingly bright and painful to read. On the other hand, facing the sun can be too dark on the page or too bright in the eyes.

I was thinking it might not be so bad if such a thing existed of non uv-blocking sunglasses, especially if I was just using them occasionally reading a book and not facing the sunlight itself.

However, I don't know if this is really technically possible.

Thoughts?

JB
_In Quantum Mechanics We Trust_

### Michalis — 2019-02-23T22:02:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-uv-blocking-sunglasses-occasionally.23180/post-263873>

I think when your solar callus is properly built there won't be any issue

### Lahelada — 2019-02-24T01:08:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-uv-blocking-sunglasses-occasionally.23180/post-263882>

I think it would be better to change habits. You are creating a time mismatch between skin and eye. I use books as a reflector to get more sun into the eye as I am myopic. Try without reading and see what happens..A hat that shades your eyes may help. there is threads about photosensitivity.

### Bob Stirling — 2019-02-24T23:21:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-uv-blocking-sunglasses-occasionally.23180/post-263905>

In regards to solar callus and the time mismatch. What about driving after dark? My eyes are sensitive to the headlights of approaching vehicles, especially ones with LED lights. At times it can be unsafe to drive. I was thinking about wearing a blue blocker with a lower tint. The sensitivity has been improving. Any thoughts on this? Thanks.

### Lahelada — 2019-02-25T00:56:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-uv-blocking-sunglasses-occasionally.23180/post-263909>

At night have your skin cvered and wear blue light blockers when exposed to artificial light. Driving is difficut as you need to anticiate that you will drive from dark to light and vice versa and orange tints take away from brightness.I do not know what tint would be appropriate for driving to meet the two criteria of still being able to discern detail and avoiding glare.

### Christine_L — 2019-02-25T16:31:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-uv-blocking-sunglasses-occasionally.23180/post-263946>

> [Bob Stirling said:](/goto/post?id=263905)
>
> In regards to solar callus and the time mismatch. What about driving after dark? My eyes are sensitive to the headlights of approaching vehicles, especially ones with LED lights. At times it can be unsafe to drive. I was thinking about wearing a blue blocker with a lower tint. The sensitivity has been improving. Any thoughts on this? Thanks.

I wear Uvex to drive

# Thread: Oil and protein mixture recipe - An experiment
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oil-and-protein-mixture-recipe-an-experiment.23182/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Frankie78 — 2019-02-22T01:48:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oil-and-protein-mixture-recipe-an-experiment.23182/post-263756>

I’ve been experimenting with how to get more Omega 3 into my diet at a lower cost point. I guess if you’ve got access to high quality, low cost seafood all year round this won’t concern you.

So I think some people may have heard of Johanna Budwig and the so called Budwig diet. In this topic I am only focusing in on the oil and protein mixture.

I think the below section is worth a quick read about Johanna and the diet if you’re interested to know more, if not just skip to my next post.

**From the wonderful world of the internet:**

‘**Johanna Budwig** (30 September 1908 – 19 May 2003) was a German biochemist and author. Budwig was a pharmacist and held doctorate degrees in physics and chemistry.’

‘Based on her research on fatty acids she developed a diet that she believed was useful in the treatment of cancer. She was the first person to be able to separate good fats from fats in general in the blood cells under the microscope. Once she was able to do this she discovered that most critically ill patients had little or no protective lining around the cells in the blood which is made up she discovered of 60% Omega 3. Healthy cells already had this layer of insulation around them.

She then worked on treating these damaged cells with as many types of Omega 3 oils as she could find, to try to replenish their structure to that of healthy cells. She describes her findings in her many books and found that the most successful source of healing the cells was a mixture of a good quality organic flax oil and cottage cheese or quark mixed with freshly ground flax seed. This mixture she found taken over time restored the damaged cells structure and put the protective layer of insulation around the cell to protect the nucleas of the cell from future damage.’

**From: Puna Wai Ora Mind-Body Cancer Clinic** [http://www.alternative-cancer-care.com/johanna-budwig-cancer-diet.html](http://www.alternative-cancer-care.com/johanna-budwig-cancer-diet.html)

“Dr. Budwig discovered the blood of those with cancer always had gross deficiencies of phosphatides and lipoproteins, while healthy persons did not have this deficiency. This deficiency resulted in reduced oxygen levels in the cells. Dr. Johanna Budwig then developed a simple diet to re-oxygenate the body’s cells. She discovered the saturated fats we were using in our Western diets did not contain pi-electrons. Pi-electrons are like spark-plugs for our cells to help them to “breathe” properly. Pi-electrons allow the body’s cells to assimilate and transport oxygen, and to detoxify cellular waste. Dr. Johanna Budwig discovered the best source of Pi-electrons was found in two unsaturated fatty acids of flaxseed oil. These unsaturated fatty acids (linolenic and linoleic), not only re-oxygenate the body’s cells, they also repair and restore the lipid membranes of all cells in the body, and they do this by replenishing the loss of critically needed phosphatides and lipoproteins that are always as stated earlier, grossly deficient in cancer patients.

But there was one problem. How could Dr. Johanna Budwig get the unsaturated fatty acids of the flaxseed oil into the cells of cancer patients? Only water-soluble solutions can enter cells, and an oil, however fantastic it is, is not water-soluble and therefore cannot enter our cells. After many years of clinical research, Dr. Budwig found that by combining flaxseed oil with the sulphurated amino acids found in cottage cheese – the flaxseed oil would become water-soluble, and immediately available for use by the body’s cells. What has also recently become apparent is just how important the cottage cheese is in the Johanna Budwig Diet. Not only does the cottage cheese make the flaxseed oil water soluble to re-oxygenate the body’s cells to prevent cancer mutations, it contains two ingredients that fight cancer in the best possible way.

The first ingredient is sulphur. Sulphur helps to detoxify the body and prevent the growth of cancer cells. According to the National Cancer Institute sulphur compounds make cells vulnerable to the stress created by products of cell division. Because cancer cells divide very quickly, they generate more stressors than most normal cells. Thus, cancer cells are damaged by the presence of sulphur compounds to a much greater extent than normal cells. The other important ingredient in the whey (the liquid part) of cottage cheese, is dextrorotatory lactic acid. This positive form of lactic acid – when given in high doses - normalizes the cell’s pH to healthy levels of around 7.4pH, which is critical to cancer recovery. Secondly, normalising the pH helps to stimulate adrenaline production, preventing a build up of glucose in cells. The prevention of glucose build-up in cells is critical in cancer recovery, as viral-bacterial-yeast-like fungus feed on and ferment glucose, breaking the cell’s Krebs (oxygen) cycle, causing cells to mutate into cancer cells. Thirdly, dextrorotatory lactic acid of the whey in cottage cheese neutralizes the toxic, levorotatory lactic acid of the tumour into a non-toxic, racemic form. This is of utmost importance, as it removes the stimulus for an increase in the cell division rate. Fourthly, the whey of cottage cheese has powerful anti-microbial and anti-viral properties. Lactoferrin and lactoferricin in whey inhibits bacteria, yeast, fungi, and parasitic protozoa. As normal cells lose their ability to exchange oxygen due to the presence of viral-bacterial-yeast-like fungus - causing them to mutate into cancer cells - killing these microbes is also paramount in cancer recovery. Finally, the whey of cottage cheese stimulates the growth of various cells of the immune defense system including lymphocytes, macrophages / monocytes, humoral immune response, and antibody response.”

### Frankie78 — 2019-02-22T01:49:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oil-and-protein-mixture-recipe-an-experiment.23182/post-263757>

**Ingredients and experimenting:**

She created her diet way before the crazy food guru’s were on the scene, so I thought it was worth experimenting with the oil protein mixture part. Plus how many food gurus do you know that have doctorate degrees in physics and chemistry and are actively researched their ideas/work and new the sunlight connection to electrons/food. So I think it’s worth checking this out even though I have no scientific background to verify all the above is true.

So for the last few years, I have been using the best quality flaxseed oil I can afford, along with good quality natural Greek Yoghurt, 100% natural whey powder and slight dab of honey. I have been essentially been mixing my own rich mixture of electrons, sulphur and proteins.

This mixture I have found when supplemented with Omega 3 from seafood provides fantastic results. With the seafood you also get your Magnesium and the Zinc that also provides benefits to the oil protein mixture. I’ve also eaten some dark green leafy vegetables like Kale grown from my garden and that’s taken it to another level all together with the bottled water from overseas (yes I am living in the wonderful land down under).

But let’s break down what’s in this oil protein mixture first. By the way if you get a multi meter and test this mixture, you will see how electron rich this protein oil mixture is.

### Frankie78 — 2019-02-22T01:50:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oil-and-protein-mixture-recipe-an-experiment.23182/post-263758>

**Flaxseed Oil**

There’s probably lots to like and not like about Flaxseed Oil. Hey it can be really toxic if not handled correctly, it goes rancid very quickly and it’s grown from a plant that only wants full sun and light. The risks involved in using this are high. So you’ve got to be on the edge to try this. I recommend looking up everything you can about Flaxseed Oil before you use it.

According to Budwig the most optimal fats are the most oxygen active. Plus it’s got all these other Omega 6s which hey on the face it, you probably most people probably don’t need more of. And of course you can get better complete Omega 3 acids from seafood. But for all those very reasons, it got me very interested.

Let’s look at this break down from the side of the black bottle which is kept refrigerated at all times. I choose an organic version and when it comes to purchasing oil, I choose the best that you can buy. I get small bottles and use it very quickly.

**Average Quantity PER 100ml**

Energy 3430kJ

Protein 0g

\-Gluten NIL

Fat, Total 92.6g

-   Saturated 8.4g

-   Trans <0.1g

-   Polyunsaturated 82g

    Omega-3 (alpha-linolenic acid) 50.5g

    Omega-6 (linoleic acid) 14g

-   Monounsaturated 17g

    Omega-9 (oleic acid) 16.8g

Carbohydrates, Total 0g

-   Sugars 0g

Sodium 0.2mg

So we’ve got this massive amount of ALA Omega 3. And you know that ALA is precursor to EPA and DHA but you’ve heard the conversion rate in our bodies is low. Some say less than one per cent, some say up to 10 per cent depending on various factors. But let’s assume it’s one per cent. That’s good enough for me.

But you can get EPA and DHA directly from those fatty fish we all know. So why bother with that tiny 1 per cent.

From my point of view wouldn’t it be great to get all the ALA, EPA and DHA into your diet to create the maximum benefit.

And also, are there ways to extract more DHA from the ALA source? I think having some DHA and EPA already in your diet helps maximise this conversion. I also have a theory that the conversion of ALA into DHA is increased through making it water soluble with the amino acids in the protein. But that’s just a theory.

So in summary, with Flaxseed Oil, we have a fat derived from a plant made up of mostly these alpha-linolenic, linoleic and oleic acids. Fat derived from plants are deuterium depleted which is a good thing right?

### Frankie78 — 2019-02-22T01:51:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oil-and-protein-mixture-recipe-an-experiment.23182/post-263759>

**Whey Protein Powder**

Now what I am talking about is 100% whey with no additives. It’s the one that doesn’t mix the best and it doesn’t taste good by itself. Just like the flaxseed oil it tastes crap by itself.

You can get WPC and WPI, types you can get grass fed cows ones in different countries NZ, USA, Australia, Europe etc.... Some have different type of processing but I am looking to get the best buck in terms of price and positive health effect. So here is what I use. It’s just sourced from either Australia or NZ grass fed cows. This is breakdown of what is in it:

**Typical Nutritional Information**

Natural Per 100g

Energy (kJ) 554

Calories 370

Protein (dry basis) 91.6g

Protein (as is) 88.0g

Fat 0.84g

\- saturated 0.29g

Carbohydrates 2.0g

\- sugars 0.7g

Sodium 200mg

**Amino Acids per 100g of Protein**

**Branch Chain Amino Acids Other Amino Acids**

Isoleucine 6.8g Arginine 2.0g

Leucine 10.2g Cystine 2.4g

Valine 6.1g Glycine 1.9g

Acids Proline 6.4g

Tyrosine 2.5g

**Other Essential Amino** A**cids** Aspartic Acid 11.5g

Lysine 8.9g Serine 4.8g

Methionine 2.0g Glutamic Acid 15.7g

Phenylalanine 3.0g Alanine 5.9g

Threonine 7.7g Histidine 1.2g

Tryptophan 0.9g

So look at this at high level. Here you have what they call one of them most bio available sources of protein. And we’ve got more acids. We’ve got acids everywhere!

But this is almost opposite of the Flaxseed Oil.

We’ve got these acids contained in a high protein source.

We’ve got all these **essential amino acids** from (**Whey Protein**) and we’ve got all these **fatty acids** (**Flaxseed Oil**). One is from a plant (fat) and the other is from grass fed cows (protein).

### Frankie78 — 2019-02-22T01:52:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oil-and-protein-mixture-recipe-an-experiment.23182/post-263760>

Now going back to the ALA in Flaxseed Oil it is highly unsaturated and it has three double bonds in its 18-carbon chain.

So according to Budwig from “**Electron Biology and Resonance Absorption of Solar Electrons” A lecture held on 17 June 1966.**

“we come to the fatty chain acids with 18 links, and can observe that the links are, in places, not firmly attached to each other. The chain is fragile there, it absorbs water easily – as if you were to fray a smooth silk thread in one place and draw it through water. The frayed part absorbs more water, or dye, more easily. In the same way, these fatty chain acids with their weak, unsaturated connections, form protein associations very easily. The fatty acids become soluble through this association with protein”.

“The moment two unsaturated double links occur together in a fatty acid chain, the effects are multiplied and in the highly unsaturated fats, the so called ‘linoleic’ acids, there is generated a field of electrons, a veritable charge which can be quickly conducted off into the body, thus causing a recharging of the living substance – especially the brain and nerves.”

And from:

**The Fats Syndrome and Photons as Solar Energy –** _A lecture held on 6 April 1972 at the 8th “Vie et Action” (Life and Action) Congress in Tours, 5-9 April 1972._

“The electrons in our food serve as the resonance system for the sun’s energy. The electrons in our food are truly the element of life. Their electro-magnetic field attracts the photons in sunlight. These photons with their own vitality and continual movement, without the physicist cannot imagine life existing, these photons, which are in resonance with the electrons in seed oils, are focused on the same wavelength as the sun’s energy, serve the life element. This interplay of solar energy photons and the electrons in seed oils, which are focused to the very last quantum on solar energy photons, governs all the vital functions.

“Fats which are in accord with solar energy photons, electron rich fats in resonance with the solar energy wavelength, do indeed govern the entire vital function systems. In my work it is fascinating to see that my utilization of the relativity theory’s related quantum physics illustrates how each and every individual vital function’s dynamics are dominated by this exchange of electrons.”

“How can you reach the peak of your development?

Freeing yourself from the influences and effects of **radiation** and from **environmental factors** which promote development into the “anti-Mensch”, seem important. These goals, set by the individual who chooses, or by the state and food industries with their organisation and planning, should be able to see that food we eat consists of electron-rich nutrition. An electron-rich food intake which supplies us with the resonance system for our sun’s energy, must once more achieve priority. Such food, as the life element, promotes our sun-attuned energy. This in turns promotes our development in space and time, into the future. The entire self can grow and continue to develop further until, in accordance with the laws of nature which govern light and life, the highest level of our being is achieved.”

### Frankie78 — 2019-02-22T01:52:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oil-and-protein-mixture-recipe-an-experiment.23182/post-263761>

**Natural Greek Yoghurt**

These yoghurts are thick and creamy and again you can them with no or minimal additives. By itself this tastes crap too.

We use this yoghurt so we can mix in more oil and powder. It helps solidify the mixture and it’s simply a thick based milk bacteria.

If you know anything about Greek Yoghurt you will know that a by-product of this is whey.

So we’re putting them back together again to create something better than the individual ingredients. It has some carbohydrates and some proteins. Again this product comes from grass fed cows.

I’ve experimented with Quark and Cottage Cheese also. These have higher amounts of protein. But I’ve found the Greek Yoghurt to be cheaper and I can easily adjust the protein mix with the Whey Powder. Notice how little carbs are in this and in the overall oil protein mix.

Ingredients Milk, Cream, Live Cultures (Acidophilus, Bifidus, Casei)

Ave. Qty per 100g

Energy 520 kJ

Protein 3.4g

Fat, total 10.4g

-   Saturated 7.1 g

Carbohydrate 4.3 g

-   Sugars 4.3 g

Sodium 33 mg

Calcium 111 mg

### Frankie78 — 2019-02-22T01:53:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oil-and-protein-mixture-recipe-an-experiment.23182/post-263763>

**Honey**

By adding a small drop of honey, I am introducing a natural sugar into the mix.

Just a small amount is needed and you can really taste it. It adds a small amount of flavour.

### Frankie78 — 2019-02-22T01:54:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oil-and-protein-mixture-recipe-an-experiment.23182/post-263764>

**Summary**

The Whey and Yoghurt help emulsify the flaxseed oil. When mixing the Flaxseed Oil, Whey Protein Powder the oil disappears. All these acids which are rich in electrons are mixed together. When you mix it the oil in no longer seen and you get this nice creamy texture.

The great thing is you can adjust all three ratios to create a mixture that best suits your electron and protein needs.

And the carbs are not coming from any plant source, only from the sugars that are fermented by the bacteria in Yoghurt.

As you can see by taking carbs out of the equation you can focus only on the fats and proteins for this mixture. If you have a little seafood and get some marine Omega 3, you will be able to magnify the effects.

Combine this with good water and sunlight you’ll be able to make the most from the sunlight and photons hitting your skin.

You can experiment with oil quantities and protein amounts to make a mixture that really suits your needs. You can search online for the so called proper mixture ratios that Johanna created but you can also experiment.

-   Going out in full sunlight in summer and not doing much exercise well bump up the flaxseed oil slightly more.

-   Not going out, it’s dark and winter, well cut down on the flaxseed oil part and add slightly more whey protein and adjust yoghurt level.

-   Heavily exercising, in full sun well add right ratio of flaxseed oil, Greek yoghurt, whey powder and honey but bump up the total quantity consumed. Maybe increase honey.

-   Heavily weight exercising and no sun, bump up whey protein levels and lower down flaxseed amount and add more Yoghurt.

-   Understand your body and experiment by taking it for breakfast, lunch or dinner. Adjust mixtures and quantities to suit.

-   Also play around with having different types of fatty fish and oysters etc…Get some marine Omega 3’s into your diet also.

-   Look at the sunlight environment you’re in and when you’ve built up enough solar callus see how you go being exposed to full sunlight in summer on this mixture.

-   Play around with adding a little Coconut Oil.

Are you concerned about the amount of Omega 6’s in the flaxseed oil? Well if you’re on a standard diet, yes it may concern you. However if you’re watching what you put into your body carefully you won’t have this issue. I never worried about ratios and I think that Omega 6’s from good sources that are integrated with other proteins, acids and healthy amounts of Omega 3 and 9 are good for you. I also eat mixed nuts and I especially like pumpkin seeds.

I’m probably right on the edge of playing around with this oil protein mixture. I’ve had great results from it combining it with things I have learnt from these forums.

Would be interested to hear from anyone else who tried this oil protein diet or heard of Johanna Budwig.

Also would be interested to know what other think of this mixture?

### Frankie78 — 2019-02-22T02:08:57+0200

>

> We’ve got all these **essential amino acids** from (**Whey Protein**) and we’ve got all these **fatty acids** (**Flaxseed Oil**). One is from a plant (fat) and the other is from grass fed cows (protein).

# Thread: Blue light filtering NOT recommended for inplanted lenses - Yay!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-filtering-not-recommended-for-inplanted-lenses-yay.23185/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glenn A — 2019-02-22T14:35:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-filtering-not-recommended-for-inplanted-lenses-yay.23185/post-263796>

A review just published yesterday, Feb. 21, 2019, of 51 studies across 17 countries attempting meta-analysis could not come to a conclusion on the advisability of using blue light filtering implanted lenses, and thus recommended against their use, stating further that designing a proper study on this subject will probably be so expensive that it may never be done.

[https://jamanetwork.com/journals/ja...ophthalmology&utm\_content=olf&utm\_term=022119](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamaophthalmology/fullarticle/2725500?guestAccessKey=1a25510a-006d-4d22-af56-0bcb76424e42&utm_source=silverchair&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=article_alert-jamaophthalmology&utm_content=olf&utm_term=022119)

Thankfully, there was an inability, due to many factors, to combine data across the studies; plus, as usual, the analysis found many deficiencies in the subject studies including insufficient quantifying if data. Only 2 of the 51 studies were even listed on a clinical trial registry. A glaring conclusion of this study was that, at least for this subject, there was a disastrous waste of money on the subject trials.

The study results were summarized as:

"**Conclusions and Relevance** Using blue light–filtering IOLs to impart benefits to the macula is currently not supported by the best available clinical research evidence, and it is important that clinicians are mindful of this evidence limitation when adopting these devices in clinical practice."

So it seems IOL recipients may be spared having the beneficial effect of the sun's blue spectrum blocked if their surgeons stay up to date on the latest recommendations.

# Thread: COMT gene mutation, dopamine, and CT.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comt-gene-mutation-dopamine-and-ct.23188/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MattD1995 — 2019-02-23T02:11:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comt-gene-mutation-dopamine-and-ct.23188/post-263842>

I finally have a clue as to why CT has been causing me so much paranoia. I had genetic testing done and found out I have a gene called COMT that causes my brain to break down dopamine much slower than the average person. CT raises dopamine and norepinephrine tremendously. I'm trying to figure out how to move forward based on this knowledge. I know that CT is essential for healing, but the elevated dopamine/norepinephrine has really been messing with my mind. Any thoughts?

### Loretta — 2019-03-29T14:55:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comt-gene-mutation-dopamine-and-ct.23188/post-266733>

I am also COMT v158m ++ and have not noticed any negative effects. There is definitely an increase in dopamine which is better than impulsive behaviors for the increase. How are you doing?

### LieselK — 2019-03-29T15:28:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comt-gene-mutation-dopamine-and-ct.23188/post-266736>

I feel like the answer is always- "get more sun". Things can get really complicated, but the bottom lines are easy. Basically you need to turn off expression of that gene if it even is being expressed right? How do we foster that? Sun, water, magnetism... maybe back off on CT until you can increase sun or only do CT IN the sun?

### drezy — 2019-03-29T16:09:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comt-gene-mutation-dopamine-and-ct.23188/post-266741>

Don't forget the "light you let in your life" also hints toward emf too. If you've got high population density and see 12 neighbors routers who knows what that gene will be experiencing. Not what people want to hear, I know...

### Nardia — 2019-03-30T00:14:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comt-gene-mutation-dopamine-and-ct.23188/post-266795>

I had genetic testing done and found out I have a gene called COMT that causes my brain to break down dopamine much slower than the average person. CT raises dopamine and norepinephrine tremendously. I'm trying to figure out how to move forward based on this knowledge. I know that CT is essential for healing, but the elevated dopamine/norepinephrine has really been messing with my mind. Any thoughts?

MattD1995 I, like many others have the same gene mutation. CT hasn't caused this problem for me either but we are all individuals and there may be something else involved in what you are experiencing. Do you take magnesium L-Threonate? I have found this helpful as it is able to cross the BBB

### Stephen W — 2019-03-30T07:03:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comt-gene-mutation-dopamine-and-ct.23188/post-266823>

I’m COMT V158M +/+ and COMT H62H +/+ and my biggest issue was trying to get used to the cold. The adrenaline hit is actually very welcome to me and all my major troubles disappear (nasal stuffiness) when I get an adrenaline kick.
So these gene things seem a bit backwards between me and you even though we share the same.
[@LieselK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21017/) you are right regarding sun I believe. I gave up CT for a few weeks because I just could not tolerate the cold baths, constant shivering. During the last month I got as much sun as possible, at least 15000 IU every day (using vitamin D app ‘Dminder’) and I’ve since returned to CT and am amazed at how much better my cold intolerance has become!
So maybe it was the sun. I just done 5 min at 4.5°C and love it very minimal shivering after. Where as I would have shivered for 2 hours previously.

### MattD1995 — 2019-04-01T07:52:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comt-gene-mutation-dopamine-and-ct.23188/post-267005>

Thank ya’ll for all the responses! Sorry I’m just seeing this.

I did some more reading on CT. I saw Jack recommends supplementation of betaine HCL, B12, and B6 when starting CT. I was curious as to why, so I dug deeper into the comments on one of his CT articles. Turns out the body can be dumping estrogen during the initial stages of CT. I guess that could explain my awful mood swings. I’ll be making another go at it using the recommended supplements this time + more sub exposure since spring is here!

### malc0088 — 2019-04-25T20:37:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/comt-gene-mutation-dopamine-and-ct.23188/post-268912>

I have the same mutation and have had periods of time when i couldn't tolerate CT...until I stopped CTing indoors and took my tub out into the back yard under direct sunlight. Now, i CT in direct sunlight, get out and warm up in the sun, get back into the tub and cool down, etc....I even CT in the evening at dusk and start up our fire pit and use the IR light from the fire pit to warm myself up after a cold CT session. These experiences are better than any meditation experience i've ever had...pure bliss. Best sleep, best libido, etc. I do find that I need to rotate through the 3 legged stool and if i overdo one aspect it doesn't seem to be as blissful.

# Thread: Blue blockers and screen temp.  app -> midtones become PINK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-and-screen-temp-app-midtones-become-pink.23189/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Andrew Marshall — 2019-02-23T05:41:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-and-screen-temp-app-midtones-become-pink.23189/post-263849>

Hello, thanks for the add ! I'm new here but I've had a question now for weeks. I quickly took my blockers off at work a few days ago when it finally dawned on me that the neutral midtones (shades of grey) were being made pretty freakin pink. Then noticed that Chrome apps and Windows 10 settings, basically identical results. PINK.

So like I said I am new on this site but I've watched the full John Ott documentary, Jack's 2016 and 2017 lectures. Each of those at least twice. So you can see what the question is, basically what the heck to do ? Is this a problem?

The only potential workaround I have so far is setting the color temp back to normal ( or taking the glasses off) while using some amount of IR backlighting placed off-center to the field of vision by, maybe 20-30 deg. But then there are huge gaps in the visible spetrum. I'm not sure anyone could know the result of this. So I haven't made any changes these are just ideas. Wondering what you all think ?

I have also changed to the darkest themes I can on Windows 10, and chrome, MS Office etc.. and I really wish there was a way, with a site such as the one we are at right now, to invert the colors. I think this would result in much less net blue light. Until I get an answer I'm also downloading videos straight to mp3. to listen, rather than gaze into the addictive death trap. More enjoyabe that way too.

About me. I was severly EHS. Now I'm moderately EHS, that is to say, I cannot 'feel' microwave radiation anymore. I had to check a phone with a meter the other day to verify it was out of airplane. So shocked it was pegging the meter and I couldn't feel it. I could not tell ! So it's exciting some degree of progress after 2 years, 30 dB shielded room. The whole deal. I still sick and I'm in a world of \_\_\_\_\_ to be honest, here at the 40th latitude, stones throw to 5G city. I couldn't hike in 2018 beyond 0.6 miles without getting weak, dizzy, low BP confused.. can barely work, I had to take a severance offer after just 20 years of service. But they are holding me until 10/31. Late release date. I don't think I will make it another winter here. The only relief I got last summer was a beach grounding with no signal 30 miles away. I went there about 4 times a week and I think it saved or at least prolonged my life. Jack your 2016 and 2017 talks truly resonate. A MILLION thanks. I'm trying to convince my family to run for cover, at least south of Atlanta because Hollick says that's where the UV year-round cutoff point is. I have almost no free cash because I've been sick for years, much has been wasted and the severance won't come until 45 days past 10/31

Any advice is welcome and appreciated.

### caroline — 2019-02-23T09:05:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-and-screen-temp-app-midtones-become-pink.23189/post-263852>

welcome Andrew......

### Andrew Marshall — 2019-02-23T16:40:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-and-screen-temp-app-midtones-become-pink.23189/post-263859>

Thank you Caroline. So if I can ask a simple question instead. Anyone, what is the next best video I can convert to mp3 and burn into my memory, such as the 2 Vermont ones mentioned above ? I feel like I need to get going, specific how to begin steps, such as proper DHA loading for example.

### Andrew Marshall — 2019-02-23T16:41:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-and-screen-temp-app-midtones-become-pink.23189/post-263860>

I am sensitized to junk radio frequency emittions from laptops and electronics. so besides the light issue I can't tolerate looking at a screen or being near for long periods of time. So I won't be able to spend hours on end reading this or any other site. I'm not trying to be dramatic, but the life-sucking feeling many of you probably get after a 2 or 3 days on a computer I feel in about 10 minutes.

### Penny — 2019-02-28T00:21:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/broken-hearts-in-the-microwave-age.23202/post-264103>

Has anyone ever walked up to a 5G tower with a pulsometer ($12.00 on Amazon) and seen the effect on oxygen? I mean, we can all agree we need oxygen right? A video like that has a chance of going viral if it's short (people do not care and do not want to know) with a short message like "We can all agree we need oxygen, right?"

Once you get into Del Gado and all the rest, you are viewed as a paranoid quack - something we want to avoid - so we should use the same methods as the Telcos and the cigarette makers before them - we plant the seed of doubt... with simple cause and effect stuff - tough but not with blood samples and darkfield microscopy...

We have hard data on the blindness factor - wouldn't it be great if there was a study on wifi's effect on vitamin a and melanopsin? Paint an image of a baby carriage sailing by one of these towers and the effect on the pedestrian's eyes...

But, truly, if you watched the proceedings in Michigan where 3 doctors testified, it does not bode well... they made their point, assuming anyone on that panel even read the tons of info they provided - people have zero focus these days so I think it has to be done in short sound bites -

This is all to sway a city council BTW...

# Thread: Low EMF wifi router?

### Pablo — 2019-02-26T23:19:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-emf-wifi-router.23204/post-264002>

Obviously it is far better to have no wifi at all, I know. It´s just that in the city house my (stupid in this regard) family won´t give up on it. I´m planning to get this "low EMF router" which supposedly reduces up to 90% of the EMF while maintainimg signal (I don´t know how that is done or if it´s possible, I´m still fairly ignorant here).

Soo... the idea would be to get this and put the wifi only when my stupid family members would use their tablets/phones with it, and most of the time having it off...

IS THIS LEGIT?? Is it at least not worse than a regular router?? Thanks!!

[https://www.jrseco.com/p/jrs-eco-wifi-01a/](https://www.jrseco.com/p/jrs-eco-wifi-01a/)

### jewishhacker — 2019-02-26T23:53:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-emf-wifi-router.23204/post-264006>

I know this brand is well respected by some in the community, but have not dug into it. I think basically hes buying routers that allow opensource software to be used on it, then he is changing the way the router works to some extent. I believe one of the big ones is that he is reducing the amount of times the wifi router pings out looking for new devices or being "visible" to new devices. Rather than the 50-100 times per second or whatever it is, it is much less. I could be wrong tho.

### Pablo — 2019-02-27T18:10:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-emf-wifi-router.23204/post-264060>

Yeah, it says it transmits the signal once every second.

I need to see if it is compatible with any service provider (I guess it is, but I´m null with tech haha) and then I´ll probably get it (unless someone advices me against it... I´m quite skeptic towards most gadgets right now).

Thanks!

# Thread: Seeing UVA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-uva.23210/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kayja — 2019-02-27T17:57:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-uva.23210/post-264058>

I’m curious, why do UVA lightbulbs appear purple? If they’re supposed to be the invisible part of the spectrum, they should be invisible, no? Same with IRA bulbs.
Is that evidence that the eyes slow down UV light?

### drezy — 2019-02-27T18:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-uva.23210/post-264062>

Cool question.

Pretty much every light source other than a laser will have a spectrum and is often spec'ed with a "Spectral Bandwidth at 50%".

This is a spectrum of a UV LED:
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Just judging by eye 50% of it's power is about 10nm wide (360-370nm).

If you looks down at 10% intensity, though, you will see nearly 380nm light.

The same is true on the IR side of the spectrum as well where IR leaks "down" into our spectrum UV leaks "up" into our visible spectrum.

FWIW I can see my 840nm IR LEDs, but cannot tell if my 950nm LEDis on unless I touch it and feel the heat..

### Kayja — 2019-02-27T19:24:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-uva.23210/post-264072>

Thanks! That makes a lot of sense. Really cool to know about your IR bulb! Had no idea there were ones that don't emit visible light. I've only ever used 1 kind/brand of infrared bulb (used to use a SaunaSpace IR sauna, still plan on using the light panel for indoor lighting on my work area. When I get my own place that is)

I can do some digging but off the top of anyone's head do you have the specific frequencies of UV and IR that life absorbs? Like, is there an easy and simple chart that's not that huge poster I have somewhere? Like, I'd like a simple chart telling me what the aromatic amino acids absorb (that one I remember was in a recent blog post so should be easy for me to find on my own), cytochromes, water, hemoglobin & porphyrins, etc.
Thinking in terms of optimizing an indoor float tank and general artificial lighting what wavelengths are more critical to get within the UV and IR spectra

### drezy — 2019-02-27T19:41:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-uva.23210/post-264074>

I removed all lights from my wife's home office and installed 2 250W IR lights. Minor complaints came through at first, but over time she grew to like the warmth she feels from them. So the wife ends up with better sleep, in a better mood, and wears less clothing. A husband can work with those variables.

The absorption info is all over this forum. Here's one mention:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/questions-about-deuterium.22975/#post-261336](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/questions-about-deuterium.22975/#post-261336)

You can start there and dig.
